{
  "file_path": "lisa/chunk.c",
  "content": "#include \"chunk.h\"\n#include <stdlib.h>\n\nvoid lisa_value_array_init(lisa_value_array *arr) {\n    arr->count = 0;\n    arr->capacity = 0;\n    arr->values = NULL;\n}\n\nvoid lisa_value_array_write(lisa_value_array *arr, lisa_value value) {\n    if (arr->count >= arr->capacity) {\n        arr->capacity = arr->capacity < 8 ? 8 : arr->capacity * 2;\n        arr->values = realloc(arr->values, sizeof(lisa_value) * (size_t)arr->capacity);\n    }\n    arr->values[arr->count++] = value;\n}\n\nvoid lisa_value_array_free(lisa_value_array *arr) {\n    free(arr->values);\n    lisa_value_array_init(arr);\n}\n\nvoid lisa_chunk_init(lisa_chunk *chunk) {\n    chunk->count = 0;\n    chunk->capacity = 0;\n    chunk->code = NULL;\n    chunk->lines = NULL;\n    lisa_value_array_init(&chunk->constants);\n}\n\nvoid lisa_chunk_write(lisa_chunk *chunk, uint8_t byte, int line) {\n    if (chunk->count >= chunk->capacity) {\n        chunk->capacity = chunk->capacity < 8 ? 8 : chunk->capacity * 2;\n        chunk->code = realloc(chunk->code, sizeof(uint8_t) * (size_t)chunk->capacity);\n        chunk->lines = realloc(chunk->lines, sizeof(int) * (size_t)chunk->capacity);\n    }\n    chunk->code[chunk->count] = byte;\n    chunk->lines[chunk->count] = line;\n    chunk->count++;\n}\n\nvoid lisa_chunk_free(lisa_chunk *chunk) {\n    free(chunk->code);\n    free(chunk->lines);\n    lisa_value_array_free(&chunk->constants);\n    lisa_chunk_init(chunk);\n}\n\nint lisa_chunk_add_constant(lisa_chunk *chunk, lisa_value value) {\n    lisa_value_array_write(&chunk->constants, value);\n    return chunk->constants.count - 1;\n}\n"
}

{
  "file_path": "lisa/debug.c",
  "content": "#include \"debug.h\"\n#include \"object.h\"\n#include <stdio.h>\n\nstatic int simple_instruction(const char *name, int offset) {\n    printf(\"%s\\n\", name);\n    return offset + 1;\n}\n\nstatic int byte_instruction(const char *name, lisa_chunk *chunk, int offset) {\n    uint8_t slot = chunk->code[offset + 1];\n    printf(\"%-20s %4d\\n\", name, slot);\n    return offset + 2;\n}\n\nstatic int constant_instruction(const char *name, lisa_chunk *chunk, int offset) {\n    uint8_t idx = chunk->code[offset + 1];\n    printf(\"%-20s %4d '\", name, idx);\n    lisa_print_value(chunk->constants.values[idx]);\n    printf(\"'\\n\");\n    return offset + 2;\n}\n\nstatic int jump_instruction(const char *name, int sign, lisa_chunk *chunk, int offset) {\n    uint8_t lo = chunk->code[offset + 1];\n    uint8_t hi = chunk->code[offset + 2];\n    uint16_t jump = (uint16_t)(lo | (hi << 8));\n    printf(\"%-20s %4d -> %d\\n\", name, offset, offset + 3 + sign * jump);\n    return offset + 3;\n}\n\nvoid lisa_disassemble_chunk(lisa_chunk *chunk, const char *name) {\n    printf(\"== %s ==\\n\", name);\n    int offset = 0;\n    while (offset < chunk->count) {\n        offset = lisa_disassemble_instruction(chunk, offset);\n    }\n}\n\nint lisa_disassemble_instruction(lisa_chunk *chunk, int offset) {\n    printf(\"%04d \", offset);\n\n    if (offset > 0 && chunk->lines[offset] == chunk->lines[offset - 1]) {\n        printf(\"   | \");\n    } else {\n        printf(\"%4d \", chunk->lines[offset]);\n    }\n\n    uint8_t instruction = chunk->code[offset];\n    switch (instruction) {\n    case OP_CONSTANT:      return constant_instruction(\"OP_CONSTANT\", chunk, offset);\n    case OP_NIL:           return simple_instruction(\"OP_NIL\", offset);\n    case OP_TRUE:          return simple_instruction(\"OP_TRUE\", offset);\n    case OP_FALSE:         return simple_instruction(\"OP_FALSE\", offset);\n    case OP_POP:           return simple_instruction(\"O

{
  "file_path": "lisa/object.h",
  "content": "#ifndef LISA_OBJECT_H\n#define LISA_OBJECT_H\n\n#include \"value.h\"\n#include \"chunk.h\"\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n\ntypedef enum {\n    OBJ_STRING,\n    OBJ_FUNCTION,\n    OBJ_CLOSURE,\n    OBJ_UPVALUE,\n    OBJ_LIST,\n    OBJ_NATIVE,\n} lisa_obj_type;\n\nstruct lisa_obj {\n    lisa_obj_type type;\n    bool is_marked;\n    struct lisa_obj *next;\n};\n\ntypedef struct {\n    lisa_obj obj;\n    int length;\n    uint32_t hash;\n    char chars[];   /* flexible array */\n} lisa_obj_string;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    lisa_obj obj;\n    int arity;\n    int upvalue_count;\n    lisa_chunk chunk;\n    lisa_obj_string *name;\n} lisa_obj_function;\n\ntypedef struct lisa_obj_upvalue {\n    lisa_obj obj;\n    lisa_value *location;\n    lisa_value closed;\n    struct lisa_obj_upvalue *next;\n} lisa_obj_upvalue;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    lisa_obj obj;\n    lisa_obj_function *function;\n    lisa_obj_upvalue **upvalues;\n    int upvalue_count;\n} lisa_obj_closure;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    lisa_obj obj;\n    lisa_value car;\n    lisa_value cdr;\n} lisa_obj_list;\n\ntypedef lisa_value (*lisa_native_fn)(int argc, lisa_value *args);\n\ntypedef struct {\n    lisa_obj obj;\n    lisa_native_fn function;\n    const char *name;\n    int arity; /* -1 for variadic */\n} lisa_obj_native;\n\n/* Type checks */\n#define OBJ_TYPE(value)    (AS_OBJ(value)->type)\n#define IS_STRING(value)   (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_STRING)\n#define IS_FUNCTION(value) (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_FUNCTION)\n#define IS_CLOSURE(value)  (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_CLOSURE)\n#define IS_NATIVE(value)   (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_NATIVE)\n#define IS_LIST_OBJ(value) (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_LIST)\n\n/* Cast macros */\n#define AS_STRING(value)   ((lisa_obj_string*)AS_OBJ(value))\n#define AS_CSTRING(value)  (((lisa_obj_string*)AS_OBJ(value))->chars)\n#define AS_FUNCTION(value) ((lisa_obj_funct

{
  "file_path": "lisa/object.c",
  "content": "#include \"object.h\"\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n\n/* --- Hashing --- */\n\nstatic uint32_t hash_string(const char *key, int length) {\n    uint32_t hash = 2166136261u;\n    for (int i = 0; i < length; i++) {\n        hash ^= (uint8_t)key[i];\n        hash *= 16777619u;\n    }\n    return hash;\n}\n\n/* --- Allocation helpers --- */\n\nstatic lisa_obj *allocate_object(lisa_gc *gc, size_t size, lisa_obj_type type) {\n    lisa_obj *obj = malloc(size);\n    obj->type = type;\n    obj->is_marked = false;\n    obj->next = gc->objects;\n    gc->objects = obj;\n    gc->bytes_allocated += size;\n    return obj;\n}\n\n#define ALLOCATE_OBJ(gc, type, obj_type) \\\n    ((type*)allocate_object(gc, sizeof(type), obj_type))\n\n/* --- String interning --- */\n\nstatic void string_table_grow(lisa_gc *gc) {\n    int new_cap = gc->string_capacity < 8 ? 8 : gc->string_capacity * 2;\n    lisa_obj_string **new_table = calloc((size_t)new_cap, sizeof(lisa_obj_string*));\n\n    /* Rehash */\n    for (int i = 0; i < gc->string_capacity; i++) {\n        lisa_obj_string *s = gc->strings[i];\n        if (s == NULL) continue;\n        int idx = (int)(s->hash % (uint32_t)new_cap);\n        while (new_table[idx] != NULL) {\n            idx = (idx + 1) % new_cap;\n        }\n        new_table[idx] = s;\n    }\n\n    free(gc->strings);\n    gc->strings = new_table;\n    gc->string_capacity = new_cap;\n}\n\nstatic lisa_obj_string *string_table_find(lisa_gc *gc, const char *chars, int length, uint32_t hash) {\n    if (gc->string_count == 0) return NULL;\n\n    int idx = (int)(hash % (uint32_t)gc->string_capacity);\n    for (;;) {\n        lisa_obj_string *s = gc->strings[idx];\n        if (s == NULL) return NULL;\n        if (s->length == length && s->hash == hash &&\n            memcmp(s->chars, chars, (size_t)length) == 0) {\n            return s;\n        }\n        idx = (idx + 1) % gc->string_capacity;\n    }\n}\n
... (truncated)

{
  "file_path": "lisa/lexer.h",
  "content": "#ifndef LISA_LEXER_H\n#define LISA_LEXER_H\n\ntypedef enum {\n    TOKEN_LPAREN,\n    TOKEN_RPAREN,\n    TOKEN_LBRACKET,\n    TOKEN_RBRACKET,\n\n    TOKEN_NUMBER,\n    TOKEN_DOUBLE,\n    TOKEN_STRING,\n    TOKEN_SYMBOL,\n\n    TOKEN_TRUE,\n    TOKEN_FALSE,\n    TOKEN_NIL,\n    TOKEN_DEF,\n    TOKEN_FN,\n    TOKEN_LET,\n    TOKEN_IF,\n    TOKEN_DO,\n\n    TOKEN_ERROR,\n    TOKEN_EOF,\n} lisa_token_type;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    lisa_token_type type;\n    const char *start;\n    int length;\n    int line;\n} lisa_token;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    const char *start;\n    const char *current;\n    int line;\n} lisa_lexer;\n\nvoid lisa_lexer_init(lisa_lexer *lexer, const char *source);\nlisa_token lisa_lexer_next(lisa_lexer *lexer);\n\n#endif\n"
}

Write — lisa/lexer.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/lexer.c",
  "content": "#include \"lexer.h\"\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n\nvoid lisa_lexer_init(lisa_lexer *lexer, const char *source) {\n    lexer->start = source;\n    lexer->current = source;\n    lexer->line = 1;\n}\n\nstatic bool is_at_end(lisa_lexer *lexer) {\n    return *lexer->current == '\\0';\n}\n\nstatic char advance(lisa_lexer *lexer) {\n    return *lexer->current++;\n}\n\nstatic char peek(lisa_lexer *lexer) {\n    return *lexer->current;\n}\n\nstatic lisa_token make_token(lisa_lexer *lexer, lisa_token_type type) {\n    lisa_token token;\n    token.type = type;\n    token.start = lexer->start;\n    token.length = (int)(lexer->current - lexer->start);\n    token.line = lexer->line;\n    return token;\n}\n\nstatic lisa_token error_token(lisa_lexer *lexer, const char *message) {\n    lisa_token token;\n    token.type = TOKEN_ERROR;\n    token.start = message;\n    token.length = (int)strlen(message);\n    token.line = lexer->line;\n    return token;\n}\n\nstatic void skip_whitespace(lisa_lexer *lexer) {\n    for (;;) {\n        char c = peek(lexer);\n        switch (c) {\n        case ' ':\n        case '\\t':\n        case '\\r':\n        case ',': /* commas are whitespace in Clojure */\n            advance(lexer);\n            break;\n        case '\\n':\n            lexer->line++;\n            advance(lexer);\n            break;\n        case ';': /* line comment */\n            while (!is_at_end(lexer) && peek(lexer) != '\\n') {\n                advance(lexer);\n            }\n            break;\n        default:\n            return;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nstatic bool is_digit(char c) {\n    return c >= '0' && c <= '9';\n}\n\nstatic bool is_symbol_char(char c) {\n    if (c == '\\0') return false;\n    if (c <= ' ') return false;\n    switch (c) {\n    case '(': case ')': case '[': case ']':\n    case '\"': case ';': case ',':\n        return false;\n    default:\n        return true;\n    }\n}\n\nstatic lisa_token
... (truncated)

{
  "file_path": "lisa/ast.h",
  "content": "#ifndef LISA_AST_H\n#define LISA_AST_H\n\n#include <stdint.h>\n\ntypedef enum {\n    AST_INT_LIT,\n    AST_DOUBLE_LIT,\n    AST_STRING_LIT,\n    AST_BOOL_LIT,\n    AST_NIL_LIT,\n    AST_SYMBOL,\n    AST_CALL,       /* (op args...) */\n    AST_DEF,        /* (def name value) */\n    AST_FN,         /* (fn [params] body...) */\n    AST_LET,        /* (let [bindings] body...) */\n    AST_IF,         /* (if cond then else?) */\n    AST_DO,         /* (do exprs...) */\n} lisa_ast_type;\n\ntypedef struct lisa_ast lisa_ast;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    lisa_ast **items;\n    int count;\n    int capacity;\n} lisa_ast_list;\n\nstruct lisa_ast {\n    lisa_ast_type type;\n    int line;\n    union {\n        int64_t int_val;\n        double double_val;\n        struct { const char *start; int length; } string_val;\n        int bool_val;\n        struct { const char *start; int length; } symbol;\n        struct { lisa_ast *callee; lisa_ast_list args; } call;\n        struct { lisa_ast *name; lisa_ast *value; } def;\n        struct { lisa_ast_list params; lisa_ast_list body; } fn;\n        struct { lisa_ast_list bindings; lisa_ast_list body; } let;\n        struct { lisa_ast *cond; lisa_ast *then_branch; lisa_ast *else_branch; } if_expr;\n        struct { lisa_ast_list exprs; } do_block;\n    } as;\n};\n\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_int(int64_t value, int line);\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_double(double value, int line);\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_string(const char *start, int length, int line);\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_bool(int value, int line);\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_nil(int line);\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_symbol(const char *start, int length, int line);\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_call(lisa_ast *callee, int line);\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_def(lisa_ast *name, lisa_ast *value, int line);\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_fn(int line);\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_let(int line);\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_if(lisa_ast *cond, lisa_ast *then_b, lisa_ast *else_b, int line);\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_do(int line);\n\nvoid lisa_a

{
  "file_path": "lisa/ast.c",
  "content": "#include \"ast.h\"\n#include <stdlib.h>\n\nstatic lisa_ast *alloc_node(lisa_ast_type type, int line) {\n    lisa_ast *node = calloc(1, sizeof(lisa_ast));\n    node->type = type;\n    node->line = line;\n    return node;\n}\n\nvoid lisa_ast_list_init(lisa_ast_list *list) {\n    list->items = NULL;\n    list->count = 0;\n    list->capacity = 0;\n}\n\nvoid lisa_ast_list_push(lisa_ast_list *list, lisa_ast *node) {\n    if (list->count >= list->capacity) {\n        list->capacity = list->capacity < 4 ? 4 : list->capacity * 2;\n        list->items = realloc(list->items, sizeof(lisa_ast*) * (size_t)list->capacity);\n    }\n    list->items[list->count++] = node;\n}\n\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_int(int64_t value, int line) {\n    lisa_ast *node = alloc_node(AST_INT_LIT, line);\n    node->as.int_val = value;\n    return node;\n}\n\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_double(double value, int line) {\n    lisa_ast *node = alloc_node(AST_DOUBLE_LIT, line);\n    node->as.double_val = value;\n    return node;\n}\n\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_string(const char *start, int length, int line) {\n    lisa_ast *node = alloc_node(AST_STRING_LIT, line);\n    node->as.string_val.start = start;\n    node->as.string_val.length = length;\n    return node;\n}\n\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_bool(int value, int line) {\n    lisa_ast *node = alloc_node(AST_BOOL_LIT, line);\n    node->as.bool_val = value;\n    return node;\n}\n\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_nil(int line) {\n    return alloc_node(AST_NIL_LIT, line);\n}\n\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_symbol(const char *start, int length, int line) {\n    lisa_ast *node = alloc_node(AST_SYMBOL, line);\n    node->as.symbol.start = start;\n    node->as.symbol.length = length;\n    return node;\n}\n\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_call(lisa_ast *callee, int line) {\n    lisa_ast *node = alloc_node(AST_CALL, line);\n    node->as.call.callee = callee;\n    lisa_ast_list_init(&node->as.call.args);\n    return node;\n}\n\nlisa_ast *lisa_ast_def(lisa_ast *name, lisa_ast *value, int l
... (truncated)

  "file_path": "lisa/parser.h",
  "content": "#ifndef LISA_PARSER_H\n#define LISA_PARSER_H\n\n#include \"lexer.h\"\n#include \"ast.h\"\n#include <stdbool.h>\n\ntypedef struct {\n    lisa_lexer lexer;\n    lisa_token current;\n    lisa_token previous;\n    bool had_error;\n    bool panic_mode;\n} lisa_parser;\n\nvoid lisa_parser_init(lisa_parser *parser, const char *source);\n\n/* Parse a single expression. Returns NULL on error. */\nlisa_ast *lisa_parse_expr(lisa_parser *parser);\n\n/* Parse all top-level expressions until EOF. Returns count, fills array. */\nlisa_ast **lisa_parse(lisa_parser *parser, int *count);\n\n/* Free an array returned by lisa_parse */\nvoid lisa_parse_free(lisa_ast **exprs, int count);\n\n#endif\n"
}

Write — lisa/parser.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/parser.c",
  "content": "#include \"parser.h\"\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <math.h>\n\nstatic void parser_advance(lisa_parser *parser);\nstatic lisa_ast *expression(lisa_parser *parser);\n\nstatic void error_at(lisa_parser *parser, lisa_token *token, const char *message) {\n    if (parser->panic_mode) return;\n    parser->panic_mode = true;\n    parser->had_error = true;\n\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[line %d] Error\", token->line);\n    if (token->type == TOKEN_EOF) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \" at end\");\n    } else if (token->type != TOKEN_ERROR) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \" at '%.*s'\", token->length, token->start);\n    }\n    fprintf(stderr, \": %s\\n\", message);\n}\n\nstatic void error(lisa_parser *parser, const char *message) {\n    error_at(parser, &parser->previous, message);\n}\n\nstatic void error_at_current(lisa_parser *parser, const char *message) {\n    error_at(parser, &parser->current, message);\n}\n\nvoid lisa_parser_init(lisa_parser *parser, const char *source) {\n    lisa_lexer_init(&parser->lexer, source);\n    parser->had_error = false;\n    parser->panic_mode = false;\n    parser_advance(parser);\n}\n\nstatic void parser_advance(lisa_parser *parser) {\n    parser->previous = parser->current;\n    for (;;) {\n        parser->current = lisa_lexer_next(&parser->lexer);\n        if (parser->current.type != TOKEN_ERROR) break;\n        error_at_current(parser, parser->current.start);\n    }\n}\n\nstatic bool check(lisa_parser *parser, lisa_token_type type) {\n    return parser->current.type == type;\n}\n\nstatic bool match(lisa_parser *parser, lisa_token_type type) {\n    if (!check(parser, type)) return false;\n    parser_advance(parser);\n    return true;\n}\n\nstatic void consume(lisa_parser *parser, lisa_token_type type, const char *message) {\n    if (parser->current.type == type) {\n        parser_advance(parser);\n        return;\n    }\n    error_at_current

}
result
File created successfully at: lisa/compiler.h
⚙ Edit

The lisa_local struct uses lisa_token for the name — let me change it to use raw char*/length instead to avoid depending on lexer.h in the compiler header.

Edit — lisa/compiler.h
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.h",
  "old_string": "typedef struct {\n    lisa_token name;\n    int depth;\n    bool is_captured;\n} lisa_local;",
  "new_string": "typedef struct {\n    const char *name;\n    int name_length;\n    int depth;\n    bool is_captured;\n} lisa_local;"
}

{
  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.c",
  "content": "#include \"compiler.h\"\n#include \"chunk.h\"\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n\nstatic bool had_error = false;\n\nstatic void compile_error(int line, const char *message) {\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[line %d] Compile error: %s\\n\", line, message);\n    had_error = true;\n}\n\n/* --- Compiler state --- */\n\nstatic void init_compiler(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_compiler *enclosing,\n                          lisa_function_type type, lisa_gc *gc, const char *name) {\n    c->enclosing = enclosing;\n    c->type = type;\n    c->gc = gc;\n    c->local_count = 0;\n    c->scope_depth = 0;\n    c->function = lisa_new_function(gc);\n\n    if (name != NULL) {\n        c->function->name = lisa_copy_string(gc, name, (int)strlen(name));\n    }\n\n    /* Reserve slot 0 for the function itself */\n    lisa_local *local = &c->locals[c->local_count++];\n    local->depth = 0;\n    local->is_captured = false;\n    local->name = \"\";\n    local->name_length = 0;\n}\n\nstatic lisa_chunk *current_chunk(lisa_compiler *c) {\n    return &c->function->chunk;\n}\n\n/* --- Emit helpers --- */\n\nstatic void emit_byte(lisa_compiler *c, uint8_t byte, int line) {\n    lisa_chunk_write(current_chunk(c), byte, line);\n}\n\nstatic void emit_bytes(lisa_compiler *c, uint8_t a, uint8_t b, int line) {\n    emit_byte(c, a, line);\n    emit_byte(c, b, line);\n}\n\nstatic void emit_return(lisa_compiler *c, int line) {\n    emit_byte(c, OP_NIL, line);\n    emit_byte(c, OP_RETURN, line);\n}\n\nstatic uint8_t make_constant(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_value value, int line) {\n    int idx = lisa_chunk_add_constant(current_chunk(c), value);\n    if (idx > 255) {\n        compile_error(line, \"Too many constants in one chunk.\");\n        return 0;\n    }\n    return (uint8_t)idx;\n}\n\nstatic void emit_constant(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_value value, int line) {\n    emit_bytes(c, OP_CONSTANT, make_constant(c, value, line), line);\n}\n\nstatic int
... (truncated)

{
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.h",
  "content": "#ifndef LISA_VM_H\n#define LISA_VM_H\n\n#include \"value.h\"\n#include \"object.h\"\n#include \"chunk.h\"\n\n#define STACK_MAX 4096\n#define FRAMES_MAX 256\n\ntypedef struct {\n    lisa_obj_closure *closure;\n    uint8_t *ip;\n    lisa_value *slots; /* pointer into vm stack */\n} lisa_call_frame;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    lisa_call_frame frames[FRAMES_MAX];\n    int frame_count;\n\n    lisa_value stack[STACK_MAX];\n    lisa_value *stack_top;\n\n    /* Global variables: hash table of string -> value */\n    lisa_obj_string **global_names;\n    lisa_value *global_values;\n    int global_count;\n    int global_capacity;\n\n    lisa_obj_upvalue *open_upvalues;\n\n    lisa_gc gc;\n} lisa_vm;\n\ntypedef enum {\n    INTERPRET_OK,\n    INTERPRET_COMPILE_ERROR,\n    INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR,\n} lisa_interpret_result;\n\nvoid lisa_vm_init(lisa_vm *vm);\nvoid lisa_vm_free(lisa_vm *vm);\nlisa_interpret_result lisa_interpret(lisa_vm *vm, const char *source);\n\n#endif\n"
}

Write — lisa/vm.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "content": "#include \"vm.h\"\n#include \"compiler.h\"\n#include \"parser.h\"\n#include \"debug.h\"\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <math.h>\n\n/* --- Stack operations --- */\n\nstatic void push(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value value) {\n    *vm->stack_top = value;\n    vm->stack_top++;\n}\n\nstatic lisa_value pop(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    vm->stack_top--;\n    return *vm->stack_top;\n}\n\nstatic lisa_value peek(lisa_vm *vm, int distance) {\n    return vm->stack_top[-1 - distance];\n}\n\nstatic void reset_stack(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    vm->stack_top = vm->stack;\n    vm->frame_count = 0;\n    vm->open_upvalues = NULL;\n}\n\nstatic void runtime_error(lisa_vm *vm, const char *format, ...) {\n    va_list args;\n    va_start(args, format);\n    vfprintf(stderr, format, args);\n    va_end(args);\n    fputs(\"\\n\", stderr);\n\n    for (int i = vm->frame_count - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\n        lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[i];\n        lisa_obj_function *fn = frame->closure->function;\n        size_t offset = (size_t)(frame->ip - fn->chunk.code - 1);\n        int line = fn->chunk.lines[offset];\n        fprintf(stderr, \"[line %d] in \", line);\n        if (fn->name == NULL) {\n            fprintf(stderr, \"script\\n\");\n        } else {\n            fprintf(stderr, \"%s()\\n\", fn->name->chars);\n        }\n    }\n\n    reset_stack(vm);\n}\n\n/* --- Globals hash table --- */\n\nstatic void globals_grow(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    int new_cap = vm->global_capacity < 8 ? 8 : vm->global_capacity * 2;\n    lisa_obj_string **new_names = calloc((size_t)new_cap, sizeof(lisa_obj_string*));\n    lisa_value *new_values = calloc((size_t)new_cap, sizeof(lisa_value));\n\n    for (int i = 0; i < vm->global_capacity; i++) {\n        if (vm->global_names[i] == NULL) continue;\n        uint32_t idx = vm->global_names[i]->hash % (uint32_t)new_cap;\n        while (new_names[idx] != NULL) {\n            idx = (idx + 1) % (uint32_t)new_cap;\n

Write — lisa/main.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/main.c",
  "content": "#include \"vm.h\"\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n\nstatic char *read_file(const char *path) {\n    FILE *file = fopen(path, \"rb\");\n    if (file == NULL) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"Could not open file '%s'.\\n\", path);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    fseek(file, 0, SEEK_END);\n    long size = ftell(file);\n    rewind(file);\n\n    char *buffer = malloc((size_t)size + 1);\n    size_t bytes_read = fread(buffer, 1, (size_t)size, file);\n    buffer[bytes_read] = '\\0';\n\n    fclose(file);\n    return buffer;\n}\n\nstatic int run_string(const char *source) {\n    lisa_vm vm;\n    lisa_vm_init(&vm);\n    lisa_interpret_result result = lisa_interpret(&vm, source);\n    lisa_vm_free(&vm);\n\n    if (result == INTERPRET_COMPILE_ERROR) return 65;\n    if (result == INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR) return 70;\n    return 0;\n}\n\nstatic int run_file(const char *path) {\n    char *source = read_file(path);\n    if (source == NULL) return 74;\n    int result = run_string(source);\n    free(source);\n    return result;\n}\n\nint main(int argc, char *argv[]) {\n    if (argc == 2) {\n        return run_file(argv[1]);\n    }\n\n    if (argc == 3 && strcmp(argv[1], \"-e\") == 0) {\n        return run_string(argv[2]);\n    }\n\n    fprintf(stderr, \"Usage: lisa <file.lisa>\\n\");\n    fprintf(stderr, \"       lisa -e \\\"<expression>\\\"\\n\");\n    return 64;\n}\n"
}
result

}

Clean build. Now let me run the verification tests.

Bash — Test basic arithmetic: should print 3
{

  "description": "Test basic arithmetic: should print 3"
}
result
3
Bash — Test closure call: should print 42

  "description": "Test closure call: should print 42"
}
result
42
Bash — Test upvalue capture: should print 8
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '(def f (fn [x] (fn [y] (+ x y)))) (println ((f 5) 3))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test upvalue capture: should print 8"
}
result
8
Bash — Test list ops: should print 1
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e \"(println (car (list 1 2 3)))\" 2>&1",
  "description": "Test list ops: should print 1"
}
result
3
⚙ Read, Grep

The list operation is returning 3 instead of 1. The OP_LIST is building the list in the wrong order. Let me check the issue — the items are on the stack in order [1, 2, 3] and we peek from the top, so peek(vm, 0) is 3 (top), peek(vm, 2) is 1 (bottom). The cons is building from right to left, but the peek indices need to go from bottom (highest distance) to top (lowest distance).

Read — lisa/vm.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "offset": 300,
  "limit": 20
}
result
300→        case OP_GET_GLOBAL: {
   301→            lisa_obj_string *name = AS_STRING(READ_CONSTANT());
   302→            int idx = globals_find(vm, name);
   303→            if (idx == -1) {
   304→                runtime_error(vm, "Undefined variable '%s'.", name->chars);
   305→                return INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR;
   306→            }
   307→            push(vm, vm->global_values[idx]);
   308→            break;
   309→        }
   310→        case OP_DEF_GLOBAL: {
   311→            lisa_obj_string *name = AS_STRING(READ_CONSTANT());
   312→            globals_set(vm, name, peek(vm, 0));
   313→            pop(vm);
   314→            break;
   315→        }
   316→
   317→        case OP_ADD: {
   318→            lisa_value b = peek(vm, 0);
   319→            lisa_value a = peek(vm, 1);
Grep — case OP_LIST:
{
  "pattern": "case OP_LIST:",
  "path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "output_mode": "content",
  "-n": true,
  "-A": 12
}
result
512:        case OP_LIST: {
513-            int n = READ_BYTE();
514-            /* Build list from right to left: (list a b c) => cons(a, cons(b, cons(c, nil))) */
515-            lisa_value result = LISA_NIL;
516-            for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
517-                lisa_value item = peek(vm, i);
518-                result = LISA_OBJ(lisa_new_list(&vm->gc, item, result));
519-            }
520-            /* Pop all N items */
521-            vm->stack_top -= n;
522-            push(vm, result);
523-            break;
524-        }
⚙ Edit, Bash

The issue is the peek indices. Stack has items pushed in order: 1, 2, 3. So peek(vm, 0) = 3, peek(vm, 1) = 2, peek(vm, 2) = 1. To build (1 2 3) we need cons(1, cons(2, cons(3, nil))). Building right-to-left:

- Start with nil

- i=2: peek(vm, 2) = 1? No, wait. With n=3, i goes from 2 to 0.

- i=2: peek(vm, 2) = item at position stack_top-3 = 1. cons(1, nil).

- i=1: peek(vm, 1) = item at position stack_top-2 = 2. cons(2, cons(1, nil)).

That's building the list backwards. We want to cons from the LAST element first. The loop should go from i=0 (top of stack = last element pushed = 3) upward.

Edit — lisa/vm.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "old_string": "        case OP_LIST: {\n            int n = READ_BYTE();\n            /* Build list from right to left: (list a b c) => cons(a, cons(b, cons(c, nil))) */\n            lisa_value result = LISA_NIL;\n            for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\n                lisa_value item = peek(vm, i);\n                result = LISA_OBJ(lisa_new_list(&vm->gc, item, result));\n            }",
  "new_string": "        case OP_LIST: {\n            int n = READ_BYTE();\n            /* Build list: (list a b c) => cons(a, cons(b, cons(c, nil)))\n             * Stack has items in push order: [..., a, b, c] where c is on top.\n             * peek(0)=c, peek(1)=b, peek(2)=a.\n             * Build from right (top of stack) to left. */\n            lisa_value result = LISA_NIL;\n            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {\n                lisa_value item = peek(vm, i);\n                result = LISA_OBJ(lisa_new_list(&vm->gc, item, result));\n            }"
}
result

⚙ Bash

Now test the recursive factorial.

Bash — Test full example program
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '\n(def factorial\n  (fn [n]\n    (if (<= n 1)\n      1\n      (* n (factorial (- n 1))))))\n\n(def make-adder (fn [x] (fn [y] (+ x y))))\n(def add5 (make-adder 5))\n\n(println (factorial 10))\n(println (add5 3))\n(println (car (list 1 2 3)))\n\n(let [x 10 y 20]\n  (println (+ x y)))\n' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test full example program"
}
result
Exit code 139
Stack overflow.

Stack overflow.
⚙ Bash

Stack overflow on factorial. The issue is likely in compile_let — the let body's result handling is leaving the stack in a bad state, or the recursive factorial is causing the issue. Let me test factorial alone.

Bash — Test recursive factorial alone
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '\n(def factorial\n  (fn [n]\n    (if (<= n 1)\n      1\n      (* n (factorial (- n 1))))))\n(println (factorial 10))\n' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test recursive factorial alone"
}
result
3628800
⚙ Bash ×2

Factorial works. The issue is with the let expression. Let me test it specifically.

Bash — Test let binding alone
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '(let [x 10 y 20] (println (+ x y)))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test let binding alone"
}
result
30
Bash — Test closures
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '\n(def make-adder (fn [x] (fn [y] (+ x y))))\n(def add5 (make-adder 5))\n(println (add5 3))\n' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test closures"
}
result
Exit code 139
Stack overflow.

Stack overflow.
⚙ Bash

The closure with make-adder is causing stack overflow. This is likely because the inner closure returned from make-adder is getting called, and something about the stack is off. Let me try a simpler closure test.

Bash — Test closure call (worked earlier)
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '(def f (fn [x] (fn [y] (+ x y)))) (println ((f 5) 3))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test closure call (worked earlier)"
}
result
8
⚙ Bash

That works but the make-adder version doesn't. The difference is that make-adder stores the closure in a variable add5 and then calls add5. Let me narrow it down.

Bash — Test: print add5 to see its value
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '\n(def make-adder (fn [x] (fn [y] (+ x y))))\n(def add5 (make-adder 5))\n(println add5)\n' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test: print add5 to see its value"
}
result
Exit code 138
Unknown opcode 232.

Unknown opcode 232.
⚙ Read, Grep

Unknown opcode — something is corrupting the bytecode. The issue might be in how def works: compile_def doesn't push a value on the stack for the expression result, since DEF_GLOBAL pops. But def at the top level is treated as an expression. Let me check.

In the compiler's compile_expr, when we hit AST_DEF, we call compile_def which emits DEF_GLOBAL that pops the value. But the main compile loop does OP_POP between top-level expressions. So after (def make-adder ...), the stack is popped once too many.

The issue is that DEF_GLOBAL pops the value, so def doesn't leave anything on the stack, but the main loop between expressions emits an extra OP_POP. Let me check the compiler.

Read — lisa/compiler.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.c",
  "offset": 340,
  "limit": 20
}
result
340→    for (int i = 0; i < params->count; i++) {
   341→        lisa_ast *param = params->items[i];
   342→        add_local(&fn_compiler, param->as.symbol.start, param->as.symbol.length, line);
   343→    }
   344→
   345→    /* Body */
   346→    lisa_ast_list *body = &node->as.fn.body;
   347→    for (int i = 0; i < body->count; i++) {
   348→        compile_expr(&fn_compiler, body->items[i]);
   349→        if (i < body->count - 1) {
   350→            emit_byte(&fn_compiler, OP_POP, body->items[i]->line);
   351→        }
   352→    }
   353→
   354→    /* Last expression is the return value */
   355→    emit_byte(&fn_compiler, OP_RETURN, line);
   356→
   357→    lisa_obj_function *fn = fn_compiler.function;
   358→
   359→    /* Emit closure instruction in the enclosing compiler */
Grep — lisa_obj_function \*lisa_compile
{
  "pattern": "lisa_obj_function \\*lisa_compile",
  "path": "lisa/compiler.c",
  "output_mode": "content",
  "-n": true,
  "-A": 20
}
result
738:lisa_obj_function *lisa_compile(lisa_gc *gc, lisa_ast **exprs, int count) {
739-    had_error = false;
740-    lisa_compiler c;
741-    init_compiler(&c, NULL, TYPE_SCRIPT, gc, NULL);
742-
743-    for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
744-        compile_expr(&c, exprs[i]);
745-        if (i < count - 1) {
746-            emit_byte(&c, OP_POP, exprs[i]->line);
747-        }
748-    }
749-
750-    if (count == 0) {
751-        emit_byte(&c, OP_NIL, 1);
752-    }
753-
754-    emit_byte(&c, OP_RETURN, count > 0 ? exprs[count-1]->line : 1);
755-
756-    if (had_error) return NULL;
757-    return c.function;
758-}
⚙ Grep, Edit

The problem is clear: compile_def emits DEF_GLOBAL which pops the value from the stack, leaving nothing. Then between expressions the main loop emits OP_POP, which pops something that shouldn't be popped, corrupting the stack.

The fix: def should push nil after defining (so it leaves a value on the stack like every other expression).

Grep — static void compile_def
{
  "pattern": "static void compile_def",
  "path": "lisa/compiler.c",
  "output_mode": "content",
  "-n": true,
  "-A": 10
}
result
178:static void compile_def(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node) {
179-    const char *name = node->as.def.name->as.symbol.start;
180-    int length = node->as.def.name->as.symbol.length;
181-    uint8_t global = identifier_constant(c, name, length);
182-
183-    compile_expr(c, node->as.def.value);
184-    emit_bytes(c, OP_DEF_GLOBAL, global, node->line);
185-}
186-
187-/* Check if a symbol AST matches a given string */
188-static bool sym_eq(lisa_ast *node, const char *s) {
Edit — lisa/compiler.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.c",
  "old_string": "    compile_expr(c, node->as.def.value);\n    emit_bytes(c, OP_DEF_GLOBAL, global, node->line);\n}",
  "new_string": "    compile_expr(c, node->as.def.value);\n    emit_bytes(c, OP_DEF_GLOBAL, global, node->line);\n    /* def is an expression that produces nil */\n    emit_byte(c, OP_NIL, node->line);\n}"
}
result
The file lisa/compiler.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Bash ×2

Similarly, println pushes nil (already handled in VM). Let me also check that the let compile correctly handles the expression value. But first, let me rebuild and test.

Bash — Rebuild
{
  "command": "make dev 2>&1 && echo \"---BUILD OK---\"",
  "description": "Rebuild"
}
result
mkdir -p bin
cc main.c value.c object.c lexer.c parser.c ast.c chunk.c compiler.c vm.c debug.c -o bin/lisa -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wno-gnu -Werror -g -O0 -lm
---BUILD OK---

{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '\n(def factorial\n  (fn [n]\n    (if (<= n 1)\n      1\n      (* n (factorial (- n 1))))))\n\n(def make-adder (fn [x] (fn [y] (+ x y))))\n(def add5 (make-adder 5))\n\n(println (factorial 10))\n(println (add5 3))\n(println (car (list 1 2 3)))\n\n(let [x 10 y 20]\n  (println (+ x y)))\n' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test full example program"
}

⚙ Read ×2

Now let me clean up the compiler.c — the compile_let function has extensive comments from the design process that should be trimmed.

Read — lisa/compiler.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.c",
  "offset": 370,
  "limit": 200
}
result
370→
   371→static void compile_let(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node) {
   372→    int line = node->line;
   373→    begin_scope(c);
   374→
   375→    lisa_ast_list *bindings = &node->as.let.bindings;
   376→    /* bindings come in pairs: name, value, name, value, ... */
   377→    for (int i = 0; i < bindings->count; i += 2) {
   378→        lisa_ast *name = bindings->items[i];
   379→        lisa_ast *val = bindings->items[i + 1];
   380→        compile_expr(c, val);
   381→        add_local(c, name->as.symbol.start, name->as.symbol.length, line);
   382→    }
   383→
   384→    /* Body */
   385→    lisa_ast_list *body = &node->as.let.body;
   386→    for (int i = 0; i < body->count; i++) {
   387→        compile_expr(c, body->items[i]);
   388→        if (i < body->count - 1) {
   389→            emit_byte(c, OP_POP, body->items[i]->line);
   390→        }
   391→    }
   392→
   393→    /* The result of the let is the last body expression.
   394→     * We need to keep it on the stack but pop the locals.
   395→     * Strategy: store result in a temp position, pop locals, push result back.
   396→     * Simpler: use end_scope which pops locals, but our result is on top.
   397→     * We need to close upvalues for the let-bound locals though.
   398→     * Let's manually unwind: pop the result, end scope, then the result is gone.
   399→     * Actually: the last body expr is on top of stack. If we end_scope, we pop
   400→     * the locals beneath it, which actually pops the WRONG values. The stack at
   401→     * this point is: [... bindings... result]. We need to keep result, pop bindings.
   402→     *
   403→     * We'll do the simple approach: store result in a local, end scope drops it.
   404→     * Actually the simplest: don't use end_scope's POP emission, do it manually.
   405→     */
   406→
   407→    /* The value of the let is on top of the stack. The locals are below it.
   408→     * We need to close upvalues and remove the local slots while keeping the top.
   409→     * The approach: pop the last expression result into a temporary, close scope,
   410→     * push it back. But we don't have a temp mechanism.
   411→     *
   412→     * Alternative: manually close/pop all locals except the result on top. */
   413→
   414→    int local_count_before = c->local_count;
   415→    c->scope_depth--;
   416→    while (c->local_count > 0 &&
   417→           c->locals[c->local_count - 1].depth > c->scope_depth) {
   418→        c->local_count--;
   419→    }
   420→    int locals_to_pop = local_count_before - c->local_count;
   421→
   422→    /* The stack is: [...outer... local0 local1 ... localN result]
   423→     * We need to get result and pop locals_to_pop slots beneath it.
   424→     * Emit a sequence: for each local, we close or pop from under the top. */
   425→
   426→    /* We can't easily pop "from under". Instead, let's use GET_LOCAL to copy the
   427→     * result, then pop everything including the old result, then w
... (truncated)
Read — lisa/compiler.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.c",
  "offset": 570,
  "limit": 120
}
result
570→     * 3. The result stays
   571→     *
   572→     * Actually let me look at this from the VM perspective:
   573→     * Stack: [base... let_local_0, let_local_1, ..., let_local_{N-1}, body_result]
   574→     * I want: [base... body_result]
   575→     *
   576→     * I'll emit: SET_LOCAL <first_let_slot>, then for each subsequent let-local
   577→     * (going from top down, i.e., locals N-1 down to 1), emit CLOSE_UPVALUE or POP.
   578→     * This removes N-1 items from the top. Now stack is:
   579→     * [base... body_result_in_let_slot0]
   580→     * This is fine! The local_count is reset to before the let. The value stays on
   581→     * stack until something else pops it. It IS the result of the let expression.
   582→     *
   583→     * Wait but this leaves an extra slot on the stack that the caller doesn't know about?
   584→     * No: the caller expects exactly one value pushed (the result of the expression).
   585→     * Before the let, suppose stack_top was at position S.
   586→     * After the let, we want stack_top at S+1 (one result pushed).
   587→     * Our approach:
   588→     *   - We pushed N locals: stack_top = S + N
   589→     *   - Body pushes result: stack_top = S + N + 1
   590→     *   - SET_LOCAL(S+0): stack_top still S + N + 1 (SET doesn't pop)
   591→     *   - POP (N-1) times: removes the extra local values, but we pop from top.
   592→     *     After first POP: stack_top = S + N (popped result? No, result was the top after SET_LOCAL)
   593→     *
   594→     * Hmm, SET_LOCAL doesn't pop in standard Lox. It peeks and sets. So:
   595→     * Stack after SET_LOCAL(slot=S): [base..., result, local1, ..., localN-1, result_copy_on_top]
   596→     * Wait no. SET_LOCAL peeks the top and stores to slot. So:
   597→     * Before: [..., local0, local1, ..., localN-1, body_result]
   598→     * SET_LOCAL(slot of local0): local0 = body_result (stack unchanged)
   599→     * Stack: [..., body_result, local1, ..., localN-1, body_result]
   600→     * Now POP N-1 times:
   601→     *   Pop body_result (top!) -> [..., body_result, local1, ..., localN-1]
   602→     *   Pop localN-1 -> [..., body_result, local1, ..., localN-2]
   603→     *   ...
   604→     * After N-1 pops: [..., body_result, local1]
   605→     * That's not right either. We have N-1 pops but need to remove (N-1 locals + 1 result copy).
   606→     * Total to remove = N. So POP N times.
   607→     * After N pops from [..., body_result, local1, ..., localN-1, body_result]:
   608→     *   [..., body_result]. Correct!
   609→     *
   610→     * So: SET_LOCAL(first_let_slot) + POP * N.
   611→     * The N POPs account for: the body_result still on top, plus N-1 remaining locals.
   612→     * After that, stack top is first_let_slot which holds body_result.
   613→     * But the compiler's local_count has been decremented, so this slot is now just
   614→     * "a value on the stack" from the compiler's perspective - exactly the result of
   615→    
... (truncated)

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.c",
  "old_string": "static void compile_let(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node) {\n    int line = node->line;\n    begin_scope(c);\n\n    lisa_ast_list *bindings = &node->as.let.bindings;\n    /* bindings come in pairs: name, value, name, value, ... */\n    for (int i = 0; i < bindings->count; i += 2) {\n        lisa_ast *name = bindings->items[i];\n        lisa_ast *val = bindings->items[i + 1];\n        compile_expr(c, val);\n        add_local(c, name->as.symbol.start, name->as.symbol.length, line);\n    }\n\n    /* Body */\n    lisa_ast_list *body = &node->as.let.body;\n    for (int i = 0; i < body->count; i++) {\n        compile_expr(c, body->items[i]);\n        if (i < body->count - 1) {\n            emit_byte(c, OP_POP, body->items[i]->line);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /* The result of the let is the last body expression.\n     * We need to keep it on the stack but pop the locals.\n     * Strategy: store result in a temp position, pop locals, push result back.\n     * Simpler: use end_scope which pops locals, but our result is on top.\n     * We need to close upvalues for the let-bound locals though.\n     * Let's manually unwind: pop the result, end scope, then the result is gone.\n     * Actually: the last body expr is on top of stack. If we end_scope, we pop\n     * the locals beneath it, which actually pops the WRONG values. The stack at\n     * this point is: [... bindings... result]. We need to keep result, pop bindings.\n     *\n     * We'll do the simple approach: store result in a local, end scope drops it.\n     * Actually the simplest: don't use end_scope's POP emission, do it manually.\n     */\n\n    /* The value of the let is on top of the stack. The locals are below it.\n     * We need to close upvalues and remove the local slots while keeping the top.\n     * The approach: pop the last expression result into a temporary, close scope,\n     * push it back. But we don't have a temp mechanism.\n

mkdir -p bin
cc main.c value.c object.c lexer.c parser.c ast.c chunk.c compiler.c vm.c debug.c -o bin/lisa -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wno-gnu -Werror -g -O0 -lm
3
---ALL GOOD---
claude

All done. Here's a summary of what was built:

Lisa — Bytecode VM for a Clojure-inspired Lisp

12 files in lisa/:

| File | Purpose | |---|---| | value.h/c | NaN-boxed value representation (int64, double, nil, bool, object pointer) | | chunk.h/c | Bytecode container with instruction stream + constant pool | | object.h/c | Heap objects (string, function, closure, upvalue, list, native) + mark-and-sweep GC | | lexer.h/c | Tokenizer for Clojure-like syntax | | ast.h/c | AST node types and constructors | | parser.h/c | Recursive descent parser for s-expressions | | compiler.h/c | AST-to-bytecode compiler with scope resolution and upvalue capture | | vm.h/c | Stack-based bytecode dispatch loop with call frames and upvalue runtime | | debug.h/c | Bytecode disassembler | | main.c | CLI entry point (lisa <file> or lisa -e "expr") | | Makefile | Build system (make dev / make all) |

All verification tests pass:

- (println (+ 1 2)) → 3

- (println ((fn [x] (+ x 1)) 41)) → 42

- Upvalue capture → 8

- (car (list 1 2 3)) → 1

- Recursive factorial(10) → 3628800

- let bindings → 30

no string literals yetg?

{

}
result
hello world
⚙ Bash

They work! String literals, string concatenation, and escape sequences are all implemented:

Bash — Test string concat, escapes, and strings in closures
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '(println (+ \"hello \" \"world\"))'\nbin/lisa -e '(println \"line1\\nline2\")'\nbin/lisa -e '(def greet (fn [name] (+ \"hello \" name))) (println (greet \"Lisa\"))'",
  "description": "Test string concat, escapes, and strings in closures"
}
result
hello world
line1
line2
hello Lisa
claude

All working — string literals, + for concatenation, escape sequences (\n), and strings as function arguments.

human

are tail calls optimized?

⚙ Bash

No, they aren't. Every call pushes a new frame, so deep recursion will overflow the stack:

Bash — Demonstrate stack overflow without TCO
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '\n(def loop (fn [n]\n  (if (<= n 0)\n    \"done\"\n    (loop (- n 1)))))\n(println (loop 100000))\n' 2>&1",
  "description": "Demonstrate stack overflow without TCO"
}
result
Exit code 70
Stack overflow.

⚙ EnterPlanMode, Task, Read
EnterPlanMode
{}
result
Entered plan mode. You should now focus on exploring the codebase and designing an implementation approach.

In plan mode, you should:
1. Thoroughly explore the codebase to understand existing patterns
2. Identify similar features and architectural approaches
3. Consider multiple approaches and their trade-offs
4. Use AskUserQuestion if you need to clarify the approach
5. Design a concrete implementation strategy
6. When ready, use ExitPlanMode to present your plan for approval

Remember: DO NOT write or edit any files yet. This is a read-only exploration and planning phase.
Task
{
  "subagent_type": "Explore",
  "description": "Explore TCO-relevant code paths",
  "prompt": "I need to add tail call optimization (TCO) to the Lisa bytecode VM. Explore the following files thoroughly and report back:\n\n1. `lisa/compiler.c` — Focus on how function calls are compiled (`compile_call`, `compile_fn`, `compile_if`) and how `OP_CALL` and `OP_RETURN` are emitted. I need to understand where a tail call can be detected (a call in tail position).\n\n2. `lisa/vm.c` — Focus on `OP_CALL` and `OP_RETURN` handling in the dispatch loop. How call frames are pushed/popped, how the stack is managed during calls and returns.\n\n3. `lisa/chunk.h` — The opcode definitions.\n\n4. `lisa/compiler.h` — The compiler struct.\n\nReport:\n- The exact code for compile_call, compile_fn, compile_if, compile_do, compile_let (how they handle tail position)\n- The exact code for OP_CALL and OP_RETURN in the VM dispatch\n- Current opcode list\n- How upvalues are closed on return\n\nThis is for planning TCO — I need to understand all the places where a call can be in tail position and how the VM handles call/return."
}
result
Excellent! I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

## Comprehensive TCO Analysis Report for Lisa Bytecode VM

Based on my thorough exploration of the Lisa codebase, here's the complete analysis for implementing tail call optimization:

---

### 1. **OPCODE DEFINITIONS** (`chunk.h`, lines 7-53)

Current opcodes:
- `OP_CONSTANT` — push constants[idx]
- `OP_NIL`, `OP_TRUE`, `OP_FALSE` — push literal values
- `OP_POP` — pop top
- `OP_GET_LOCAL`, `OP_SET_LOCAL` — local variable access
- `OP_GET_UPVALUE`, `OP_SET_UPVALUE` — closure variable access
- `OP_GET_GLOBAL`, `OP_DEF_GLOBAL` — global variable access
- `OP_ADD`, `OP_SUB`, `OP_MUL`, `OP_DIV`, `OP_MOD`, `OP_NEGATE` — arithmetic
- `OP_EQUAL`, `OP_NOT_EQUAL`, `OP_LESS`, `OP_LESS_EQUAL`, `OP_GREATER`, `OP_GREATER_EQUAL` — comparisons
- `OP_NOT` — boolean negation
- `OP_JUMP`, `OP_JUMP_IF_FALSE`, `OP_LOOP` — control flow
- `OP_CLOSURE` — create function closure
- **`OP_CALL`** (line 42) — `[argc]` call top function with argc args
- **`OP_RETURN`** (line 43) — return top of stack
- `OP_CLOSE_UPVALUE` — close upvalue at stack top
- `OP_CONS`, `OP_CAR`, `OP_CDR` — list operations
- `OP_LIST` — build list from N elements
- `OP_PRINTLN` — print with argc values

---

### 2. **COMPILE_CALL** (`compiler.c`, lines 197-331)

```c
static void compile_call(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node) {
    lisa_ast *callee = node->as.call.callee;
    lisa_ast_list *args = &node->as.call.args;
    int line = node->line;

    /* Built-in operators: compile to dedicated opcodes */
    if (callee->type == AST_SYMBOL) {
        /* Binary arithmetic */
        if (args->count == 2) {
            if (sym_eq(callee, "+")) {
                compile_expr(c, args->items[0]);
                compile_expr(c, args->items[1]);
                emit_byte(c, OP_ADD, line);
                return;
            }
            /* ... all other binary ops ... */
        }
        /* Unary ops */
        if (args->count == 1) {
            if (sym_eq(callee, "-")) { /* ... */ }
            /* ... */
        }
        /* println & list special ops */
        if (sym_eq(callee, "println")) { /* ... */ }
        if (sym_eq(callee, "list")) { /* ... */ }
    }

    /* General function call */
    compile_expr(c, callee);
    for (int i = 0; i < args->count; i++) {
        compile_expr(c, args->items[i]);
    }
    emit_bytes(c, OP_CALL, (uint8_t)args->count, line);
}
```

**Key insights:**
- Calls to user-defined functions end with `emit_bytes(c, OP_CALL, argc, line)`
- Built-in operators are compiled to specialized opcodes (OP_ADD, OP_SUB, etc.)
- **No context about whether the call is in tail position** — compiler always emits regular OP_CALL

---

### 3. **COMPILE_FN** (`compiler.c`, lines 333-369)

```c
static void compile_fn(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node) {
    int line = node->line;
    lisa_compiler fn_compiler;
    init_compiler(&fn_compiler, c, TYPE_FUNCTION, c->gc, NULL);
    begin_scope(&fn_compiler);

 
... (truncated)
Read — lisa/compiler.c

  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.c"
}
result
1→#include "compiler.h"
     2→#include "chunk.h"
     3→#include <stdio.h>
     4→#include <stdlib.h>
     5→#include <string.h>
     6→
     7→static bool had_error = false;
     8→
     9→static void compile_error(int line, const char *message) {
    10→    fprintf(stderr, "[line %d] Compile error: %s\n", line, message);
    11→    had_error = true;
    12→}
    13→
    14→/* --- Compiler state --- */
    15→
    16→static void init_compiler(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_compiler *enclosing,
    17→                          lisa_function_type type, lisa_gc *gc, const char *name) {
    18→    c->enclosing = enclosing;
    19→    c->type = type;
    20→    c->gc = gc;
    21→    c->local_count = 0;
    22→    c->scope_depth = 0;
    23→    c->function = lisa_new_function(gc);
    24→
    25→    if (name != NULL) {
    26→        c->function->name = lisa_copy_string(gc, name, (int)strlen(name));
    27→    }
    28→
    29→    /* Reserve slot 0 for the function itself */
    30→    lisa_local *local = &c->locals[c->local_count++];
    31→    local->depth = 0;
    32→    local->is_captured = false;
    33→    local->name = "";
    34→    local->name_length = 0;
    35→}
    36→
    37→static lisa_chunk *current_chunk(lisa_compiler *c) {
    38→    return &c->function->chunk;
    39→}
    40→
    41→/* --- Emit helpers --- */
    42→
    43→static void emit_byte(lisa_compiler *c, uint8_t byte, int line) {
    44→    lisa_chunk_write(current_chunk(c), byte, line);
    45→}
    46→
    47→static void emit_bytes(lisa_compiler *c, uint8_t a, uint8_t b, int line) {
    48→    emit_byte(c, a, line);
    49→    emit_byte(c, b, line);
    50→}
    51→
    52→
    53→static uint8_t make_constant(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_value value, int line) {
    54→    int idx = lisa_chunk_add_constant(current_chunk(c), value);
    55→    if (idx > 255) {
    56→        compile_error(line, "Too many constants in one chunk.");
    57→        return 0;
    58→    }
    59→    return (uint8_t)idx;
    60→}
    61→
    62→static void emit_constant(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_value value, int line) {
    63→    emit_bytes(c, OP_CONSTANT, make_constant(c, value, line), line);
    64→}
    65→
    66→static int emit_jump(lisa_compiler *c, uint8_t instruction, int line) {
    67→    emit_byte(c, instruction, line);
    68→    emit_byte(c, 0xFF, line);
    69→    emit_byte(c, 0xFF, line);
    70→    return current_chunk(c)->count - 2;
    71→}
    72→
    73→static void patch_jump(lisa_compiler *c, int offset, int line) {
    74→    int jump = current_chunk(c)->count - offset - 2;
    75→    if (jump > 65535) {
    76→        compile_error(line, "Jump too large.");
    77→        return;
    78→    }
    79→    current_chunk(c)->code[offset] = (uint8_t)(jump & 0xFF);
    80→    current_chunk(c)->code[offset + 1] = (uint8_t)((jump >> 8) & 0xFF);
    81→}
    82→
    83→/* --- Scope management --- */
    84→
    85→static void begin_scope(lisa_compiler *c) {
    86→    c->scope_depth++;
    87→}
... (truncated)

Write — <claude-internal>
{
  "file_path": "<claude-internal>",
  "content": "# Tail Call Optimization for Lisa\n\n## Context\n\nLisa currently pushes a new call frame for every function call. Self-recursive or mutually-recursive functions in tail position blow the 256-frame stack. TCO reuses the current call frame for calls in tail position, turning them into constant-space jumps.\n\n## Approach\n\nThread a `bool tail` parameter through the compiler. When a general function call is in tail position, emit `OP_TAIL_CALL` instead of `OP_CALL`. In the VM, `OP_TAIL_CALL` closes upvalues, slides the arguments down over the current frame, and resets the IP — no new frame pushed.\n\n## Files to modify\n\n- `lisa/chunk.h` — add `OP_TAIL_CALL` opcode\n- `lisa/compiler.c` — add `tail` parameter to `compile_expr` and friends; emit `OP_TAIL_CALL`\n- `lisa/vm.c` — handle `OP_TAIL_CALL` in dispatch\n- `lisa/debug.c` — disassemble `OP_TAIL_CALL`\n\n## Compiler changes (`compiler.c`)\n\nReplace `compile_expr(c, node)` signature with `compile_expr(c, node, tail)`. Add matching `_tail` variants or just pass the bool through.\n\n**Tail position rules** — `tail=true` propagates to:\n- `compile_fn`: last body expression\n- `compile_if`: both branches (when parent is tail)\n- `compile_do`: last expression (when parent is tail)\n- `compile_call`: emits `OP_TAIL_CALL` instead of `OP_CALL` when `tail=true` and it's a general call (not a built-in opcode like `+`, `println`, etc.)\n\n**Not tail:** `compile_let` body last expression. The let cleanup code (SET_LOCAL + POP sequence) sits after the body, so a tail call there would skip cleanup. Keep it simple and don't TCO inside let bodies. Arguments to calls, conditions in `if`, binding values in `let`, non-last expressions everywhere — all `tail=false`.\n\n**Top-level `lisa_compile`**: last top-level expression gets `tail=true`.\n\n## VM changes (`vm.c`)\n\nNew `OP_TAIL_CALL` handler:\n\n```\ncase OP_TAIL_CALL: {\n    int argc = READ_BYTE();\n    lisa_value callee_val 
... (truncated)

result
1→# Lisa: Bytecode VM for a Clojure-inspired Lisp
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→Build a Lisp called Lisa inside the cj JIT framework repo. Phase 1 is a bytecode interpreter in `lisa/` with its own Makefile. The bytecode design must be JIT-friendly so we can later lower ops to native code via cj. The language has Clojure-like syntax.
     6→
     7→**Types**: int64, double, string, nil, bool, symbol
     8→**Features**: def, fn, let, if, do, arithmetic/comparison, println, closures with upvalues, list ops (cons, car, cdr, list)
     9→**Interface**: CLI with file eval (`lisa foo.lisa`) and string eval (`lisa -e "(+ 1 2)"`)
    10→
    11→## Architecture
    12→
    13→```
    14→Source -> Lexer (tokens) -> Parser (AST) -> Compiler (bytecode) -> VM (interpreter)
    15→```
    16→
    17→**Stack-based VM** with NaN-boxed values (`uint64_t`) and Lua-style upvalue capture for closures. Each bytecode op does exactly one thing, making future JIT translation to cj instructions straightforward.
    18→
    19→## File Layout
    20→
    21→```
    22→lisa/
    23→  Makefile
    24→  main.c              -- CLI entry point
    25→  value.h / value.c   -- NaN-boxed value representation
    26→  object.h / object.c -- heap objects (string, function, closure, upvalue, list) + mark-and-sweep GC
    27→  lexer.h / lexer.c   -- tokenizer
    28→  ast.h / ast.c       -- AST node types and construction
    29→  parser.h / parser.c -- recursive descent parser
    30→  chunk.h / chunk.c   -- bytecode chunk (instruction stream + constant pool)
    31→  compiler.h / .c     -- AST -> bytecode with scope resolution and upvalue capture
    32→  vm.h / vm.c         -- bytecode dispatch loop, call frames, upvalue runtime
    33→  debug.h / debug.c   -- bytecode disassembler
    34→```
    35→
    36→## Key Design Decisions
    37→
    38→### NaN-boxing (`value.h`)
    39→
    40→All values are `uint64_t`. Doubles are stored as-is. Non-double values use the quiet NaN payload space:
    41→
    42→| Tag (bits 50:48) | Type | Payload |
    43→|---|---|---|
    44→| 000 | nil | none |
    45→| 001 | bool | bit 0 |
    46→| 010 | int | 48-bit sign-extended integer |
    47→| 011 | object | 48-bit pointer |
    48→
    49→Untagged (not a NaN) = double. This means values fit in machine registers for future JIT.
    50→
    51→### Object system (`object.h`)
    52→
    53→Common header (`lisa_obj`) with type tag, GC mark bit, and intrusive next pointer. Types:
    54→
    55→- `lisa_obj_string` — interned, with FNV-1a hash, flexible array member for chars
    56→- `lisa_obj_function` — compiled bytecode prototype (arity, upvalue_count, chunk, name)
    57→- `lisa_obj_closure` — function + array of captured upvalue pointers
    58→- `lisa_obj_upvalue` — Lua-style: `location` pointer (into stack while open, into `closed` field after closing)
    59→- `lisa_obj_list` — cons cell (car + cdr)
    60→- `lisa_obj_native` — C function wrapper for built-ins as first-class values

... (truncated)
Write — <claude-internal>
{
  "file_path": "<claude-internal>",
  "content": "# Tail Call Optimization for Lisa\n\n## Context\n\nLisa currently pushes a new call frame for every function call. Self-recursive or mutually-recursive functions in tail position blow the 256-frame stack. TCO reuses the current call frame for calls in tail position, turning them into constant-space jumps.\n\n## Approach\n\nThread a `bool tail` parameter through the compiler. When a general function call is in tail position, emit `OP_TAIL_CALL` instead of `OP_CALL`. In the VM, `OP_TAIL_CALL` closes upvalues, slides the arguments down over the current frame, and resets the IP — no new frame pushed.\n\n## Files to modify\n\n- `lisa/chunk.h` — add `OP_TAIL_CALL` opcode (after `OP_CALL`)\n- `lisa/compiler.c` — add `tail` parameter to `compile_expr` and callers; emit `OP_TAIL_CALL`\n- `lisa/vm.c` — handle `OP_TAIL_CALL` in dispatch loop\n- `lisa/debug.c` — disassemble `OP_TAIL_CALL`\n\n## Compiler changes (`compiler.c`)\n\nChange `compile_expr(c, node)` to `compile_expr(c, node, tail)`. Thread `tail` through:\n\n- **`compile_fn`**: last body expression gets `tail=true`\n- **`compile_if`**: both branches inherit parent's `tail`\n- **`compile_do`**: last expression inherits parent's `tail`\n- **`compile_call`**: when `tail=true` and it's a general call (not a built-in opcode), emit `OP_TAIL_CALL` instead of `OP_CALL`\n- **`compile_let`**: body is always `tail=false` — the stack cleanup code after the body prevents TCO here\n- **`lisa_compile` (top-level)**: last expression gets `tail=true`\n\nEverything else (literal, symbol, def, fn, let bindings, call arguments, `if` condition, non-last body expressions) is always `tail=false`.\n\n## VM changes (`vm.c`)\n\nNew `OP_TAIL_CALL` handler:\n1. Read argc\n2. For native functions, fall through to normal call (no frame to reuse)\n3. For closures: validate arity, close upvalues for current frame, slide callee+args down to `frame->slots`, reset `stack_top`, update `frame->closure`

session 3. tail call optimization

human

Implement the following plan:

Tail Call Optimization for Lisa

Context

Lisa currently pushes a new call frame for every function call. Self-recursive or mutually-recursive functions in tail position blow the 256-frame stack. TCO reuses the current call frame for calls in tail position, turning them into constant-space jumps.

Approach

Thread a bool tail parameter through the compiler. When a general function call is in tail position, emit OP_TAIL_CALL instead of OP_CALL. In the VM, OP_TAIL_CALL closes upvalues, slides the arguments down over the current frame, and resets the IP — no new frame pushed.

Files to modify

- lisa/chunk.h — add OP_TAIL_CALL opcode (after OP_CALL)

- lisa/compiler.c — add tail parameter to compile_expr and callers; emit OP_TAIL_CALL

- lisa/vm.c — handle OP_TAIL_CALL in dispatch loop

- lisa/debug.c — disassemble OP_TAIL_CALL

Compiler changes (compiler.c)

Change compile_expr(c, node) to compile_expr(c, node, tail). Thread tail through:

- compile_fn: last body expression gets tail=true

- compile_if: both branches inherit parent's tail

- compile_do: last expression inherits parent's tail

- compile_call: when tail=true and it's a general call (not a built-in opcode), emit OP_TAIL_CALL instead of OP_CALL

- compile_let: body is always tail=false — the stack cleanup code after the body prevents TCO here

- lisa_compile (top-level): last expression gets tail=true

Everything else (literal, symbol, def, fn, let bindings, call arguments, if condition, non-last body expressions) is always tail=false.

VM changes (vm.c)

New OP_TAIL_CALL handler:

1. Read argc

2. For native functions, fall through to normal call (no frame to reuse)

3. For closures: validate arity, close upvalues for current frame, slide callee+args down to frame->slots, reset stack_top, update frame->closure and frame->ip

Key detail: memmove the argc+1 values (callee + args) from stack_top - argc - 1 down to frame->slots, then stack_top = frame->slots + argc + 1.

Verification

1. make dev — compiles cleanly with -Werror

2. Deep self-recursion that would overflow without TCO:

```

bin/lisa -e '(def loop (fn [n] (if (<= n 0) "done" (loop (- n 1))))) (println (loop 1000000))'

```

3. All existing tests still pass (factorial, closures, let, list ops)

4. Mutual recursion:

```

bin/lisa -e '(def e (fn [n] (if (= n 0) true (o (- n 1))))) (def o (fn [n] (if (= n 0) false (e (- n 1))))) (println (e 100000))'

```

If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: <claude-internal>

⚙ Read ×4

I'll start by reading all four files that need modification in parallel.

Read — lisa/chunk.h
{
  "file_path": "lisa/chunk.h"
}
result
1→#ifndef LISA_CHUNK_H
     2→#define LISA_CHUNK_H
     3→
     4→#include "value.h"
     5→#include <stdint.h>
     6→
     7→typedef enum {
     8→    OP_CONSTANT,      /* [idx]          push constants[idx] */
     9→    OP_NIL,           /*                push nil */
    10→    OP_TRUE,          /*                push true */
    11→    OP_FALSE,         /*                push false */
    12→    OP_POP,           /*                pop top */
    13→
    14→    OP_GET_LOCAL,     /* [slot]         push stack[base+slot] */
    15→    OP_SET_LOCAL,     /* [slot]         stack[base+slot] = peek */
    16→    OP_GET_UPVALUE,   /* [idx]          push *upvalues[idx]->location */
    17→    OP_SET_UPVALUE,   /* [idx]          *upvalues[idx]->location = peek */
    18→    OP_GET_GLOBAL,    /* [idx]          push globals[constants[idx]] */
    19→    OP_DEF_GLOBAL,    /* [idx]          globals[constants[idx]] = pop */
    20→
    21→    OP_ADD,
    22→    OP_SUB,
    23→    OP_MUL,
    24→    OP_DIV,
    25→    OP_MOD,
    26→    OP_NEGATE,
    27→
    28→    OP_EQUAL,
    29→    OP_NOT_EQUAL,
    30→    OP_LESS,
    31→    OP_LESS_EQUAL,
    32→    OP_GREATER,
    33→    OP_GREATER_EQUAL,
    34→
    35→    OP_NOT,
    36→
    37→    OP_JUMP,          /* [lo][hi]       ip += offset */
    38→    OP_JUMP_IF_FALSE, /* [lo][hi]       if falsey(pop) ip += offset */
    39→    OP_LOOP,          /* [lo][hi]       ip -= offset */
    40→
    41→    OP_CLOSURE,       /* [idx] then pairs of [is_local, index] */
    42→    OP_CALL,          /* [argc]         call top function with argc args */
    43→    OP_RETURN,        /*                return top of stack */
    44→
    45→    OP_CLOSE_UPVALUE, /*                close upvalue at stack top */
    46→
    47→    OP_CONS,          /*                push cons(pop2, pop1) */
    48→    OP_CAR,           /*                push car(pop) */
    49→    OP_CDR,           /*                push cdr(pop) */
    50→    OP_LIST,          /* [n]            pop n items, build list */
    51→
    52→    OP_PRINTLN,       /* [argc]         print argc values with spaces, newline */
    53→} lisa_op;
    54→
    55→/* Dynamic array of constants */
    56→typedef struct {
    57→    int count;
    58→    int capacity;
    59→    lisa_value *values;
    60→} lisa_value_array;
    61→
    62→void lisa_value_array_init(lisa_value_array *arr);
    63→void lisa_value_array_write(lisa_value_array *arr, lisa_value value);
    64→void lisa_value_array_free(lisa_value_array *arr);
    65→
    66→/* Bytecode chunk */
    67→typedef struct {
    68→    int count;
    69→    int capacity;
    70→    uint8_t *code;
    71→    int *lines;          /* source line per bytecode byte */
    72→    lisa_value_array constants;
    73→} lisa_chunk;
    74→
    75→void lisa_chunk_init(lisa_chunk *chunk);
    76→void lisa_chunk_write(lisa_chunk *chunk, uint8_t byte, int line);
    77→void lisa_chunk_free(lisa_chunk *chunk);
    78→int lisa_chunk_add_c
... (truncated)

1→#include "compiler.h"
     2→#include "chunk.h"
     3→#include <stdio.h>
     4→#include <stdlib.h>
     5→#include <string.h>
     6→
     7→static bool had_error = false;
     8→
     9→static void compile_error(int line, const char *message) {
    10→    fprintf(stderr, "[line %d] Compile error: %s\n", line, message);
    11→    had_error = true;
    12→}
    13→
    14→/* --- Compiler state --- */
    15→
    16→static void init_compiler(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_compiler *enclosing,
    17→                          lisa_function_type type, lisa_gc *gc, const char *name) {
    18→    c->enclosing = enclosing;
    19→    c->type = type;
    20→    c->gc = gc;
    21→    c->local_count = 0;
    22→    c->scope_depth = 0;
    23→    c->function = lisa_new_function(gc);
    24→
    25→    if (name != NULL) {
    26→        c->function->name = lisa_copy_string(gc, name, (int)strlen(name));
    27→    }
    28→
    29→    /* Reserve slot 0 for the function itself */
    30→    lisa_local *local = &c->locals[c->local_count++];
    31→    local->depth = 0;
    32→    local->is_captured = false;
    33→    local->name = "";
    34→    local->name_length = 0;
    35→}
    36→
    37→static lisa_chunk *current_chunk(lisa_compiler *c) {
    38→    return &c->function->chunk;
    39→}
    40→
    41→/* --- Emit helpers --- */
    42→
    43→static void emit_byte(lisa_compiler *c, uint8_t byte, int line) {
    44→    lisa_chunk_write(current_chunk(c), byte, line);
    45→}
    46→
    47→static void emit_bytes(lisa_compiler *c, uint8_t a, uint8_t b, int line) {
    48→    emit_byte(c, a, line);
    49→    emit_byte(c, b, line);
    50→}
    51→
    52→
    53→static uint8_t make_constant(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_value value, int line) {
    54→    int idx = lisa_chunk_add_constant(current_chunk(c), value);
    55→    if (idx > 255) {
    56→        compile_error(line, "Too many constants in one chunk.");
    57→        return 0;
    58→    }
    59→    return (uint8_t)idx;
    60→}
    61→
    62→static void emit_constant(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_value value, int line) {
    63→    emit_bytes(c, OP_CONSTANT, make_constant(c, value, line), line);
    64→}
    65→
    66→static int emit_jump(lisa_compiler *c, uint8_t instruction, int line) {
    67→    emit_byte(c, instruction, line);
    68→    emit_byte(c, 0xFF, line);
    69→    emit_byte(c, 0xFF, line);
    70→    return current_chunk(c)->count - 2;
    71→}
    72→
    73→static void patch_jump(lisa_compiler *c, int offset, int line) {
    74→    int jump = current_chunk(c)->count - offset - 2;
    75→    if (jump > 65535) {
    76→        compile_error(line, "Jump too large.");
    77→        return;
    78→    }
    79→    current_chunk(c)->code[offset] = (uint8_t)(jump & 0xFF);
    80→    current_chunk(c)->code[offset + 1] = (uint8_t)((jump >> 8) & 0xFF);
    81→}
    82→
    83→/* --- Scope management --- */
    84→
    85→static void begin_scope(lisa_compiler *c) {
    86→    c->scope_depth++;
    87→}
... (truncated)

result
1→#include "vm.h"
     2→#include "compiler.h"
     3→#include "parser.h"
     4→#include <stdarg.h>
     5→#include <stdio.h>
     6→#include <stdlib.h>
     7→#include <string.h>
     8→#include <math.h>
     9→
    10→/* --- Stack operations --- */
    11→
    12→static void push(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value value) {
    13→    *vm->stack_top = value;
    14→    vm->stack_top++;
    15→}
    16→
    17→static lisa_value pop(lisa_vm *vm) {
    18→    vm->stack_top--;
    19→    return *vm->stack_top;
    20→}
    21→
    22→static lisa_value peek(lisa_vm *vm, int distance) {
    23→    return vm->stack_top[-1 - distance];
    24→}
    25→
    26→static void reset_stack(lisa_vm *vm) {
    27→    vm->stack_top = vm->stack;
    28→    vm->frame_count = 0;
    29→    vm->open_upvalues = NULL;
    30→}
    31→
    32→static void runtime_error(lisa_vm *vm, const char *format, ...) {
    33→    va_list args;
    34→    va_start(args, format);
    35→    vfprintf(stderr, format, args);
    36→    va_end(args);
    37→    fputs("\n", stderr);
    38→
    39→    for (int i = vm->frame_count - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
    40→        lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[i];
    41→        lisa_obj_function *fn = frame->closure->function;
    42→        size_t offset = (size_t)(frame->ip - fn->chunk.code - 1);
    43→        int line = fn->chunk.lines[offset];
    44→        fprintf(stderr, "[line %d] in ", line);
    45→        if (fn->name == NULL) {
    46→            fprintf(stderr, "script\n");
    47→        } else {
    48→            fprintf(stderr, "%s()\n", fn->name->chars);
    49→        }
    50→    }
    51→
    52→    reset_stack(vm);
    53→}
    54→
    55→/* --- Globals hash table --- */
    56→
    57→static void globals_grow(lisa_vm *vm) {
    58→    int new_cap = vm->global_capacity < 8 ? 8 : vm->global_capacity * 2;
    59→    lisa_obj_string **new_names = calloc((size_t)new_cap, sizeof(lisa_obj_string*));
    60→    lisa_value *new_values = calloc((size_t)new_cap, sizeof(lisa_value));
    61→
    62→    for (int i = 0; i < vm->global_capacity; i++) {
    63→        if (vm->global_names[i] == NULL) continue;
    64→        uint32_t idx = vm->global_names[i]->hash % (uint32_t)new_cap;
    65→        while (new_names[idx] != NULL) {
    66→            idx = (idx + 1) % (uint32_t)new_cap;
    67→        }
    68→        new_names[idx] = vm->global_names[i];
    69→        new_values[idx] = vm->global_values[i];
    70→    }
    71→
    72→    free(vm->global_names);
    73→    free(vm->global_values);
    74→    vm->global_names = new_names;
    75→    vm->global_values = new_values;
    76→    vm->global_capacity = new_cap;
    77→}
    78→
    79→static int globals_find(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_string *name) {
    80→    if (vm->global_capacity == 0) return -1;
    81→    uint32_t idx = name->hash % (uint32_t)vm->global_capacity;
    82→    for (;;) {
    83→        if (vm->global_names[idx] == NULL) return -1;
    84→        if (vm->global_names[idx]
... (truncated)

1→#include "debug.h"
     2→#include "object.h"
     3→#include <stdio.h>
     4→
     5→static int simple_instruction(const char *name, int offset) {
     6→    printf("%s\n", name);
     7→    return offset + 1;
     8→}
     9→
    10→static int byte_instruction(const char *name, lisa_chunk *chunk, int offset) {
    11→    uint8_t slot = chunk->code[offset + 1];
    12→    printf("%-20s %4d\n", name, slot);
    13→    return offset + 2;
    14→}
    15→
    16→static int constant_instruction(const char *name, lisa_chunk *chunk, int offset) {
    17→    uint8_t idx = chunk->code[offset + 1];
    18→    printf("%-20s %4d '", name, idx);
    19→    lisa_print_value(chunk->constants.values[idx]);
    20→    printf("'\n");
    21→    return offset + 2;
    22→}
    23→
    24→static int jump_instruction(const char *name, int sign, lisa_chunk *chunk, int offset) {
    25→    uint8_t lo = chunk->code[offset + 1];
    26→    uint8_t hi = chunk->code[offset + 2];
    27→    uint16_t jump = (uint16_t)(lo | (hi << 8));
    28→    printf("%-20s %4d -> %d\n", name, offset, offset + 3 + sign * jump);
    29→    return offset + 3;
    30→}
    31→
    32→void lisa_disassemble_chunk(lisa_chunk *chunk, const char *name) {
    33→    printf("== %s ==\n", name);
    34→    int offset = 0;
    35→    while (offset < chunk->count) {
    36→        offset = lisa_disassemble_instruction(chunk, offset);
    37→    }
    38→}
    39→
    40→int lisa_disassemble_instruction(lisa_chunk *chunk, int offset) {
    41→    printf("%04d ", offset);
    42→
    43→    if (offset > 0 && chunk->lines[offset] == chunk->lines[offset - 1]) {
    44→        printf("   | ");
    45→    } else {
    46→        printf("%4d ", chunk->lines[offset]);
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    uint8_t instruction = chunk->code[offset];
    50→    switch (instruction) {
    51→    case OP_CONSTANT:      return constant_instruction("OP_CONSTANT", chunk, offset);
    52→    case OP_NIL:           return simple_instruction("OP_NIL", offset);
    53→    case OP_TRUE:          return simple_instruction("OP_TRUE", offset);
    54→    case OP_FALSE:         return simple_instruction("OP_FALSE", offset);
    55→    case OP_POP:           return simple_instruction("OP_POP", offset);
    56→
    57→    case OP_GET_LOCAL:     return byte_instruction("OP_GET_LOCAL", chunk, offset);
    58→    case OP_SET_LOCAL:     return byte_instruction("OP_SET_LOCAL", chunk, offset);
    59→    case OP_GET_UPVALUE:   return byte_instruction("OP_GET_UPVALUE", chunk, offset);
    60→    case OP_SET_UPVALUE:   return byte_instruction("OP_SET_UPVALUE", chunk, offset);
    61→    case OP_GET_GLOBAL:    return constant_instruction("OP_GET_GLOBAL", chunk, offset);
    62→    case OP_DEF_GLOBAL:    return constant_instruction("OP_DEF_GLOBAL", chunk, offset);
    63→
    64→    case OP_ADD:           return simple_instruction("OP_ADD", offset);
    65→    case OP_SUB:           return simple_instruction("OP_SUB", offset);
    66→    c

Now I need to update compile_call to accept and use the tail parameter:

Edit — lisa/compiler.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.c",
  "old_string": "static void compile_call(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node) {\n    lisa_ast *callee = node->as.call.callee;\n    lisa_ast_list *args = &node->as.call.args;\n    int line = node->line;\n\n    /* Built-in operators: compile to dedicated opcodes */\n    if (callee->type == AST_SYMBOL) {\n        /* Binary arithmetic */\n        if (args->count == 2) {\n            if (sym_eq(callee, \"+\")) {\n                compile_expr(c, args->items[0]);\n                compile_expr(c, args->items[1]);\n                emit_byte(c, OP_ADD, line);\n                return;\n            }\n            if (sym_eq(callee, \"-\")) {\n                compile_expr(c, args->items[0]);\n                compile_expr(c, args->items[1]);\n                emit_byte(c, OP_SUB, line);\n                return;\n            }\n            if (sym_eq(callee, \"*\")) {\n                compile_expr(c, args->items[0]);\n                compile_expr(c, args->items[1]);\n                emit_byte(c, OP_MUL, line);\n                return;\n            }\n            if (sym_eq(callee, \"/\")) {\n                compile_expr(c, args->items[0]);\n                compile_expr(c, args->items[1]);\n                emit_byte(c, OP_DIV, line);\n                return;\n            }\n            if (sym_eq(callee, \"%\") || sym_eq(callee, \"mod\")) {\n                compile_expr(c, args->items[0]);\n                compile_expr(c, args->items[1]);\n                emit_byte(c, OP_MOD, line);\n                return;\n            }\n            /* Comparisons */\n            if (sym_eq(callee, \"=\") || sym_eq(callee, \"==\")) {\n                compile_expr(c, args->items[0]);\n                compile_expr(c, args->items[1]);\n                emit_byte(c, OP_EQUAL, line);\n                return;\n            }\n            if (sym_eq(callee, \"!=\") || sym_eq(callee, \"not=\")) {\n                compile_expr(c, args->items[0]);\n   
... (truncated)

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.c",
  "old_string": "    /* Body */\n    lisa_ast_list *body = &node->as.fn.body;\n    for (int i = 0; i < body->count; i++) {\n        compile_expr(&fn_compiler, body->items[i]);\n        if (i < body->count - 1) {\n            emit_byte(&fn_compiler, OP_POP, body->items[i]->line);\n        }\n    }",
  "new_string": "    /* Body */\n    lisa_ast_list *body = &node->as.fn.body;\n    for (int i = 0; i < body->count; i++) {\n        bool is_last = (i == body->count - 1);\n        compile_expr(&fn_compiler, body->items[i], is_last);\n        if (!is_last) {\n            emit_byte(&fn_compiler, OP_POP, body->items[i]->line);\n        }\n    }"
}
