Now update compile_let — bindings and body are tail=false:

Edit — lisa/compiler.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.c",
  "old_string": "    lisa_ast_list *bindings = &node->as.let.bindings;\n    for (int i = 0; i < bindings->count; i += 2) {\n        lisa_ast *name = bindings->items[i];\n        lisa_ast *val = bindings->items[i + 1];\n        compile_expr(c, val);\n        add_local(c, name->as.symbol.start, name->as.symbol.length, line);\n    }\n\n    lisa_ast_list *body = &node->as.let.body;\n    for (int i = 0; i < body->count; i++) {\n        compile_expr(c, body->items[i]);\n        if (i < body->count - 1) {\n            emit_byte(c, OP_POP, body->items[i]->line);\n        }\n    }",
  "new_string": "    lisa_ast_list *bindings = &node->as.let.bindings;\n    for (int i = 0; i < bindings->count; i += 2) {\n        lisa_ast *name = bindings->items[i];\n        lisa_ast *val = bindings->items[i + 1];\n        compile_expr(c, val, false);\n        add_local(c, name->as.symbol.start, name->as.symbol.length, line);\n    }\n\n    lisa_ast_list *body = &node->as.let.body;\n    for (int i = 0; i < body->count; i++) {\n        compile_expr(c, body->items[i], false);\n        if (i < body->count - 1) {\n            emit_byte(c, OP_POP, body->items[i]->line);\n        }\n    }"
}

⚙ Edit

Now update compile_if — both branches inherit parent's tail, condition is false:

Edit — lisa/compiler.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.c",
  "old_string": "static void compile_if(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node) {\n    int line = node->line;\n    compile_expr(c, node->as.if_expr.cond);\n\n    int then_jump = emit_jump(c, OP_JUMP_IF_FALSE, line);\n\n    compile_expr(c, node->as.if_expr.then_branch);\n    int else_jump = emit_jump(c, OP_JUMP, line);\n\n    patch_jump(c, then_jump, line);\n\n    if (node->as.if_expr.else_branch != NULL) {\n        compile_expr(c, node->as.if_expr.else_branch);\n    } else {\n        emit_byte(c, OP_NIL, line);\n    }\n\n    patch_jump(c, else_jump, line);\n}",
  "new_string": "static void compile_if(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node, bool tail) {\n    int line = node->line;\n    compile_expr(c, node->as.if_expr.cond, false);\n\n    int then_jump = emit_jump(c, OP_JUMP_IF_FALSE, line);\n\n    compile_expr(c, node->as.if_expr.then_branch, tail);\n    int else_jump = emit_jump(c, OP_JUMP, line);\n\n    patch_jump(c, then_jump, line);\n\n    if (node->as.if_expr.else_branch != NULL) {\n        compile_expr(c, node->as.if_expr.else_branch, tail);\n    } else {\n        emit_byte(c, OP_NIL, line);\n    }\n\n    patch_jump(c, else_jump, line);\n}"
}
result
The file lisa/compiler.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Now update compile_do — last expression inherits parent's tail:

Edit — lisa/compiler.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.c",
  "old_string": "static void compile_do(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node) {\n    lisa_ast_list *exprs = &node->as.do_block.exprs;\n    for (int i = 0; i < exprs->count; i++) {\n        compile_expr(c, exprs->items[i]);\n        if (i < exprs->count - 1) {\n            emit_byte(c, OP_POP, exprs->items[i]->line);\n        }\n    }\n}",
  "new_string": "static void compile_do(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node, bool tail) {\n    lisa_ast_list *exprs = &node->as.do_block.exprs;\n    for (int i = 0; i < exprs->count; i++) {\n        bool is_last = (i == exprs->count - 1);\n        compile_expr(c, exprs->items[i], is_last ? tail : false);\n        if (!is_last) {\n            emit_byte(c, OP_POP, exprs->items[i]->line);\n        }\n    }\n}"
}
result
The file lisa/compiler.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Now update the main compile_expr function to accept and pass tail:

Edit — lisa/compiler.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.c",
  "old_string": "static void compile_expr(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node) {\n    switch (node->type) {\n    case AST_INT_LIT:\n        emit_constant(c, LISA_INT(node->as.int_val), node->line);\n        break;\n    case AST_DOUBLE_LIT:\n        emit_constant(c, lisa_double(node->as.double_val), node->line);\n        break;\n    case AST_STRING_LIT:\n        compile_string_literal(c, node);\n        break;\n    case AST_BOOL_LIT:\n        emit_byte(c, node->as.bool_val ? OP_TRUE : OP_FALSE, node->line);\n        break;\n    case AST_NIL_LIT:\n        emit_byte(c, OP_NIL, node->line);\n        break;\n    case AST_SYMBOL:\n        compile_symbol(c, node);\n        break;\n    case AST_CALL:\n        compile_call(c, node);\n        break;\n    case AST_DEF:\n        compile_def(c, node);\n        break;\n    case AST_FN:\n        compile_fn(c, node);\n        break;\n    case AST_LET:\n        compile_let(c, node);\n        break;\n    case AST_IF:\n        compile_if(c, node);\n        break;\n    case AST_DO:\n        compile_do(c, node);\n        break;\n    }\n}",
  "new_string": "static void compile_expr(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node, bool tail) {\n    switch (node->type) {\n    case AST_INT_LIT:\n        emit_constant(c, LISA_INT(node->as.int_val), node->line);\n        break;\n    case AST_DOUBLE_LIT:\n        emit_constant(c, lisa_double(node->as.double_val), node->line);\n        break;\n    case AST_STRING_LIT:\n        compile_string_literal(c, node);\n        break;\n    case AST_BOOL_LIT:\n        emit_byte(c, node->as.bool_val ? OP_TRUE : OP_FALSE, node->line);\n        break;\n    case AST_NIL_LIT:\n        emit_byte(c, OP_NIL, node->line);\n        break;\n    case AST_SYMBOL:\n        compile_symbol(c, node);\n        break;\n    case AST_CALL:\n        compile_call(c, node, tail);\n        break;\n    case AST_DEF:\n        compile_def(c, node);\n        break;\n    case AST_FN:\n

480→    free(buf);
   481→    emit_constant(c, LISA_OBJ(str), node->line);
   482→}
   483→
   484→static void compile_expr(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node, bool tail) {
   485→    switch (node->type) {
   486→    case AST_INT_LIT:
   487→        emit_constant(c, LISA_INT(node->as.int_val), node->line);
   488→        break;
   489→    case AST_DOUBLE_LIT:
   490→        emit_constant(c, lisa_double(node->as.double_val), node->line);
   491→        break;
   492→    case AST_STRING_LIT:
   493→        compile_string_literal(c, node);
   494→        break;
   495→    case AST_BOOL_LIT:
   496→        emit_byte(c, node->as.bool_val ? OP_TRUE : OP_FALSE, node->line);
   497→        break;
   498→    case AST_NIL_LIT:
   499→        emit_byte(c, OP_NIL, node->line);
   500→        break;
   501→    case AST_SYMBOL:
   502→        compile_symbol(c, node);
   503→        break;
   504→    case AST_CALL:
   505→        compile_call(c, node, tail);
   506→        break;
   507→    case AST_DEF:
   508→        compile_def(c, node);
   509→        break;
   510→    case AST_FN:
   511→        compile_fn(c, node);
   512→        break;
   513→    case AST_LET:
   514→        compile_let(c, node);
   515→        break;
   516→    case AST_IF:
   517→        compile_if(c, node, tail);
   518→        break;
   519→    case AST_DO:
   520→        compile_do(c, node, tail);
   521→        break;
   522→    }
   523→}
   524→
   525→lisa_obj_function *lisa_compile(lisa_gc *gc, lisa_ast **exprs, int count) {
   526→    had_error = false;
   527→    lisa_compiler c;
   528→    init_compiler(&c, NULL, TYPE_SCRIPT, gc, NULL);
   529→
   530→    for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
   531→        bool is_last = (i == count - 1);
   532→        compile_expr(&c, exprs[i], is_last);
   533→        if (!is_last) {
   534→            emit_byte(&c, OP_POP, exprs[i]->line);
   535→        }
   536→    }
   537→
   538→    if (count == 0) {
   539→        emit_byte(&c, OP_NIL, 1);

Add the OP_TAIL_CALL handler in the VM dispatch loop, right after OP_CALL:

Edit — lisa/vm.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "old_string": "        case OP_CALL: {\n            int argc = READ_BYTE();\n            if (!call_value(vm, peek(vm, argc), argc)) {\n                return INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR;\n            }\n            frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];\n            break;\n        }",
  "new_string": "        case OP_CALL: {\n            int argc = READ_BYTE();\n            if (!call_value(vm, peek(vm, argc), argc)) {\n                return INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR;\n            }\n            frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];\n            break;\n        }\n\n        case OP_TAIL_CALL: {\n            int argc = READ_BYTE();\n            lisa_value callee = peek(vm, argc);\n\n            /* Native functions: no frame to reuse, fall through to normal call */\n            if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {\n                if (!call_value(vm, callee, argc)) {\n                    return INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR;\n                }\n                frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];\n                break;\n            }\n\n            if (!IS_OBJ(callee) || OBJ_TYPE(callee) != OBJ_CLOSURE) {\n                runtime_error(vm, \"Can only call functions and closures.\");\n                return INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR;\n            }\n\n            lisa_obj_closure *closure = AS_CLOSURE(callee);\n            if (argc != closure->function->arity) {\n                runtime_error(vm, \"Expected %d arguments but got %d.\",\n                              closure->function->arity, argc);\n                return INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR;\n            }\n\n            /* Close upvalues for the current frame */\n            close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);\n\n            /* Slide callee + args down over the current frame */\n            lisa_value *src = vm->stack_top - argc - 1;\n            memmove(frame->slots, src, (size_t)(argc + 1) * sizeof(lisa_value));\n            vm->stack_top =
... (truncated)

  "description": "Test mutual recursion (would stack overflow without TCO)"
}
result
true

claude

All done. Here's a summary of the changes:

lisa/chunk.h — Added OP_TAIL_CALL opcode after OP_CALL

lisa/compiler.c — Threaded a bool tail parameter through the compiler:

- compile_expr(c, node, tail) — dispatches tail to compile_call, compile_if, compile_do

- compile_fn — last body expression gets tail=true

- compile_if — both branches inherit parent's tail; condition is false

- compile_do — last expression inherits parent's tail

- compile_call — emits OP_TAIL_CALL instead of OP_CALL when tail=true and it's a general call (not a built-in opcode)

- compile_let — body always tail=false (stack cleanup prevents TCO)

- lisa_compile (top-level) — last expression gets tail=true

lisa/vm.c — OP_TAIL_CALL handler: for native functions falls through to normal call; for closures validates arity, closes upvalues, memmoves callee+args down to frame->slots, resets stack_top, and reuses the frame with the new closure's IP.

lisa/debug.c — Added disassembly case for OP_TAIL_CALL

Verified: deep self-recursion (1M iterations), mutual recursion (100K), tail-recursive accumulator (1M), factorial, let bindings, list ops all work correctly.

That's a big step — using cj to JIT-compile Lisa's hot paths (or the whole VM dispatch). Let me think about what that could look like.

There are several possible approaches with very different scopes. Let me ask before diving in.

AskUserQuestion
{
  "questions": [
    {
      "question": "What level of JIT integration do you have in mind?",
      "header": "JIT scope",
      "options": [
        {
          "label": "Whole-function JIT",
          "description": "Compile entire Lisa functions to native code via cj when they're called (or after a call threshold). Replaces the bytecode interpreter for JIT'd functions."
        },
        {
          "label": "Tracing / hot-loop JIT",
          "description": "Detect hot loops at runtime, trace them, and compile the trace to native code with cj. Bytecode interpreter remains the baseline."
        },
        {
          "label": "AOT-style codegen",
          "description": "At compile time (after bytecode is produced), translate the whole bytecode chunk to native code via cj. No interpreter fallback needed."
        },
        {
          "label": "Something else",
          "description": "You have a different integration model in mind."
        }
      ],
      "multiSelect": false
    }
  ]
}

Task
{
  "description": "Explore cj JIT API surface",
  "subagent_type": "Explore",
  "prompt": "Thoroughly explore the cj JIT framework in this repository, focusing on what a consumer needs to know to use it. I need to understand:\n\n1. **Builder API** (`src/builder.h`): How to create functions — prologue/epilogue, how to emit code (arithmetic, comparisons, jumps, calls to C functions), how to work with registers/scratch registers, calling convention helpers, if/else, loops, labels.\n\n2. **Context API** (`src/ctx.h`, `src/ctx.c`): How to create a JIT context, emit bytes, finalize code into executable memory (`create_cj_fn`), labels and fixups, how to get callable function pointers (`cj_resolve_label`).\n\n3. **Operands** (`src/op.h`): How operands work — registers, constants, memory operands.\n\n4. **Examples**: Look at `examples/hl_fibonacci.c` and `examples/simple.c` and `examples/add.c` to see the typical usage pattern.\n\n5. **Registers** (`src/register.h`, `src/arch/arm64/registers.h`): What registers are available, calling convention (which are callee-saved, caller-saved, argument registers).\n\nPlease provide detailed findings including function signatures, key macros, and usage patterns. Focus especially on the builder layer since that's the cross-platform API."
}
Task
{
  "description": "Explore Lisa VM internals",
  "subagent_type": "Explore",
  "prompt": "Thoroughly explore the Lisa language implementation focusing on the runtime/VM internals that would matter for JIT compilation. I need to understand:\n\n1. **Value representation** (`lisa/value.h`): How lisa_value is represented (NaN-boxing? tagged union?), all value types, macros for creating/checking/extracting values.\n\n2. **Object system** (`lisa/object.h`, `lisa/object.c`): How objects are laid out in memory — especially closures (lisa_obj_closure), functions (lisa_obj_function), upvalues (lisa_obj_upvalue), natives, strings, lists. The GC interaction.\n\n3. **VM state** (`lisa/vm.h`): The vm struct, call frames, stack layout, globals.\n\n4. **Bytecode opcodes** (`lisa/chunk.h`): Complete list and semantics.\n\n5. **How function calls work** in the VM (`lisa/vm.c`): The call_closure, call_value functions, how frames are pushed/popped, how return works, how tail calls work (OP_TAIL_CALL).\n\n6. **How closures/upvalues work**: capture_upvalue, close_upvalues, the upvalue chain.\n\n7. **GC** (`lisa/gc.h`, `lisa/gc.c`): How GC is triggered, what roots it scans, any write barriers.\n\nThe key question is: if we wanted to JIT-compile a Lisa function to native code, what VM state does the native code need to access, and what C functions does it need to call back into?"
}
result
Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary document by analyzing what I've found. Since I'm in read-only mode, I'll provide a detailed written summary to the user.

## Comprehensive Lisa Language Runtime/VM Analysis for JIT Compilation

Based on my thorough exploration of the Lisa language codebase, here's a detailed analysis of the runtime/VM internals relevant to JIT compilation:

### 1. Value Representation (NaN-Boxing)

**File:** `lisa/value.h`

Lisa uses **IEEE 754 NaN-boxing** for value representation:

```c
typedef uint64_t lisa_value;

/* The quiet NaN mask: sign(1) + exponent(0x7FF) + quiet(1) = bits 63,62:52,51 */
#define QNAN    ((uint64_t)0x7FFC000000000000)

/* Tag values shifted into bits 50:48 */
#define TAG_NIL    ((uint64_t)0x0000000000000000)  /* 000 */
#define TAG_BOOL   ((uint64_t)0x0001000000000000)  /* 001 */
#define TAG_INT    ((uint64_t)0x0002000000000000)  /* 010 */
#define TAG_OBJ    ((uint64_t)0x0003000000000000)  /* 011 */

#define TAG_MASK   ((uint64_t)0x0003000000000000)
#define PAYLOAD_MASK ((uint64_t)0x0000FFFFFFFFFFFF) /* 48 bits */
```

**Value Creation:**
- `LISA_NIL`: constant nil value
- `LISA_TRUE` / `LISA_FALSE`: boolean constants
- `LISA_BOOL(b)`: construct boolean
- `LISA_INT(i)`: 48-bit signed integer (sign-extended)
- `LISA_OBJ(ptr)`: pointer to heap object (48-bit address)
- `lisa_double(d)`: IEEE 754 double (any pattern that isn't a tagged NaN)

**Type Checks:**
- `IS_NIL(v)`, `IS_BOOL(v)`, `IS_INT(v)`, `IS_OBJ(v)`, `IS_DOUBLE(v)`

**Value Extraction:**
- `AS_BOOL(v)`: extract boolean (bit 0 of payload)
- `AS_INT(v)`: extract 48-bit signed int with sign extension
- `AS_DOUBLE(v)`: reinterpret bits as double
- `AS_OBJ(v)`: extract pointer (48-bit, zero-extended)

**Numeric Coercion:**
- `lisa_as_number(v)`: returns `double` (converts int to double if needed)
- `lisa_is_number(v)`: checks if `IS_INT(v) || IS_DOUBLE(v)`

### 2. Object System

**File:** `lisa/object.h`

All heap objects share a common header:

```c
struct lisa_obj {
    lisa_obj_type type;      /* 1 byte */
    bool is_marked;          /* 1 byte (for GC) */
    struct lisa_obj *next;   /* pointer to next in linked list */
};
```

**Object Types:**

1. **OBJ_STRING** (`lisa_obj_string`):
   - Flexible array member for chars
   - `int length`
   - `uint32_t hash` (for interning)
   - String interning: identical strings point to same object
   - Allocation: `sizeof(lisa_obj_string) + length + 1`

2. **OBJ_FUNCTION** (`lisa_obj_function`):
   - `int arity`: number of parameters
   - `int upvalue_count`: number of captured variables
   - `lisa_chunk chunk`: bytecode + constants + line info
   - `lisa_obj_string *name`: function name (nullable)
   - Raw function object (not directly callable)

3. **OBJ_CLOSURE** (`lisa_obj_closure`):
   - `lisa_obj_function *function`: points to function code
   - `lisa_obj_upvalue **upvalues`: array of captured variables
   - `int upvalue_count`: size of upvalues array
   - **
... (truncated)
result
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information about the cj JIT framework. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report.

## Comprehensive Guide to the cj JIT Framework

Based on my thorough exploration of the cj repository, here's everything a consumer needs to know about using this minimal JIT framework:

---

## 1. Context API (`src/ctx.h`, `src/ctx.c`)

### Core Structures and Functions

**Context Creation and Cleanup:**
```c
cj_ctx *create_cj_ctx(void);              // Create a JIT compilation context
void destroy_cj_ctx(cj_ctx *ctx);         // Free context and buffers
```

**Key Context Structure:**
```c
typedef struct {
  uint8_t *mem;                    // Bytecode buffer (grows dynamically)
  uint64_t len;                    // Current length of emitted code
  uint64_t size;                   // Allocated buffer size
  
  uint64_t *label_positions;       // Array of label offsets in code
  int num_labels, label_capacity;  // Label management
  
  cj_fixup *fixups;                // Forward references for branches
  int num_fixups, fixup_capacity;
  
  uint8_t *executable_base;        // Pointer to executable code in mmap'd region
  uint8_t *executable_raw;         // Start of mmap'd memory (includes size header)
  size_t executable_size;
  uint64_t executable_code_size;
} cj_ctx;
```

**Byte Emission Functions:**
```c
void cj_add_u8(cj_ctx *, uint8_t);       // Add single byte
void cj_add_u16(cj_ctx *, uint16_t);     // Add 16-bit value (little-endian)
void cj_add_u32(cj_ctx *, uint32_t);     // Add 32-bit value
void cj_add_u64(cj_ctx *, uint64_t);     // Add 64-bit value
void cj_add_bytes(cj_ctx *, uint8_t *, uint64_t);  // Add raw bytes
```

### Creating Executable Functions

**Core Function Creation:**
```c
typedef void (*cj_fn)(void);  // Type for JIT-compiled functions

cj_fn create_cj_fn(cj_ctx *ctx);         // Create executable function
void destroy_cj_fn(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_fn);  // Free executable memory and clear cache
```

**Key Implementation Details:**
- `create_cj_fn()` allocates memory using `mmap(MAP_PRIVATE | MAP_ANONYMOUS)` with `PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE`
- Prepends an 8-byte size header storing code size (for later cleanup)
- Changes protection to `PROT_READ | PROT_EXEC` via `mprotect()`
- Clears instruction cache with `__builtin___clear_cache()` for ARM64
- Returns a pointer to executable code (past the size header)

### Labels and Forward References

**Label Management:**
```c
typedef struct {
  int id;  // Unique label identifier
} cj_label;

cj_label cj_create_label(cj_ctx *ctx);           // Create a label
void cj_mark_label(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_label label); // Mark label position at current offset
```

**Fixup System:**
```c
typedef enum {
  CJ_FIXUP_KIND_ARM_BRANCH,   // ARM64 branch offset patching
  CJ_FIXUP_KIND_X86_RELATIVE  // x86-64 relative displacement patching
} cj_fixup_kind;

void cj_emit_branch(cj_ctx *ctx, uint32_t base_instr, cj_label label,
                    uint8_t offset_bits, uint8_t offset_shift
... (truncated)

Excellent exploration results. Now let me launch a Plan agent with this context.

Task
{
  "description": "Design Lisa whole-function JIT",
  "subagent_type": "Plan",
  "prompt": "Design a whole-function JIT compiler for the Lisa bytecode VM using the cj JIT framework. Both live in the same repository.\n\n## Lisa VM Architecture (lisa/ directory)\n\n**Values**: NaN-boxed uint64_t (`lisa_value`). Tags: NIL, BOOL, INT (48-bit signed), OBJ (48-bit pointer), DOUBLE (any non-tagged NaN pattern). Key macros: IS_INT, IS_DOUBLE, IS_OBJ, AS_INT, AS_DOUBLE, AS_OBJ, LISA_INT, LISA_OBJ, lisa_double. `lisa_is_number()`, `lisa_as_number()`.\n\n**Objects**: Common header (type, is_marked, next). Types: OBJ_STRING, OBJ_FUNCTION (arity, upvalue_count, chunk, name), OBJ_CLOSURE (function + upvalues array), OBJ_UPVALUE (location pointer, closed value, next), OBJ_NATIVE (C function pointer, name, arity), OBJ_LIST (car, cdr).\n\n**VM state** (`lisa_vm`):\n- `frames[256]` (lisa_call_frame: closure, ip, slots pointer)\n- `stack[4096]` (lisa_value), stack_top pointer\n- Globals: hash table (global_names, global_values)\n- open_upvalues linked list\n- GC (mark-sweep, no write barriers)\n\n**Call convention**: Push closure+args on stack → call_closure sets frame->slots = stack_top - argc - 1, frame->ip = chunk.code → dispatch loop runs → OP_RETURN pops frame, restores stack_top, pushes result.\n\n**Bytecodes**: OP_CONSTANT, OP_NIL/TRUE/FALSE, OP_POP, OP_GET/SET_LOCAL (slot), OP_GET/SET_UPVALUE (idx), OP_GET/DEF_GLOBAL (const idx for name string), OP_ADD/SUB/MUL/DIV/MOD/NEGATE, OP_EQUAL/NOT_EQUAL/LESS/LESS_EQUAL/GREATER/GREATER_EQUAL, OP_NOT, OP_JUMP/JUMP_IF_FALSE (16-bit offset)/LOOP (16-bit backward offset), OP_CLOSURE (const idx + upvalue pairs), OP_CALL (argc), OP_TAIL_CALL (argc), OP_RETURN, OP_CLOSE_UPVALUE, OP_CONS/CAR/CDR, OP_LIST (count), OP_PRINTLN (argc).\n\n**Key VM helpers**: call_value, call_closure, close_upvalues, capture_upvalue, concatenate, runtime_error. The run() function is a big dispatch loop.\n\n## cj JIT Framework (src/ directory)\n\n**Context**: `creat
... (truncated)

⚙ Read ×2

The Plan agent hit a rate limit, but I have all the context I need from the exploration phase. Let me read the existing plan file and then write the full plan myself.

Read — <claude-internal>
{
  "file_path": "<claude-internal>"
}
result
1→# Tail Call Optimization for Lisa
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→Lisa currently pushes a new call frame for every function call. Self-recursive or mutually-recursive functions in tail position blow the 256-frame stack. TCO reuses the current call frame for calls in tail position, turning them into constant-space jumps.
     6→
     7→## Approach
     8→
     9→Thread a `bool tail` parameter through the compiler. When a general function call is in tail position, emit `OP_TAIL_CALL` instead of `OP_CALL`. In the VM, `OP_TAIL_CALL` closes upvalues, slides the arguments down over the current frame, and resets the IP — no new frame pushed.
    10→
    11→## Files to modify
    12→
    13→- `lisa/chunk.h` — add `OP_TAIL_CALL` opcode (after `OP_CALL`)
    14→- `lisa/compiler.c` — add `tail` parameter to `compile_expr` and callers; emit `OP_TAIL_CALL`
    15→- `lisa/vm.c` — handle `OP_TAIL_CALL` in dispatch loop
    16→- `lisa/debug.c` — disassemble `OP_TAIL_CALL`
    17→
    18→## Compiler changes (`compiler.c`)
    19→
    20→Change `compile_expr(c, node)` to `compile_expr(c, node, tail)`. Thread `tail` through:
    21→
    22→- **`compile_fn`**: last body expression gets `tail=true`
    23→- **`compile_if`**: both branches inherit parent's `tail`
    24→- **`compile_do`**: last expression inherits parent's `tail`
    25→- **`compile_call`**: when `tail=true` and it's a general call (not a built-in opcode), emit `OP_TAIL_CALL` instead of `OP_CALL`
    26→- **`compile_let`**: body is always `tail=false` — the stack cleanup code after the body prevents TCO here
    27→- **`lisa_compile` (top-level)**: last expression gets `tail=true`
    28→
    29→Everything else (literal, symbol, def, fn, let bindings, call arguments, `if` condition, non-last body expressions) is always `tail=false`.
    30→
    31→## VM changes (`vm.c`)
    32→
    33→New `OP_TAIL_CALL` handler:
    34→1. Read argc
    35→2. For native functions, fall through to normal call (no frame to reuse)
    36→3. For closures: validate arity, close upvalues for current frame, slide callee+args down to `frame->slots`, reset `stack_top`, update `frame->closure` and `frame->ip`
    37→
    38→Key detail: `memmove` the `argc+1` values (callee + args) from `stack_top - argc - 1` down to `frame->slots`, then `stack_top = frame->slots + argc + 1`.
    39→
    40→## Verification
    41→
    42→1. `make dev` — compiles cleanly with `-Werror`
    43→2. Deep self-recursion that would overflow without TCO:
    44→   ```
    45→   bin/lisa -e '(def loop (fn [n] (if (<= n 0) "done" (loop (- n 1))))) (println (loop 1000000))'
    46→   ```
    47→3. All existing tests still pass (factorial, closures, let, list ops)
    48→4. Mutual recursion:
    49→   ```
    50→   bin/lisa -e '(def e (fn [n] (if (= n 0) true (o (- n 1))))) (def o (fn [n] (if (= n 0) false (e (- n 1))))) (println (e 100000))'
    51→   ```
    52→

1→CC ?= cc
     2→CFLAGS = -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wno-gnu -O2
     3→DEVFLAGS = -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wno-gnu -Werror -g -O0
     4→SOURCES = main.c value.c object.c lexer.c parser.c ast.c chunk.c compiler.c vm.c debug.c
     5→TARGET = bin/lisa
     6→
     7→.PHONY: all dev clean
     8→
     9→all:
    10→	mkdir -p bin
    11→	$(CC) $(SOURCES) -o $(TARGET) $(CFLAGS) -lm
    12→
    13→dev:
    14→	mkdir -p bin
    15→	$(CC) $(SOURCES) -o $(TARGET) $(DEVFLAGS) -lm
    16→
    17→clean:
    18→	rm -rf bin
    19→

1→#ifndef LISA_OBJECT_H
     2→#define LISA_OBJECT_H
     3→
     4→#include "value.h"
     5→#include "chunk.h"
     6→#include <stdint.h>
     7→#include <stdbool.h>
     8→
     9→typedef enum {
    10→    OBJ_STRING,
    11→    OBJ_FUNCTION,
    12→    OBJ_CLOSURE,
    13→    OBJ_UPVALUE,
    14→    OBJ_LIST,
    15→    OBJ_NATIVE,
    16→} lisa_obj_type;
    17→
    18→struct lisa_obj {
    19→    lisa_obj_type type;
    20→    bool is_marked;
    21→    struct lisa_obj *next;
    22→};
    23→
    24→typedef struct {
    25→    lisa_obj obj;
    26→    int length;
    27→    uint32_t hash;
    28→    char chars[];   /* flexible array */
    29→} lisa_obj_string;
    30→
    31→typedef struct {
    32→    lisa_obj obj;
    33→    int arity;
    34→    int upvalue_count;
    35→    lisa_chunk chunk;
    36→    lisa_obj_string *name;
    37→} lisa_obj_function;
    38→
    39→typedef struct lisa_obj_upvalue {
    40→    lisa_obj obj;
    41→    lisa_value *location;
    42→    lisa_value closed;
    43→    struct lisa_obj_upvalue *next;
    44→} lisa_obj_upvalue;
    45→
    46→typedef struct {
    47→    lisa_obj obj;
    48→    lisa_obj_function *function;
    49→    lisa_obj_upvalue **upvalues;
    50→    int upvalue_count;
    51→} lisa_obj_closure;
    52→
    53→typedef struct {
    54→    lisa_obj obj;
    55→    lisa_value car;
    56→    lisa_value cdr;
    57→} lisa_obj_list;
    58→
    59→typedef lisa_value (*lisa_native_fn)(int argc, lisa_value *args);
    60→
    61→typedef struct {
    62→    lisa_obj obj;
    63→    lisa_native_fn function;
    64→    const char *name;
    65→    int arity; /* -1 for variadic */
    66→} lisa_obj_native;
    67→
    68→/* Type checks */
    69→#define OBJ_TYPE(value)    (AS_OBJ(value)->type)
    70→#define IS_STRING(value)   (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_STRING)
    71→#define IS_FUNCTION(value) (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_FUNCTION)
    72→#define IS_CLOSURE(value)  (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_CLOSURE)
    73→#define IS_NATIVE(value)   (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_NATIVE)
    74→#define IS_LIST_OBJ(value) (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_LIST)
    75→
    76→/* Cast macros */
    77→#define AS_STRING(value)   ((lisa_obj_string*)AS_OBJ(value))
    78→#define AS_CSTRING(value)  (((lisa_obj_string*)AS_OBJ(value))->chars)
    79→#define AS_FUNCTION(value) ((lisa_obj_function*)AS_OBJ(value))
    80→#define AS_CLOSURE(value)  ((lisa_obj_closure*)AS_OBJ(value))
    81→#define AS_UPVALUE(value)  ((lisa_obj_upvalue*)AS_OBJ(value))
    82→#define AS_NATIVE(value)   ((lisa_obj_native*)AS_OBJ(value))
    83→#define AS_LIST(value)     ((lisa_obj_list*)AS_OBJ(value))
    84→
    85→/* GC state */
    86→typedef struct {
    87→    lisa_obj *objects;        /* linked list of all allocated objects */
    88→    lisa_obj_string **strings; /* interning hash table */
    89→    int string_count;
    90→    int string_capacity;
    91→    size_t bytes_alloca

1→#ifndef LISA_VM_H

     5→#include "object.h"

    10→
    11→typedef struct {
    12→    lisa_obj_closure *closure;
    13→    uint8_t *ip;
    14→    lisa_value *slots; /* pointer into vm stack */

    16→
    17→typedef struct {
    18→    lisa_call_frame frames[FRAMES_MAX];
    19→    int frame_count;
    20→
    21→    lisa_value stack[STACK_MAX];

    23→
    24→    /* Global variables: hash table of string -> value */

    27→    int global_count;
    28→    int global_capacity;
    29→
    30→    lisa_obj_upvalue *open_upvalues;
    31→

⚙ Grep ×3

Good — cj_builder_if just calls cj_cmp internally, which should handle 64-bit operands fine. Let me verify what cj_cmp accepts for 64-bit registers.

⚙ Grep ×5, Read

Good — both backends support indirect calls. x86 has cj_call_impl(ctx, register_operand) and ARM64 has cj_blr(ctx, register_operand). Let me also check how to load a 64-bit immediate (function pointer) into a register.

result
7172→void cj_mov(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_operand src) {
  7173→  if (dst.type == CJ_REGISTER && detect_reg_size(dst.reg) == 16 && src.type == CJ_CONSTANT && src.constant <= 0xFFFF) {
  7174→    int8_t dst_reg = parse_reg(dst.reg);
  7175→    if (dst_reg < 0) return;
  7176→    cj_add_u8(ctx, 0x66);
  7177→    uint8_t rex_w = 0;
  7178→    uint8_t rex_r = 0;
  7179→    uint8_t rex_x = 0;
  7180→    uint8_t rex_b = (dst_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  7181→    uint8_t need_rex = rex_w || rex_r || rex_x || rex_b;
  7182→    if (need_rex) emit_rex(ctx, rex_w, rex_r, rex_x, rex_b);
  7183→    cj_add_u8(ctx, 0xb8 + (dst_reg & 7));
  7184→    cj_add_u16(ctx, (uint16_t)src.constant);
  7185→    return;
  7186→  }
  7187→  if (dst.type == CJ_REGISTER && detect_reg_size(dst.reg) == 16 && src.type == CJ_REGISTER && detect_reg_size(src.reg) == 16) {
  7188→    int8_t dst_reg = parse_reg(dst.reg);
  7189→    if (dst_reg < 0) return;
  7190→    int8_t src_reg = parse_reg(src.reg);
  7191→    if (src_reg < 0) return;
  7192→    cj_add_u8(ctx, 0x66);
  7193→    uint8_t rex_w = 0;
  7194→    uint8_t rex_r = (dst_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  7195→    uint8_t rex_x = 0;
  7196→    uint8_t rex_b = (src_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  7197→    uint8_t need_rex = rex_w || rex_r || rex_x || rex_b;
  7198→    if (need_rex) emit_rex(ctx, rex_w, rex_r, rex_x, rex_b);
  7199→    cj_add_u8(ctx, 0x8b);
  7200→    emit_modrm(ctx, 3, dst_reg & 7, src_reg & 7);
  7201→    return;
  7202→  }
  7203→  if (dst.type == CJ_REGISTER && detect_reg_size(dst.reg) == 16 && src.type == CJ_MEMORY) {
  7204→    int8_t dst_reg = parse_reg(dst.reg);
  7205→    if (dst_reg < 0) return;
  7206→    int8_t src_base_reg = src.mem.base ? parse_reg(src.mem.base) : -1;
  7207→    int8_t src_index_reg = src.mem.index ? parse_reg(src.mem.index) : -1;
  7208→    uint8_t src_rex_b = (src_base_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  7209→    uint8_t src_rex_x = (src_index_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  7210→    cj_add_u8(ctx, 0x66);
  7211→    uint8_t rex_w = 0;
  7212→    uint8_t rex_r = (dst_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  7213→    uint8_t rex_x = src_rex_x;
  7214→    uint8_t rex_b = src_rex_b;
  7215→    uint8_t need_rex = rex_w || rex_r || rex_x || rex_b;
  7216→    if (need_rex) emit_rex(ctx, rex_w, rex_r, rex_x, rex_b);
  7217→    cj_add_u8(ctx, 0x8b);
  7218→    int mod = emit_memory_modrm(ctx, dst_reg & 7, src.mem.base, src.mem.index, src.mem.scale, src.mem.disp);
  7219→    if (mod == 1) cj_add_u8(ctx, (int8_t)src.mem.disp);
  7220→    else if (mod == 2) cj_add_u32(ctx, (uint32_t)src.mem.disp);
  7221→    return;
  7222→  }
  7223→  if (dst.type == CJ_REGISTER && detect_reg_size(dst.reg) == 32 && src.type == CJ_CONSTANT && src.constant <= 0xFFFFFFFFu) {
  7224→    int8_t dst_reg = parse_reg(dst.reg);

⚙ Grep, Read ×2

Those are shift instructions, not mov. Let me find the 64-bit mov with constant in cj_mov:

⚙ Read

Line 7270 — cj_mov supports mov rax, imm64 (movabs). So cj_mov(ctx, cj_rax, cj_make_constant((uint64_t)ptr)) works for loading a 64-bit function pointer. And cj_builder_assign on ARM64 uses movz/movk for large constants. Now I have everything I need.

Let me also check the cj_call_operand_adapter (for indirect calls on x86):

Read — cj/src/arch/x86_64/backend.h
{
  "file_path": "cj/src/arch/x86_64/backend.h",
  "offset": 99774,
  "limit": 20
}
result
99774→static inline void cj_call_label(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_label label) {
 99775→  const uint8_t opcode[] = { 0xe8 };
 99776→  cj_emit_x86_rel(ctx, opcode, sizeof(opcode), 4, label);
 99777→}
 99778→
 99779→static inline void cj_call_operand_adapter(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand target) {
 99780→  cj_call_impl(ctx, target);
 99781→}
 99782→
 99783→#define cj_call(ctx, target) \
 99784→  _Generic((target), \
 99785→      cj_label: cj_call_label, \
 99786→      default: cj_call_operand_adapter)(ctx, target)
 99787→
 99788→static inline void cj_jmp_label(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_label label) {
 99789→  const uint8_t opcode[] = { 0xe9 };
 99790→  cj_emit_x86_rel(ctx, opcode, sizeof(opcode), 4, label);
 99791→}
 99792→
 99793→static inline void cj_jmp_operand_adapter(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand target) {
⚙ Write, ExitPlanMode

cj_call uses _Generic — works with both labels and register operands. Same pattern for cj_jmp. Now I have all the information needed. Let me write the plan.

Write — <claude-internal>
{
  "file_path": "<claude-internal>",
  "content": "# Whole-Function JIT for Lisa using cj\n\n## Context\n\nLisa currently interprets bytecode through a switch-dispatch loop. The cj JIT framework lives in the same repository. Integrating cj as a whole-function JIT compiler eliminates dispatch overhead and enables inline fast paths for common operations (integer arithmetic, local variable access, comparisons, branches).\n\nThe JIT compiles Lisa functions to native code on first call. The bytecode interpreter remains as fallback. Both x86-64 and ARM64 are supported via cj's cross-platform primitives.\n\n## Architecture\n\n### JIT calling convention\n\n```c\ntypedef lisa_value (*lisa_jit_fn)(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_closure *closure, lisa_value *slots);\n```\n\n- `vm`: full VM state (stack, frames, globals, GC)\n- `closure`: the closure being executed (for upvalue access)\n- `slots`: pointer to local variable slots (== `frame->slots`)\n- Returns the function's result value\n\n### Register layout inside JIT'd code\n\nCallee-saved registers hold persistent state across helper calls:\n\n| Purpose | x86-64 | ARM64 |\n|---------|--------|-------|\n| `vm` pointer | `rbx` | `x19` |\n| `slots` pointer | `r12` | `x20` |\n| `closure` pointer | `r13` | `x21` |\n| `stack_top` shadow | `r14` | `x22` |\n| `constants` pointer | `r15` | `x23` |\n\nCaller-saved registers (`rax`/`x0`, `rcx`/`x1`, `rdx`/`x2`, etc.) are used as temporaries for per-instruction work.\n\n`stack_top` is kept in a register for fast push/pop. session 4. whole-function jit

human

Implement the following plan:

Whole-Function JIT for Lisa using cj

Context

Lisa currently interprets bytecode through a switch-dispatch loop. The cj JIT framework lives in the same repository. Integrating cj as a whole-function JIT compiler eliminates dispatch overhead and enables inline fast paths for common operations (integer arithmetic, local variable access, comparisons, branches).

The JIT compiles Lisa functions to native code on first call. The bytecode interpreter remains as fallback. Both x86-64 and ARM64 are supported via cj's cross-platform primitives.

Architecture

JIT calling convention
typedef lisa_value (*lisa_jit_fn)(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_closure *closure, lisa_value *slots);

- vm: full VM state (stack, frames, globals, GC)

- closure: the closure being executed (for upvalue access)

- slots: pointer to local variable slots (== frame->slots)

- Returns the function's result value

Register layout inside JIT'd code

Callee-saved registers hold persistent state across helper calls:

| Purpose | x86-64 | ARM64 | |---------|--------|-------| | vm pointer | rbx | x19 | | slots pointer | r12 | x20 | | closure pointer | r13 | x21 | | stack_top shadow | r14 | x22 | | constants pointer | r15 | x23 |

Caller-saved registers (rax/x0, rcx/x1, rdx/x2, etc.) are used as temporaries for per-instruction work.

stack_top is kept in a register for fast push/pop. It's synced to vm->stack_top before any helper call and reloaded after.

How calls work

Interpreter calling JIT'd function (in OP_CALL / OP_TAIL_CALL): After call_closure pushes a frame, check frame->closure->function->jit_code. If non-NULL, call the JIT function directly instead of entering the dispatch loop. On return, pop the frame and push the result — same as OP_RETURN.

JIT'd function calling another function: Call lisa_jit_call_helper(vm, argc) — a C helper that:

1. Calls call_value(vm, callee, argc) (handles natives inline, pushes frame for closures)

2. For closures: checks if callee has JIT code → calls JIT directly, else runs interpreter via run(vm, target_depth)

3. Returns the result value

Self-tail-calls: Detected at JIT compile time (callee is a known self-reference). Emit a jump back to the function entry point — true zero-cost TCO.

Non-self tail calls: Call lisa_jit_tail_call_helper(vm, argc) which does the frame-reuse memmove, then dispatches to JIT or interpreter. A trampoline loop at the entry point re-dispatches if the tail-called function also tail-calls, giving constant-frame-depth mutual recursion.

When to JIT

On first call — call_closure checks fn->jit_code == NULL and compiles if so. Compilation walks the bytecode linearly and emits native code per instruction.

Files to create

lisa/jit.h
#ifndef LISA_JIT_H
#define LISA_JIT_H

#include "object.h"
#include "vm.h"

/* Compile a function's bytecode to native code via cj.
   Sets fn->jit_code and fn->jit_ctx on success. */
bool lisa_jit_compile(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_function *fn);

/* Free JIT-compiled code for a function */
void lisa_jit_free(lisa_obj_function *fn);

#endif
lisa/jit.c

The main JIT compiler. Walks bytecode, emits native code using cj. ~500-800 lines.

Structure:

- Emit prologue: save callee-saved registers, load vm/closure/slots/stack_top/constants into dedicated registers

- Create a cj_label for each bytecode offset (used as branch targets for JUMP/LOOP)

- Walk bytecode, for each instruction emit native code (see opcode details below)

- Emit epilogue: restore callee-saved registers, return result in rax/x0

Cross-platform emission helpers (static functions in jit.c):

- emit_push(ctx) — store value (in temp reg) to [stack_top], increment stack_top register

- emit_pop(ctx, dst_reg) — decrement stack_top register, load value into dst_reg

- emit_peek(ctx, dst_reg, distance) — load stack_top[-1-distance] into dst_reg

- emit_sync_stack_top(ctx) — store stack_top register to vm->stack_top

- emit_reload_stack_top(ctx) — load vm->stack_top into stack_top register

- emit_call_c(ctx, fn_ptr, arg_count, ...) — load fn pointer, set up args in ABI registers, call, handle stack_top sync

Files to modify

lisa/object.h — add JIT fields to lisa_obj_function
typedef struct {
    lisa_obj obj;
    int arity;
    int upvalue_count;
    lisa_chunk chunk;
    lisa_obj_string *name;
    void *jit_code;    /* JIT-compiled native code, or NULL */
    void *jit_ctx;     /* cj_ctx* for cleanup, or NULL */
} lisa_obj_function;

Also add cleanup in lisa_new_function (init to NULL) and the GC free path.

lisa/object.c — init JIT fields, free on GC sweep

In lisa_new_function: set fn->jit_code = NULL; fn->jit_ctx = NULL;

In free_object for OBJ_FUNCTION: call lisa_jit_free(fn) before freeing the function.

lisa/vm.h — expose helpers for JIT

Add declarations for C helper functions that JIT'd code calls:

/* Helpers called by JIT-compiled code */
lisa_value lisa_jit_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc);
lisa_value lisa_jit_tail_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc);
lisa/vm.c — JIT call path + helpers

1. Modify run() signature to run(lisa_vm *vm, int base_frame). The OP_RETURN handler checks vm->frame_count == base_frame instead of vm->frame_count == 0. Current top-level call from lisa_interpret passes base_frame = 0.

2. Add JIT dispatch in OP_CALL: after call_value succeeds and pushes a closure frame, check frame->closure->function->jit_code. If set, call the JIT function, handle return (close upvalues, pop frame, push result).

3. Add JIT dispatch in OP_TAIL_CALL: similar — after setting up the tail call frame, check for JIT code.

4. Add lisa_jit_call_helper: called by JIT'd code for OP_CALL. Syncs stack, calls call_value, dispatches to JIT or interpreter, returns result.

5. Add lisa_jit_tail_call_helper: called by JIT'd code for OP_TAIL_CALL. Does frame-reuse memmove, then dispatches.

6. Add other helpers called by JIT'd code (thin wrappers around existing VM operations):

- lisa_jit_get_global(vm, name_idx) — global variable lookup

- lisa_jit_def_global(vm, name_idx, value) — global variable definition

- lisa_jit_get_upvalue(closure, idx) — upvalue read

- lisa_jit_set_upvalue(closure, idx, value) — upvalue write

- lisa_jit_close_upvalue(vm) — close upvalue at stack top

- lisa_jit_make_closure(vm, fn_idx) — closure creation (handles upvalue capture)

- lisa_jit_runtime_error(vm, msg) — error reporting

- lisa_jit_add(vm) / lisa_jit_concat_or_add(vm) — fallback for non-int add

- lisa_jit_println(vm, argc) — println

- lisa_jit_cons(vm), lisa_jit_car(vm), lisa_jit_cdr(vm), lisa_jit_list(vm, n) — list ops

lisa/debug.c — optional: show "(jit)" annotation for JIT'd functions
lisa/Makefile — add jit.c, link cj
SOURCES = main.c value.c object.c lexer.c parser.c ast.c chunk.c compiler.c vm.c debug.c jit.c
CJ_DIR = ../src
CFLAGS = -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wno-gnu -O2 -I$(CJ_DIR)
# Link with cj (ctx.c is the only .c file in cj)
LDFLAGS = -lm
CJ_OBJ = $(CJ_DIR)/ctx.c

Opcode emission details

Inlined (fast path in native code)

OP_CONSTANT [idx]: tmp = constants[idx]; *stack_top++ = tmp

OP_NIL/TRUE/FALSE: push known 64-bit constant values (LISA_NIL, LISA_TRUE, LISA_FALSE are compile-time constants)

OP_POP: stack_top--

OP_GET_LOCAL [slot]: tmp = slots[slot]; *stack_top++ = tmp

OP_SET_LOCAL [slot]: slots[slot] = stack_top[-1] (peek, no pop)

OP_ADD (int fast path):

a = stack_top[-2]; b = stack_top[-1]
if ((a & QNAN|TAG_MASK) == QNAN|TAG_INT && (b & QNAN|TAG_MASK) == QNAN|TAG_INT):
    result = (a + b - QNAN|TAG_INT)  // add payloads, re-add single tag
    // actually: extract AS_INT from both, add, re-box with LISA_INT
    stack_top -= 1; stack_top[-1] = result
else:
    call lisa_jit_add(vm)  // handles double+string cases

For OP_SUB, OP_MUL: same pattern (int fast path inline, helper fallback).

OP_LESS/LESS_EQUAL/GREATER/GREATER_EQUAL (int fast path): check both are tagged int, compare payloads, push LISA_TRUE/LISA_FALSE. Fallback to helper for doubles.

OP_EQUAL/NOT_EQUAL: inline bit-equality check (works for ints, bools, nil, and interned strings). For doubles, fallback to helper.

OP_NOT: v = *--stack_top; push(lisa_is_falsey(v) ? LISA_TRUE : LISA_FALSE) — could inline falsey check (nil or false).

OP_NEGATE: int fast path (negate payload, re-box), helper fallback for double.

OP_JUMP [lo][hi]: unconditional branch to target label.

OP_JUMP_IF_FALSE [lo][hi]: pop, test for falsey (nil or false), conditional branch.

OP_LOOP [lo][hi]: unconditional backward branch to target label.

OP_RETURN: move stack_top[-1] to return register, restore callee-saved regs, return.

Helper calls (complex operations)

OP_GET_UPVALUE/SET_UPVALUE: call helper (pointer chasing through upvalue->location).

OP_GET_GLOBAL/DEF_GLOBAL: call helper (hash table lookup).

OP_CLOSURE: call helper (complex: reads upvalue descriptors from bytecode, creates closure object, captures upvalues). The JIT'd code passes the current bytecode offset so the helper knows which upvalue pairs to read.

OP_CALL [argc]: sync stack_top → call lisa_jit_call_helper(vm, argc) → reload stack_top.

OP_TAIL_CALL [argc]: For self-tail-call (detected at compile time): write args to slots, jump to function entry. For general tail calls: sync stack_top → call lisa_jit_tail_call_helper(vm, argc) → return result (the function is done).

OP_CLOSE_UPVALUE: call close_upvalues(vm, stack_top - 1); stack_top--.

OP_CONS/CAR/CDR/LIST: call helpers (may allocate, need GC).

OP_PRINTLN: call helper.

OP_DIV/OP_MOD: call helpers (always involve doubles or edge cases).

Label mapping for branches

Before emitting code, scan the bytecode to find all branch targets. Create a cj_label for each target offset. When emitting code for an instruction at offset i, call cj_mark_label(ctx, labels[i]) if offset i is a branch target. When emitting jumps, use the label for the target offset.

The pre-scan also collects the mapping from bytecode offsets to labels, so JUMP/JUMP_IF_FALSE/LOOP can look up their target label.

GC integration

JIT'd code must keep vm->stack_top in sync before any operation that might allocate (which could trigger GC). The pattern:

1. Before calling any helper that allocates: emit_sync_stack_top(ctx) stores the register to vm->stack_top

2. Also update vm->gc.stack_count = (int)(stack_top - vm->stack)

3. After the helper returns: emit_reload_stack_top(ctx) reloads (the helper may have changed it)

Helpers that allocate: cons, list, closure, car/cdr (only if they error), println (string allocation), global def (hash table grow), string concat in add.

Self-tail-call detection

At compile time, for OP_TAIL_CALL: check if the callee is a known self-reference. In Lisa, self-recursion typically goes through a global. The compiler could mark this, but for simplicity the JIT can't statically detect it in all cases. Instead:

- For OP_TAIL_CALL preceded by OP_GET_GLOBAL where the global name matches the function being compiled, emit a self-tail-call jump

- Otherwise, emit the general tail call helper path

- If fn->name == NULL (anonymous functions), always use the general path

Verification

1. make -C lisa dev compiles cleanly with -Werror

2. All existing programs work (factorial, closures, let, list ops, fibonacci)

3. Deep recursion that exercises JIT:

```

bin/lisa -e '(def loop (fn [n] (if (<= n 0) "done" (loop (- n 1))))) (println (loop 1000000))'

```

4. Mutual recursion:

```

bin/lisa -e '(def e (fn [n] (if (= n 0) true (o (- n 1))))) (def o (fn [n] (if (= n 0) false (e (- n 1))))) (println (e 100000))'

```

5. Builder-level fibonacci:

```

bin/lisa -e '(def fib (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) n (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2)))))) (println (fib 30))'

```

If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: <claude-internal>

I'll start by exploring the existing codebase to understand the current structures before implementing the JIT.

Lisa's VM architecture:\n   - Read lisa/vm.h and lisa/vm.c fully - understand the run() function, call_value, call_closure, OP_CALL, OP_TAIL_CALL, OP_RETURN handling\n   - Read lisa/object.h and lisa/object.c - understand lisa_obj_function, free_object, lisa_new_function\n   - Read lisa/value.h - understand lisa_value representation (NaN boxing), LISA_NIL, LISA_TRUE, LISA_FALSE, LISA_INT, AS_INT, IS_INT, etc.\n   - Read lisa/chunk.h - understand bytecode chunks, opcodes\n   - Read lisa/compiler.h and lisa/compiler.c - understand opcode enum, what opcodes exist\n   - Read lisa/debug.c - understand disassembly\n\n2. cj JIT framework:\n   - Read src/ctx.h - understand cj_ctx, cj_label, cj_mark_label, cj_create_label, create_cj_fn, cj_resolve_label\n   - Read src/op.h - understand cj_operand, cj_reg, cj_imm, cj_mem\n   - Read src/register.h - understand register naming\n   - Read src/builder.h - understand the builder API (prologue, epilogue, helpers)\n   - Look at examples/ for usage patterns, especially minilang.c and hl_fibonacci.c\n\n3. Lisa's Makefile - read lisa/Makefile to understand build setup\n\n4. Read lisa/main.c to understand how lisa_interpret is called\n\nReport back the complete contents of the key files (vm.h, vm.c, object.h, object.c, value.h, chunk.h, the opcode enum from compiler or wherever it's defined, and the Makefile). Also report the key cj APIs from ctx.h, op.h, builder.h, and the minilang.c example.",

result
Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary document. Let me compile all the key findings:

## Complete Analysis Report

I've thoroughly explored the Lisa language implementation and cj JIT framework. Here's the comprehensive breakdown:

---

## LISA VM ARCHITECTURE

### Core Data Structures

**lisa/value.h** - NaN-boxed value representation (64-bit):
- Uses IEEE 754 quiet NaN exploitation to pack multiple types into 64 bits
- Layout: `[sign=1][exp=0x7FF][quiet=1][tag 50:48][payload 47:0]`
- **Tags**: NIL (000), BOOL (001), INT (010, 48-bit sign-extended), OBJ (011, 48-bit pointer)
- **Key macros**: `LISA_NIL`, `LISA_TRUE`, `LISA_FALSE`, `LISA_INT(i)`, `LISA_OBJ(ptr)`, `LISA_BOOL(b)`, `lisa_double(d)`
- **Type checks**: `IS_NIL(v)`, `IS_BOOL(v)`, `IS_INT(v)`, `IS_OBJ(v)`, `IS_DOUBLE(v)`
- **Extractors**: `AS_BOOL(v)`, `AS_INT(v)`, `AS_DOUBLE(v)`, `AS_OBJ(v)`
- **Numeric coercion**: `lisa_as_number()` converts INT to double for mixed arithmetic

**lisa/object.h** - Object types and allocation:
```c
typedef enum {
    OBJ_STRING,    // String interning with hash table
    OBJ_FUNCTION,  // Bytecode function, arity, upvalue_count
    OBJ_CLOSURE,   // Function + captured upvalues array
    OBJ_UPVALUE,   // Mutable cell for closure capture
    OBJ_LIST,      // Cons cells (car/cdr)
    OBJ_NATIVE,    // Native C function pointers
} lisa_obj_type;
```
- **Linked allocation**: All objects in linked list via `gc->objects`
- **String interning**: Hash table at `gc->strings` for deduplication
- **Garbage collection**: Mark-and-sweep, triggered at `gc->next_gc` threshold

**lisa/object.c** - Key functions:
- `lisa_new_function(gc)` - Create empty function
- `lisa_new_closure(gc, function)` - Wrap function with upvalue array
- `lisa_new_upvalue(gc, slot)` - Create mutable cell pointing to stack value
- `lisa_copy_string(gc, chars, length)` - Intern/deduplicate string
- `lisa_take_string(gc, chars, length)` - Take ownership of string buffer
- GC functions: `mark_object()`, `mark_value()`, `mark_roots()`, `sweep()`, `lisa_gc_collect()`

**lisa/chunk.h** - Bytecode representation:
```c
typedef enum {
    // Stack operations
    OP_CONSTANT,      // [idx] push constants[idx]
    OP_NIL, OP_TRUE, OP_FALSE, OP_POP,
    
    // Variables
    OP_GET_LOCAL, OP_SET_LOCAL,           // [slot] stack[base+slot]
    OP_GET_UPVALUE, OP_SET_UPVALUE,       // [idx] *upvalues[idx]->location
    OP_GET_GLOBAL, OP_DEF_GLOBAL,         // [idx] globals[constants[idx]]
    
    // Arithmetic/logic
    OP_ADD, OP_SUB, OP_MUL, OP_DIV, OP_MOD, OP_NEGATE,
    OP_EQUAL, OP_NOT_EQUAL, OP_LESS, OP_LESS_EQUAL, OP_GREATER, OP_GREATER_EQUAL,
    OP_NOT,
    
    // Control flow
    OP_JUMP, OP_JUMP_IF_FALSE, OP_LOOP,   // [lo][hi] 16-bit signed offset
    
    // Functions/closures
    OP_CLOSURE,       // [idx] then pairs [is_local, index]
    OP_CALL,          // [argc] call top function
    OP_TAIL_CALL,     // [argc] reuse frame for tail recursion
    OP_RETURN,        // retur
... (truncated)

1→#ifndef LISA_OBJECT_H
     2→#define LISA_OBJECT_H
     3→
     4→#include "value.h"
     5→#include "chunk.h"
     6→#include <stdint.h>
     7→#include <stdbool.h>
     8→
     9→typedef enum {
    10→    OBJ_STRING,
    11→    OBJ_FUNCTION,
    12→    OBJ_CLOSURE,
    13→    OBJ_UPVALUE,
    14→    OBJ_LIST,
    15→    OBJ_NATIVE,
    16→} lisa_obj_type;
    17→
    18→struct lisa_obj {
    19→    lisa_obj_type type;
    20→    bool is_marked;
    21→    struct lisa_obj *next;
    22→};
    23→
    24→typedef struct {
    25→    lisa_obj obj;
    26→    int length;
    27→    uint32_t hash;
    28→    char chars[];   /* flexible array */
    29→} lisa_obj_string;
    30→
    31→typedef struct {
    32→    lisa_obj obj;
    33→    int arity;
    34→    int upvalue_count;
    35→    lisa_chunk chunk;
    36→    lisa_obj_string *name;
    37→} lisa_obj_function;
    38→
    39→typedef struct lisa_obj_upvalue {
    40→    lisa_obj obj;
    41→    lisa_value *location;
    42→    lisa_value closed;
    43→    struct lisa_obj_upvalue *next;
    44→} lisa_obj_upvalue;
    45→
    46→typedef struct {
    47→    lisa_obj obj;
    48→    lisa_obj_function *function;
    49→    lisa_obj_upvalue **upvalues;
    50→    int upvalue_count;
    51→} lisa_obj_closure;
    52→
    53→typedef struct {
    54→    lisa_obj obj;
    55→    lisa_value car;
    56→    lisa_value cdr;
    57→} lisa_obj_list;
    58→
    59→typedef lisa_value (*lisa_native_fn)(int argc, lisa_value *args);
    60→
    61→typedef struct {
    62→    lisa_obj obj;
    63→    lisa_native_fn function;
    64→    const char *name;
    65→    int arity; /* -1 for variadic */
    66→} lisa_obj_native;
    67→
    68→/* Type checks */
    69→#define OBJ_TYPE(value)    (AS_OBJ(value)->type)
    70→#define IS_STRING(value)   (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_STRING)
    71→#define IS_FUNCTION(value) (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_FUNCTION)
    72→#define IS_CLOSURE(value)  (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_CLOSURE)
    73→#define IS_NATIVE(value)   (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_NATIVE)
    74→#define IS_LIST_OBJ(value) (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_LIST)
    75→
    76→/* Cast macros */
    77→#define AS_STRING(value)   ((lisa_obj_string*)AS_OBJ(value))
    78→#define AS_CSTRING(value)  (((lisa_obj_string*)AS_OBJ(value))->chars)
    79→#define AS_FUNCTION(value) ((lisa_obj_function*)AS_OBJ(value))
    80→#define AS_CLOSURE(value)  ((lisa_obj_closure*)AS_OBJ(value))
    81→#define AS_UPVALUE(value)  ((lisa_obj_upvalue*)AS_OBJ(value))
    82→#define AS_NATIVE(value)   ((lisa_obj_native*)AS_OBJ(value))
    83→#define AS_LIST(value)     ((lisa_obj_list*)AS_OBJ(value))
    84→
    85→/* GC state */
    86→typedef struct {
    87→    lisa_obj *objects;        /* linked list of all allocated objects */
    88→    lisa_obj_string **strings; /* interning hash table */
    89→    int string_count;

result
1→#include "object.h"
     2→#include <stdio.h>
     3→#include <stdlib.h>
     4→#include <string.h>
     5→
     6→/* --- Hashing --- */
     7→
     8→static uint32_t hash_string(const char *key, int length) {
     9→    uint32_t hash = 2166136261u;
    10→    for (int i = 0; i < length; i++) {
    11→        hash ^= (uint8_t)key[i];
    12→        hash *= 16777619u;
    13→    }
    14→    return hash;
    15→}
    16→
    17→/* --- Allocation helpers --- */
    18→
    19→static lisa_obj *allocate_object(lisa_gc *gc, size_t size, lisa_obj_type type) {
    20→    lisa_obj *obj = malloc(size);
    21→    obj->type = type;
    22→    obj->is_marked = false;
    23→    obj->next = gc->objects;
    24→    gc->objects = obj;
    25→    gc->bytes_allocated += size;
    26→    return obj;
    27→}
    28→
    29→#define ALLOCATE_OBJ(gc, type, obj_type) \
    30→    ((type*)allocate_object(gc, sizeof(type), obj_type))
    31→
    32→/* --- String interning --- */
    33→
    34→static void string_table_grow(lisa_gc *gc) {
    35→    int new_cap = gc->string_capacity < 8 ? 8 : gc->string_capacity * 2;
    36→    lisa_obj_string **new_table = calloc((size_t)new_cap, sizeof(lisa_obj_string*));
    37→
    38→    /* Rehash */
    39→    for (int i = 0; i < gc->string_capacity; i++) {
    40→        lisa_obj_string *s = gc->strings[i];
    41→        if (s == NULL) continue;
    42→        int idx = (int)(s->hash % (uint32_t)new_cap);
    43→        while (new_table[idx] != NULL) {
    44→            idx = (idx + 1) % new_cap;
    45→        }
    46→        new_table[idx] = s;
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    free(gc->strings);
    50→    gc->strings = new_table;
    51→    gc->string_capacity = new_cap;
    52→}
    53→
    54→static lisa_obj_string *string_table_find(lisa_gc *gc, const char *chars, int length, uint32_t hash) {
    55→    if (gc->string_count == 0) return NULL;
    56→
    57→    int idx = (int)(hash % (uint32_t)gc->string_capacity);
    58→    for (;;) {
    59→        lisa_obj_string *s = gc->strings[idx];
    60→        if (s == NULL) return NULL;
    61→        if (s->length == length && s->hash == hash &&
    62→            memcmp(s->chars, chars, (size_t)length) == 0) {
    63→            return s;
    64→        }
    65→        idx = (idx + 1) % gc->string_capacity;
    66→    }
    67→}
    68→
    69→static void string_table_set(lisa_gc *gc, lisa_obj_string *str) {
    70→    if (gc->string_count + 1 > gc->string_capacity * 3 / 4) {
    71→        string_table_grow(gc);
    72→    }
    73→    int idx = (int)(str->hash % (uint32_t)gc->string_capacity);
    74→    while (gc->strings[idx] != NULL) {
    75→        idx = (idx + 1) % gc->string_capacity;
    76→    }
    77→    gc->strings[idx] = str;
    78→    gc->string_count++;
    79→}
    80→
    81→static void string_table_remove(lisa_gc *gc, lisa_obj_string *str) {
    82→    if (gc->string_count == 0) return;
    83→    int idx = (int)(str->hash % (uint32_t)gc->string_capa
... (truncated)

1→#ifndef LISA_VM_H
     2→#define LISA_VM_H
     3→
     4→#include "value.h"
     5→#include "object.h"
     6→#include "chunk.h"
     7→
     8→#define STACK_MAX 4096
     9→#define FRAMES_MAX 256
    10→
    11→typedef struct {
    12→    lisa_obj_closure *closure;
    13→    uint8_t *ip;
    14→    lisa_value *slots; /* pointer into vm stack */
    15→} lisa_call_frame;
    16→
    17→typedef struct {
    18→    lisa_call_frame frames[FRAMES_MAX];
    19→    int frame_count;
    20→
    21→    lisa_value stack[STACK_MAX];
    22→    lisa_value *stack_top;
    23→
    24→    /* Global variables: hash table of string -> value */
    25→    lisa_obj_string **global_names;
    26→    lisa_value *global_values;
    27→    int global_count;
    28→    int global_capacity;
    29→
    30→    lisa_obj_upvalue *open_upvalues;
    31→
    32→    lisa_gc gc;
    33→} lisa_vm;
    34→
    35→typedef enum {
    36→    INTERPRET_OK,
    37→    INTERPRET_COMPILE_ERROR,
    38→    INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR,
    39→} lisa_interpret_result;
    40→
    41→void lisa_vm_init(lisa_vm *vm);
    42→void lisa_vm_free(lisa_vm *vm);
    43→lisa_interpret_result lisa_interpret(lisa_vm *vm, const char *source);
    44→
    45→#endif

1→#include "vm.h"
     2→#include "compiler.h"
     3→#include "parser.h"
     4→#include <stdarg.h>
     5→#include <stdio.h>
     6→#include <stdlib.h>
     7→#include <string.h>
     8→#include <math.h>
     9→
    10→/* --- Stack operations --- */
    11→
    12→static void push(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value value) {
    13→    *vm->stack_top = value;
    14→    vm->stack_top++;
    15→}
    16→
    17→static lisa_value pop(lisa_vm *vm) {
    18→    vm->stack_top--;
    19→    return *vm->stack_top;
    20→}
    21→
    22→static lisa_value peek(lisa_vm *vm, int distance) {
    23→    return vm->stack_top[-1 - distance];
    24→}
    25→
    26→static void reset_stack(lisa_vm *vm) {
    27→    vm->stack_top = vm->stack;
    28→    vm->frame_count = 0;
    29→    vm->open_upvalues = NULL;
    30→}
    31→
    32→static void runtime_error(lisa_vm *vm, const char *format, ...) {
    33→    va_list args;
    34→    va_start(args, format);
    35→    vfprintf(stderr, format, args);
    36→    va_end(args);
    37→    fputs("\n", stderr);
    38→
    39→    for (int i = vm->frame_count - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
    40→        lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[i];
    41→        lisa_obj_function *fn = frame->closure->function;
    42→        size_t offset = (size_t)(frame->ip - fn->chunk.code - 1);
    43→        int line = fn->chunk.lines[offset];
    44→        fprintf(stderr, "[line %d] in ", line);
    45→        if (fn->name == NULL) {
    46→            fprintf(stderr, "script\n");
    47→        } else {
    48→            fprintf(stderr, "%s()\n", fn->name->chars);
    49→        }
    50→    }
    51→
    52→    reset_stack(vm);
    53→}
    54→
    55→/* --- Globals hash table --- */
    56→
    57→static void globals_grow(lisa_vm *vm) {
    58→    int new_cap = vm->global_capacity < 8 ? 8 : vm->global_capacity * 2;
    59→    lisa_obj_string **new_names = calloc((size_t)new_cap, sizeof(lisa_obj_string*));
    60→    lisa_value *new_values = calloc((size_t)new_cap, sizeof(lisa_value));
    61→
    62→    for (int i = 0; i < vm->global_capacity; i++) {
    63→        if (vm->global_names[i] == NULL) continue;
    64→        uint32_t idx = vm->global_names[i]->hash % (uint32_t)new_cap;
    65→        while (new_names[idx] != NULL) {
    66→            idx = (idx + 1) % (uint32_t)new_cap;
    67→        }
    68→        new_names[idx] = vm->global_names[i];
    69→        new_values[idx] = vm->global_values[i];
    70→    }
    71→
    72→    free(vm->global_names);
    73→    free(vm->global_values);
    74→    vm->global_names = new_names;
    75→    vm->global_values = new_values;
    76→    vm->global_capacity = new_cap;
    77→}
    78→
    79→static int globals_find(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_string *name) {
    80→    if (vm->global_capacity == 0) return -1;
    81→    uint32_t idx = name->hash % (uint32_t)vm->global_capacity;
    82→    for (;;) {
    83→        if (vm->global_names[idx] == NULL) return -1;
    84→        if (vm->global_names[idx]

1→#ifndef LISA_VALUE_H
     2→#define LISA_VALUE_H
     3→
     4→#include <stdbool.h>
     5→#include <stdint.h>
     6→#include <stdio.h>
     7→
     8→/*
     9→ * NaN-boxed value representation.
    10→ *
    11→ * IEEE 754 double: if bits [62:52] are all 1 and bit 51 is 1, it's a quiet NaN.
    12→ * We use the remaining payload bits for non-double values.
    13→ *
    14→ * Layout:
    15→ *   Double: any bit pattern that is NOT a quiet NaN with our tag prefix
    16→ *   Tagged: [sign=1][exp=0x7FF][quiet=1][tag 50:48][payload 47:0]
    17→ *
    18→ * Tags (bits 50:48):
    19→ *   000 = nil
    20→ *   001 = bool (payload bit 0)
    21→ *   010 = int  (48-bit sign-extended integer)
    22→ *   011 = object pointer (48-bit)
    23→ */
    24→
    25→typedef uint64_t lisa_value;
    26→
    27→/* The quiet NaN mask: sign(1) + exponent(0x7FF) + quiet(1) = bits 63,62:52,51 */
    28→#define QNAN    ((uint64_t)0x7FFC000000000000)
    29→#define SIGN_BIT ((uint64_t)0x8000000000000000)
    30→
    31→/* Tag values shifted into bits 50:48 */
    32→#define TAG_NIL    ((uint64_t)0x0000000000000000)  /* 000 */
    33→#define TAG_BOOL   ((uint64_t)0x0001000000000000)  /* 001 */
    34→#define TAG_INT    ((uint64_t)0x0002000000000000)  /* 010 */
    35→#define TAG_OBJ    ((uint64_t)0x0003000000000000)  /* 011 */
    36→
    37→#define TAG_MASK   ((uint64_t)0x0003000000000000)
    38→#define PAYLOAD_MASK ((uint64_t)0x0000FFFFFFFFFFFF) /* 48 bits */
    39→
    40→/* Construct values */
    41→#define LISA_NIL        (QNAN | TAG_NIL)
    42→#define LISA_TRUE       (QNAN | TAG_BOOL | 1)
    43→#define LISA_FALSE      (QNAN | TAG_BOOL | 0)
    44→#define LISA_BOOL(b)    ((b) ? LISA_TRUE : LISA_FALSE)
    45→#define LISA_INT(i)     (QNAN | TAG_INT | ((uint64_t)(i) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
    46→#define LISA_OBJ(ptr)   (QNAN | TAG_OBJ | ((uint64_t)(uintptr_t)(ptr) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
    47→
    48→static inline lisa_value lisa_double(double d) {
    49→    union { double d; uint64_t u; } conv;
    50→    conv.d = d;
    51→    return conv.u;
    52→}
    53→
    54→/* Type checks */
    55→#define IS_NIL(v)    ((v) == LISA_NIL)
    56→#define IS_BOOL(v)   (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_BOOL))
    57→#define IS_INT(v)    (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_INT))
    58→#define IS_OBJ(v)    (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_OBJ))
    59→#define IS_DOUBLE(v) (((v) & QNAN) != QNAN)
    60→
    61→/* Extract values */
    62→#define AS_BOOL(v)   ((v) & 1)
    63→
    64→static inline int64_t AS_INT(lisa_value v) {
    65→    /* Sign-extend the 48-bit payload */
    66→    uint64_t raw = v & PAYLOAD_MASK;
    67→    if (raw & ((uint64_t)1 << 47)) {
    68→        raw |= (uint64_t)0xFFFF000000000000;
    69→    }
    70→    return (int64_t)raw;
    71→}
    72→
    73→static inline double AS_DOUBLE(lisa_value v) {
    74→    union { uint64_t u; double d; } conv;
    75→    conv.u = v;
    76→    return conv.d;
    77→}
    78→
    79→#define AS_
... (truncated)

1→#ifndef LISA_CHUNK_H
     2→#define LISA_CHUNK_H
     3→
     4→#include "value.h"
     5→#include <stdint.h>
     6→
     7→typedef enum {
     8→    OP_CONSTANT,      /* [idx]          push constants[idx] */
     9→    OP_NIL,           /*                push nil */
    10→    OP_TRUE,          /*                push true */
    11→    OP_FALSE,         /*                push false */
    12→    OP_POP,           /*                pop top */
    13→
    14→    OP_GET_LOCAL,     /* [slot]         push stack[base+slot] */
    15→    OP_SET_LOCAL,     /* [slot]         stack[base+slot] = peek */
    16→    OP_GET_UPVALUE,   /* [idx]          push *upvalues[idx]->location */
    17→    OP_SET_UPVALUE,   /* [idx]          *upvalues[idx]->location = peek */
    18→    OP_GET_GLOBAL,    /* [idx]          push globals[constants[idx]] */
    19→    OP_DEF_GLOBAL,    /* [idx]          globals[constants[idx]] = pop */
    20→
    21→    OP_ADD,
    22→    OP_SUB,
    23→    OP_MUL,
    24→    OP_DIV,
    25→    OP_MOD,
    26→    OP_NEGATE,
    27→
    28→    OP_EQUAL,
    29→    OP_NOT_EQUAL,
    30→    OP_LESS,
    31→    OP_LESS_EQUAL,
    32→    OP_GREATER,
    33→    OP_GREATER_EQUAL,
    34→
    35→    OP_NOT,
    36→
    37→    OP_JUMP,          /* [lo][hi]       ip += offset */
    38→    OP_JUMP_IF_FALSE, /* [lo][hi]       if falsey(pop) ip += offset */
    39→    OP_LOOP,          /* [lo][hi]       ip -= offset */
    40→
    41→    OP_CLOSURE,       /* [idx] then pairs of [is_local, index] */
    42→    OP_CALL,          /* [argc]         call top function with argc args */
    43→    OP_TAIL_CALL,     /* [argc]         tail call: reuse current frame */
    44→    OP_RETURN,        /*                return top of stack */
    45→
    46→    OP_CLOSE_UPVALUE, /*                close upvalue at stack top */
    47→
    48→    OP_CONS,          /*                push cons(pop2, pop1) */
    49→    OP_CAR,           /*                push car(pop) */
    50→    OP_CDR,           /*                push cdr(pop) */
    51→    OP_LIST,          /* [n]            pop n items, build list */
    52→
    53→    OP_PRINTLN,       /* [argc]         print argc values with spaces, newline */
    54→} lisa_op;
    55→
    56→/* Dynamic array of constants */
    57→typedef struct {
    58→    int count;
    59→    int capacity;
    60→    lisa_value *values;
    61→} lisa_value_array;
    62→
    63→void lisa_value_array_init(lisa_value_array *arr);
    64→void lisa_value_array_write(lisa_value_array *arr, lisa_value value);
    65→void lisa_value_array_free(lisa_value_array *arr);
    66→
    67→/* Bytecode chunk */
    68→typedef struct {
    69→    int count;
    70→    int capacity;
    71→    uint8_t *code;
    72→    int *lines;          /* source line per bytecode byte */
    73→    lisa_value_array constants;
    74→} lisa_chunk;
    75→
    76→void lisa_chunk_init(lisa_chunk *chunk);
    77→void lisa_chunk_write(lisa_chunk *chunk, uint8_t byte, int l
... (truncated)

1→CC ?= cc
     2→CFLAGS = -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wno-gnu -O2
     3→DEVFLAGS = -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wno-gnu -Werror -g -O0
     4→SOURCES = main.c value.c object.c lexer.c parser.c ast.c chunk.c compiler.c vm.c debug.c
     5→TARGET = bin/lisa
     6→
     7→.PHONY: all dev clean
     8→
     9→all:
    10→	mkdir -p bin
    11→	$(CC) $(SOURCES) -o $(TARGET) $(CFLAGS) -lm
    12→
    13→dev:
    14→	mkdir -p bin
    15→	$(CC) $(SOURCES) -o $(TARGET) $(DEVFLAGS) -lm
    16→
    17→clean:
    18→	rm -rf bin

1→#include "debug.h"
     2→#include "object.h"
     3→#include <stdio.h>
     4→
     5→static int simple_instruction(const char *name, int offset) {
     6→    printf("%s\n", name);
     7→    return offset + 1;
     8→}
     9→
    10→static int byte_instruction(const char *name, lisa_chunk *chunk, int offset) {
    11→    uint8_t slot = chunk->code[offset + 1];
    12→    printf("%-20s %4d\n", name, slot);
    13→    return offset + 2;
    14→}
    15→
    16→static int constant_instruction(const char *name, lisa_chunk *chunk, int offset) {
    17→    uint8_t idx = chunk->code[offset + 1];
    18→    printf("%-20s %4d '", name, idx);
    19→    lisa_print_value(chunk->constants.values[idx]);
    20→    printf("'\n");
    21→    return offset + 2;
    22→}
    23→
    24→static int jump_instruction(const char *name, int sign, lisa_chunk *chunk, int offset) {
    25→    uint8_t lo = chunk->code[offset + 1];
    26→    uint8_t hi = chunk->code[offset + 2];
    27→    uint16_t jump = (uint16_t)(lo | (hi << 8));
    28→    printf("%-20s %4d -> %d\n", name, offset, offset + 3 + sign * jump);
    29→    return offset + 3;
    30→}
    31→
    32→void lisa_disassemble_chunk(lisa_chunk *chunk, const char *name) {
    33→    printf("== %s ==\n", name);
    34→    int offset = 0;
    35→    while (offset < chunk->count) {
    36→        offset = lisa_disassemble_instruction(chunk, offset);
    37→    }
    38→}
    39→
    40→int lisa_disassemble_instruction(lisa_chunk *chunk, int offset) {
    41→    printf("%04d ", offset);
    42→
    43→    if (offset > 0 && chunk->lines[offset] == chunk->lines[offset - 1]) {
    44→        printf("   | ");
    45→    } else {
    46→        printf("%4d ", chunk->lines[offset]);
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    uint8_t instruction = chunk->code[offset];
    50→    switch (instruction) {
    51→    case OP_CONSTANT:      return constant_instruction("OP_CONSTANT", chunk, offset);
    52→    case OP_NIL:           return simple_instruction("OP_NIL", offset);
    53→    case OP_TRUE:          return simple_instruction("OP_TRUE", offset);
    54→    case OP_FALSE:         return simple_instruction("OP_FALSE", offset);
    55→    case OP_POP:           return simple_instruction("OP_POP", offset);
    56→
    57→    case OP_GET_LOCAL:     return byte_instruction("OP_GET_LOCAL", chunk, offset);
    58→    case OP_SET_LOCAL:     return byte_instruction("OP_SET_LOCAL", chunk, offset);
    59→    case OP_GET_UPVALUE:   return byte_instruction("OP_GET_UPVALUE", chunk, offset);
    60→    case OP_SET_UPVALUE:   return byte_instruction("OP_SET_UPVALUE", chunk, offset);
    61→    case OP_GET_GLOBAL:    return constant_instruction("OP_GET_GLOBAL", chunk, offset);
    62→    case OP_DEF_GLOBAL:    return constant_instruction("OP_DEF_GLOBAL", chunk, offset);
    63→
    64→    case OP_ADD:           return simple_instruction("OP_ADD", offset);
    65→    case OP_SUB:           return simple_instruction("OP_SUB", offset);
    66→    c
... (truncated)

1→#pragma once
     2→
     3→#include <stddef.h>
     4→
     5→#include "ctx.h"
     6→#include "op.h"
     7→
     8→typedef struct {
     9→  size_t stack_size;
    10→  size_t lr_offset;
    11→  int save_lr;
    12→} cj_builder_frame;
    13→
    14→typedef struct {
    15→  cj_label entry;
    16→  cj_label exit;
    17→} cj_builder_block;
    18→
    19→typedef struct {
    20→  cj_label else_label;
    21→  cj_label end_label;
    22→  int has_else;
    23→} cj_builder_if_block;
    24→
    25→typedef struct {
    26→  cj_builder_block block;
    27→  cj_operand counter;
    28→  cj_operand limit;
    29→  cj_operand step;
    30→  cj_condition exit_cond;
    31→} cj_builder_for_loop;
    32→
    33→typedef struct {
    34→  unsigned depth;
    35→} cj_builder_scratch;
    36→
    37→static inline void cj_builder_fn_prologue(cj_ctx *ctx, size_t requested_stack_bytes,
    38→                                          cj_builder_frame *frame);
    39→static inline void cj_builder_fn_prologue_with_link_save(cj_ctx *ctx, size_t requested_stack_bytes,
    40→                                                         cj_builder_frame *frame);
    41→static inline void cj_builder_fn_prologue_ex(cj_ctx *ctx, size_t requested_stack_bytes,
    42→                                             cj_builder_frame *frame, int save_lr);
    43→static inline void cj_builder_fn_epilogue(cj_ctx *ctx, const cj_builder_frame *frame);
    44→static inline void cj_builder_return(cj_ctx *ctx, const cj_builder_frame *frame);
    45→
    46→static inline cj_builder_block cj_builder_loop_begin(cj_ctx *ctx);
    47→static inline void cj_builder_loop_condition(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_builder_block block, cj_operand lhs,
    48→                                             cj_operand rhs, cj_condition exit_cond);
    49→static inline void cj_builder_loop_continue(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_builder_block block);
    50→static inline void cj_builder_loop_break(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_builder_block block);
    51→static inline void cj_builder_loop_end(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_builder_block block);
    52→
    53→static inline cj_builder_if_block cj_builder_if(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_operand lhs, cj_operand rhs,
    54→                                                cj_condition cond);
    55→static inline void cj_builder_else(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_builder_if_block *block);
    56→static inline void cj_builder_endif(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_builder_if_block *block);
    57→
    58→static inline cj_builder_for_loop cj_builder_for_begin(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_operand counter,
    59→                                                       cj_operand start, cj_operand limit,
    60→                                                       cj_operand step, cj_condition exit_cond);
    61→static inline void cj_builder_for_continue(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_builder_for_loop *loop);
    62→static inline void cj_builder_for_break(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_builder_for_loop *loop);
    63→static inline void cj_builder_for_end(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_builder_for_loop *loop);
    64→
