session 5. jit rewrite: register cache + inline fast paths

human

Implement the following plan:

Rewrite JIT: Register Cache + Inline Fast Paths

Context

The current lisa/jit.c implements a "stack machine in native code" — every operation pushes/pops values through memory and calls C helpers for all arithmetic and comparisons. Benchmarks show no speedup over the interpreter. The user wants a "real JIT, no shortcuts."

This plan rewrites jit.c to use a compile-time register cache and inline integer fast paths. Only jit.c changes — all other files (vm.c helpers, object.h, jit.h, Makefile) remain as-is.

Core Concept: Register Cache

A compile-time tracker that knows the top N values of the bytecode stack are currently in CPU registers instead of memory. This eliminates load/store traffic for common instruction sequences like GET_LOCAL; GET_LOCAL; ADD.

Design
reg_cache_t cache;  // compile-time state
cache.depth = 0;    // how many stack values are in registers (0..MAX_CACHE)
cache.regs[0..3];   // fixed register names for cache slots

- MAX_CACHE = 4 — 4 caller-saved registers per platform

- cache.regs[0] = bottom of cache (deepest stack value), cache.regs[depth-1] = top of stack

- Push to cache: if depth < MAX_CACHE, place value in cache.regs[depth++]; otherwise, spill cache.regs[0] to memory, shift entries down, put new value in cache.regs[MAX_CACHE-1]

- Pop from cache: if depth > 0, value is in cache.regs[--depth]; otherwise, decrement REG_STKTOP and load from memory

- Flush: write all cached values to memory stack (store at [REG_STKTOP + i*8]), advance REG_STKTOP by depth * 8, set depth = 0

- Required flush points: before any helper call, before any branch/jump, at every branch target label

Register Assignments
| Purpose | x86-64 | ARM64 | |---------|--------|-------| | Callee-saved (persistent) | | | | vm pointer | rbx | x19 | | slots pointer | r12 | x20 | | closure pointer | r13 | x21 | | stack_top (committed) | r14 | x22 | | constants pointer | r15 | x23 | | Cache (4 slots) | | | | cache[0] | r8 | x10 | | cache[1] | r9 | x11 | | cache[2] | r10 | x12 | | cache[3] | r11 | x13 | | Scratch (computation) | | | | scratch / return | rax | x0 | | scratch / arg regs | rcx,rdx,rsi,rdi | x1-x9,x14,x15 |

Cache registers are caller-saved, so they're naturally clobbered by C calls. Since we always flush before calling, this is consistent.

Inline Fast Paths

Integer type check
// IS_INT(v): top 16 bits == 0x7FFE
mov scratch, value
shr scratch, 48          // x86: shr; ARM64: lsr
cmp scratch, 0x7FFE      // fits in imm16
jne/bne slow_path
Integer payload extraction (shl/shr trick)
shl reg, 16    // clears top 16 tag bits, shifts payload to bits 63:16
shr reg, 16    // shifts back to bits 47:0, zero-extends (masks to 48 bits)

For signed operations (comparison), use sar (x86) / asr (ARM64) instead of shr/lsr for sign extension.

OP_ADD / OP_SUB / OP_MUL (int fast path)
// a = cache[depth-2], b = cache[depth-1]  (already in registers)
// Type-check both (non-destructive, uses scratch reg):
mov scratch, a_reg; shr scratch, 48; cmp scratch, 0x7FFE; jne slow
mov scratch, b_reg; shr scratch, 48; cmp scratch, 0x7FFE; jne slow

// Extract payloads:
shl a_reg, 16; shr a_reg, 16     // payload(a) in a_reg
shl b_reg, 16; shr b_reg, 16     // payload(b) in b_reg (can use scratch instead)

// Compute:
add/sub/imul a_reg, b_reg

// Mask to 48 bits (handle overflow):
shl a_reg, 16; shr a_reg, 16

// Re-tag:
movabs scratch, 0x7FFE000000000000    // QNAN | TAG_INT
or a_reg, scratch

// Result is in a_reg = cache[depth-2]; depth--
jmp done

slow:
  // Flush cache to memory, call lisa_jit_add/sub/mul(vm, a, b), cache result
done:

For the slow path: save the original operand values (which haven't been modified since type checks are non-destructive) before flushing. After flush, pass them as function args.

OP_LESS / OP_LESS_EQUAL / OP_GREATER / OP_GREATER_EQUAL (int fast path)
// Type-check both operands (same as above)
// Shift both to align sign bit for signed comparison:
mov scratch1, a_reg; shl scratch1, 16
mov scratch2, b_reg; shl scratch2, 16
cmp scratch1, scratch2     // signed comparison of shifted values

// Produce LISA_TRUE or LISA_FALSE:
// LISA_FALSE = 0x7FFD000000000000, LISA_TRUE = 0x7FFD000000000001 (differ in bit 0)
movabs result, LISA_FALSE
xor scratch, scratch
setl/setle/setg/setge scratch_8bit    // x86: set byte; ARM64: use cset
or result, scratch

// result goes into cache[depth-2]; depth--

ARM64 alternative: use csel to select between pre-loaded TRUE/FALSE values.

OP_EQUAL / OP_NOT_EQUAL

Inline bitwise equality — correct for ints, bools, nil, interned strings. Doubles with exotic values (NaN, ±0) fall through to helper.

// Check if either operand is a double (top bits < 0x7FFC)
mov scratch, a_reg; shr scratch, 48; cmp scratch, 0x7FFC; jb slow
mov scratch, b_reg; shr scratch, 48; cmp scratch, 0x7FFC; jb slow

// Both are tagged (not double). Bitwise compare:
cmp a_reg, b_reg
// Produce LISA_TRUE/LISA_FALSE as above (setz for equal, setnz for not_equal)
jmp done

slow:
  // Flush + call lisa_jit_equal/not_equal
done:
OP_NEGATE (int fast path)
// Type-check: shr scratch, 48; cmp 0x7FFE; jne slow
// Extract payload:
shl reg, 16; sar reg, 16     // sign-extended payload
neg reg                       // negate
shl reg, 16; shr reg, 16     // mask to 48 bits
movabs scratch, QNAN|TAG_INT
or reg, scratch
OP_NOT (inline)
// Falsey = nil or false
// LISA_NIL = 0x7FFC000000000000, LISA_FALSE = 0x7FFD000000000000
// Check: val == LISA_NIL || val == LISA_FALSE
movabs scratch, LISA_NIL; cmp val, scratch; jz/beq is_falsey
movabs scratch, LISA_FALSE; cmp val, scratch; jz/beq is_falsey
// Truthy → push LISA_FALSE
movabs result, LISA_FALSE; jmp done
is_falsey:
// Falsey → push LISA_TRUE
movabs result, LISA_TRUE
done:
OP_JUMP_IF_FALSE (inline)

Same falsey check as NOT, but branches to the target label instead of pushing a boolean.

OP_GET_LOCAL / OP_SET_LOCAL / OP_CONSTANT (cache-aware)

These are the primary beneficiaries of the cache. GET_LOCAL loads from slots[slot] into a cache register instead of pushing to memory. SET_LOCAL reads from a cache register (if cached) instead of loading from memory.

OP_RETURN

Pop return value from cache (or memory), sync stack_top, emit epilogue.

Helper-Only Operations (flush + call)

These always flush the cache and call the existing C helpers:

- OP_GET_GLOBAL / OP_DEF_GLOBAL — hash table lookup

- OP_GET_UPVALUE / OP_SET_UPVALUE — pointer chasing (could inline but marginal benefit)

- OP_CLOSURE — complex upvalue capture

- OP_CALL / OP_TAIL_CALL — function dispatch

- OP_CLOSE_UPVALUE — upvalue closing

- OP_CONS / OP_CAR / OP_CDR / OP_LIST — list ops (allocate, need GC)

- OP_PRINTLN — I/O

- OP_DIV / OP_MOD — always produce doubles or have edge cases

Flush + Call Pattern

static void flush_cache(cj_ctx *ctx, reg_cache_t *cache) {
    for (int i = 0; i < cache->depth; i++) {
        emit_store64(ctx, cache->regs[i], REG_STKTOP, i * 8);
    }
    if (cache->depth > 0) {
        cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_STKTOP), imm(cache->depth * 8));
    }
    cache->depth = 0;
}

Before calling any C helper:

1. flush_cache(ctx, &cache)

2. emit_sync_stack_top(ctx) — write REG_STKTOP to vm->stack_top

3. Set up args in ABI registers

4. emit_call_abs(ctx, fn_ptr)

5. emit_reload_stack_top(ctx) — reload REG_STKTOP from vm->stack_top

6. Push result to cache if applicable

Branch Handling

At any branch source (JUMP, JUMP_IF_FALSE, LOOP) and at every branch target label: the cache must be empty (depth == 0). This means:

- Before emitting a jump: flush the cache

- At each cj_mark_label for a branch target: assert depth == 0

This is conservative but correct. It ensures that at every point where control flow merges, there's a single known state.

x86 Portability Fix

The current code uses cj_je/cj_jne which don't exist in cj's x86 backend. The correct equivalents:

- cj_je → cj_jz (same encoding: ZF=1)

- cj_jne → cj_jnz (same encoding: ZF=0)

Use platform macros:

#if defined(__x86_64__)
#define EMIT_JEQ(ctx, label) cj_jz(ctx, label)
#define EMIT_JNE(ctx, label) cj_jnz(ctx, label)
#define EMIT_JLT(ctx, label) cj_jl(ctx, label)
#define EMIT_JLE(ctx, label) cj_jle(ctx, label)
#define EMIT_JGT(ctx, label) cj_jg(ctx, label)
#define EMIT_JGE(ctx, label) cj_jge(ctx, label)
#define EMIT_JMP(ctx, label) cj_jmp(ctx, label)
#define EMIT_JB(ctx, label)  cj_jb(ctx, label)   // unsigned below (for tag < 0x7FFC)
#elif defined(__aarch64__)
#define EMIT_JEQ(ctx, label) cj_beq(ctx, label)
#define EMIT_JNE(ctx, label) cj_bne(ctx, label)
#define EMIT_JLT(ctx, label) cj_blt(ctx, label)
#define EMIT_JLE(ctx, label) cj_ble(ctx, label)
#define EMIT_JGT(ctx, label) cj_bgt(ctx, label)
#define EMIT_JGE(ctx, label) cj_bge(ctx, label)
#define EMIT_JMP(ctx, label) cj_b(ctx, label)
#define EMIT_JB(ctx, label)  cj_blo(ctx, label)   // unsigned lower
#endif

Similarly for other platform-divergent instructions (shr/lsr, shl/lsl, imul/mul, neg, setcc/cset, etc.).

File Modified

lisa/jit.c — complete rewrite (~700-1000 lines). No other files change.

Structure of New jit.c

1. Includes + pragma for cj warnings
2. lisa_jit_fn typedef
3. Platform register name defines (callee-saved, cache, scratch)
4. Platform branch/shift/cmp macros (EMIT_JEQ, EMIT_SHR, etc.)
5. Operand helpers (reg, imm, mem)
6. reg_cache_t struct and cache manipulation functions:
   - cache_init, cache_push, cache_pop, cache_peek, cache_flush
   - cache_ensure_flushed (assert depth==0 for branch targets)
7. Low-level emit helpers:
   - emit_load_imm64, emit_load64, emit_store64
   - emit_call_abs, emit_sync_stack_top, emit_reload_stack_top
   - emit_prologue, emit_epilogue
8. Fast-path emit helpers:
   - emit_int_type_check (shr+cmp, branch to slow label)
   - emit_extract_payload (shl+shr)
   - emit_retag_int (mask + or with QNAN|TAG_INT)
   - emit_bool_result (produce LISA_TRUE/LISA_FALSE from condition)
   - emit_falsey_check (check nil or false)
9. scan_branch_targets (unchanged from current)
10. lisa_jit_compile — main compilation loop
11. lisa_jit_free (unchanged from current)

Verification

1. make -C lisa dev compiles cleanly with -Werror

2. All existing programs work:

```

bin/lisa -e '(def fac (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) 1 (* n (fac (- n 1)))))) (println (fac 20))'

bin/lisa -e '(def fib (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) n (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2)))))) (println (fib 30))'

bin/lisa -e '(def loop (fn [n] (if (<= n 0) "done" (loop (- n 1))))) (println (loop 1000000))'

```

3. Benchmark JIT vs interpreter:

```

time bin/lisa -e '(def fib (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) n (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2)))))) (println (fib 35))'

time bin/lisa --no-jit -e '(def fib (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) n (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2)))))) (println (fib 35))'

```

Expect measurable speedup from inline integer arithmetic and register caching.

4. Mutual recursion (100K depth):

```

bin/lisa -e '(def e (fn [n] (if (= n 0) true (o (- n 1))))) (def o (fn [n] (if (= n 0) false (e (- n 1))))) (println (e 100000))'

```

5. Closures and list operations:

```

bin/lisa -e '(def make-adder (fn [x] (fn [y] (+ x y)))) (def add5 (make-adder 5)) (println (add5 10))'

bin/lisa -e '(println (car (cons 1 (cons 2 nil))))'

```

If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: <claude-internal>

Now let me read the value representation and chunk definitions, plus check the cj API for specific instructions I'll need.

Read — lisa/value.h
{
  "file_path": "lisa/value.h"
}
result
1→#ifndef LISA_VALUE_H
     2→#define LISA_VALUE_H
     3→
     4→#include <stdbool.h>
     5→#include <stdint.h>
     6→#include <stdio.h>
     7→
     8→/*
     9→ * NaN-boxed value representation.
    10→ *
    11→ * IEEE 754 double: if bits [62:52] are all 1 and bit 51 is 1, it's a quiet NaN.
    12→ * We use the remaining payload bits for non-double values.
    13→ *
    14→ * Layout:
    15→ *   Double: any bit pattern that is NOT a quiet NaN with our tag prefix
    16→ *   Tagged: [sign=1][exp=0x7FF][quiet=1][tag 50:48][payload 47:0]
    17→ *
    18→ * Tags (bits 50:48):
    19→ *   000 = nil
    20→ *   001 = bool (payload bit 0)
    21→ *   010 = int  (48-bit sign-extended integer)
    22→ *   011 = object pointer (48-bit)
    23→ */
    24→
    25→typedef uint64_t lisa_value;
    26→
    27→/* The quiet NaN mask: sign(1) + exponent(0x7FF) + quiet(1) = bits 63,62:52,51 */
    28→#define QNAN    ((uint64_t)0x7FFC000000000000)
    29→#define SIGN_BIT ((uint64_t)0x8000000000000000)
    30→
    31→/* Tag values shifted into bits 50:48 */
    32→#define TAG_NIL    ((uint64_t)0x0000000000000000)  /* 000 */
    33→#define TAG_BOOL   ((uint64_t)0x0001000000000000)  /* 001 */
    34→#define TAG_INT    ((uint64_t)0x0002000000000000)  /* 010 */
    35→#define TAG_OBJ    ((uint64_t)0x0003000000000000)  /* 011 */
    36→
    37→#define TAG_MASK   ((uint64_t)0x0003000000000000)
    38→#define PAYLOAD_MASK ((uint64_t)0x0000FFFFFFFFFFFF) /* 48 bits */
    39→
    40→/* Construct values */
    41→#define LISA_NIL        (QNAN | TAG_NIL)
    42→#define LISA_TRUE       (QNAN | TAG_BOOL | 1)
    43→#define LISA_FALSE      (QNAN | TAG_BOOL | 0)
    44→#define LISA_BOOL(b)    ((b) ? LISA_TRUE : LISA_FALSE)
    45→#define LISA_INT(i)     (QNAN | TAG_INT | ((uint64_t)(i) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
    46→#define LISA_OBJ(ptr)   (QNAN | TAG_OBJ | ((uint64_t)(uintptr_t)(ptr) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
    47→
    48→static inline lisa_value lisa_double(double d) {
    49→    union { double d; uint64_t u; } conv;
    50→    conv.d = d;
    51→    return conv.u;
    52→}
    53→
    54→/* Type checks */
    55→#define IS_NIL(v)    ((v) == LISA_NIL)
    56→#define IS_BOOL(v)   (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_BOOL))
    57→#define IS_INT(v)    (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_INT))
    58→#define IS_OBJ(v)    (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_OBJ))
    59→#define IS_DOUBLE(v) (((v) & QNAN) != QNAN)
    60→
    61→/* Extract values */
    62→#define AS_BOOL(v)   ((v) & 1)
    63→
    64→static inline int64_t AS_INT(lisa_value v) {
    65→    /* Sign-extend the 48-bit payload */
    66→    uint64_t raw = v & PAYLOAD_MASK;
    67→    if (raw & ((uint64_t)1 << 47)) {
    68→        raw |= (uint64_t)0xFFFF000000000000;
    69→    }
    70→    return (int64_t)raw;
    71→}
    72→
    73→static inline double AS_DOUBLE(lisa_value v) {
    74→    union { uint64_t u; double d; } conv;
    75→    conv.u = v;
    76→    return conv.d;
    77→}
    78→
    79→#define AS_
... (truncated)

result
1→#ifndef LISA_CHUNK_H
     2→#define LISA_CHUNK_H
     3→
     4→#include "value.h"
     5→#include <stdint.h>
     6→
     7→typedef enum {
     8→    OP_CONSTANT,      /* [idx]          push constants[idx] */
     9→    OP_NIL,           /*                push nil */
    10→    OP_TRUE,          /*                push true */
    11→    OP_FALSE,         /*                push false */
    12→    OP_POP,           /*                pop top */
    13→
    14→    OP_GET_LOCAL,     /* [slot]         push stack[base+slot] */
    15→    OP_SET_LOCAL,     /* [slot]         stack[base+slot] = peek */
    16→    OP_GET_UPVALUE,   /* [idx]          push *upvalues[idx]->location */
    17→    OP_SET_UPVALUE,   /* [idx]          *upvalues[idx]->location = peek */
    18→    OP_GET_GLOBAL,    /* [idx]          push globals[constants[idx]] */
    19→    OP_DEF_GLOBAL,    /* [idx]          globals[constants[idx]] = pop */
    20→
    21→    OP_ADD,
    22→    OP_SUB,
    23→    OP_MUL,
    24→    OP_DIV,
    25→    OP_MOD,
    26→    OP_NEGATE,
    27→
    28→    OP_EQUAL,
    29→    OP_NOT_EQUAL,
    30→    OP_LESS,
    31→    OP_LESS_EQUAL,
    32→    OP_GREATER,
    33→    OP_GREATER_EQUAL,
    34→
    35→    OP_NOT,
    36→
    37→    OP_JUMP,          /* [lo][hi]       ip += offset */
    38→    OP_JUMP_IF_FALSE, /* [lo][hi]       if falsey(pop) ip += offset */
    39→    OP_LOOP,          /* [lo][hi]       ip -= offset */
    40→
    41→    OP_CLOSURE,       /* [idx] then pairs of [is_local, index] */
    42→    OP_CALL,          /* [argc]         call top function with argc args */
    43→    OP_TAIL_CALL,     /* [argc]         tail call: reuse current frame */
    44→    OP_RETURN,        /*                return top of stack */
    45→
    46→    OP_CLOSE_UPVALUE, /*                close upvalue at stack top */
    47→
    48→    OP_CONS,          /*                push cons(pop2, pop1) */
    49→    OP_CAR,           /*                push car(pop) */
    50→    OP_CDR,           /*                push cdr(pop) */
    51→    OP_LIST,          /* [n]            pop n items, build list */
    52→
    53→    OP_PRINTLN,       /* [argc]         print argc values with spaces, newline */
    54→} lisa_op;
    55→
    56→/* Dynamic array of constants */
    57→typedef struct {
    58→    int count;
    59→    int capacity;
    60→    lisa_value *values;
    61→} lisa_value_array;
    62→
    63→void lisa_value_array_init(lisa_value_array *arr);
    64→void lisa_value_array_write(lisa_value_array *arr, lisa_value value);
    65→void lisa_value_array_free(lisa_value_array *arr);
    66→
    67→/* Bytecode chunk */
    68→typedef struct {
    69→    int count;
    70→    int capacity;
    71→    uint8_t *code;
    72→    int *lines;          /* source line per bytecode byte */
    73→    lisa_value_array constants;
    74→} lisa_chunk;
    75→
    76→void lisa_chunk_init(lisa_chunk *chunk);
    77→void lisa_chunk_write(lisa_chunk *chunk, uint8_t byte, int l
... (truncated)

1→#pragma once
     2→
     3→#include "ctx.h"
     4→
     5→typedef enum { CJ_REGISTER, CJ_CONSTANT, CJ_MEMORY, CJ_REGISTER_LIST } cj_operand_type;
     6→
     7→typedef enum {
     8→  CJ_SHIFT_KIND_NONE = 0,
     9→  CJ_SHIFT_KIND_LSL,
    10→  CJ_SHIFT_KIND_LSR,
    11→  CJ_SHIFT_KIND_ASR,
    12→  CJ_SHIFT_KIND_ROR
    13→} cj_shift_kind;
    14→
    15→typedef enum {
    16→  CJ_EXTEND_KIND_NONE = 0,
    17→  CJ_EXTEND_KIND_UXTB,
    18→  CJ_EXTEND_KIND_UXTH,
    19→  CJ_EXTEND_KIND_UXTW,
    20→  CJ_EXTEND_KIND_UXTX,
    21→  CJ_EXTEND_KIND_SXTB,
    22→  CJ_EXTEND_KIND_SXTH,
    23→  CJ_EXTEND_KIND_SXTW,
    24→  CJ_EXTEND_KIND_SXTX
    25→} cj_extend_kind;
    26→
    27→typedef enum { CJ_MEM_MODE_OFFSET = 0, CJ_MEM_MODE_PRE, CJ_MEM_MODE_POST } cj_mem_mode;
    28→
    29→typedef struct {
    30→  uint8_t kind;       // cj_shift_kind
    31→  uint8_t amount;     // Shift amount (bits depend on instruction)
    32→  uint8_t has_amount; // Explicit amount provided
    33→} cj_shift_info;
    34→
    35→typedef struct {
    36→  uint8_t kind;       // cj_extend_kind
    37→  uint8_t amount;     // Optional left shift applied after extend
    38→  uint8_t has_amount; // Explicit shift amount provided
    39→} cj_extend_info;
    40→
    41→typedef enum {
    42→  CJ_ROUND_DEFAULT = 0,
    43→  CJ_ROUND_NEAREST,
    44→  CJ_ROUND_DOWN,
    45→  CJ_ROUND_UP,
    46→  CJ_ROUND_ZERO
    47→} cj_rounding_mode;
    48→
    49→typedef struct {
    50→  cj_operand_type type;
    51→  const char *mask;
    52→  uint8_t zero_mask;
    53→  uint8_t sae;
    54→  uint8_t rounding;
    55→  union {
    56→    const char *reg;
    57→    uint64_t constant;
    58→    struct {
    59→      const char *base;
    60→      const char *index;
    61→      uint8_t scale;
    62→      int32_t disp;
    63→      cj_mem_mode mode;
    64→    } mem;
    65→    struct {
    66→      const char *const *regs;
    67→      uint8_t count;
    68→    } reg_list;
    69→  };
    70→  cj_shift_info shift;
    71→  cj_extend_info extend;
    72→} cj_operand;
    73→
    74→static inline cj_operand cj_make_register(const char *name) {
    75→  cj_operand op = {
    76→      .type = CJ_REGISTER,
    77→      .mask = NULL,
    78→      .zero_mask = 0,
    79→      .sae = 0,
    80→      .rounding = CJ_ROUND_DEFAULT,
    81→      .reg = name,
    82→      .shift = {.kind = CJ_SHIFT_KIND_NONE, .amount = 0, .has_amount = 0},
    83→      .extend = {.kind = CJ_EXTEND_KIND_NONE, .amount = 0, .has_amount = 0},
    84→  };
    85→  return op;
    86→}
    87→
    88→static inline cj_operand cj_make_constant(uint64_t value) {
    89→  cj_operand op = {
    90→      .type = CJ_CONSTANT,
    91→      .mask = NULL,
    92→      .zero_mask = 0,
    93→      .sae = 0,
    94→      .rounding = CJ_ROUND_DEFAULT,
    95→      .constant = value,
    96→      .shift = {.kind = CJ_SHIFT_KIND_NONE, .amount = 0, .has_amount = 0},
    97→      .extend = {.kind = CJ_EXTEND_KIND_NONE, .amount = 0,

Let me check which cj instructions are available for the operations I'll need.

Task
{
  "description": "Find available cj instructions",
  "prompt": "I need to find which cj backend instructions are available for the JIT rewrite. Search the codebase for:\n\n1. In `src/arch/x86_64/backend.h`, find if these functions exist: `cj_shr`, `cj_shl`, `cj_sar`, `cj_imul`, `cj_neg`, `cj_or`, `cj_xor`, `cj_and`, `cj_test`, `cj_jz`, `cj_jnz`, `cj_jl`, `cj_jle`, `cj_jg`, `cj_jge`, `cj_jb`, `cj_jmp`, `cj_je`, `cj_jne`, `cj_setl`, `cj_setle`, `cj_setg`, `cj_setge`, `cj_sete`, `cj_setz`, `cj_setnz`, `cj_movzx`\n\n2. In `src/arch/arm64/backend.h`, find if these functions exist: `cj_lsr`, `cj_lsl`, `cj_asr`, `cj_neg`, `cj_orr`, `cj_eor`, `cj_and`, `cj_tst`, `cj_beq`, `cj_bne`, `cj_blt`, `cj_ble`, `cj_bgt`, `cj_bge`, `cj_blo`, `cj_b`, `cj_cset`, `cj_csel`, `cj_mul`, `cj_madd`, `cj_msub`\n\nFor each one, just tell me if it exists (yes/no) and what its signature looks like (the parameters). Just give me a summary table.\n\nAlso check: does `cj_movabs` exist on x86? Does `cj_cmp` work with immediates on both platforms?",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
result
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary:

## Backend Instructions Availability Summary

Based on my search of the codebase, here's what I found:

### X86_64 Backend (`src/arch/x86_64/backend.h`)

| Instruction | Exists | Signature |
|---|---|---|
| `cj_shr` | ✓ | `void cj_shr(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_operand src)` |
| `cj_shl` | ✓ | `void cj_shl(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_operand src)` |
| `cj_sar` | ✓ | `void cj_sar(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_operand src)` |
| `cj_imul` | ✓ | `void cj_imul(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_operand src)` |
| `cj_neg` | ✓ | `void cj_neg(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand value)` |
| `cj_or` | ✓ | `void cj_or(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_operand src)` |
| `cj_xor` | ✓ | `void cj_xor(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_operand src)` |
| `cj_and` | ✓ | `void cj_and(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_operand src)` |
| `cj_test` | ✓ | `void cj_test(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_operand src)` |
| `cj_jz` | ✓ | `#define cj_jz(ctx, target)` (macro, accepts label or operand) |
| `cj_jnz` | ✓ | `#define cj_jnz(ctx, target)` (macro, accepts label or operand) |
| `cj_jl` | ✓ | `#define cj_jl(ctx, target)` (macro, accepts label or operand) |
| `cj_jle` | ✓ | `#define cj_jle(ctx, target)` (macro, accepts label or operand) |
| `cj_jg` | ✓ | `#define cj_jg(ctx, target)` (macro, accepts label or operand) |
| `cj_jge` | ✓ | `#define cj_jge(ctx, target)` (macro, accepts label or operand) |
| `cj_jb` | ✓ | `#define cj_jb(ctx, target)` (macro, accepts label or operand) |
| `cj_jmp` | ✓ | `#define cj_jmp(ctx, target)` (macro, accepts label or operand) |
| `cj_je` | ✗ | **Not found** (use `cj_jz` as alias) |
| `cj_jne` | ✗ | **Not found** (use `cj_jnz` as alias) |
| `cj_setl` | ✓ | `void cj_setl(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst)` |
| `cj_setle` | ✓ | `void cj_setle(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst)` |
| `cj_setg` | ✓ | `void cj_setg(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst)` |
| `cj_setge` | ✓ | `void cj_setge(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst)` |
| `cj_sete` | ✓ | `void cj_sete(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst)` |
| `cj_setz` | ✓ | `void cj_setz(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst)` (alias for sete) |
| `cj_setnz` | ✓ | `void cj_setnz(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst)` |
| `cj_movzx` | ✓ | `void cj_movzx(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_operand src)` |
| `cj_movabs` | ✗ | **Not found** (use `cj_mov` instead) |
| `cj_cmp` | ✓ | `void cj_cmp(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_operand src)` |

**cj_cmp on x86_64**: Works with immediates (tested on `al` register, supports signed 8-bit immediates; extends to full register sizes where applicable).

---

 11160→    instr = arm64_is_64bit(dst.reg) ? 0xCB0003E0 : 0x4B0003E0;
 11161→    instr &= ~((1u << 5) - 1u);
 11162→    instr |= (rd & ((1u << 5) - 1u));
 11163→    instr &= ~(((1u << 5) - 1u) << 16);
 11164→    instr |= ((rn & ((1u << 5) - 1u)) << 16);
 11165→    uint32_t shift_mode = 0;
 11166→    switch (src.shift.kind) {
 11167→      case CJ_SHIFT_KIND_NONE:
 11168→      case CJ_SHIFT_KIND_LSL:
 11169→        shift_mode = 0;
 11170→        break;
 11171→      case CJ_SHIFT_KIND_LSR:
 11172→        shift_mode = 1;
 11173→        break;
 11174→      case CJ_SHIFT_KIND_ASR:
 11175→        shift_mode = 2;
 11176→        break;
 11177→      case CJ_SHIFT_KIND_ROR:
 11178→        return;
 11179→      default:
 11180→        return;
 11181→    }
 11182→    instr &= ~(((1u << 2) - 1u) << 22);
 11183→    instr |= ((shift_mode & ((1u << 2) - 1u)) << 22);
 11184→    uint32_t shift_amount = (src.shift.kind == CJ_SHIFT_KIND_NONE && !src.shift.has_amount) ? 0u : src.shift.amount;
 11185→    if (arm64_is_64bit(dst.reg)) {
 11186→      if (shift_amount > 63u) return;
 11187→    } else {
 11188→      if (shift_amount > 31u) return;
 11189→    }
 11190→    instr &= ~(((1u << 6) - 1u) << 10);
 11191→    instr |= ((shift_amount & ((1u << 6) - 1u)) << 10);
 11192→    cj_add_u32(ctx, instr);
 11193→    return;
 11194→  }
 11195→}

  1189→    int8_t dst_base_reg = dst.mem.base ? parse_reg(dst.mem.base) : -1;
  1190→    int8_t dst_index_reg = dst.mem.index ? parse_reg(dst.mem.index) : -1;
  1191→    uint8_t dst_rex_b = (dst_base_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  1192→    uint8_t dst_rex_x = (dst_index_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  1193→    uint8_t rex_w = 1;
  1194→    uint8_t rex_r = 0;
  1195→    uint8_t rex_x = dst_rex_x;
  1196→    uint8_t rex_b = dst_rex_b;
  1197→    uint8_t need_rex = rex_w || rex_r || rex_x || rex_b;
  1198→    if (need_rex) emit_rex(ctx, rex_w, rex_r, rex_x, rex_b);
  1199→    cj_add_u8(ctx, 0xd1);
  1200→    int mod = emit_memory_modrm(ctx, 5, dst.mem.base, dst.mem.index, dst.mem.scale, dst.mem.disp);
  1201→    if (mod == 1) cj_add_u8(ctx, (int8_t)dst.mem.disp);
  1202→    else if (mod == 2) cj_add_u32(ctx, (uint32_t)dst.mem.disp);
  1203→    return;
  1204→  }
  1205→  if (dst.type == CJ_REGISTER && detect_reg_size(dst.reg) == 64 && src.type == CJ_REGISTER && src.reg && strcmp(src.reg, "cl") == 0) {
  1206→    int8_t dst_reg = parse_reg(dst.reg);
  1207→    if (dst_reg < 0) return;
  1208→    int8_t src_reg = parse_reg(src.reg);
  1209→    if (src_reg < 0) return;
  1210→    uint8_t rex_w = 1;
  1211→    uint8_t rex_r = 0;
  1212→    uint8_t rex_x = 0;
  1213→    uint8_t rex_b = (dst_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  1214→    uint8_t need_rex = rex_w || rex_r || rex_x || rex_b;
  1215→    if (need_rex) emit_rex(ctx, rex_w, rex_r, rex_x, rex_b);
  1216→    cj_add_u8(ctx, 0xd3);
  1217→    emit_modrm(ctx, 3, 5, dst_reg & 7);
  1218→    return;
  1219→  }
  1220→  if (dst.type == CJ_MEMORY && src.type == CJ_REGISTER && src.reg && strcmp(src.reg, "cl") == 0) {
  1221→    int8_t dst_base_reg = dst.mem.base ? parse_reg(dst.mem.base) : -1;
  1222→    int8_t dst_index_reg = dst.mem.index ? parse_reg(dst.mem.index) : -1;
  1223→    uint8_t dst_rex_b = (dst_base_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  1224→    uint8_t dst_rex_x = (dst_index_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  1225→    int8_t src_reg = parse_reg(src.reg);
  1226→    if (src_reg < 0) return;
  1227→    uint8_t rex_w = 1;
  1228→    uint8_t rex_r = 0;
  1229→    uint8_t rex_x = dst_rex_x;
  1230→    uint8_t rex_b = dst_rex_b;
  1231→    uint8_t need_rex = rex_w || rex_r || rex_x || rex_b;
  1232→    if (need_rex) emit_rex(ctx, rex_w, rex_r, rex_x, rex_b);
  1233→    cj_add_u8(ctx, 0xd3);
  1234→    i
... (truncated)
⚙ Grep ×5, Read ×7

Good. x86 cj_shr/cj_shl/cj_sar all support reg64, imm. On ARM64, I'll need raw cj_add_u32 for immediate shifts. I also need to check one more thing: cj_or on x86 with immediate.

  "-A": 1,
  "head_limit": 15
}

100→}
   101→
   102→static inline cj_operand cj_make_memory(const char *base, const char *index, uint8_t scale,
   103→                                        int32_t disp) {
   104→  cj_operand op = {
   105→      .type = CJ_MEMORY,
   106→      .mask = NULL,
   107→      .zero_mask = 0,
   108→      .sae = 0,
   109→      .rounding = CJ_ROUND_DEFAULT,
   110→      .mem =
   111→          {.base = base, .index = index, .scale = scale, .disp = disp, .mode = CJ_MEM_MODE_OFFSET},
   112→      .shift = {.kind = CJ_SHIFT_KIND_NONE, .amount = 0, .has_amount = 0},
   113→      .extend = {.kind = CJ_EXTEND_KIND_NONE, .amount = 0, .has_amount = 0},
   114→  };
   115→  return op;
   116→}
   117→
   118→static inline cj_operand cj_make_preindexed(const char *base, int32_t disp) {
   119→  cj_operand op = cj_make_memory(base, NULL, 1, disp);
   120→  op.mem.mode = CJ_MEM_MODE_PRE;
   121→  return op;
   122→}
   123→
   124→static inline cj_operand cj_make_postindexed(const char *base, int32_t disp) {
   125→  cj_operand op = cj_make_memory(base, NULL, 1, disp);
   126→  op.mem.mode = CJ_MEM_MODE_POST;
   127→  return op;
   128→}
   129→
   130→static inline cj_operand cj_make_reg_list(const char *const *regs, uint8_t count) {
   131→  cj_operand op = {
   132→      .type = CJ_REGISTER_LIST,
   133→      .mask = NULL,
   134→      .zero_mask = 0,
   135→      .sae = 0,
   136→      .rounding = CJ_ROUND_DEFAULT,
   137→      .reg_list = {.regs = regs, .count = count},
   138→      .shift = {.kind = CJ_SHIFT_KIND_NONE, .amount = 0, .has_amount = 0},
   139→      .extend = {.kind = CJ_EXTEND_KIND_NONE, .amount = 0, .has_amount = 0},
   140→  };
   141→  return op;
   142→}
   143→
   144→static inline cj_operand cj_apply_shift(cj_operand base, cj_shift_kind kind, int amount) {
   145→  if (kind == CJ_SHIFT_KIND_NONE) {
   146→    base.shift.kind = CJ_SHIFT_KIND_NONE;
   147→    base.shift.amount = 0;
   148→    base.shift.has_amount = 0;
   149→    return base;
   150→  }
   151→  base.shift.kind = kind;
   152→  if (amount >= 0) {
   153→    base.shift.amount = (uint8_t)amount;
   154→    base.shift.has_amount = 1;
   155→  } else {
   156→    base.shift.amount = 0;
   157→    base.shift.has_amount = 0;
   158→  }
   159→  return base;
   160→}
   161→
   162→static inline cj_operand cj_apply_extend(cj_operand base, cj_extend_kind kind, int amount) {
   163→  if (kind == CJ_EXTEND_KIND_NONE) {
   164→    base.extend.kind = CJ_EXTEND_KIND_NONE;
   165→    base.extend.amount = 0;
   166→    base.extend.has_amount = 0;
   167→    return base;
   168→  }
   169→  base.extend.kind = kind;
   170→  if (amount >= 0) {
   171→    base.extend.amount = (uint8_t)amount;
   172→    base.extend.has_amount = 1;
   173→  } else {
   174→    base.extend.amount = 0;
   175→    base.extend.has_amount = 0;
   176→  }
   177→  return base;
   178→}
   179→
   180→static inline cj_operand cj_operand_lsl(cj_operand base, int amount) {
   181→  return cj_apply_shift(base, CJ_SH

⚙ Bash, Grep ×7, Read ×4

op.h includes the arch-specific backend.h at the bottom. So arm64_parse_reg is available. Now I have everything I need. Let me also quickly check the Makefile for lisa to understand build flags.

Bash — Read lisa Makefile
{
  "command": "cat lisa/Makefile",
  "description": "Read lisa Makefile"
}
result
CC ?= cc
CJ_DIR = ../src
CFLAGS = -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wno-gnu -O2 -I$(CJ_DIR)
DEVFLAGS = -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wno-gnu -Werror -g -O0 -I$(CJ_DIR)
SOURCES = main.c value.c object.c lexer.c parser.c ast.c chunk.c compiler.c vm.c debug.c jit.c $(CJ_DIR)/ctx.c
TARGET = bin/lisa

.PHONY: all dev clean

all:
	mkdir -p bin
	$(CC) $(SOURCES) -o $(TARGET) $(CFLAGS) -lm

dev:
	mkdir -p bin
	$(CC) $(SOURCES) -o $(TARGET) $(DEVFLAGS) -lm

clean:
	rm -rf bin
Grep — lisa_jit_add\b
{
  "pattern": "lisa_jit_add\\b",
  "path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "output_mode": "content",
  "-A": 15
}
result
838:lisa_value lisa_jit_add(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value a, lisa_value b) {
839-    if (IS_STRING(a) && IS_STRING(b)) {
840-        /* String concatenation */
841-        lisa_obj_string *sa = AS_STRING(a);
842-        lisa_obj_string *sb = AS_STRING(b);
843-        int length = sa->length + sb->length;
844-        char *chars = malloc((size_t)length + 1);
845-        memcpy(chars, sa->chars, (size_t)sa->length);
846-        memcpy(chars + sa->length, sb->chars, (size_t)sb->length);
847-        chars[length] = '\0';
848-        sync_gc_roots(vm);
849-        lisa_obj_string *result = lisa_take_string(&vm->gc, chars, length);
850-        return LISA_OBJ(result);
851-    }
852-    if (IS_INT(a) && IS_INT(b)) return LISA_INT(AS_INT(a) + AS_INT(b));
853-    if (lisa_is_number(a) && lisa_is_number(b))
Grep — lisa_jit_call_helper
{
  "pattern": "lisa_jit_call_helper",
  "path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "output_mode": "content",
  "-A": 60
}
result
714:lisa_value lisa_jit_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc) {
715-    lisa_value callee = vm->stack_top[-1 - argc];
716-    if (!call_value(vm, callee, argc)) {
717-        return LISA_NIL; /* error already reported */
718-    }
719-    /* Check if callee was a native (call_value already handled it) */
720-    if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {
721-        return vm->stack_top[-1]; /* result already on stack */
722-    }
723-    /* Closure call — dispatch to JIT or interpreter */
724-    lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
725-    lisa_value result;
726-    if (frame->closure->function->jit_code) {
727-        lisa_jit_fn jit_fn = (lisa_jit_fn)frame->closure->function->jit_code;
728-        result = jit_fn(vm, frame->closure, frame->slots);
729-    } else {
730-        int target_depth = vm->frame_count - 1;
731-        lisa_run(vm, target_depth);
732-        result = vm->stack_top[-1];
733-    }
734-    /* Pop the callee's frame */
735-    close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);
736-    vm->frame_count--;
737-    vm->stack_top = frame->slots;
738-    push(vm, result);
739-    return result;
740-}
741-
742-lisa_value lisa_jit_tail_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc) {
743-    lisa_value callee = vm->stack_top[-1 - argc];
744-
745-    /* Native functions: just call directly */
746-    if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {
747-        call_value(vm, callee, argc);
748-        return vm->stack_top[-1];
749-    }
750-
751-    if (!IS_OBJ(callee) || OBJ_TYPE(callee) != OBJ_CLOSURE) {
752-        runtime_error(vm, "Can only call functions and closures.");
753-        return LISA_NIL;
754-    }
755-
756-    lisa_obj_closure *closure = AS_CLOSURE(callee);
757-    if (argc != closure->function->arity) {
758-        runtime_error(vm, "Expected %d arguments but got %d.",
759-                      closure->function->arity, argc);
760-        return LISA_NIL;
761-    }
762-
763-    /* The JIT caller's frame is the current top frame; reuse it */
764-    lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
765-    close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);
766-
767-    /* Slide callee + args down over the current frame */
768-    lisa_value *src = vm->stack_top - argc - 1;
769-    memmove(frame->slots, src, (size_t)(argc + 1) * sizeof(lisa_value));
770-    vm->stack_top = frame->slots + argc + 1;
771-
772-    frame->closure = closure;
773-    frame->ip = closure->function->chunk.code;
774-

  3998→    int mod = emit_memory_modrm(ctx, 7, dst.mem.base, dst.mem.index, dst.mem.scale, dst.mem.disp);
  3999→    if (mod == 1) cj_add_u8(ctx, (int8_t)dst.mem.disp);
  4000→    else if (mod == 2) cj_add_u32(ctx, (uint32_t)dst.mem.disp);
  4001→    cj_add_u8(ctx, (uint8_t)src_imm);
  4002→    return;
  4003→  }
  4004→  if (dst.type == CJ_REGISTER && detect_reg_size(dst.reg) == 16 && src.type == CJ_REGISTER && detect_reg_size(src.reg) == 16) {
  4005→    int8_t dst_reg = parse_reg(dst.reg);
  4006→    if (dst_reg < 0) return;
  4007→    int8_t src_reg = parse_reg(src.reg);
  4008→    if (src_reg < 0) return;
  4009→    cj_add_u8(ctx, 0x66);
  4010→    uint8_t rex_w = 0;
  4011→    uint8_t rex_r = (src_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  4012→    uint8_t rex_x = 0;
  4013→    uint8_t rex_b = (dst_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  4014→    uint8_t need_rex = rex_w || rex_r || rex_x || rex_b;
  4015→    if (need_rex) emit_rex(ctx, rex_w, rex_r, rex_x, rex_b);
  4016→    cj_add_u8(ctx, 0x39);
  4017→    emit_modrm(ctx, 3, src_reg & 7, dst_reg & 7);
  4018→    return;
  4019→  }
  4020→  if (dst.type == CJ_MEMORY && src.type == CJ_REGISTER && detect_reg_size(src.reg) == 16) {
  4021→    int8_t dst_base_reg = dst.mem.base ? parse_reg(dst.mem.base) : -1;
  4022→    int8_t dst_index_reg = dst.mem.index ? parse_reg(dst.mem.index) : -1;
  4023→    uint8_t dst_rex_b = (dst_base_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  4024→    uint8_t dst_rex_x = (dst_index_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  4025→    int8_t src_reg = parse_reg(src.reg);
  4026→    if (src_reg < 0) return;
  4027→    cj_add_u8(ctx, 0x66);
  4028→    uint8_t rex_w = 0;
  4029→    uint8_t rex_r = (src_reg >= 8) ? 1 : 0;
  4030→    uint8_t rex_x = dst_rex_x;
  4031→    uint8_t rex_b = dst_rex_b;
  4032→    uint8_t need_rex = rex_w || rex_r || rex_x || rex_b;
  4033→    if (need_rex) emit_rex(ctx, rex_w, rex_r, rex_x, rex_b);
  4034→    cj_add_u8(ctx, 0x39);
  4035→    int mod = emit_memory_modrm(ctx, src_reg & 7, dst.mem.base, dst.mem.index, dst.mem.scale, dst.mem.disp);
  4036→    if (mod == 1) cj_add_u8(ctx, (int8_t)dst.mem.disp);
  4037→    else if (mod == 2) cj_add_u32(ctx, (uint32_t)dst.mem.disp);
  4038→    return;
  4039→  }
  4040→  if (dst.type == CJ_REGISTER && detect_reg_size(dst.reg) == 32 && src.type == CJ_CONSTANT && src.constant <= 0xFFFFFFFFu) {
  4041→    int8_t dst_reg = parse_reg(dst.reg);
  4042→    if (dst_reg < 0) return;
  4043→    uint8_t rex_w = 0;
  4044→    uint8_t rex_r = 0;

... (truncated)
⚙ Write

Now I have all the information I need. Let me create the rewrite. The key findings are:

- x86: cj_cmp with immediate only works reliably for RAX register; use reg-reg for others

- ARM64: immediate shifts need raw instruction emission; cj_cset is a stub

- All branch macros, setcc, and basic arithmetic instructions are available

Let me write the complete rewrite now.

{
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "content": "#include \"jit.h\"\n#include \"chunk.h\"\n#include \"vm.h\"\n\n#pragma GCC diagnostic push\n#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored \"-Wunused-parameter\"\n#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored \"-Wunused-but-set-variable\"\n#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored \"-Wunused-variable\"\n#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored \"-Wc23-extensions\"\n#include \"ctx.h\"\n#include \"op.h\"\n#include \"register.h\"\n#pragma GCC diagnostic pop\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n\ntypedef lisa_value (*lisa_jit_fn)(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_closure *closure,\n                                  lisa_value *slots);\n\n/* ===== Platform Register Definitions ===== */\n\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n\n#define REG_VM       \"rbx\"\n#define REG_SLOTS    \"r12\"\n#define REG_CLOSURE  \"r13\"\n#define REG_STKTOP   \"r14\"\n#define REG_CONSTS   \"r15\"\n\n#define REG_CACHE0   \"r8\"\n#define REG_CACHE1   \"r9\"\n#define REG_CACHE2   \"r10\"\n#define REG_CACHE3   \"r11\"\n\n#define REG_TMP1     \"rax\"\n#define REG_TMP2     \"rcx\"\n#define REG_TMP3     \"rdx\"\n#define REG_TMP4     \"rsi\"\n#define REG_TMP5     \"rdi\"\n#define REG_CALLADDR \"r10\"\n\n#define REG_ARG0     \"rdi\"\n#define REG_ARG1     \"rsi\"\n#define REG_ARG2     \"rdx\"\n#define REG_ARG3     \"rcx\"\n#define REG_RET      \"rax\"\n\n#define EMIT_JEQ(ctx, label) cj_jz(ctx, label)\n#define EMIT_JNE(ctx, label) cj_jnz(ctx, label)\n#define EMIT_JLT(ctx, label) cj_jl(ctx, label)\n#define EMIT_JLE(ctx, label) cj_jle(ctx, label)\n#define EMIT_JGT(ctx, label) cj_jg(ctx, label)\n#define EMIT_JGE(ctx, label) cj_jge(ctx, label)\n#define EMIT_JMP(ctx, label) cj_jmp(ctx, label)\n#define EMIT_JB(ctx, label)  cj_jb(ctx, label)\n\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n\n#define REG_VM       \"x19\"\n#define REG_SLOTS    \"x20\"\n#define REG_CLOSURE  \"x21\"\n#define REG_STKTOP   \"x22\"\n#define REG_CONSTS   \"x23\"\n\n#define REG

human

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:

Let me chronologically analyze the conversation to capture all important details.

1. The user provided a detailed plan for rewriting lisa/jit.c to use a register cache and inline integer fast paths. The plan was comprehensive, covering:

- Register cache design (4 slots, compile-time tracking)

- Register assignments for x86-64 and ARM64

- Inline fast paths for arithmetic, comparison, equality, negate, not, jump_if_false

- Helper-only operations that always flush and call C helpers

- Branch handling (flush at all branch points)

- File structure for the new jit.c

2. I read multiple files to understand the codebase:

- lisa/jit.c (existing implementation - stack machine approach)

- lisa/jit.h (interface)

- lisa/object.h (object types, struct layouts)

- lisa/vm.h (VM struct, JIT helper declarations)

- lisa/value.h (NaN-boxing scheme)

- lisa/chunk.h (opcodes, bytecode format)

- src/op.h (cj operand types, backend inclusion)

- src/ctx.h (cj context, labels)

- src/register.h and ARM64 registers

3. I explored the cj backend capabilities extensively:

- x86: cj_shr/shl/sar support reg64+imm, cj_jz/jnz (not je/jne), setl/setle/setg/setge/setz/setnz work with "al"

- ARM64: cj_lsl/lsr/asr only support variable (register) shifts, cj_cset is empty stub, cj_csel hardcoded to EQ condition, cj_bcc exists for unsigned lower

- cj_cmp on x86 only supports immediate with RAX register (CMP RAX, imm32), not arbitrary 64-bit registers

- ARM64 cj_cmp doesn't support immediates > 4095

4. Key design decisions made during investigation:

- ARM64 immediate shifts: use raw cj_add_u32 with UBFM/SBFM instruction encoding

- ARM64 CSET: write raw CSINC instruction since cj_cset is a stub

- ARM64 payload extraction: use UBFM Xd, Xn, #0, #47 (single instruction vs two shifts)

- x86 type check: always shift into RAX (REG_TMP1) since CMP with immediate only works for RAX

- Boolean result: x86 uses MOV rax, LISA_FALSE + setcc al; ARM64 uses CSET + ORR with LISA_FALSE

- Cache flush at branch targets, before calls, before jumps

- cache_flush_to(ctx, cache, 2) for normalizing before binary ops

5. I wrote the complete rewrite of jit.c and attempted to build it.

6. Build errors encountered:

- emit_push unused (replaced by cache_push throughout)

- emit_asr_imm unused (emit_sign_extend48 uses raw instructions directly)

- I fixed the first error by removing emit_push, was about to remove emit_asr_imm

7. The build was attempted with make -C lisa dev which uses -Werror.

Let me note the exact state of the code and what still needs to be done.

The user's message was "Implement the following plan:" followed by the detailed plan. So the task is straightforward - implement the plan in jit.c.

Current state: I wrote the complete jit.c, attempted to build, got 2 unused function errors. I fixed one (emit_push removed) but haven't yet removed emit_asr_imm. Need to rebuild and test.

The vm.c helper functions were checked - lisa_jit_call_helper pushes result to stack via push(vm, result), so OP_CALL should NOT cache_push the result.

Let me capture the key code patterns and decisions more thoroughly.

Summary:

1. Primary Request and Intent:

The user asked to implement a detailed plan to rewrite lisa/jit.c — replacing the "stack machine in native code" JIT with a proper register-cache-based JIT with inline integer fast paths. The goal is measurable speedup over the interpreter. Only jit.c changes; all other files (vm.c helpers, object.h, jit.h, Makefile) remain as-is. The plan specifies:

- A compile-time register cache (4 slots) tracking which stack values are in CPU registers

- Inline fast paths for integer arithmetic (ADD/SUB/MUL), comparisons (LT/LE/GT/GE), equality (EQ/NE), NEGATE, NOT, JUMP_IF_FALSE

- Flush-and-call pattern for complex ops (globals, closures, calls, list ops, div, mod)

- Specific register assignments for x86-64 and ARM64

- Conservative branch handling (flush cache at every branch source/target)

- Verification via multiple test programs (factorial, fibonacci, mutual recursion, closures, list ops)

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- NaN-boxing: Values are uint64_t with tag bits in top 16 bits. QNAN=0x7FFC000000000000, TAG_INT=0x0002000000000000. IS_INT checks top 16 bits == 0x7FFE. Payload is lower 48 bits.

- cj JIT framework: Header-only backends for x86-64 and ARM64. Registers identified by string names. Labels with forward-reference fixup system. cj_add_u32() for raw instruction emission.

- Register cache: Compile-time tracker. depth indicates how many stack values are in CPU registers. Push/pop/flush operations generate native code to maintain consistency.

- ARM64 backend limitations: cj_lsl/cj_lsr/cj_asr only support variable (register-register) shifts, not immediates. cj_cset is an empty stub. cj_csel has hardcoded EQ condition. cj_cmp only supports 12-bit immediates.

- x86 backend limitations: No cj_je/cj_jne (use cj_jz/cj_jnz). cj_cmp with immediate only works for RAX register (CMP RAX, imm32 encoding), not arbitrary 64-bit registers.

- ARM64 raw instruction encodings: UBFM for LSR/LSL immediate, SBFM for ASR immediate, CSINC for CSET.

- Boolean result trick: LISA_FALSE=0x7FFD000000000000, LISA_TRUE=0x7FFD000000000001 (differ in bit 0). Load LISA_FALSE then set bit 0 via setcc (x86) or CSET+ORR (ARM64).

3. Files and Code Sections:

- lisa/jit.c — The file being completely rewritten. Was 886 lines of stack-machine JIT, now ~700 lines with register cache + inline fast paths.

- Changes: Complete rewrite with new sections: platform register defines (including REG_CACHE0-3), operand helpers, low-level emit helpers, platform-specific shift helpers using raw ARM64 instructions, ARM64 CSET helper, register cache struct and functions (cache_init/flush/flush_to/push/pop), prologue/epilogue (unchanged), inline type-check helpers, inline fast-path generators (emit_binop_int_fast, emit_cmpop_int_fast, emit_eqop_fast), wrapper functions (emit_binop, emit_cmpop, emit_eqop), branch target scanner (unchanged), main compile loop (rewritten with cache-aware opcodes), lisa_jit_free (unchanged).

- Most recent edit: Removed unused emit_push function. Still need to remove unused emit_asr_imm.

- lisa/jit.h — Interface file (read-only, not modified). Declares lisa_jit_compile() and lisa_jit_free().

- lisa/object.h — Object type definitions (read-only). Key structs: lisa_obj_function (has jit_code, jit_ctx), lisa_obj_closure (has function, upvalues), lisa_obj_upvalue (has location, closed).

- lisa/vm.h — VM struct and JIT helper declarations (read-only). Key: lisa_vm struct with stack_top, frames, frame_count. JIT helpers: lisa_jit_call_helper, lisa_jit_add/sub/mul/div/mod, lisa_jit_less/less_equal/greater/greater_equal, lisa_jit_equal/not_equal, lisa_jit_negate, lisa_jit_cons/car/cdr, etc.

- lisa/vm.c — Helper implementations (read-only). Key finding: lisa_jit_call_helper pushes result to memory stack via push(vm, result) before returning, so OP_CALL should NOT cache_push the result.

- lisa/value.h — NaN-boxing scheme (read-only). QNAN=0x7FFC000000000000, TAG_INT=0x0002000000000000, PAYLOAD_MASK=0x0000FFFFFFFFFFFF. LISA_NIL=0x7FFC000000000000, LISA_TRUE=0x7FFD000000000001, LISA_FALSE=0x7FFD000000000000.

- lisa/chunk.h — Opcode enum and chunk struct (read-only). All opcodes from OP_CONSTANT through OP_PRINTLN.

- src/op.h — cj operand types, includes backend.h at bottom. Provides cj_make_register, cj_make_constant, cj_make_memory, cj_apply_shift, condition codes.

- src/ctx.h — cj context struct and API. cj_add_u32() for raw instruction emission. cj_create_label, cj_mark_label, create_cj_fn, cj_resolve_label.

- src/arch/arm64/backend.h — ARM64 backend (read-only). Key findings:

- cj_lsl/cj_lsr: Only register-register (LSLV/LSRV), no immediate support

- cj_cset: Empty stub (does nothing)

- cj_csel: Hardcoded EQ condition only

- cj_neg(dst, src): Two-operand form, scalar path works

- cj_mul(dst, src): Two-operand, MADD-based

- cj_orr(dst, src): Two-operand, register only

- cj_bcc: Exists (condition code 3 = unsigned lower)

- cj_cmp: Supports reg-reg and reg-imm12

- src/arch/x86_64/backend.h — x86 backend (read-only). Key findings:

- cj_shr/cj_shl/cj_sar: Support reg64 + imm8

- cj_jz/cj_jnz: Exist (no cj_je/cj_jne)

- cj_setl/cj_setle/cj_setg/cj_setge/cj_setz/cj_setnz: Work with 8-bit registers like "al"

- cj_cmp: Only RAX supports imm32 comparison (CMP RAX, imm32 encoding). Other 64-bit registers only support reg-reg.

- cj_imul: Two-operand form

- cj_neg: One-operand form (in-place)

- lisa/Makefile — Build config (read-only). make dev uses -Werror -g -O0. Sources include jit.c and ../src/ctx.c.

Key code from the rewritten jit.c - ARM64 shift helpers using raw instructions:

```c

static void emit_lsr_imm(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *src, int shift) {

#if defined(__x86_64__)

if (strcmp(dst, src) != 0) cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src));

cj_shr(ctx, reg(dst), imm((uint64_t)shift));

#elif defined(__aarch64__)

int rd = arm64_parse_reg(dst);

int rn = arm64_parse_reg(src);

uint32_t instr = 0xD340FC00 | ((uint32_t)(shift & 0x3f) << 16)

| ((uint32_t)(rn & 0x1f) << 5) | (uint32_t)(rd & 0x1f);

cj_add_u32(ctx, instr);

#endif

}

```

ARM64 CSET helper:

```c

static void emit_cset(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, int invert_cond) {

int rd = arm64_parse_reg(dst);

uint32_t instr = 0x9A9F07E0 | ((uint32_t)(invert_cond & 0xf) << 12)

| (uint32_t)(rd & 0x1f);

cj_add_u32(ctx, instr);

}

```

Register cache functions:

```c

static void cache_flush_to(cj_ctx *ctx, reg_cache_t *cache, int keep) {

if (keep >= cache->depth) return;

int n = cache->depth - keep;

for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)

emit_store64(ctx, cache->regs[i], REG_STKTOP, i * 8);

if (n > 0)

cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_STKTOP), imm((uint64_t)n * 8));

for (int i = 0; i < keep; i++)

cj_mov(ctx, reg(cache->regs[i]), reg(cache->regs[n + i]));

cache->depth = keep;

}

```

4. Errors and fixes:

- Build error: unused function 'emit_push': The emit_push function was defined but never called in the new code because all push operations go through cache_push. Fixed by removing the emit_push function definition.

- Build error: unused function 'emit_asr_imm': The emit_asr_imm function was defined but never called because emit_sign_extend48 directly uses cj_shl+cj_sar (x86) or raw SBFM instruction (ARM64) instead of calling emit_asr_imm. Not yet fixed — still needs to be removed.

- x86 cj_je/cj_jne don't exist: Discovered during exploration. The plan noted this and the solution uses cj_jz/cj_jnz via EMIT_JEQ/EMIT_JNE macros.

- ARM64 cj_cset is empty stub: Discovered during exploration. Solved by writing raw CSINC instruction emission via emit_cset helper.

- ARM64 immediate shifts not supported by cj API: Discovered during exploration. Solved by writing raw UBFM/SBFM instruction emission.

- x86 cj_cmp with immediate only works for RAX: Discovered during exploration. Solved by always shifting type-check values into REG_TMP1="rax" before comparing.

5. Problem Solving:

- Cache state consistency across fast/slow paths: Solved by using cache_flush_to(ctx, cache, 2) before binary ops to normalize cache state, ensuring both fast (inline) and slow (helper call) paths end with cache.depth=1 and result in cache.regs[0].

- Register conflicts in ABI call setup: Analyzed carefully. Cache registers (r8-r11/x10-x13) never overlap with ABI argument registers, so no conflicts when setting up helper calls from cache registers. For fallback paths where values are in TMP regs that overlap ARG regs (ARM64: x0=ARG0, x1=ARG1), the reverse-order move pattern (ARG2 first, then ARG1, then ARG0) prevents clobbering.

- REG_CALLADDR overlaps with cache[2] on x86: Not a problem because CALLADDR is only used after cache flush (when cache registers are free).

- OP_CALL result handling: lisa_jit_call_helper pushes result to memory stack via push(vm, result). After reload_stack_top, result is already on memory stack. Cache stays at depth=0 — no cache_push needed.

6. All user messages:

- Message 1: "Implement the following plan: [detailed plan for Register Cache + Inline Fast Paths rewrite of jit.c]" — This was the sole user request, containing the complete implementation specification with register assignments, inline fast path designs, helper-only operations list, flush+call pattern, branch handling rules, file structure outline, and verification commands.

7. Pending Tasks:

- Remove unused emit_asr_imm function from jit.c

- Rebuild with make -C lisa dev and verify clean compilation with -Werror

- Run verification tests from the plan:

- bin/lisa -e '(def fac (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) 1 (* n (fac (- n 1)))))) (println (fac 20))'

- bin/lisa -e '(def fib (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) n (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2)))))) (println (fib 30))'

- bin/lisa -e '(def loop (fn [n] (if (<= n 0) "done" (loop (- n 1))))) (println (loop 1000000))'

- Benchmark JIT vs interpreter with fib(35)

- Mutual recursion test

- Closures and list operations tests

8. Current Work:

The complete rewrite of lisa/jit.c has been written and saved. The first build attempt produced two unused function warnings treated as errors (-Werror). The first error (emit_push unused) was fixed by removing the function. The second error (emit_asr_imm unused) has NOT yet been fixed. The build has not been retried after the first fix.

9. Optional Next Step:

Remove the unused emit_asr_imm function from lisa/jit.c, then rebuild with make -C lisa dev to verify clean compilation. This directly continues the implementation task: "Implement the following plan: Rewrite JIT: Register Cache + Inline Fast Paths". The last action was editing jit.c to remove emit_push, and the build still fails due to the remaining emit_asr_imm unused function error.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: <claude-internal>

Please continue the conversation from where we left off without asking the user any further questions. Continue with the last task that you were asked to work on.

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "/* Arithmetic shift right: dst = (signed)src >> shift (sign-extend) */\nstatic void emit_asr_imm(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *src, int shift) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    if (strcmp(dst, src) != 0)\n        cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src));\n    cj_sar(ctx, reg(dst), imm((uint64_t)shift));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    /* SBFM Xd, Xn, #shift, #63 */\n    int rd = arm64_parse_reg(dst);\n    int rn = arm64_parse_reg(src);\n    uint32_t instr = 0x9340FC00\n        | ((uint32_t)(shift & 0x3f) << 16)\n        | ((uint32_t)(rn & 0x1f) << 5)\n        | (uint32_t)(rd & 0x1f);\n    cj_add_u32(ctx, instr);\n#endif\n}\n\n/* Clear top 16 bits",
  "new_string": "/* Clear top 16 bits"
}
result
The file lisa/jit.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Read

Now let me also check for the diagnostics about cj_movz, cj_movk, cj_ldr, cj_str — those look like ARM64-specific functions that may not exist in the backend. Let me check the current code around those lines.

Read — lisa/jit.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "offset": 110,
  "limit": 60
}
result
110→
   111→/* ===== Low-level Emit Helpers ===== */
   112→
   113→static void emit_load_imm64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, uint64_t value) {
   114→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   115→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), imm(value));
   116→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   117→    cj_operand d = reg(dst);
   118→    if (value == 0) {
   119→        cj_mov(ctx, d, reg("xzr"));
   120→        return;
   121→    }
   122→    uint16_t chunk0 = (uint16_t)(value & 0xFFFF);
   123→    cj_movz(ctx, d, imm(chunk0));
   124→    for (int shift = 16; shift < 64; shift += 16) {
   125→        uint16_t part = (uint16_t)((value >> shift) & 0xFFFF);
   126→        if (!part) continue;
   127→        uint64_t encoded = (uint64_t)part | ((uint64_t)(shift / 16) << 16);
   128→        cj_movk(ctx, d, imm(encoded));
   129→    }
   130→#endif
   131→}
   132→
   133→static void emit_load64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *base, int32_t disp) {
   134→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   135→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));
   136→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   137→    cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));
   138→#endif
   139→}
   140→
   141→static void emit_store64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *src, const char *base, int32_t disp) {
   142→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   143→    cj_mov(ctx, mem(base, disp), reg(src));
   144→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   145→    cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(base, disp));
   146→#endif
   147→}
   148→
   149→static void emit_call_abs(cj_ctx *ctx, void *fn_ptr) {
   150→    emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_CALLADDR, (uint64_t)(uintptr_t)fn_ptr);
   151→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   152→    cj_call(ctx, reg(REG_CALLADDR));
   153→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   154→    cj_blr(ctx, reg(REG_CALLADDR));
   155→#endif
   156→}
   157→
   158→static void emit_pop(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst_reg) {
   159→    cj_sub(ctx, reg(REG_STKTOP), imm(8));
   160→    emit_load64(ctx, dst_reg, REG_STKTOP, 0);
   161→}
   162→
   163→static void emit_peek(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst_reg, int distance) {
   164→    int32_t offset = (int32_t)(-8 * (1 + distance));
   165→    emit_load64(ctx, dst_reg, REG_STKTOP, offset);
   166→}
   167→
   168→static void emit_sync_stack_top(cj_ctx *ctx) {
   169→    emit_store64(ctx, REG_STKTOP, REG_VM,
⚙ Grep ×4

The diagnostic warnings about cj_movz, cj_movk, cj_ldr, cj_str are from the clang language server analyzing the code on the ARM64 platform — those are just LSP diagnostics for the #elif branches that don't apply on the current compile target. They compile fine within #if defined(__aarch64__) blocks. Let me verify these functions exist in the ARM64 backend.

Grep — ^static inline void cj_movz
{
  "pattern": "^static inline void cj_movz",
  "path": "cj/src/arch/arm64/backend.h",
  "output_mode": "content"
}
result

}

}

⚙ Bash

Good, all those functions exist in the ARM64 backend. The LSP diagnostics are just because the clang server on this macOS ARM64 machine is seeing the x86 #if branch. Now let me rebuild.

Bash — Build lisa with dev flags (-Werror)
{
  "command": "make -C lisa dev 2>&1",
  "description": "Build lisa with dev flags (-Werror)"
}
result
mkdir -p bin
cc main.c value.c object.c lexer.c parser.c ast.c chunk.c compiler.c vm.c debug.c jit.c ../src/ctx.c -o bin/lisa -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wno-gnu -Werror -g -O0 -I../src -lm
⚙ Bash ×3

Clean build. Now let me run the verification tests from the plan.

Bash — Test factorial(20)
{
  "command": "cd cj/lisa && bin/lisa -e '(def fac (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) 1 (* n (fac (- n 1)))))) (println (fac 20))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test factorial(20)"
}
result
113784466440192
Bash — Test fibonacci(30)
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '(def fib (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) n (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2)))))) (println (fib 30))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test fibonacci(30)"
}
result
832040
Bash — Test tail-call loop (1M iterations)
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '(def loop (fn [n] (if (<= n 0) \"done\" (loop (- n 1))))) (println (loop 1000000))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test tail-call loop (1M iterations)"
}
result
done
⚙ Bash ×2

Incorrect factorial result. fac(20) should be 2432902008176640000, but we got 113784466440192. Let me verify the expected result and check what's happening.

Bash — Test factorial(20) without JIT for comparison
{
  "command": "bin/lisa --no-jit -e '(def fac (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) 1 (* n (fac (- n 1)))))) (println (fac 20))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test factorial(20) without JIT for comparison"
}
result
113784466440192
Bash — Test fibonacci(30) without JIT for comparison
{
  "command": "bin/lisa --no-jit -e '(def fib (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) n (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2)))))) (println (fib 30))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test fibonacci(30) without JIT for comparison"
}
result
832040
⚙ Bash ×3

Interesting — the interpreter produces the same result for factorial! So 113784466440192 is actually the correct answer for this language's integer representation (48-bit payload with overflow wrapping). The JIT matches the interpreter. Let me continue with the remaining tests.

Bash — Test mutual recursion (100K depth)
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '(def e (fn [n] (if (= n 0) true (o (- n 1))))) (def o (fn [n] (if (= n 0) false (e (- n 1))))) (println (e 100000))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test mutual recursion (100K depth)"
}
result
true
Bash — Test closures
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '(def make-adder (fn [x] (fn [y] (+ x y)))) (def add5 (make-adder 5)) (println (add5 10))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test closures"
}
result
15
Bash — Test list operations
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '(println (car (cons 1 (cons 2 nil))))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test list operations"
}
result
1
⚙ Bash ×2

All tests pass. Now let me run the benchmark — JIT vs interpreter with fib(35).

Bash — Benchmark fib(35) with JIT
{
  "command": "echo \"=== JIT ===\" && time bin/lisa -e '(def fib (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) n (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2)))))) (println (fib 35))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Benchmark fib(35) with JIT",
  "timeout": 120000
}
Bash — Benchmark fib(35) without JIT
{
  "command": "echo \"=== Interpreter ===\" && time bin/lisa --no-jit -e '(def fib (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) n (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2)))))) (println (fib 35))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Benchmark fib(35) without JIT",
  "timeout": 120000
}
result
=== JIT ===
9227465
bin/lisa -e  2>&1 < /dev/null  3.54s user 0.01s system 99% cpu 3.555 total
result
=== Interpreter ===
9227465
bin/lisa --no-jit -e  2>&1 < /dev/null  3.51s user 0.01s system 99% cpu 3.524 total
⚙ Bash ×3

The JIT and interpreter are nearly identical at 3.5s — no speedup yet. This is with -O0 (dev build). Let me try with the optimized build and also a more arithmetic-heavy benchmark.

  "command": "make -C lisa all 2>&1",

}

{

⚙ Grep

The JIT matches interpreter speed but doesn't beat it. The fib benchmark is call-dominated — let me check the JIT code path for efficiency issues. Let me examine the OP_JUMP_IF_FALSE handling, which is the hot path in fib.

⚙ Grep

I see an issue in JUMP_IF_FALSE. We cache_pop the value into a register (possibly a cache register), then cache_flush — but the flush only writes remaining cache entries, and the popped value's register was already removed from the cache. The sequence is correct but let me check if the pop lands in a register that might conflict.

Let me look at the cache_pop implementation.

Grep — cache_pop
{
  "pattern": "cache_pop",
  "path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "output_mode": "content",
  "-n": true,
  "-A": 10
}
result
335:static const char *cache_pop(cj_ctx *ctx, reg_cache_t *cache) {
336-    if (cache->depth > 0) {
337-        cache->depth--;
338-        return cache->regs[cache->depth];
339-    }
340-    cj_sub(ctx, reg(REG_STKTOP), imm(8));
341-    emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_STKTOP, 0);
342-    return REG_TMP1;
343-}
344-
345-/* ===== Prologue / Epilogue ===== */
--
866:            const char *val = cache_pop(ctx, &cache);
867-            cache_flush(ctx, &cache);
868-            emit_sync_stack_top(ctx);
869-            cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG2), reg(val));
870-            cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG0), reg(REG_VM));
871-            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_ARG1, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)idx);
872-            emit_call_abs(ctx, (void *)lisa_jit_def_global);
873-            emit_reload_stack_top(ctx);
874-            i += 2;
875-            break;
876-        }

⚙ Read

Let me check the register definitions to trace through the hot path.

Read — lisa/jit.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "offset": 1,
  "limit": 110
}
result
1→#include "jit.h"
     2→#include "chunk.h"
     3→#include "vm.h"
     4→
     5→#pragma GCC diagnostic push
     6→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wunused-parameter"
     7→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wunused-but-set-variable"
     8→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wunused-variable"
     9→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wc23-extensions"
    10→#include "ctx.h"
    11→#include "op.h"
    12→#include "register.h"
    13→#pragma GCC diagnostic pop
    14→
    15→#include <assert.h>
    16→#include <stddef.h>
    17→#include <stdio.h>
    18→#include <stdlib.h>
    19→#include <string.h>
    20→
    21→typedef lisa_value (*lisa_jit_fn)(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_closure *closure,
    22→                                  lisa_value *slots);
    23→
    24→/* ===== Platform Register Definitions ===== */
    25→
    26→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
    27→
    28→#define REG_VM       "rbx"
    29→#define REG_SLOTS    "r12"
    30→#define REG_CLOSURE  "r13"
    31→#define REG_STKTOP   "r14"
    32→#define REG_CONSTS   "r15"
    33→
    34→#define REG_CACHE0   "r8"
    35→#define REG_CACHE1   "r9"
    36→#define REG_CACHE2   "r10"
    37→#define REG_CACHE3   "r11"
    38→
    39→#define REG_TMP1     "rax"
    40→#define REG_TMP2     "rcx"
    41→#define REG_TMP3     "rdx"
    42→#define REG_TMP4     "rsi"
    43→#define REG_TMP5     "rdi"
    44→#define REG_CALLADDR "r10"
    45→
    46→#define REG_ARG0     "rdi"
    47→#define REG_ARG1     "rsi"
    48→#define REG_ARG2     "rdx"
    49→#define REG_ARG3     "rcx"
    50→#define REG_RET      "rax"
    51→
    52→#define EMIT_JEQ(ctx, label) cj_jz(ctx, label)
    53→#define EMIT_JNE(ctx, label) cj_jnz(ctx, label)
    54→#define EMIT_JLT(ctx, label) cj_jl(ctx, label)
    55→#define EMIT_JLE(ctx, label) cj_jle(ctx, label)
    56→#define EMIT_JGT(ctx, label) cj_jg(ctx, label)
    57→#define EMIT_JGE(ctx, label) cj_jge(ctx, label)
    58→#define EMIT_JMP(ctx, label) cj_jmp(ctx, label)
    59→#define EMIT_JB(ctx, label)  cj_jb(ctx, label)
    60→
    61→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
    62→
    63→#define REG_VM       "x19"
    64→#define REG_SLOTS    "x20"
    65→#define REG_CLOSURE  "x21"
    66→#define REG_STKTOP   "x22"
    67→#define REG_CONSTS   "x23"
    68→
    69→#define REG_CACHE0   "x10"
    70→#define REG_CACHE1   "x11"
    71→#define REG_CACHE2   "x12"
    72→#define REG_CACHE3   "x13"
    73→
    74→#define REG_TMP1     "x0"
    75→#define REG_TMP2     "x1"
    76→#define REG_TMP3     "x2"
    77→#define REG_TMP4     "x3"
    78→#define REG_TMP5     "x4"
    79→#define REG_CALLADDR "x9"
    80→
    81→#define REG_ARG0     "x0"
    82→#define REG_ARG1     "x1"
    83→#define REG_ARG2     "x2"
    84→#define REG_ARG3     "x3"
    85→#define REG_RET      "x0"
    86→
    87→#define EMIT_JEQ(ctx, label) cj_beq(ctx, label)
    88→#define EMIT_JNE(ctx, label) cj_bne(ctx, label)
    89→#define EMIT_JLT(ctx, label) cj_blt(ctx, label)

⚙ Read

Let me look at the type check and bool_from_flags helpers.

Read — lisa/jit.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "offset": 400,
  "limit": 100
}
result
400→}
   401→
   402→/* ===== Inline Type-Check Helpers ===== */
   403→
   404→/* Check if val_reg is an integer. Jumps to fail_label if not.
   405→   Clobbers REG_TMP1 (and REG_TMP2 on ARM64). */
   406→static void emit_int_type_check(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *val_reg, cj_label fail_label) {
   407→    emit_lsr_imm(ctx, REG_TMP1, val_reg, 48);
   408→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   409→    cj_cmp(ctx, reg(REG_TMP1), imm(TAG_INT_HI));
   410→    cj_jnz(ctx, fail_label);
   411→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   412→    cj_movz(ctx, reg(REG_TMP2), imm(TAG_INT_HI));
   413→    cj_cmp(ctx, reg(REG_TMP1), reg(REG_TMP2));
   414→    cj_bne(ctx, fail_label);
   415→#endif
   416→}
   417→
   418→/* Check if val_reg is NOT a double (top 16 bits >= 0x7FFC).
   419→   Jumps to fail_label if it IS a double.
   420→   Clobbers REG_TMP1 (and REG_TMP2 on ARM64). */
   421→static void emit_non_double_check(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *val_reg, cj_label fail_label) {
   422→    emit_lsr_imm(ctx, REG_TMP1, val_reg, 48);
   423→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   424→    cj_cmp(ctx, reg(REG_TMP1), imm(TAG_NONDBL));
   425→    cj_jb(ctx, fail_label);
   426→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   427→    cj_movz(ctx, reg(REG_TMP2), imm(TAG_NONDBL));
   428→    cj_cmp(ctx, reg(REG_TMP1), reg(REG_TMP2));
   429→    cj_bcc(ctx, fail_label);
   430→#endif
   431→}
   432→
   433→/* Emit boolean result (LISA_TRUE or LISA_FALSE) from comparison flags.
   434→   On x86: uses REG_TMP1 = "rax", writes setcc into "al".
   435→   On ARM64: uses CSET into REG_TMP1, then OR with LISA_FALSE.
   436→   Result is left in REG_TMP1. */
   437→typedef enum { CMP_LT, CMP_LE, CMP_GT, CMP_GE, CMP_EQ, CMP_NE } cmp_kind;
   438→
   439→static void emit_bool_from_flags(cj_ctx *ctx, cmp_kind kind) {
   440→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   441→    /* MOV doesn't affect flags, so we can load LISA_FALSE first */
   442→    emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP1, LISA_FALSE);
   443→    switch (kind) {
   444→    case CMP_LT: cj_setl(ctx, reg("al")); break;
   445→    case CMP_LE: cj_setle(ctx, reg("al")); break;
   446→    case CMP_GT: cj_setg(ctx, reg("al")); break;
   447→    case CMP_GE: cj_setge(ctx, reg("al")); break;
   448→    case CMP_EQ: cj_setz(ctx, reg("al")); break;
   449→    case CMP_NE: cj_setnz(ctx, reg("al")); break;
   450→    }
   451→    /* rax = LISA_FALSE | 0/1 = LISA_FALSE or LISA_TRUE */
   452→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   453→    /* CSET first (reads flags), then load constant (doesn't affect flags) */
   454→    int invert;
   455→    switch (kind) {
   456→    case CMP_LT: invert = ARM64_COND_GE; break;
   457→    case CMP_LE: invert = ARM64_COND_GT; break;
   458→    case CMP_GT: invert = ARM64_COND_LE; break;
   459→    case CMP_GE: invert = ARM64_COND_LT; break;
   460→    case CMP_EQ: invert = ARM64_COND_NE; break;
   461→    case CMP_NE: invert = ARM64_COND_EQ; break;
   462→    }
   463→
... (truncated)

265-
266-#if defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
267-/* CSINC Xd, XZR, XZR, invert_cond → Xd = (cond) ? 1 : 0 */
268-#define ARM64_COND_EQ 0x0
269-#define ARM64_COND_NE 0x1
270-#define ARM64_COND_LO 0x3
271-#define ARM64_COND_GE 0xA
--
465:    emit_or(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_TMP2); /* TMP1 = LISA_FALSE | 0/1 */
466-#endif
467-}

469-/* ===== Call Helpers (flush-aware) ===== */
470-
471-static void emit_call_vm_int(cj_ctx *ctx, void *fn_ptr, int int_arg) {
472-    emit_sync_stack_top(ctx);
473-    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG0), reg(REG_VM));
474-    emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_ARG1, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)int_arg);

⚙ Read

Let me check the binop fast path too.

Read — lisa/jit.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "offset": 497,
  "limit": 45
}
result
497→/* Emit inline integer fast path for ADD/SUB/MUL.
   498→   Expects cache->depth >= 2 and cache already flushed to depth 2.
   499→   After this, cache->depth = 1, result in cache->regs[0]. */
   500→static void emit_binop_int_fast(cj_ctx *ctx, reg_cache_t *cache,
   501→                                 arith_op op, void *slow_fn) {
   502→    const char *a_reg = cache->regs[0];
   503→    const char *b_reg = cache->regs[1];
   504→
   505→    cj_label slow = cj_create_label(ctx);
   506→    cj_label done = cj_create_label(ctx);
   507→
   508→    /* Type-check both operands (non-destructive: only REG_TMP1/TMP2 clobbered) */
   509→    emit_int_type_check(ctx, a_reg, slow);
   510→    emit_int_type_check(ctx, b_reg, slow);
   511→
   512→    /* Fast path: extract payloads, compute, mask, retag */
   513→    emit_mask48(ctx, a_reg);
   514→    emit_mask48(ctx, b_reg);
   515→    emit_arith_compute(ctx, a_reg, b_reg, op);
   516→    emit_mask48(ctx, a_reg);
   517→    emit_retag_int(ctx, a_reg);
   518→
   519→    EMIT_JMP(ctx, done);
   520→
   521→    cj_mark_label(ctx, slow);
   522→    /* a and b are unchanged (type checks non-destructive). */
   523→    emit_sync_stack_top(ctx);
   524→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG2), reg(b_reg));
   525→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG1), reg(a_reg));
   526→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG0), reg(REG_VM));
   527→    emit_call_abs(ctx, slow_fn);
   528→    emit_reload_stack_top(ctx);
   529→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(cache->regs[0]), reg(REG_RET));
   530→
   531→    cj_mark_label(ctx, done);
   532→    cache->depth = 1;
   533→}
   534→
   535→/* Emit inline integer fast path for comparison ops (LT/LE/GT/GE).
   536→   Uses signed comparison of shifted payloads.
   537→   After this, cache->depth = 1, result in cache->regs[0]. */
   538→static void emit_cmpop_int_fast(cj_ctx *ctx, reg_cache_t *cache,
   539→                                 cmp_kind kind, void *slow_fn) {
   540→    const char *a_reg = cache->regs[0];
   541→    const char *b_reg = cache->regs[1];

⚙ Bash, Grep ×2

The fib benchmark is call-dominated, so the inline fast paths have minimal impact there. Let me try an arithmetic-heavy benchmark to validate the inline paths work.

Bash — Benchmark arithmetic-heavy tail-recursive sum
{
  "command": "echo \"=== JIT ===\" && time bin/lisa -e '(def sum (fn [n acc] (if (<= n 0) acc (sum (- n 1) (+ acc n))))) (println (sum 10000000 0))' 2>&1 && echo && echo \"=== Interpreter ===\" && time bin/lisa --no-jit -e '(def sum (fn [n acc] (if (<= n 0) acc (sum (- n 1) (+ acc n))))) (println (sum 10000000 0))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Benchmark arithmetic-heavy tail-recursive sum",
  "timeout": 120000
}

⚙ Grep, Bash ×2

All iteration in Lisa is via recursion, so function call overhead dominates. Let me check if the prologue/epilogue overhead is hurting us.

⚙ Grep ×2, Read

The timings are identical — let me verify the JIT is actually being used by checking the compilation path.

Grep — lisa_jit_compile
{
  "pattern": "lisa_jit_compile",

  "-n": true,
  "-A": 20,

result
736:bool lisa_jit_compile(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_function *fn) {
737-    (void)vm;
738-
739-    if (fn->jit_code) return true;
740-
741-    cj_ctx *ctx = create_cj_ctx();
742-    if (!ctx) return false;
743-
744-    lisa_chunk *chunk = &fn->chunk;

     6→static char *read_file(const char *path) {
     7→    FILE *file = fopen(path, "rb");
     8→    if (file == NULL) {
     9→        fprintf(stderr, "Could not open file '%s'.\n", path);
    10→        return NULL;
    11→    }
    12→
    13→    fseek(file, 0, SEEK_END);
    14→    long size = ftell(file);
    15→    rewind(file);
    16→
    17→    char *buffer = malloc((size_t)size + 1);
    18→    size_t bytes_read = fread(buffer, 1, (size_t)size, file);
    19→    buffer[bytes_read] = '\0';
    20→
    21→    fclose(file);
    22→    return buffer;
    23→}
    24→
    25→static int run_string(const char *source, bool jit) {
    26→    lisa_vm vm;
    27→    lisa_vm_init(&vm);
    28→    vm.jit_enabled = jit;
    29→    lisa_interpret_result result = lisa_interpret(&vm, source);
    30→    lisa_vm_free(&vm);
    31→
    32→    if (result == INTERPRET_COMPILE_ERROR) return 65;
    33→    if (result == INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR) return 70;
    34→    return 0;
    35→}
    36→
    37→static int run_file(const char *path, bool jit) {
    38→    char *source = read_file(path);
    39→    if (source == NULL) return 74;
    40→    int result = run_string(source, jit);
    41→    free(source);
    42→    return result;
    43→}
    44→
    45→int main(int argc, char *argv[]) {
    46→    bool jit = true;
    47→    int argi = 1;
    48→
    49→    if (argi < argc && strcmp(argv[argi], "--no-jit") == 0) {
    50→        jit = false;
    51→        argi++;
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    if (argi < argc && argi == argc - 1 && strcmp(argv[argi], "-e") != 0) {
    55→        return run_file(argv[argi], jit);
    56→    }
    57→
    58→    if (argi + 1 < argc && strcmp(argv[argi], "-e") == 0) {
    59→        return run_string(argv[argi + 1], jit);
    60→    }
    61→
    62→    fprintf(stderr, "Usage: lisa [--no-jit] <file.lisa>\n");

result
206→static bool call_value(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value callee, int argc) {
   207→    if (IS_OBJ(callee)) {
   208→        switch (OBJ_TYPE(callee)) {
   209→        case OBJ_CLOSURE:
   210→            return call_closure(vm, AS_CLOSURE(callee), argc);
   211→        case OBJ_NATIVE: {
   212→            lisa_obj_native *native = AS_NATIVE(callee);
   213→            if (native->arity != -1 && native->arity != argc) {
   214→                runtime_error(vm, "Expected %d arguments but got %d.", native->arity, argc);
   215→                return false;
   216→            }
   217→            lisa_value result = native->function(argc, vm->stack_top - argc);
   218→            vm->stack_top -= argc + 1;
   219→            push(vm, result);
   220→            return true;
   221→        }
   222→        default:
   223→            break;
   224→        }
   225→    }
   226→    runtime_error(vm, "Can only call functions and closures.");
   227→    return false;
   228→}
   229→
   230→/* --- String concatenation --- */

{
  "file_path": "lisa/compiler.c",

result
1→#include "compiler.h"
     2→#include "chunk.h"
     3→#include <stdio.h>
     4→#include <stdlib.h>
     5→#include <string.h>
     6→
     7→static bool had_error = false;
     8→
     9→static void compile_error(int line, const char *message) {
    10→    fprintf(stderr, "[line %d] Compile error: %s\n", line, message);
    11→    had_error = true;
    12→}
    13→
    14→/* --- Compiler state --- */
    15→
    16→static void init_compiler(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_compiler *enclosing,
    17→                          lisa_function_type type, lisa_gc *gc, const char *name) {
    18→    c->enclosing = enclosing;
    19→    c->type = type;
    20→    c->gc = gc;
    21→    c->local_count = 0;
    22→    c->scope_depth = 0;
    23→    c->function = lisa_new_function(gc);
    24→
    25→    if (name != NULL) {
    26→        c->function->name = lisa_copy_string(gc, name, (int)strlen(name));
    27→    }
    28→
    29→    /* Reserve slot 0 for the function itself */
    30→    lisa_local *local = &c->locals[c->local_count++];
    31→    local->depth = 0;
    32→    local->is_captured = false;
    33→    local->name = "";
    34→    local->name_length = 0;
    35→}

300→                compile_expr(c, args->items[0], false);
   301→                emit_byte(c, OP_CDR, line);
   302→                return;
   303→            }
   304→        }
   305→
   306→        /* println: special opcode */
   307→        if (sym_eq(callee, "println")) {
   308→            for (int i = 0; i < args->count; i++) {
   309→                compile_expr(c, args->items[i], false);
   310→            }
   311→            emit_bytes(c, OP_PRINTLN, (uint8_t)args->count, line);
   312→            return;
   313→        }
   314→
   315→        /* list: build a list from N elements */
   316→        if (sym_eq(callee, "list")) {
   317→            for (int i = 0; i < args->count; i++) {
   318→                compile_expr(c, args->items[i], false);
   319→            }
   320→            emit_bytes(c, OP_LIST, (uint8_t)args->count, line);
   321→            return;
