   328→        compile_expr(c, args->items[i], false);
   329→    }
   330→    emit_bytes(c, tail ? OP_TAIL_CALL : OP_CALL, (uint8_t)args->count, line);
   331→}
   332→
   333→static void compile_fn(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node) {
   334→    int line = node->line;
   335→    lisa_compiler fn_compiler;
   336→    init_compiler(&fn_compiler, c, TYPE_FUNCTION, c->gc, NULL);
   337→    begin_scope(&fn_compiler);
   338→
   339→    /* Parameters */
   340→    lisa_ast_list *params = &node->as.fn.params;
   341→    fn_compiler.function->arity = params->count;
   342→    for (int i = 0; i < params->count; i++) {
   343→        lisa_ast *param = params->items[i];
   344→        add_local(&fn_compiler, param->as.symbol.start, param->as.symbol.length, line);
   345→    }
   346→
   347→    /* Body */
   348→    lisa_ast_list *body = &node->as.fn.body;
   349→    for (int i = 0; i < body->count; i++) {
   350→        bool is_last = (i == body->count - 1);
   351→        compile_expr(&fn_compiler, body->items[i], is_last);
   352→        if (!is_last) {
   353→            emit_byte(&fn_compiler, OP_POP, body->items[i]->line);
   354→        }
   355→    }
   356→
   357→    /* Last expression is the return value */
   358→    emit_byte(&fn_compiler, OP_RETURN, line);
   359→
   360→    lisa_obj_function *fn = fn_compiler.function;
   361→
   362→    /* Emit closure instruction in the enclosing compiler */
   363→    uint8_t idx = make_constant(c, LISA_OBJ(fn), line);
   364→    emit_bytes(c, OP_CLOSURE, idx, line);
   365→
   366→    for (int i = 0; i < fn->upvalue_count; i++) {
   367→        emit_byte(c, fn_compiler.upvalues[i].is_local ? 1 : 0, line);
   368→        emit_byte(c, fn_compiler.upvalues[i].index, line);
   369→    }
   370→}
   371→
   372→static void compile_let(lisa_compiler *c, lisa_ast *node) {
   373→    int line = node->line;
   374→    begin_scope(c);
   375→
   376→    lisa_ast_list *bin
... (truncated)
⚙ Edit, Bash

Found the issue — all functions have name == NULL because the compiler never sets function names, but vm.c gates JIT compilation on closure->function->name != NULL. The JIT is never triggered. I need to fix the condition in vm.c.

Edit — lisa/vm.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "old_string": "    /* JIT compile on first call (skip top-level scripts) */\n    if (vm->jit_enabled && !closure->function->jit_code &&\n        closure->function->name != NULL) {",
  "new_string": "    /* JIT compile on first call */\n    if (vm->jit_enabled && !closure->function->jit_code) {"
}
result
The file lisa/vm.c has been updated successfully.

  "description": "Rebuild with vm.c fix and test"
}

{
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",

result
219→/* Clear top 16 bits: r &= 0x0000FFFFFFFFFFFF (unsigned 48-bit payload) */
   220→static void emit_mask48(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *r) {
   221→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   222→    cj_shl(ctx, reg(r), imm(16));
   223→    cj_shr(ctx, reg(r), imm(16));
   224→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   225→    /* UBFM Xd, Xd, #0, #47 = extract bits 47:0, zero-extend */
   226→    int rd = arm64_parse_reg(r);
   227→    uint32_t instr = 0xD340BC00
   228→        | ((uint32_t)(rd & 0x1f) << 5)
   229→        | (uint32_t)(rd & 0x1f);
   230→    cj_add_u32(ctx, instr);
   231→#endif
   232→}
   233→
   234→/* Sign-extend from bit 47: r = sign_extend_48(r) */
   235→static void emit_sign_extend48(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *r) {
   236→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   237→    cj_shl(ctx, reg(r), imm(16));
   238→    cj_sar(ctx, reg(r), imm(16));
   239→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   240→    /* SBFM Xd, Xd, #0, #47 */
   241→    int rd = arm64_parse_reg(r);
   242→    uint32_t instr = 0x9340BC00
   243→        | ((uint32_t)(rd & 0x1f) << 5)
   244→        | (uint32_t)(rd & 0x1f);
   245→    cj_add_u32(ctx, instr);
   246→#endif
   247→}
   248→
   249→/* OR dst |= src */
   250→static void emit_or(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *src) {
   251→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   252→    cj_or(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src));
   253→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   254→    cj_orr(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src));
   255→#endif
   256→}
   257→
   258→/* Re-tag a masked 48-bit payload as an integer. Uses REG_TMP1 as scratch. */

⚙ Grep

The stack is corrupted by the JIT code. Let me check cj_stp and cj_ldp with pre/post-indexed modes.

Grep — static inline void cj_stp
{
  "pattern": "static inline void cj_stp",
  "path": "cj/src/arch/arm64/backend.h",
  "output_mode": "content",
  "-n": true,
  "-A": 50,
  "head_limit": 55
}

14514:static inline void cj_stp(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_operand rt1, cj_operand rt2, cj_operand mem) {
14515-  if (rt1.type == CJ_REGISTER && rt2.type == CJ_REGISTER && mem.type == CJ_MEMORY) {
14516-    int reg1 = arm64_parse_reg(rt1.reg);
14517-    if (reg1 < 0) return;
14518-    int reg2 = arm64_parse_reg(rt2.reg);
14519-    if (reg2 < 0) return;
14520-    int rn = arm64_parse_reg(mem.mem.base);
14521-    if (rn < 0) return;
14522-    int32_t offset = mem.mem.disp;
14523-    
14524-    int is64 = arm64_is_64bit(rt1.reg);

14526-    
14527-    if ((offset % scale) != 0) return;
14528-    int32_t imm7 = offset / scale;
14529-    if (imm7 < -64 || imm7 > 63) return;

14533-    instr |= (reg1 & 0x1f);

14535-    instr |= ((reg2 & 0x1f) << 10);
14536-    instr |= ((imm7 & 0x7f) << 15);
14537-    
14538-    cj_add_u32(ctx, instr);
14539-    return;
14540-  }
14541-}

[Showing results with pagination = limit: 55, offset: 0]
⚙ Read, Edit ×2

Found the bug. cj_stp ignores the pre-indexed/post-indexed addressing mode — it always emits the signed-offset variant. The prologue never decrements SP, so callee-saved registers corrupt the stack. Let me fix the prologue/epilogue to manually adjust SP.

Read — lisa/jit.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "offset": 347,
  "limit": 55
}
result
347→static void emit_prologue(cj_ctx *ctx) {
   348→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   349→    cj_push(ctx, reg("rbp"));
   350→    cj_mov(ctx, reg("rbp"), reg("rsp"));
   351→    cj_push(ctx, reg("rbx"));
   352→    cj_push(ctx, reg("r12"));
   353→    cj_push(ctx, reg("r13"));
   354→    cj_push(ctx, reg("r14"));
   355→    cj_push(ctx, reg("r15"));
   356→    cj_sub(ctx, reg("rsp"), imm(8)); /* 16-byte alignment */
   357→
   358→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_VM), reg("rdi"));
   359→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_CLOSURE), reg("rsi"));
   360→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_SLOTS), reg("rdx"));
   361→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   362→    cj_stp(ctx, reg("x29"), reg("x30"), cj_make_preindexed("sp", -80));
   363→    cj_mov(ctx, reg("x29"), reg("sp"));
   364→    cj_stp(ctx, reg("x19"), reg("x20"), mem("sp", 16));
   365→    cj_stp(ctx, reg("x21"), reg("x22"), mem("sp", 32));
   366→    cj_str(ctx, reg("x23"), mem("sp", 48));
   367→
   368→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_VM), reg("x0"));
   369→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_CLOSURE), reg("x1"));
   370→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_SLOTS), reg("x2"));
   371→#endif
   372→    emit_reload_stack_top(ctx);
   373→
   374→    /* Load constants pointer: closure->function->chunk.constants.values */
   375→    emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_CLOSURE,
   376→                (int32_t)offsetof(lisa_obj_closure, function));
   377→    emit_load64(ctx, REG_CONSTS, REG_TMP1,
   378→                (int32_t)(offsetof(lisa_obj_function, chunk)
   379→                        + offsetof(lisa_chunk, constants)
   380→                        + offsetof(lisa_value_array, values)));
   381→}
   382→
   383→static void emit_epilogue(cj_ctx *ctx) {
   384→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   385→    cj_add(ctx, reg("rsp"), imm(8));
   386→    cj_pop(ctx, reg("r15"));
   387→    cj_pop(ctx, reg("r14"));
   388→    cj_pop(ctx, reg("r13"));
   389→    cj_pop(ctx, reg("r12"));
   390→    cj_pop(ctx, reg("rbx"));
   391→    cj_pop(ctx, reg("rbp"));
   392→    cj_ret(ctx);
   393→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   394→    cj_ldp(ctx, reg("x19"), reg("x20"), mem("sp", 16));
   395→    cj_ldp(ctx, reg("x21"), reg("x22"), mem("sp", 32));
   396→    cj_ldr(ctx, reg("x23"), mem("sp", 48));
   397→    cj_ldp(ctx, reg("x29"), reg("x30"), cj_make_postindexed("sp", 80));
   398→    cj_ret(ctx);
   399→#endif
   400→}
   401→
Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    cj_stp(ctx, reg(\"x29\"), reg(\"x30\"), cj_make_preindexed(\"sp\", -80));\n    cj_mov(ctx, reg(\"x29\"), reg(\"sp\"));\n    cj_stp(ctx, reg(\"x19\"), reg(\"x20\"), mem(\"sp\", 16));\n    cj_stp(ctx, reg(\"x21\"), reg(\"x22\"), mem(\"sp\", 32));\n    cj_str(ctx, reg(\"x23\"), mem(\"sp\", 48));\n\n    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_VM), reg(\"x0\"));\n    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_CLOSURE), reg(\"x1\"));\n    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_SLOTS), reg(\"x2\"));\n#endif",
  "new_string": "#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    /* cj_stp ignores pre-indexed mode, so manually adjust SP */\n    cj_sub(ctx, reg(\"sp\"), imm(80));\n    cj_stp(ctx, reg(\"x29\"), reg(\"x30\"), mem(\"sp\", 0));\n    cj_add(ctx, reg(\"x29\"), reg(\"sp\"), imm(0));\n    cj_stp(ctx, reg(\"x19\"), reg(\"x20\"), mem(\"sp\", 16));\n    cj_stp(ctx, reg(\"x21\"), reg(\"x22\"), mem(\"sp\", 32));\n    cj_str(ctx, reg(\"x23\"), mem(\"sp\", 48));\n\n    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_VM), reg(\"x0\"));\n    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_CLOSURE), reg(\"x1\"));\n    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_SLOTS), reg(\"x2\"));\n#endif"
}
result

⚙ Edit, Grep ×7, Read

Now fix the epilogue:

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "    cj_ldp(ctx, reg(\"x19\"), reg(\"x20\"), mem(\"sp\", 16));\n    cj_ldp(ctx, reg(\"x21\"), reg(\"x22\"), mem(\"sp\", 32));\n    cj_ldr(ctx, reg(\"x23\"), mem(\"sp\", 48));\n    cj_ldp(ctx, reg(\"x29\"), reg(\"x30\"), cj_make_postindexed(\"sp\", 80));\n    cj_ret(ctx);",
  "new_string": "    cj_ldp(ctx, reg(\"x19\"), reg(\"x20\"), mem(\"sp\", 16));\n    cj_ldp(ctx, reg(\"x21\"), reg(\"x22\"), mem(\"sp\", 32));\n    cj_ldr(ctx, reg(\"x23\"), mem(\"sp\", 48));\n    cj_ldp(ctx, reg(\"x29\"), reg(\"x30\"), mem(\"sp\", 0));\n    cj_add(ctx, reg(\"sp\"), imm(80));\n    cj_ret(ctx);"
}

⚙ Grep ×2, Read

Good, cj_sub(sp, 80) and cj_add(sp, 80) should work with SP. Let me also verify the cj_mov(x29, sp) — the original code also used this.

⚙ Edit, Bash ×3, Grep, TaskOutput ×2, TaskStop ×2

Found another cj bug: cj_mov(x29, sp) generates ORR x29, XZR, XZR = 0 because ORR encoding treats register 31 as XZR, not SP. Need to emit raw ADD x29, sp, #0 instead.

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "    cj_sub(ctx, reg(\"sp\"), imm(80));\n    cj_stp(ctx, reg(\"x29\"), reg(\"x30\"), mem(\"sp\", 0));\n    cj_mov(ctx, reg(\"x29\"), reg(\"sp\"));",
  "new_string": "    cj_sub(ctx, reg(\"sp\"), imm(80));\n    cj_stp(ctx, reg(\"x29\"), reg(\"x30\"), mem(\"sp\", 0));\n    /* cj_mov(x29, sp) generates ORR x29,XZR,XZR=0 (backend bug:\n       reg 31 is XZR in ORR, not SP). Use raw ADD x29, sp, #0. */\n    cj_add_u32(ctx, 0x910003FD); /* ADD x29, sp, #0 */"
}
result
The file lisa/jit.c has been updated successfully.

{
  "replace_all": false,

  "new_string": "    void *entry = cj_resolve_label(ctx, module, entry_label);\n    fn->jit_code = entry;\n    fn->jit_ctx = ctx;\n\n    /* Debug: dump generated code */\n    {\n        size_t code_size = ctx->code_len;\n        uint32_t *code = (uint32_t *)entry;\n        fprintf(stderr, \"[JIT] compiled '%s' (%d bytes bytecode, %zu bytes native, entry=%p)\\n\",\n                fn->name ? fn->name->chars : \"<anon>\", chunk->count, code_size, entry);\n        size_t n_instr = code_size / 4;\n        if (n_instr > 100) n_instr = 100;\n        for (size_t j = 0; j < n_instr; j++) {\n            fprintf(stderr, \"  %04zx: %08x\\n\", j * 4, code[j]);\n        }\n    }"

⚙ Read ×2, Edit, Bash

Found the bug! The entry label is marked AFTER the prologue, so jit_code points past the prologue — the function setup is completely skipped when called. I need to mark the label BEFORE the prologue.

Read — lisa/jit.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "offset": 748,
  "limit": 15
}
result
748→    fflush(stdout);
   749→
   750→    cj_ctx *ctx = create_cj_ctx();
   751→    if (!ctx) {
   752→        fprintf(stderr, "[JIT] create_cj_ctx failed\n");
   753→        return false;
   754→    }
   755→
   756→    lisa_chunk *chunk = &fn->chunk;
   757→
   758→    label_map map;
   759→    scan_branch_targets(chunk, &map, ctx);
   760→
   761→    cj_label entry_label = cj_create_label(ctx);
   762→

⚙ Edit ×2, Bash

It works! The output is 6 which is f(5) = 5 + 1 = 6. Now let me remove the debug prints and run the full test suite.

Now let me run all the verification tests.

Bash — Run all verification tests
{
  "command": "cd cj/lisa && echo \"=== fac(20) ===\" && bin/lisa -e '(def fac (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) 1 (* n (fac (- n 1)))))) (println (fac 20))' && echo \"=== fib(30) ===\" && bin/lisa -e '(def fib (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) n (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2)))))) (println (fib 30))' && echo \"=== tail loop 1M ===\" && bin/lisa -e '(def loop (fn [n] (if (<= n 0) \"done\" (loop (- n 1))))) (println (loop 1000000))' && echo \"=== mutual recursion 100K ===\" && bin/lisa -e '(def e (fn [n] (if (= n 0) true (o (- n 1))))) (def o (fn [n] (if (= n 0) false (e (- n 1))))) (println (e 100000))' && echo \"=== closures ===\" && bin/lisa -e '(def make-adder (fn [x] (fn [y] (+ x y)))) (def add5 (make-adder 5)) (println (add5 10))' && echo \"=== list ops ===\" && bin/lisa -e '(println (car (cons 1 (cons 2 nil))))'",
  "description": "Run all verification tests",
  "timeout": 30000
}

Exit code 139
=== fac(20) ===

=== fac(20) ===
Bash — Simple test
{
  "command": "cd cj/lisa && bin/lisa -e '(def f (fn [x] (+ x 1))) (println (f 5))' 2>&1; echo \"EXIT: $?\"",
  "description": "Simple test"
}
result
6
EXIT: 0
Bash — Factorial test (small)
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '(def fac (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) 1 (* n (fac (- n 1)))))) (println (fac 5))' 2>&1; echo \"EXIT: $?\"",
  "description": "Factorial test (small)"
}
result
EXIT: 139
⚙ Bash ×3

Simple function works, recursive factorial crashes. The issue is likely with how OP_CALL or recursive calls interact with the JIT. Let me test simpler recursive patterns.

Bash — Simple recursive test
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '(def g (fn [n] (if (<= n 0) 0 (g (- n 1))))) (println (g 3))' 2>&1; echo \"EXIT: $?\"",
  "description": "Simple recursive test"
}
result
EXIT: 139
Bash — Test trivial identity function
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '(def g (fn [n] n)) (println (g 42))' 2>&1; echo \"EXIT: $?\"",
  "description": "Test trivial identity function"
}
result
42
EXIT: 0
Bash — Test conditional without recursion
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '(def g (fn [n] (if (<= n 0) 0 1))) (println (g 3))' 2>&1; echo \"EXIT: $?\"",
  "description": "Test conditional without recursion"
}
result
EXIT: 139
⚙ Edit, Bash ×2, Read

Even simple conditionals crash. Let me add the debug dump back for this case.

Read — lisa/chunk.h
{
  "file_path": "lisa/chunk.h"
}
result
1→#ifndef LISA_CHUNK_H
     2→#define LISA_CHUNK_H
     3→
     4→#include "value.h"
     5→#include <stdint.h>
     6→
     7→typedef enum {
     8→    OP_CONSTANT,      /* [idx]          push constants[idx] */
     9→    OP_NIL,           /*                push nil */
    10→    OP_TRUE,          /*                push true */
    11→    OP_FALSE,         /*                push false */
    12→    OP_POP,           /*                pop top */
    13→
    14→    OP_GET_LOCAL,     /* [slot]         push stack[base+slot] */
    15→    OP_SET_LOCAL,     /* [slot]         stack[base+slot] = peek */
    16→    OP_GET_UPVALUE,   /* [idx]          push *upvalues[idx]->location */
    17→    OP_SET_UPVALUE,   /* [idx]          *upvalues[idx]->location = peek */
    18→    OP_GET_GLOBAL,    /* [idx]          push globals[constants[idx]] */
    19→    OP_DEF_GLOBAL,    /* [idx]          globals[constants[idx]] = pop */
    20→
    21→    OP_ADD,
    22→    OP_SUB,
    23→    OP_MUL,
    24→    OP_DIV,
    25→    OP_MOD,
    26→    OP_NEGATE,
    27→
    28→    OP_EQUAL,
    29→    OP_NOT_EQUAL,
    30→    OP_LESS,
    31→    OP_LESS_EQUAL,
    32→    OP_GREATER,
    33→    OP_GREATER_EQUAL,
    34→
    35→    OP_NOT,
    36→
    37→    OP_JUMP,          /* [lo][hi]       ip += offset */
    38→    OP_JUMP_IF_FALSE, /* [lo][hi]       if falsey(pop) ip += offset */
    39→    OP_LOOP,          /* [lo][hi]       ip -= offset */
    40→
    41→    OP_CLOSURE,       /* [idx] then pairs of [is_local, index] */
    42→    OP_CALL,          /* [argc]         call top function with argc args */
    43→    OP_TAIL_CALL,     /* [argc]         tail call: reuse current frame */
    44→    OP_RETURN,        /*                return top of stack */
    45→
    46→    OP_CLOSE_UPVALUE, /*                close upvalue at stack top */
    47→
    48→    OP_CONS,          /*                push cons(pop2, pop1) */
    49→    OP_CAR,           /*                push car(pop) */
    50→    OP_CDR,           /*                push cdr(pop) */
    51→    OP_LIST,          /* [n]            pop n items, build list */
    52→
    53→    OP_PRINTLN,       /* [argc]         print argc values with spaces, newline */
    54→} lisa_op;
    55→
    56→/* Dynamic array of constants */
    57→typedef struct {
    58→    int count;
    59→    int capacity;
    60→    lisa_value *values;
    61→} lisa_value_array;
    62→
    63→void lisa_value_array_init(lisa_value_array *arr);
    64→void lisa_value_array_write(lisa_value_array *arr, lisa_value value);
    65→void lisa_value_array_free(lisa_value_array *arr);
    66→
    67→/* Bytecode chunk */
    68→typedef struct {
    69→    int count;
    70→    int capacity;
    71→    uint8_t *code;
    72→    int *lines;          /* source line per bytecode byte */
    73→    lisa_value_array constants;
    74→} lisa_chunk;
    75→
    76→void lisa_chunk_init(lisa_chunk *chunk);
    77→void lisa_chunk_write(lisa_chunk *chunk, uint8_t byte, int l
... (truncated)
⚙ Bash

Let me check what offset stack_top is at in the VM struct — it might be too large for ARM64 LDR/STR immediates.

Bash — Check VM struct offsets
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/check_offset.c <<'EOF'\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include \"../lisa/vm.h\"\nint main() {\n    printf(\"offsetof(lisa_vm, stack_top) = %zu\\n\", offsetof(lisa_vm, stack_top));\n    printf(\"offsetof(lisa_vm, frame_count) = %zu\\n\", offsetof(lisa_vm, frame_count));\n    printf(\"offsetof(lisa_vm, frames) = %zu\\n\", offsetof(lisa_vm, frames));\n    printf(\"sizeof(lisa_call_frame) = %zu\\n\", sizeof(lisa_call_frame));\n    printf(\"sizeof(lisa_value) = %zu\\n\", sizeof(lisa_value));\n    printf(\"sizeof(lisa_vm) = %zu\\n\", sizeof(lisa_vm));\n    return 0;\n}\nEOF\ncc -Isrc -Ilisa /tmp/check_offset.c -o /tmp/check_offset && /tmp/check_offset",
  "description": "Check VM struct offsets"
}
result
offsetof(lisa_vm, stack_top) = 38920
offsetof(lisa_vm, frame_count) = 6144
offsetof(lisa_vm, frames) = 0
sizeof(lisa_call_frame) = 24
sizeof(lisa_value) = 8
sizeof(lisa_vm) = 39032
⚙ Read, Edit, Bash

Found it! offsetof(lisa_vm, stack_top) = 38920 — way too large for ARM64 LDR/STR (max offset = 32760). The cj_str/cj_ldr silently drop instructions when the offset doesn't fit. The sync/reload of stack_top never happens.

I need to fix emit_load64 and emit_store64 to handle large offsets on ARM64.

Read — lisa/jit.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "offset": 133,
  "limit": 25
}
result
133→static void emit_load64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *base, int32_t disp) {
   134→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   135→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));
   136→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   137→    cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));
   138→#endif
   139→}
   140→
   141→static void emit_store64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *src, const char *base, int32_t disp) {
   142→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   143→    cj_mov(ctx, mem(base, disp), reg(src));
   144→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   145→    cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(base, disp));
   146→#endif
   147→}
   148→
   149→static void emit_call_abs(cj_ctx *ctx, void *fn_ptr) {
   150→    emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_CALLADDR, (uint64_t)(uintptr_t)fn_ptr);
   151→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   152→    cj_call(ctx, reg(REG_CALLADDR));
   153→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   154→    cj_blr(ctx, reg(REG_CALLADDR));
   155→#endif
   156→}
   157→
Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "static void emit_load64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *base, int32_t disp) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));\n#endif\n}\n\nstatic void emit_store64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *src, const char *base, int32_t disp) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    cj_mov(ctx, mem(base, disp), reg(src));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(base, disp));\n#endif\n}",
  "new_string": "static void emit_load64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *base, int32_t disp) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    /* ARM64 LDR unsigned offset max = 4095*8 = 32760. For larger offsets,\n       add the offset to the base in a scratch register first. */\n    if (disp >= 0 && disp <= 32760 && (disp % 8) == 0) {\n        cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));\n    } else {\n        /* Use dst as scratch for the address computation */\n        emit_load_imm64(ctx, dst, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp);\n        cj_add(ctx, reg(dst), reg(base));\n        cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(dst, 0));\n    }\n#endif\n}\n\nstatic void emit_store64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *src, const char *base, int32_t disp) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    cj_mov(ctx, mem(base, disp), reg(src));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    if (disp >= 0 && disp <= 32760 && (disp % 8) == 0) {\n        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(base, disp));\n    } else {\n        /* Use REG_TMP4 as scratch for address computation */\n        emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP4, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp);\n        cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));\n        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(REG_TMP4, 0));\n    }\n#endif\n}"
}
result

⚙ Bash

It works! Output is 1 (correct for g(3) where 3 > 0). Let me now run all the tests.

Bash — Run all verification tests
{

{

result
742:lisa_value lisa_jit_tail_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc) {
743-    lisa_value callee = vm->stack_top[-1 - argc];
744-
745-    /* Native functions: just call directly */
746-    if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {
747-        call_value(vm, callee, argc);
748-        return vm->stack_top[-1];
749-    }
750-
751-    if (!IS_OBJ(callee) || OBJ_TYPE(callee) != OBJ_CLOSURE) {
752-        runtime_error(vm, "Can only call functions and closures.");
753-        return LISA_NIL;
754-    }
755-
756-    lisa_obj_closure *closure = AS_CLOSURE(callee);
757-    if (argc != closure->function->arity) {
758-        runtime_error(vm, "Expected %d arguments but got %d.",
759-                      closure->function->arity, argc);
760-        return LISA_NIL;
761-    }
762-
763-    /* The JIT caller's frame is the current top frame; reuse it */
764-    lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
765-    close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);
766-
767-    /* Slide callee + args down over the current frame */
768-    lisa_value *src = vm->stack_top - argc - 1;
769-    memmove(frame->slots, src, (size_t)(argc + 1) * sizeof(lisa_value));
770-    vm->stack_top = frame->slots + argc + 1;
771-
772-    frame->closure = closure;
773-    frame->ip = closure->function->chunk.code;
774-
775-    /* Dispatch to JIT or interpreter */
776-    lisa_value result;
777-    if (closure->function->jit_code) {
778-        lisa_jit_fn jit_fn = (lisa_jit_fn)closure->function->jit_code;
779-        result = jit_fn(vm, frame->closure, frame->slots);
780-    } else {
781-        int target_depth = vm->frame_count - 1;
782-        lisa_run(vm, target_depth);
783-        result = vm->stack_top[-1];
784-    }
785-    return result;
786-}
787-
⚙ Read

The tail call goes through lisa_jit_tail_call_helper which recursively calls JIT functions, causing C stack overflow for deep tail recursion. The self-call optimization is disabled because fn->name == NULL. Let me fix the self-call detection.

Read — lisa/jit.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "offset": 1128,
  "limit": 35
}
result
1128→        }
  1129→
  1130→        case OP_TAIL_CALL: {
  1131→            int argc = chunk->code[i + 1];
  1132→            cache_flush(ctx, &cache);
  1133→
  1134→            bool is_self_call = false;
  1135→            if (fn->name != NULL && i >= 2 && chunk->code[i - 2] == OP_GET_GLOBAL) {
  1136→                uint8_t name_idx = chunk->code[i - 1];
  1137→                lisa_value name_val = chunk->constants.values[name_idx];
  1138→                if (IS_STRING(name_val)) {
  1139→                    lisa_obj_string *name_str = AS_STRING(name_val);
  1140→                    if (name_str == fn->name)
  1141→                        is_self_call = true;
  1142→                }
  1143→            }
  1144→
  1145→            if (is_self_call) {
  1146→                for (int a = 0; a < argc; a++) {
  1147→                    int32_t src_off = (int32_t)(-8 * (argc - a));
  1148→                    emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_STKTOP, src_off);
  1149→                    emit_store64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_SLOTS, (int32_t)((1 + a) * 8));
  1150→                }
  1151→                cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_STKTOP), reg(REG_SLOTS));
  1152→                cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_STKTOP), imm((uint64_t)(argc + 1) * 8));
  1153→                EMIT_JMP(ctx, entry_label);
  1154→            } else {
  1155→                emit_call_vm_int(ctx, (void *)lisa_jit_tail_call_helper, argc);
  1156→                cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_RET), reg(REG_RET));
  1157→                emit_epilogue(ctx);
  1158→            }
  1159→            i += 2;
  1160→            break;
  1161→        }
  1162→
⚙ Read, Edit

I need to fix two things in the self-call optimization:

1. Add a body_label after the prologue (since entry_label is now before the prologue)

2. Use runtime closure comparison instead of compile-time name comparison

Read — lisa/jit.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "offset": 775,
  "limit": 15
}
result
775→    reg_cache_t cache;
   776→    cache_init(&cache);
   777→
   778→    int i = 0;
   779→    while (i < chunk->count) {
   780→        /* At branch targets, ensure cache is empty */
   781→        if (map.is_target[i]) {
   782→            cache_flush(ctx, &cache);
   783→            cj_mark_label(ctx, map.labels[i]);
   784→        }
   785→
   786→        uint8_t op = chunk->code[i];
   787→        switch (op) {
   788→
   789→        case OP_CONSTANT: {
Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "    cj_mark_label(ctx, entry_label);\n    emit_prologue(ctx);\n\n    reg_cache_t cache;\n    cache_init(&cache);",
  "new_string": "    cj_mark_label(ctx, entry_label);\n    emit_prologue(ctx);\n\n    /* body_label: target for self-tail-call loop (after prologue) */\n    cj_label body_label = cj_create_label(ctx);\n    cj_mark_label(ctx, body_label);\n\n    reg_cache_t cache;\n    cache_init(&cache);"
}
result
The file lisa/jit.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit, Bash, Grep

Now update the tail call to use runtime closure comparison and body_label:

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "        case OP_TAIL_CALL: {\n            int argc = chunk->code[i + 1];\n            cache_flush(ctx, &cache);\n\n            bool is_self_call = false;\n            if (fn->name != NULL && i >= 2 && chunk->code[i - 2] == OP_GET_GLOBAL) {\n                uint8_t name_idx = chunk->code[i - 1];\n                lisa_value name_val = chunk->constants.values[name_idx];\n                if (IS_STRING(name_val)) {\n                    lisa_obj_string *name_str = AS_STRING(name_val);\n                    if (name_str == fn->name)\n                        is_self_call = true;\n                }\n            }\n\n            if (is_self_call) {\n                for (int a = 0; a < argc; a++) {\n                    int32_t src_off = (int32_t)(-8 * (argc - a));\n                    emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_STKTOP, src_off);\n                    emit_store64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_SLOTS, (int32_t)((1 + a) * 8));\n                }\n                cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_STKTOP), reg(REG_SLOTS));\n                cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_STKTOP), imm((uint64_t)(argc + 1) * 8));\n                EMIT_JMP(ctx, entry_label);\n            } else {\n                emit_call_vm_int(ctx, (void *)lisa_jit_tail_call_helper, argc);\n                cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_RET), reg(REG_RET));\n                emit_epilogue(ctx);\n            }\n            i += 2;\n            break;\n        }",
  "new_string": "        case OP_TAIL_CALL: {\n            int argc = chunk->code[i + 1];\n            cache_flush(ctx, &cache);\n            emit_sync_stack_top(ctx);\n\n            /* Runtime self-call check: compare callee with current closure */\n            cj_label not_self = cj_create_label(ctx);\n            int32_t callee_off = (int32_t)(-8 * (argc + 1));\n            emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_STKTOP, callee_off);\n            cj_cmp(ctx, reg(REG_TMP1), reg(REG_CLOSURE));\n            EMIT_JNE(ctx, not_self);\n\n

⚙ Edit ×2

The negative displacement in emit_load64 wraps to a huge positive number due to (uint32_t) cast. Let me fix the fallback path for negative offsets.

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "static void emit_load64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *base, int32_t disp) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    /* ARM64 LDR unsigned offset max = 4095*8 = 32760. For larger offsets,\n       add the offset to the base in a scratch register first. */\n    if (disp >= 0 && disp <= 32760 && (disp % 8) == 0) {\n        cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));\n    } else {\n        /* Use dst as scratch for the address computation */\n        emit_load_imm64(ctx, dst, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp);\n        cj_add(ctx, reg(dst), reg(base));\n        cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(dst, 0));\n    }\n#endif\n}\n\nstatic void emit_store64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *src, const char *base, int32_t disp) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    cj_mov(ctx, mem(base, disp), reg(src));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    if (disp >= 0 && disp <= 32760 && (disp % 8) == 0) {\n        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(base, disp));\n    } else {\n        /* Use REG_TMP4 as scratch for address computation */\n        emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP4, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp);\n        cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));\n        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(REG_TMP4, 0));\n    }\n#endif\n}",
  "new_string": "static void emit_load64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *base, int32_t disp) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    /* ARM64 LDR unsigned offset max = 4095*8 = 32760. */\n    if (disp >= 0 && disp <= 32760 && (disp % 8) == 0) {\n        cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));\n    } else {\n        /* Use dst as scratch for address computation.\n           Handle both positive and negative displacements. */\n        if (disp >= 0) {\n            emit_load_imm64(ctx, dst, (
... (truncated)
result
The file lisa/jit.c has been updated successfully.
Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "    } else {\n        /* Use dst as scratch for address computation.\n           Handle both positive and negative displacements. */\n        if (disp >= 0) {\n            emit_load_imm64(ctx, dst, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp);\n            cj_add(ctx, reg(dst), reg(base));\n        } else {\n            emit_load_imm64(ctx, dst, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp));\n            cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), reg(base));\n            cj_sub(ctx, reg(dst), reg(dst));\n        }\n        cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(dst, 0));\n    }",
  "new_string": "    } else if (disp < 0 && (-disp) <= 4095) {\n        /* Small negative offset: SUB then LDR */\n        cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), reg(base));\n        cj_sub(ctx, reg(dst), imm((uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp)));\n        cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(dst, 0));\n    } else {\n        /* Large offset: load into dst, add base, load */\n        if (disp >= 0) {\n            emit_load_imm64(ctx, dst, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp);\n            cj_add(ctx, reg(dst), reg(base));\n        } else {\n            cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), reg(base));\n            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP4, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp));\n            cj_sub(ctx, reg(dst), reg(REG_TMP4));\n        }\n        cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(dst, 0));\n    }"
}
result
The file lisa/jit.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit, Grep ×5, Read ×2

Now fix emit_store64 for negative offsets too:

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "static void emit_store64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *src, const char *base, int32_t disp) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    cj_mov(ctx, mem(base, disp), reg(src));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    if (disp >= 0 && disp <= 32760 && (disp % 8) == 0) {\n        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(base, disp));\n    } else {\n        /* Use REG_TMP4 as scratch for address computation */\n        emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP4, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp);\n        cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));\n        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(REG_TMP4, 0));\n    }\n#endif\n}",
  "new_string": "static void emit_store64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *src, const char *base, int32_t disp) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    cj_mov(ctx, mem(base, disp), reg(src));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    if (disp >= 0 && disp <= 32760 && (disp % 8) == 0) {\n        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(base, disp));\n    } else if (disp < 0 && (-disp) <= 4095) {\n        cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));\n        cj_sub(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), imm((uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp)));\n        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(REG_TMP4, 0));\n    } else {\n        /* Large offset: compute address in REG_TMP4 */\n        if (disp >= 0) {\n            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP4, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp);\n            cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));\n        } else {\n            cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));\n            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP5, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp));\n            cj_sub(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(REG_TMP5));\n        }\n        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(REG_TMP4, 0));\n    }\n#endif\n}"
}
result
<tool_use_error>String to replace not found in file.
String: static void emit_store64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *src, const char *base, int32_t disp) {
#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
    cj_mov(ctx, mem(base, disp), reg(src));
#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
    if (disp >= 0 && disp <= 32760 && (disp % 8) == 0) {
        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(base, disp));
    } else {
        /* Use REG_TMP4 as scratch for address computation */
        emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP4, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp);
        cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));
        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(REG_TMP4, 0));
    }
#endif
}</tool_use_error>

I see — emit_store64 was accidentally removed. It should be right after emit_load64. Let me add it back.

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",

  "new_string": "#endif\n}\n\nstatic void emit_store64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *src, const char *base, int32_t disp) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    cj_mov(ctx, mem(base, disp), reg(src));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    if (disp >= 0 && disp <= 32760 && (disp % 8) == 0) {\n        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(base, disp));\n    } else if (disp < 0 && (-disp) <= 4095) {\n        cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));\n        cj_sub(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), imm((uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp)));\n        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(REG_TMP4, 0));\n    } else {\n        if (disp >= 0) {\n            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP4, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp);\n            cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));\n        } else {\n            cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));\n            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP5, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp));\n            cj_sub(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(REG_TMP5));\n        }\n        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(REG_TMP4, 0));\n    }\n#endif\n}\n\nstatic void emit_call_abs(cj_ctx *ctx, void *fn_ptr) {"

human

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:

Let me analyze the conversation chronologically to capture all important details.

1. The conversation starts with a system reminder about a previous session that was summarized. The previous session had a plan to rewrite lisa/jit.c with register cache + inline fast paths.

2. The current session continues from where the previous one left off. The key task is implementing the plan to rewrite lisa/jit.c.

3. At the start of this session, there were two remaining issues from the previous session:

- Remove unused emit_asr_imm function

- Rebuild and test

4. I removed emit_asr_imm and built successfully.

5. All verification tests passed (correctness), but benchmarks showed NO speedup - JIT and interpreter had identical performance (~1.05s for fib(35) with -O2).

6. I investigated why - discovered that the JIT was NEVER being triggered because call_closure() in vm.c gated JIT compilation on closure->function->name != NULL, but the compiler never sets function names (all functions have name == NULL).

7. Fixed vm.c to use vm->frame_count > 0 instead of name != NULL check. This triggered JIT compilation but caused crashes.

8. Crash debugging revealed multiple cj backend bugs:

- cj_stp/cj_ldp ignore pre-indexed/post-indexed addressing modes (always emit signed offset)

- cj_mov(x29, sp) generates ORR x29, XZR, XZR = 0 because register 31 is XZR in ORR encoding, not SP

- The entry_label was marked AFTER the prologue, so jit_code pointed past the prologue (function setup was skipped)

- offsetof(lisa_vm, stack_top) = 38920 exceeds ARM64 LDR/STR immediate range (max 32760), causing cj_str/cj_ldr to silently drop instructions

- Negative displacements in emit_load64 caused (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp to wrap to huge positive numbers

9. Fixed each issue:

- Prologue/epilogue: manually adjust SP with SUB/ADD instead of pre/post-indexed STP/LDP

- Frame pointer: raw ADD x29, sp, #0 instruction (0x910003FD)

- Entry label: moved before prologue

- Large offsets: added fallback paths in emit_load64/emit_store64 for ARM64

- Negative offsets: added MOV+SUB handling

10. After fixes, simple functions worked but tail recursion crashed at 100K iterations. Root cause: self-call optimization was disabled (checked fn->name != NULL), so all tail calls went through lisa_jit_tail_call_helper which recursively calls JIT functions, causing C stack overflow.

11. Fixed self-call optimization to use runtime closure comparison instead of compile-time name comparison. Added body_label after prologue for self-call loop jumps.

12. Still crashing at 100K (works at 10K). Most recent investigation revealed the comparison between NaN-boxed closure value on stack vs raw pointer in REG_CLOSURE is incorrect - they have different representations.

The user's only message was asking to continue from the previous session. All work follows the plan to implement the JIT rewrite.

Let me capture the current state of the code precisely.

Summary:

1. Primary Request and Intent:

The user asked to continue implementing a detailed plan to rewrite lisa/jit.c — replacing a "stack machine in native code" JIT with a proper register-cache-based JIT with inline integer fast paths. The plan specifies register cache (4 slots), inline fast paths for integer arithmetic/comparisons, flush-and-call pattern for complex ops, and conservative branch handling. The plan says "Only jit.c changes" but vm.c also needed modification to fix a bug preventing JIT from ever being triggered.

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- NaN-boxing: Values are uint64_t with tag bits in top 16 bits. QNAN=0x7FFC000000000000, TAG_INT=0x0002000000000000, TAG_OBJ=0x0004000000000000. IS_INT checks top 16 bits == 0x7FFE. OBJ values: LISA_OBJ(ptr) = QNAN | TAG_OBJ | ((uint64_t)(uintptr_t)(ptr) & PAYLOAD_MASK). AS_OBJ(v) = (lisa_obj*)(uintptr_t)((v) & PAYLOAD_MASK).

- cj JIT framework: Header-only backends for x86-64 and ARM64. Registers by string names. Labels with forward-reference fixup. cj_add_u32() for raw instruction emission.

- Register cache: Compile-time tracker with depth and 4 cache register slots (x10-x13 on ARM64, r8-r11 on x86-64)

- ARM64 backend bugs found: cj_stp/cj_ldp ignore pre/post-indexed modes; cj_mov with SP generates wrong instruction; cj_ldr/cj_str silently drop instructions when offset > 32760

- ARM64 raw instruction encodings used: UBFM for LSR/LSL immediate, SBFM for ASR immediate, CSINC for CSET, raw ADD for MOV from SP

- VM struct layout: offsetof(lisa_vm, stack_top) = 38920 — too large for ARM64 LDR/STR unsigned offset

3. Files and Code Sections:

- lisa/jit.c — Complete rewrite (the main deliverable)

- Removed unused emit_asr_imm and emit_push functions

- Fixed ARM64 prologue to manually adjust SP instead of using pre-indexed STP:

```c

cj_sub(ctx, reg("sp"), imm(80));

cj_stp(ctx, reg("x29"), reg("x30"), mem("sp", 0));

cj_add_u32(ctx, 0x910003FD); /* ADD x29, sp, #0 */

cj_stp(ctx, reg("x19"), reg("x20"), mem("sp", 16));

cj_stp(ctx, reg("x21"), reg("x22"), mem("sp", 32));

cj_str(ctx, reg("x23"), mem("sp", 48));

```

- Fixed epilogue to use signed-offset LDP + manual SP restore:

```c

cj_ldp(ctx, reg("x19"), reg("x20"), mem("sp", 16));

cj_ldp(ctx, reg("x21"), reg("x22"), mem("sp", 32));

cj_ldr(ctx, reg("x23"), mem("sp", 48));

cj_ldp(ctx, reg("x29"), reg("x30"), mem("sp", 0));

cj_add(ctx, reg("sp"), imm(80));

cj_ret(ctx);

```

- Moved entry_label before prologue: cj_mark_label(ctx, entry_label); emit_prologue(ctx);

- Added body_label after prologue for self-tail-call loop jumps

- Rewrote emit_load64 for ARM64 to handle large and negative offsets:

```c

if (disp >= 0 && disp <= 32760 && (disp % 8) == 0) {

cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));

} else if (disp < 0 && (-disp) <= 4095) {

cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), reg(base));

cj_sub(ctx, reg(dst), imm((uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp)));

cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(dst, 0));

} else { /* large offset fallback with load-add-load */ }

```

- Rewrote emit_store64 similarly for ARM64 large/negative offsets (uses REG_TMP4 as scratch)

- Rewrote OP_TAIL_CALL to use runtime closure comparison instead of compile-time name check:

```c

cj_label not_self = cj_create_label(ctx);

int32_t callee_off = (int32_t)(-8 * (argc + 1));

emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_STKTOP, callee_off);

cj_cmp(ctx, reg(REG_TMP1), reg(REG_CLOSURE));

EMIT_JNE(ctx, not_self);

// Self-call: move args to slots, reset stack, jump to body_label

// ...

cj_mark_label(ctx, not_self);

emit_call_vm_int(ctx, (void *)lisa_jit_tail_call_helper, argc);

```

- All debug prints have been removed from the current version

- lisa/vm.c — Modified JIT trigger condition

- Changed from closure->function->name != NULL to vm->frame_count > 0:

```c

/* JIT compile on first call (skip top-level script) */

if (vm->jit_enabled && !closure->function->jit_code &&

vm->frame_count > 0) {

```

- lisa/vm.h — Read-only. VM struct with stack_top at offset 38920. JIT helper declarations.

- lisa/object.h — Read-only. lisa_obj_function has jit_code, jit_ctx. lisa_obj_closure has function, upvalues.

- lisa/value.h — Read-only. NaN-boxing: LISA_OBJ(ptr) = QNAN | TAG_OBJ | (ptr & PAYLOAD_MASK), AS_OBJ(v) = (lisa_obj*)(v & PAYLOAD_MASK).

- lisa/chunk.h — Read-only. All opcodes enumerated (OP_CONSTANT=0 through OP_PRINTLN).

- lisa/compiler.c — Read-only. init_compiler at line 336 passes NULL as function name. +,-,*,/,<= etc. compiled to dedicated opcodes, NOT function calls.

- src/arch/arm64/backend.h — Read-only. Multiple bugs found: cj_stp always uses signed-offset encoding (ignores pre/post-indexed mode), cj_mov for reg-reg uses ORR (register 31=XZR not SP), cj_ldr/cj_str silently fail for large offsets.

4. Errors and fixes:

- Unused emit_push function: Removed the function definition

- Unused emit_asr_imm function: Removed the function definition

- JIT never triggered: call_closure() in vm.c checked closure->function->name != NULL but compiler never sets names. Fixed by changing to vm->frame_count > 0

- Crash: cj_stp ignores pre-indexed mode: SP never decremented in prologue. Fixed by manually emitting SUB sp, sp, #80 before STP with signed offset

- Crash: cj_mov(x29, sp) produces x29=0: ORR encoding treats reg 31 as XZR not SP. Fixed with raw instruction cj_add_u32(ctx, 0x910003FD) (= ADD x29, sp, #0)

- Crash: entry_label after prologue: JIT function pointer skipped prologue entirely. Fixed by marking label BEFORE prologue

- Silent instruction drops: large offsets: offsetof(lisa_vm, stack_top) = 38920 exceeds ARM64 LDR/STR max (32760). cj_ldr/cj_str silently return without emitting. Fixed by adding fallback paths in emit_load64/emit_store64

- Negative offset wrapping: (uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-16) = 0xFFFFFFF0 instead of -16. Fixed by adding special handling for negative displacements (MOV base + SUB abs_disp)

- Stack overflow on tail recursion: Self-call optimization disabled (checked fn->name). All tail calls went through C helper which recursively calls JIT functions. Fixed by adding runtime closure comparison and body_label for loop jumps

- emit_store64 function accidentally deleted: During an edit replacing emit_load64, emit_store64 was lost. Re-added it with proper large/negative offset handling

- CURRENT BUG (unfixed): Self-call comparison compares NaN-boxed lisa_value (from stack) with raw lisa_obj_closure * pointer (from REG_CLOSURE). These are different representations! The NaN-boxed value is QNAN | TAG_OBJ | (ptr & PAYLOAD_MASK) while REG_CLOSURE is the raw pointer. The comparison always fails, causing all tail calls to go through the recursive C helper.

5. Problem Solving:

- Successfully fixed all ARM64 backend workarounds for prologue/epilogue, large offsets, and negative offsets

- Simple functions work correctly (identity, arithmetic, conditionals)

- Recursive functions work (fac(20), fib(30))

- Tail recursion works for small depths (up to ~10K) but crashes at ~100K due to the NaN-boxing mismatch in the self-call comparison (current bug being investigated)

- The root cause was just identified in the last message: stack value is NaN-boxed (LISA_OBJ(ptr)) but REG_CLOSURE is a raw pointer — they can never be equal

6. All user messages:

- "This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. [detailed summary of previous work]. Please continue the conversation from where we left off without asking the user any further questions. Continue with the last task that you were asked to work on."

7. Pending Tasks:

- Fix the self-call comparison in OP_TAIL_CALL: need to either NaN-box REG_CLOSURE or extract the raw pointer from the stack value before comparing

- Verify tail recursion works at 1M iterations

- Run all verification tests from the plan (fac, fib, tail loop, mutual recursion, closures, list ops)

- Run benchmarks (JIT vs interpreter) with the optimized build (-O2)

- Remove any remaining debug code

8. Current Work:

Debugging why the self-tail-call optimization doesn't work at large depths (100K+). The most recent discovery (last lines of conversation) revealed the root cause:

The OP_TAIL_CALL self-call check loads the callee value from the stack (a NaN-boxed lisa_value) and compares it with REG_CLOSURE (a raw lisa_obj_closure * pointer). From value.h:

```c

#define LISA_OBJ(ptr) (QNAN | TAG_OBJ | ((uint64_t)(uintptr_t)(ptr) & PAYLOAD_MASK))

#define AS_OBJ(v) ((lisa_obj*)(uintptr_t)((v) & PAYLOAD_MASK))

```

The NaN-boxed value includes QNAN and TAG_OBJ bits, so it will never equal the raw pointer. The comparison always fails, falling through to the recursive lisa_jit_tail_call_helper path, causing C stack overflow for deep recursion.

9. Optional Next Step:

Fix the self-call comparison in OP_TAIL_CALL by either:

- NaN-boxing REG_CLOSURE at the comparison point: emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP2, QNAN | TAG_OBJ); emit_or(ctx, REG_TMP2, REG_CLOSURE); cj_cmp(ctx, reg(REG_TMP1), reg(REG_TMP2));

- Or extracting the raw pointer from the stack value: mask with PAYLOAD_MASK before comparing

This directly continues the task of implementing the JIT rewrite plan, specifically making tail recursion work correctly. The last work was investigating why bin/lisa -e '(def loop (fn [n] (if (<= n 0) "done" (loop (- n 1))))) (println (loop 100000))' crashes while loop 10000 works.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: <claude-internal>

Please continue the conversation from where we left off without asking the user any further questions. Continue with the last task that you were asked to work on.

⚙ Grep

I need to fix the self-call comparison in OP_TAIL_CALL. The callee on the stack is NaN-boxed (QNAN | TAG_OBJ | ptr) but REG_CLOSURE is a raw pointer — they can never be equal. Let me read the current code to find the exact spot.

⚙ Grep ×2

Now I can see the issue. On line 1162, we compare the NaN-boxed callee value with the raw pointer in REG_CLOSURE. I need to NaN-box REG_CLOSURE before comparing. Let me also check the register/constant definitions.

Grep — QNAN|TAG_OBJ|PAYLOAD_MASK
{
  "pattern": "QNAN|TAG_OBJ|PAYLOAD_MASK",
  "path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "output_mode": "content",
  "-n": true
}
result
99:#define TAG_INT_FULL (QNAN | TAG_INT) /* 0x7FFE000000000000 */
Grep — QNAN|TAG_OBJ
{
  "pattern": "QNAN|TAG_OBJ",
  "path": "lisa/value.h",
  "output_mode": "content",
  "-n": true
}
result
28:#define QNAN    ((uint64_t)0x7FFC000000000000)
35:#define TAG_OBJ    ((uint64_t)0x0003000000000000)  /* 011 */
41:#define LISA_NIL        (QNAN | TAG_NIL)
42:#define LISA_TRUE       (QNAN | TAG_BOOL | 1)
43:#define LISA_FALSE      (QNAN | TAG_BOOL | 0)
45:#define LISA_INT(i)     (QNAN | TAG_INT | ((uint64_t)(i) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
46:#define LISA_OBJ(ptr)   (QNAN | TAG_OBJ | ((uint64_t)(uintptr_t)(ptr) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
56:#define IS_BOOL(v)   (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_BOOL))
57:#define IS_INT(v)    (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_INT))
58:#define IS_OBJ(v)    (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_OBJ))
59:#define IS_DOUBLE(v) (((v) & QNAN) != QNAN)
⚙ Grep

So QNAN | TAG_OBJ = 0x7FFC000000000000 | 0x0003000000000000 = 0x7FFF000000000000. I need to NaN-box REG_CLOSURE before comparing. Let me check which temp registers are available.

⚙ Edit, Read

Now I'll fix the self-call comparison. Instead of comparing the raw REG_CLOSURE with the NaN-boxed callee, I'll NaN-box REG_CLOSURE into a temp register first.

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "            /* Runtime self-call check: compare callee with current closure */\n            cj_label not_self = cj_create_label(ctx);\n            int32_t callee_off = (int32_t)(-8 * (argc + 1));\n            emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_STKTOP, callee_off);\n            cj_cmp(ctx, reg(REG_TMP1), reg(REG_CLOSURE));\n            EMIT_JNE(ctx, not_self);",
  "new_string": "            /* Runtime self-call check: compare callee with current closure.\n               Callee on stack is NaN-boxed (QNAN|TAG_OBJ|ptr), but REG_CLOSURE\n               is a raw pointer. NaN-box REG_CLOSURE into TMP2 for comparison. */\n            cj_label not_self = cj_create_label(ctx);\n            int32_t callee_off = (int32_t)(-8 * (argc + 1));\n            emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_STKTOP, callee_off);\n            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP2, QNAN | TAG_OBJ);\n            cj_orr(ctx, reg(REG_TMP2), reg(REG_CLOSURE));\n            cj_cmp(ctx, reg(REG_TMP1), reg(REG_TMP2));\n            EMIT_JNE(ctx, not_self);"
}
result

⚙ Edit

Good, there's an emit_or wrapper. Let me use that instead of calling cj_orr directly. Now let me apply the fix:

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "            /* Runtime self-call check: compare callee with current closure */\n            cj_label not_self = cj_create_label(ctx);\n            int32_t callee_off = (int32_t)(-8 * (argc + 1));\n            emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_STKTOP, callee_off);\n            cj_cmp(ctx, reg(REG_TMP1), reg(REG_CLOSURE));\n            EMIT_JNE(ctx, not_self);",
  "new_string": "            /* Runtime self-call check: compare callee with current closure.\n               Callee on stack is NaN-boxed (QNAN|TAG_OBJ|ptr), but REG_CLOSURE\n               is a raw pointer. NaN-box REG_CLOSURE into TMP2 for comparison. */\n            cj_label not_self = cj_create_label(ctx);\n            int32_t callee_off = (int32_t)(-8 * (argc + 1));\n            emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_STKTOP, callee_off);\n            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP2, QNAN | TAG_OBJ);\n            emit_or(ctx, REG_TMP2, REG_CLOSURE);\n            cj_cmp(ctx, reg(REG_TMP1), reg(REG_TMP2));\n            EMIT_JNE(ctx, not_self);"
}
result
The file lisa/jit.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Grep

Wait — there's a subtlety. LISA_OBJ(ptr) masks the pointer with PAYLOAD_MASK (48 bits). The raw closure pointer in REG_CLOSURE might have bits set above bit 47. On ARM64 with current macOS, userspace pointers are typically 48-bit or less, so ptr & PAYLOAD_MASK == ptr in practice. But to be safe and correct, I should also mask REG_CLOSURE. Let me check PAYLOAD_MASK.

Grep — PAYLOAD_MASK
{
  "pattern": "PAYLOAD_MASK",
  "path": "lisa/value.h",
  "output_mode": "content",
  "-n": true
}
result
38:#define PAYLOAD_MASK ((uint64_t)0x0000FFFFFFFFFFFF) /* 48 bits */
45:#define LISA_INT(i)     (QNAN | TAG_INT | ((uint64_t)(i) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
46:#define LISA_OBJ(ptr)   (QNAN | TAG_OBJ | ((uint64_t)(uintptr_t)(ptr) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
66:    uint64_t raw = v & PAYLOAD_MASK;
79:#define AS_OBJ(v)    ((lisa_obj*)(uintptr_t)((v) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
⚙ Grep

  "path": "lisa/jit.c",

}

1159:               Callee on stack is NaN-boxed (QNAN|TAG_OBJ|ptr), but REG_CLOSURE
1160:               is a raw pointer. NaN-box REG_CLOSURE into TMP2 for comparison. */

⚙ Bash

Good — the JIT function receives frame->closure as a raw lisa_obj_closure * pointer (line 474). This pointer comes from AS_OBJ() which masks with PAYLOAD_MASK, so it's already a clean 48-bit pointer. The OR with QNAN | TAG_OBJ will produce the correct NaN-boxed value. The fix is correct.

}

⚙ Bash ×2

Factorial and fibonacci work (fac(20) overflows 48-bit payload, which is expected behavior). But mutual recursion crashes (segfault). Let me also verify the other tests work, and then investigate the crash.

Bash — Test closures

result

⚙ Bash

Good — closures (15) and list ops (1) work correctly. Now let me verify the factorial result matches the interpreter:

Bash — Test fac(20) with interpreter for comparison
{
  "command": "lisa/bin/lisa --no-jit -e '(def fac (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) 1 (* n (fac (- n 1)))))) (println (fac 20))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test fac(20) with interpreter for comparison"
}
result
113784466440192
⚙ Grep, Read

Same result — the overflow is consistent between JIT and interpreter (48-bit integer payload).

Now the mutual recursion crash. This is expected: e tail-calls o and o tail-calls e — neither is a self-call, so both go through lisa_jit_tail_call_helper which recursively invokes JIT functions, overflowing the C stack at 100K depth. Let me look at how the tail call helper works:

Grep — lisa_jit_tail_call_helper
{
  "pattern": "lisa_jit_tail_call_helper",
  "path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "output_mode": "content",
  "-n": true,
  "-A": 30
}
result
742:lisa_value lisa_jit_tail_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc) {
743-    lisa_value callee = vm->stack_top[-1 - argc];
744-
745-    /* Native functions: just call directly */
746-    if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {
747-        call_value(vm, callee, argc);
748-        return vm->stack_top[-1];
749-    }
750-
751-    if (!IS_OBJ(callee) || OBJ_TYPE(callee) != OBJ_CLOSURE) {
752-        runtime_error(vm, "Can only call functions and closures.");
753-        return LISA_NIL;
754-    }
755-
756-    lisa_obj_closure *closure = AS_CLOSURE(callee);
757-    if (argc != closure->function->arity) {
758-        runtime_error(vm, "Expected %d arguments but got %d.",
759-                      closure->function->arity, argc);
760-        return LISA_NIL;
761-    }
762-
763-    /* The JIT caller's frame is the current top frame; reuse it */
764-    lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
765-    close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);
766-
767-    /* Slide callee + args down over the current frame */
768-    lisa_value *src = vm->stack_top - argc - 1;
769-    memmove(frame->slots, src, (size_t)(argc + 1) * sizeof(lisa_value));
770-    vm->stack_top = frame->slots + argc + 1;
771-
772-    frame->closure = closure;
Read — lisa/vm.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "offset": 772,
  "limit": 25
}
result
772→    frame->closure = closure;
   773→    frame->ip = closure->function->chunk.code;
   774→
   775→    /* Dispatch to JIT or interpreter */
   776→    lisa_value result;
   777→    if (closure->function->jit_code) {
   778→        lisa_jit_fn jit_fn = (lisa_jit_fn)closure->function->jit_code;
   779→        result = jit_fn(vm, frame->closure, frame->slots);
   780→    } else {
   781→        int target_depth = vm->frame_count - 1;
   782→        lisa_run(vm, target_depth);
   783→        result = vm->stack_top[-1];
   784→    }
   785→    return result;
   786→}
   787→
   788→lisa_value lisa_jit_get_global(lisa_vm *vm, int name_idx) {
   789→    lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
   790→    lisa_obj_string *name = AS_STRING(frame->closure->function->chunk.constants.values[name_idx]);
   791→    int idx = globals_find(vm, name);
   792→    if (idx == -1) {
   793→        runtime_error(vm, "Undefined variable '%s'.", name->chars);
   794→        return LISA_NIL;
   795→    }
   796→    return vm->global_values[idx];
⚙ Read

The mutual recursion test crashes because non-self tail calls (e→o→e→o...) recursively call lisa_jit_tail_call_helper → JIT → helper → JIT..., overflowing the C stack. I need to implement a trampoline. Let me look at the full call chain:

Read — lisa/vm.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "offset": 460,
  "limit": 80
}
result
460→                    closure->upvalues[i] = frame->closure->upvalues[index];
   461→                }
   462→            }
   463→            break;
   464→        }
   465→
   466→        case OP_CALL: {
   467→            int argc = READ_BYTE();
   468→            if (!call_value(vm, peek(vm, argc), argc)) {
   469→                return INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR;
   470→            }
   471→            frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
   472→            /* JIT dispatch: if the callee has JIT'd code, call it directly */
   473→            if (frame->closure->function->jit_code) {
   474→                lisa_jit_fn jit_fn = (lisa_jit_fn)frame->closure->function->jit_code;
   475→                lisa_value result = jit_fn(vm, frame->closure, frame->slots);
   476→                /* JIT function returned; pop its frame */
   477→                close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);
   478→                vm->frame_count--;
   479→                vm->stack_top = frame->slots;
   480→                push(vm, result);
   481→                frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
   482→            }
   483→            break;
   484→        }
   485→
   486→        case OP_TAIL_CALL: {
   487→            int argc = READ_BYTE();
   488→            lisa_value callee = peek(vm, argc);
   489→
   490→            /* Native functions: no frame to reuse, fall through to normal call */
   491→            if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {
   492→                if (!call_value(vm, callee, argc)) {
   493→                    return INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR;
   494→                }
   495→                frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
   496→                break;
   497→            }
   498→
   499→            if (!IS_OBJ(callee) || OBJ_TYPE(callee) != OBJ_CLOSURE) {
   500→                runtime_error(vm, "Can only call functions and closures.");
   501→                return INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR;
   502→            }
   503→
   504→            lisa_obj_closure *closure = AS_CLOSURE(callee);
   505→            if (argc != closure->function->arity) {
   506→                runtime_error(vm, "Expected %d arguments but got %d.",
   507→                              closure->function->arity, argc);
   508→                return INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR;
   509→            }
   510→
   511→            /* Close upvalues for the current frame */
   512→            close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);
   513→
   514→            /* Slide callee + args down over the current frame */
   515→            lisa_value *src = vm->stack_top - argc - 1;
   516→            memmove(frame->slots, src, (size_t)(argc + 1) * sizeof(lisa_value));
   517→            vm->stack_top = frame->slots + argc + 1;
   518→
   519→            /* Reuse the current frame */
   520→            frame->closure = closure;
   521→            frame->ip = closure->function->chunk.code;
   522→            /* JIT dispatch for tail calls */
   523→            if (clo
... (truncated)

{
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",

result
742→lisa_value lisa_jit_tail_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc) {
   743→    lisa_value callee = vm->stack_top[-1 - argc];
   744→
   745→    /* Native functions: just call directly */
   746→    if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {
   747→        call_value(vm, callee, argc);
   748→        return vm->stack_top[-1];
   749→    }
   750→
   751→    if (!IS_OBJ(callee) || OBJ_TYPE(callee) != OBJ_CLOSURE) {
   752→        runtime_error(vm, "Can only call functions and closures.");
   753→        return LISA_NIL;
   754→    }
   755→
   756→    lisa_obj_closure *closure = AS_CLOSURE(callee);
   757→    if (argc != closure->function->arity) {
   758→        runtime_error(vm, "Expected %d arguments but got %d.",
   759→                      closure->function->arity, argc);
   760→        return LISA_NIL;
   761→    }
   762→
   763→    /* The JIT caller's frame is the current top frame; reuse it */
   764→    lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
   765→    close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);
   766→
   767→    /* Slide callee + args down over the current frame */
   768→    lisa_value *src = vm->stack_top - argc - 1;
   769→    memmove(frame->slots, src, (size_t)(argc + 1) * sizeof(lisa_value));
   770→    vm->stack_top = frame->slots + argc + 1;
   771→
   772→    frame->closure = closure;
   773→    frame->ip = closure->function->chunk.code;
   774→
   775→    /* Dispatch to JIT or interpreter */
   776→    lisa_value result;
   777→    if (closure->function->jit_code) {
   778→        lisa_jit_fn jit_fn = (lisa_jit_fn)closure->function->jit_code;
   779→        result = jit_fn(vm, frame->closure, frame->slots);
   780→    } else {
   781→        int target_depth = vm->frame_count - 1;
   782→        lisa_run(vm, target_depth);
   783→        result = vm->stack_top[-1];
   784→    }
   785→    return result;
   786→}
   787→
   788→lisa_value lisa_jit_get_global(lisa_vm *vm, int name_idx) {
   789→    lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
   790→    lisa_obj_string *name = AS_STRING(frame->closure->function->chunk.constants.values[name_idx]);
   791→    int idx = globals_find(vm, name);
⚙ Read

Let me check the value tag space to find a safe sentinel value:

Read — lisa/value.h
{
  "file_path": "lisa/value.h",
  "offset": 25,
  "limit": 45
}
result
25→typedef uint64_t lisa_value;
    26→
    27→/* The quiet NaN mask: sign(1) + exponent(0x7FF) + quiet(1) = bits 63,62:52,51 */
    28→#define QNAN    ((uint64_t)0x7FFC000000000000)
    29→#define SIGN_BIT ((uint64_t)0x8000000000000000)
    30→
    31→/* Tag values shifted into bits 50:48 */
    32→#define TAG_NIL    ((uint64_t)0x0000000000000000)  /* 000 */
    33→#define TAG_BOOL   ((uint64_t)0x0001000000000000)  /* 001 */
    34→#define TAG_INT    ((uint64_t)0x0002000000000000)  /* 010 */
    35→#define TAG_OBJ    ((uint64_t)0x0003000000000000)  /* 011 */
    36→
    37→#define TAG_MASK   ((uint64_t)0x0003000000000000)
    38→#define PAYLOAD_MASK ((uint64_t)0x0000FFFFFFFFFFFF) /* 48 bits */
    39→
    40→/* Construct values */
    41→#define LISA_NIL        (QNAN | TAG_NIL)
    42→#define LISA_TRUE       (QNAN | TAG_BOOL | 1)
    43→#define LISA_FALSE      (QNAN | TAG_BOOL | 0)
    44→#define LISA_BOOL(b)    ((b) ? LISA_TRUE : LISA_FALSE)
    45→#define LISA_INT(i)     (QNAN | TAG_INT | ((uint64_t)(i) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
    46→#define LISA_OBJ(ptr)   (QNAN | TAG_OBJ | ((uint64_t)(uintptr_t)(ptr) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
    47→
    48→static inline lisa_value lisa_double(double d) {
    49→    union { double d; uint64_t u; } conv;
    50→    conv.d = d;
    51→    return conv.u;
    52→}
    53→
    54→/* Type checks */
    55→#define IS_NIL(v)    ((v) == LISA_NIL)
    56→#define IS_BOOL(v)   (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_BOOL))
    57→#define IS_INT(v)    (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_INT))
    58→#define IS_OBJ(v)    (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_OBJ))
    59→#define IS_DOUBLE(v) (((v) & QNAN) != QNAN)
    60→
    61→/* Extract values */
    62→#define AS_BOOL(v)   ((v) & 1)
    63→
    64→static inline int64_t AS_INT(lisa_value v) {
    65→    /* Sign-extend the 48-bit payload */
    66→    uint64_t raw = v & PAYLOAD_MASK;
    67→    if (raw & ((uint64_t)1 << 47)) {
    68→        raw |= (uint64_t)0xFFFF000000000000;
    69→    }
⚙ Grep ×2, Read

I need to implement a trampoline pattern. The approach:

1. JIT returns a sentinel for non-self tail calls (instead of calling the helper)

2. All JIT callers loop on the sentinel

Let me check the VM struct:

I'll implement a trampoline pattern. The key insight: encode the argc in the sentinel return value itself, avoiding any VM struct changes.

⚙ Edit

Now let me add the sentinel macros to vm.h and remove the lisa_jit_tail_call_helper declaration (we'll make the trampoline internal):

Edit — lisa/vm.h
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.h",
  "old_string": "/* Helpers called by JIT-compiled code */\nlisa_value lisa_jit_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc);\nlisa_value lisa_jit_tail_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc);",
  "new_string": "/* JIT trampoline sentinel: top 16 bits = 0xDEAD (invalid as any lisa_value tag),\n   low 8 bits = argc for the pending tail call. */\n#define LISA_TAIL_PENDING_BASE ((uint64_t)0xDEAD000000000000)\n#define LISA_TAIL_PENDING(argc) (LISA_TAIL_PENDING_BASE | (uint64_t)(argc))\n#define IS_TAIL_PENDING(v) (((v) >> 48) == 0xDEAD)\n#define TAIL_PENDING_ARGC(v) ((int)((v) & 0xFF))\n\n/* Helpers called by JIT-compiled code */\nlisa_value lisa_jit_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc);"
}
result
The file lisa/vm.h has been updated successfully.

⚙ Read

Four call sites. Let me read the full context around each to understand how to add the trampoline loop. Let me read the call_helper and tail_call_helper functions fully:

Read — lisa/vm.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "offset": 714,
  "limit": 80
}
result
714→lisa_value lisa_jit_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc) {
   715→    lisa_value callee = vm->stack_top[-1 - argc];
   716→    if (!call_value(vm, callee, argc)) {
   717→        return LISA_NIL; /* error already reported */
   718→    }
   719→    /* Check if callee was a native (call_value already handled it) */
   720→    if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {
   721→        return vm->stack_top[-1]; /* result already on stack */
   722→    }
   723→    /* Closure call — dispatch to JIT or interpreter */
   724→    lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
   725→    lisa_value result;
   726→    if (frame->closure->function->jit_code) {
   727→        lisa_jit_fn jit_fn = (lisa_jit_fn)frame->closure->function->jit_code;
   728→        result = jit_fn(vm, frame->closure, frame->slots);
   729→    } else {
   730→        int target_depth = vm->frame_count - 1;
   731→        lisa_run(vm, target_depth);
   732→        result = vm->stack_top[-1];
   733→    }
   734→    /* Pop the callee's frame */
   735→    close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);
   736→    vm->frame_count--;
   737→    vm->stack_top = frame->slots;
   738→    push(vm, result);
   739→    return result;
   740→}
   741→
   742→lisa_value lisa_jit_tail_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc) {
   743→    lisa_value callee = vm->stack_top[-1 - argc];
   744→
   745→    /* Native functions: just call directly */
   746→    if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {
   747→        call_value(vm, callee, argc);
   748→        return vm->stack_top[-1];
   749→    }
   750→
   751→    if (!IS_OBJ(callee) || OBJ_TYPE(callee) != OBJ_CLOSURE) {
   752→        runtime_error(vm, "Can only call functions and closures.");
   753→        return LISA_NIL;
   754→    }
   755→
   756→    lisa_obj_closure *closure = AS_CLOSURE(callee);
   757→    if (argc != closure->function->arity) {
   758→        runtime_error(vm, "Expected %d arguments but got %d.",
   759→                      closure->function->arity, argc);
   760→        return LISA_NIL;
   761→    }
   762→
   763→    /* The JIT caller's frame is the current top frame; reuse it */
   764→    lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
   765→    close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);
   766→
   767→    /* Slide callee + args down over the current frame */
   768→    lisa_value *src = vm->stack_top - argc - 1;
   769→    memmove(frame->slots, src, (size_t)(argc + 1) * sizeof(lisa_value));
   770→    vm->stack_top = frame->slots + argc + 1;
   771→
   772→    frame->closure = closure;
   773→    frame->ip = closure->function->chunk.code;
   774→
   775→    /* Dispatch to JIT or interpreter */
   776→    lisa_value result;
   777→    if (closure->function->jit_code) {
   778→        lisa_jit_fn jit_fn = (lisa_jit_fn)closure->function->jit_code;
   779→        result = jit_fn(vm, frame->closure, frame->slots);
   780→    } else {
   781→        int target_depth = vm->frame_count - 1;
   782→ 
... (truncated)
⚙ Edit

Now I'll implement the trampoline. The approach:

1. JIT returns LISA_TAIL_PENDING(argc) for non-self tail calls (instead of calling the helper)

2. Add a jit_trampoline() function in vm.c that loops on the sentinel

3. All JIT call sites check for the sentinel

Let me start with vm.c changes. First, add the trampoline function and update all call sites:

Edit — lisa/vm.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "old_string": "lisa_value lisa_jit_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc) {",
  "new_string": "/* Handle pending JIT tail calls iteratively (trampoline).\n   Called when a JIT function returns LISA_TAIL_PENDING(argc). */\nstatic lisa_value jit_trampoline(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value result) {\n    while (IS_TAIL_PENDING(result)) {\n        int argc = TAIL_PENDING_ARGC(result);\n        lisa_value callee = vm->stack_top[-1 - argc];\n\n        if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {\n            call_value(vm, callee, argc);\n            return vm->stack_top[-1];\n        }\n\n        if (!IS_OBJ(callee) || OBJ_TYPE(callee) != OBJ_CLOSURE) {\n            runtime_error(vm, \"Can only call functions and closures.\");\n            return LISA_NIL;\n        }\n\n        lisa_obj_closure *closure = AS_CLOSURE(callee);\n        if (argc != closure->function->arity) {\n            runtime_error(vm, \"Expected %d arguments but got %d.\",\n                          closure->function->arity, argc);\n            return LISA_NIL;\n        }\n\n        /* Reuse the current top frame */\n        lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];\n        close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);\n\n        lisa_value *src = vm->stack_top - argc - 1;\n        memmove(frame->slots, src, (size_t)(argc + 1) * sizeof(lisa_value));\n        vm->stack_top = frame->slots + argc + 1;\n\n        frame->closure = closure;\n        frame->ip = closure->function->chunk.code;\n\n        if (closure->function->jit_code) {\n            lisa_jit_fn jit_fn = (lisa_jit_fn)closure->function->jit_code;\n            result = jit_fn(vm, frame->closure, frame->slots);\n            /* If result is TAIL_PENDING, loop continues */\n        } else {\n            int target_depth = vm->frame_count - 1;\n            lisa_run(vm, target_depth);\n            return vm->stack_top[-1];\n        }\n    }\n    return result;\n}\n\nlisa_value lisa_jit_call
... (truncated)
result

757→        }
   758→    }
   759→    return result;
   760→}
   761→
   762→lisa_value lisa_jit_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc) {
   763→    lisa_value callee = vm->stack_top[-1 - argc];
   764→    if (!call_value(vm, callee, argc)) {
   765→        return LISA_NIL; /* error already reported */
   766→    }
   767→    /* Check if callee was a native (call_value already handled it) */
   768→    if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {
   769→        return vm->stack_top[-1]; /* result already on stack */
   770→    }
   771→    /* Closure call — dispatch to JIT or interpreter */
   772→    lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
   773→    lisa_value result;
   774→    if (frame->closure->function->jit_code) {
   775→        lisa_jit_fn jit_fn = (lisa_jit_fn)frame->closure->function->jit_code;
   776→        result = jit_fn(vm, frame->closure, frame->slots);
   777→    } else {
   778→        int target_depth = vm->frame_count - 1;
   779→        lisa_run(vm, target_depth);
   780→        result = vm->stack_top[-1];
   781→    }
   782→    /* Pop the callee's frame */
   783→    close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);
   784→    vm->frame_count--;
   785→    vm->stack_top = frame->slots;
   786→    push(vm, result);
Edit — lisa/vm.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "old_string": "    /* Closure call — dispatch to JIT or interpreter */\n    lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];\n    lisa_value result;\n    if (frame->closure->function->jit_code) {\n        lisa_jit_fn jit_fn = (lisa_jit_fn)frame->closure->function->jit_code;\n        result = jit_fn(vm, frame->closure, frame->slots);\n    } else {\n        int target_depth = vm->frame_count - 1;\n        lisa_run(vm, target_depth);\n        result = vm->stack_top[-1];\n    }\n    /* Pop the callee's frame */\n    close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);\n    vm->frame_count--;\n    vm->stack_top = frame->slots;\n    push(vm, result);\n    return result;\n}",
  "new_string": "    /* Closure call — dispatch to JIT or interpreter */\n    lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];\n    lisa_value result;\n    if (frame->closure->function->jit_code) {\n        lisa_jit_fn jit_fn = (lisa_jit_fn)frame->closure->function->jit_code;\n        result = jit_fn(vm, frame->closure, frame->slots);\n        if (IS_TAIL_PENDING(result))\n            result = jit_trampoline(vm, result);\n    } else {\n        int target_depth = vm->frame_count - 1;\n        lisa_run(vm, target_depth);\n        result = vm->stack_top[-1];\n    }\n    /* Pop the callee's frame */\n    close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);\n    vm->frame_count--;\n    vm->stack_top = frame->slots;\n    push(vm, result);\n    return result;\n}"
}

Edit — lisa/vm.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "old_string": "lisa_value lisa_jit_tail_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc) {\n    lisa_value callee = vm->stack_top[-1 - argc];\n\n    /* Native functions: just call directly */\n    if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {\n        call_value(vm, callee, argc);\n        return vm->stack_top[-1];\n    }\n\n    if (!IS_OBJ(callee) || OBJ_TYPE(callee) != OBJ_CLOSURE) {\n        runtime_error(vm, \"Can only call functions and closures.\");\n        return LISA_NIL;\n    }\n\n    lisa_obj_closure *closure = AS_CLOSURE(callee);\n    if (argc != closure->function->arity) {\n        runtime_error(vm, \"Expected %d arguments but got %d.\",\n                      closure->function->arity, argc);\n        return LISA_NIL;\n    }\n\n    /* The JIT caller's frame is the current top frame; reuse it */\n    lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];\n    close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);\n\n    /* Slide callee + args down over the current frame */\n    lisa_value *src = vm->stack_top - argc - 1;\n    memmove(frame->slots, src, (size_t)(argc + 1) * sizeof(lisa_value));\n    vm->stack_top = frame->slots + argc + 1;\n\n    frame->closure = closure;\n    frame->ip = closure->function->chunk.code;\n\n    /* Dispatch to JIT or interpreter */\n    lisa_value result;\n    if (closure->function->jit_code) {\n        lisa_jit_fn jit_fn = (lisa_jit_fn)closure->function->jit_code;\n        result = jit_fn(vm, frame->closure, frame->slots);\n    } else {\n        int target_depth = vm->frame_count - 1;\n        lisa_run(vm, target_depth);\n        result = vm->stack_top[-1];\n    }\n    return result;\n}\n\n",
  "new_string": "\n"
}
result
