User has approved your plan. You can now start coding. Start with updating your todo list if applicable

Your plan has been saved to: <claude-internal>
You can refer back to it if needed during implementation.

## Approved Plan:
# Add Cross-Platform Builder Functions to `src/builder.h`

## Context

The JIT rewrite (`lisa/jit.c`) is complete and working (~2x speedup), but uses extensive `#ifdef __x86_64__ / __aarch64__` blocks because the cj builder layer lacks several primitives that the JIT needs. Meanwhile, several ARM64 backend functions have encoding bugs (shift-by-immediate never encodes the shift amount, CSET is empty, etc.). The backend is auto-generated from codegen scripts, so fixing those is a separate task.

This plan adds **7 new cross-platform builder functions** to `src/builder.h`. On x86-64, they delegate to working backend functions. On ARM64, they use raw `cj_add_u32()` encoding to work around backend bugs.

## Files Modified

- **`src/builder.h`** — add 7 new functions + internal helpers
- **`tests/test_harness_builder.c`** — add tests for each new function

## New Builder Functions

### 1. `cj_builder_shl(ctx, dst, shift_amount)`
Shift left by immediate. `dst` is modified in place.

- **x86**: `cj_shl(ctx, dst, cj_make_constant(shift))` — backend works correctly
- **ARM64**: raw-encode `UBFM Xd, Xn, #(64-N), #(63-N)` via `cj_add_u32()` (backend has no immediate path for LSL)

ARM64 encoding detail:
```
LSL Xd, Xn, #N  →  UBFM Xd, Xn, #(-N MOD 64), #(63-N)
int immr = (64 - shift) & 63;
int imms = 63 - shift;
uint32_t instr = 0xD3400000 | (rd & 0x1f) | ((rn & 0x1f) << 5)
               | ((imms & 0x3f) << 10) | ((immr & 0x3f) << 16);
// For 32-bit (w-regs): base=0x53000000, size=32 instead of 64
```

### 2. `cj_builder_shr(ctx, dst, shift_amount)`
Logical shift right by immediate. `dst` modified in place.

- **x86**: `cj_shr(ctx, dst, cj_make_constant(shift))` — backend works
- **ARM64**: raw-encode `UBFM Xd, Xn, #N, #63` via `cj_add_u32()` (backend bug: never reads `src.constant`)

ARM64 encoding:
```
LSR Xd, Xn, #N  →  UBFM Xd, Xn, #N, #63
uint32_t instr = 0xD340FC00 | (rd & 0x1f) | ((rn & 0x1f) << 5)
               | ((shift & 0x3f) << 16);
// 0xD340FC00 = sf=1, N=1, immr=0, imms=63 baked in
// For 32-bit: base=0x53007C00, imms=31
```

### 3. `cj_builder_sar(ctx, dst, shift_amount)`
Arithmetic shift right by immediate. `dst` modified in place.

- **x86**: `cj_sar(ctx, dst, cj_make_constant(shift))` — backend works
- **ARM64**: raw-encode `SBFM Xd, Xn, #N, #63` via `cj_add_u32()` (backend bug: same as LSR)

ARM64 encoding:
```
ASR Xd, Xn, #N  →  SBFM Xd, Xn, #N, #63
uint32_t instr = 0x9340FC00 | (rd & 0x1f) | ((rn & 0x1f) << 5)
               | ((shift & 0x3f) << 16);
// For 32-bit: base=0x13007C00
```

### 4. `cj_builder_cset(ctx, dst, cond)`
Set `dst` to 1 if condition is true, 0 otherwise. Reads CPU flags set by a prior `cj_cmp`.

- **x86**: clear `dst` with `xor`, then `SETcc` into 8-bit sub-register + `MOVZX` into full reg
... (truncated)

     7→typedef int (*fn1_t)(int);

    10→static void test_assign_and_add(void) {
    11→  cj_ctx *cj = create_cj_ctx();
    12→  cj_builder_frame frame;
    13→  cj_builder_fn_prologue(cj, 0, &frame);
    14→
    15→  cj_operand arg0 = cj_builder_arg_int(cj, 0);
    16→  cj_operand temp = cj_builder_scratch_reg(0);
    17→  cj_operand five = cj_make_constant(5);
    18→
    19→  cj_builder_assign(cj, temp, five);
    20→  cj_builder_add_assign(cj, temp, arg0);
    21→  cj_builder_return_value(cj, &frame, temp);
    22→
    23→  fn1_t fn = (fn1_t)create_cj_fn(cj);
    24→  assert(fn);
    25→  assert(fn(7) == 12);
    26→  assert(fn(10) == 15);
    27→
    28→  destroy_cj_fn(cj, (cj_fn)fn);
    29→  destroy_cj_ctx(cj);
    30→}
    31→
    32→static void test_scratch_helpers(void) {
    33→  cj_ctx *cj = create_cj_ctx();
    34→  cj_builder_frame frame;
    35→  cj_builder_fn_prologue(cj, 0, &frame);
    36→
    37→  cj_builder_scratch scratch;
    38→  cj_builder_scratch_init(&scratch);
    39→
    40→  cj_operand acc = cj_builder_scratch_acquire(&scratch);
    41→  cj_operand tmp = cj_builder_scratch_acquire(&scratch);
    42→
    43→  cj_builder_assign(cj, acc, cj_builder_arg_int(cj, 0));
    44→  cj_builder_assign(cj, tmp, cj_make_constant(7));
    45→  cj_builder_add_assign(cj, acc, tmp);
    46→
    47→  cj_builder_scratch_release(&scratch); // release tmp
    48→
    49→  cj_operand adjust = cj_builder_scratch_acquire(&scratch);
    50→  cj_builder_assign(cj, adjust, cj_make_constant(3));
    51→  cj_builder_sub_assign(cj, acc, adjust);
    52→
    53→  cj_builder_scratch_release(&scratch); // release adjust
    54→
    55→  cj_builder_return_value(cj, &frame, acc);
    56→  cj_builder_scratch_release(&scratch); // release acc
    57→
    58→  fn1_t fn = (fn1_t)create_cj_fn(cj);
    59→  assert(fn);
    60→  assert(fn(0) == 4);
    61→  assert(fn(5) == 9);
    62→
    63→  destroy_cj_fn(cj, (cj_fn)fn);
    64→  destroy_cj_ctx(cj);
    65→}
    66→
    67→static void test_call_helper(void) {
    68→  cj_ctx *cj = create_cj_ctx();
    69→  cj_label entry = cj_create_label(cj);
    70→  cj_label callee = cj_create_label(cj);
    71→
    72→  // Main function
    73→  cj_mark_label(cj, entry);
    74→  cj_builder_frame main_frame;
    75→  cj_builder_fn_prologue_with_link_save(cj, 0, &main_frame);
    76→  cj_builder_scratch scratch;
    77→  cj_builder_scratch_init(&scratch);
    78→
    79→  cj_operand arg = cj_builder_scratch_acquire(&scratch);
    80→  cj_builder_assign(cj, arg, cj_builder_arg_int(cj, 0));
    81→  cj_builder_add_assign(cj, arg, cj_make_constant(2));
    82→
    83→  cj_operand call_result = cj_builder_call_unary(cj, &scratch, callee, arg);
    84→  cj_builder_return_value(cj, &main_frame, call_result);
    85→  cj_builder_scratch_release(&scratch);
    86→

    38→
    39→#define REG_TMP1     "rax"
    40→#define REG_TMP2     "rcx"
    41→#define REG_TMP3     "rdx"
    42→#define REG_TMP4     "rsi"
    43→#define REG_TMP5     "rdi"
    44→#define REG_CALLADDR "r10"
    45→
    46→#define REG_ARG0     "rdi"
    47→#define REG_ARG1     "rsi"
    48→#define REG_ARG2     "rdx"
    49→#define REG_ARG3     "rcx"
    50→#define REG_RET      "rax"
    51→
    52→#define EMIT_JEQ(ctx, label) cj_jz(ctx, label)
    53→#define EMIT_JNE(ctx, label) cj_jnz(ctx, label)
    54→#define EMIT_JLT(ctx, label) cj_jl(ctx, label)
    55→#define EMIT_JLE(ctx, label) cj_jle(ctx, label)
    56→#define EMIT_JGT(ctx, label) cj_jg(ctx, label)
    57→#define EMIT_JGE(ctx, label) cj_jge(ctx, label)
    58→#define EMIT_JMP(ctx, label) cj_jmp(ctx, label)
    59→#define EMIT_JB(ctx, label)  cj_jb(ctx, label)
    60→
    61→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
    62→
    63→#define REG_VM       "x19"
    64→#define REG_SLOTS    "x20"
    65→#define REG_CLOSURE  "x21"
    66→#define REG_STKTOP   "x22"
    67→#define REG_CONSTS   "x23"
    68→
    69→#define REG_CACHE0   "x10"
    70→#define REG_CACHE1   "x11"
    71→#define REG_CACHE2   "x12"
    72→#define REG_CACHE3   "x13"
    73→
    74→#define REG_TMP1     "x0"
    75→#define REG_TMP2     "x1"
    76→#define REG_TMP3     "x2"
    77→#define REG_TMP4     "x3"
    78→#define REG_TMP5     "x4"
    79→#define REG_CALLADDR "x9"
    80→
    81→#define REG_ARG0     "x0"
    82→#define REG_ARG1     "x1"
    83→#define REG_ARG2     "x2"
    84→#define REG_ARG3     "x3"
    85→#define REG_RET      "x0"

    87→#define EMIT_JEQ(ctx, label) cj_beq(ctx, label)

120→        return;
   121→    }
   122→    uint16_t chunk0 = (uint16_t)(value & 0xFFFF);
   123→    cj_movz(ctx, d, imm(chunk0));
   124→    for (int shift = 16; shift < 64; shift += 16) {
   125→        uint16_t part = (uint16_t)((value >> shift) & 0xFFFF);
   126→        if (!part) continue;
   127→        uint64_t encoded = (uint64_t)part | ((uint64_t)(shift / 16) << 16);
   128→        cj_movk(ctx, d, imm(encoded));
   129→    }
   130→#endif
   131→}
   132→
   133→static void emit_load64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *base, int32_t disp) {
   134→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   135→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));
   136→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   137→    /* ARM64 LDR unsigned offset max = 4095*8 = 32760. */
   138→    if (disp >= 0 && disp <= 32760 && (disp % 8) == 0) {
   139→        cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));
   140→    } else if (disp < 0 && (-disp) <= 4095) {
   141→        /* Small negative offset: SUB then LDR */
   142→        cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), reg(base));
   143→        cj_sub(ctx, reg(dst), imm((uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp)));
   144→        cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(dst, 0));
   145→    } else {
   146→        /* Large offset: load into dst, add base, load */
   147→        if (disp >= 0) {
   148→            emit_load_imm64(ctx, dst, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp);
   149→            cj_add(ctx, reg(dst), reg(base));
   150→        } else {
   151→            cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), reg(base));
   152→            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP4, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp));
   153→            cj_sub(ctx, reg(dst), reg(REG_TMP4));
   154→        }
   155→        cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(dst, 0));
   156→    }
   157→#endif
   158→}
   159→
   160→static void emit_store64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *src, const char *base, int32_t disp) {
   161→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   162→    cj_mov(ctx, mem(base, disp), reg(src));
   163→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   164→    if (disp >= 0 && disp <= 32760 && (disp % 8) == 0) {
   165→        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(base, disp));
   166→    } else if (disp < 0 && (-disp) <= 4095) {
   167→        cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));
   168→        cj_sub(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), imm((uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp)));
   169→        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(REG_TMP4, 0));
   170→    } else {
   171→        if (disp >= 0) {
   172→            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP4, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp);
   173→            cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));
   174→        } else {
   175→            cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));
   176→            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP5, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp));
   177→            cj_sub(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(REG_TMP5));
   178→        }
   179→        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(REG_TMP4, 0));
   180→    }
   181→#endif
   182→}
   183→
   184→static void emit_call_abs(cj_ctx *ctx, void *fn_ptr) {
   185→    emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_CALLADDR,
... (truncated)

319→#endif
   320→
   321→/* ===== Register Cache ===== */
   322→
   323→#define MAX_CACHE 4
   324→
   325→typedef struct {
   326→    int depth;
   327→    const char *regs[MAX_CACHE];
   328→} reg_cache_t;
   329→
   330→static void cache_init(reg_cache_t *cache) {
   331→    cache->depth = 0;
   332→    cache->regs[0] = REG_CACHE0;
   333→    cache->regs[1] = REG_CACHE1;
   334→    cache->regs[2] = REG_CACHE2;
   335→    cache->regs[3] = REG_CACHE3;
   336→}
   337→
   338→static void cache_flush(cj_ctx *ctx, reg_cache_t *cache) {
   339→    for (int i = 0; i < cache->depth; i++)
   340→        emit_store64(ctx, cache->regs[i], REG_STKTOP, i * 8);
   341→    if (cache->depth > 0)
   342→        cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_STKTOP), imm((uint64_t)cache->depth * 8));
   343→    cache->depth = 0;
   344→}
   345→
   346→/* Flush all entries except the top `keep` entries.
   347→   Shifts kept entries down to regs[0..keep-1]. */
   348→static void cache_flush_to(cj_ctx *ctx, reg_cache_t *cache, int keep) {
   349→    if (keep >= cache->depth) return;
   350→    int n = cache->depth - keep;
   351→    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
   352→        emit_store64(ctx, cache->regs[i], REG_STKTOP, i * 8);
   353→    if (n > 0)
   354→        cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_STKTOP), imm((uint64_t)n * 8));
   355→    for (int i = 0; i < keep; i++)
   356→        cj_mov(ctx, reg(cache->regs[i]), reg(cache->regs[n + i]));
   357→    cache->depth = keep;
   358→}
   359→
   360→static void cache_push(cj_ctx *ctx, reg_cache_t *cache, const char *src) {
   361→    if (cache->depth >= MAX_CACHE)
   362→        cache_flush(ctx, cache);
   363→    if (strcmp(src, cache->regs[cache->depth]) != 0)
   364→        cj_mov(ctx, reg(cache->regs[cache->depth]), reg(src));
   365→    cache->depth++;
   366→}
   367→
   368→/* Pop top value. Returns register name holding the value.
   369→   If cache empty, loads from memory stack into REG_TMP1. */
   370→static const char *cache_pop(cj_ctx *ctx, reg_cache_t *cache) {
   371→    if (cache->depth > 0) {
   372→        cache->depth--;
   373→        return cache->regs[cache->depth];
   374→    }
   375→    cj_sub(ctx, reg(REG_STKTOP), imm(8));
   376→    emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_STKTOP, 0);
   377→    return REG_TMP1;
   378→}
   379→
   380→/* ===== Prologue / Epilogue ===== */
   381→
   382→static void emit_prologue(cj_ctx *ctx) {
   383→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   384→    cj_push(ctx, reg("rbp"));
   385→    cj_mov(ctx, reg("rbp"), reg("rsp"));
   386→    cj_push(ctx, reg("rbx"));
   387→    cj_push(ctx, reg("r12"));
   388→    cj_push(ctx, reg("r13"));
   389→    cj_push(ctx, reg("r14"));
   390→    cj_push(ctx, reg("r15"));
   391→    cj_sub(ctx, reg("rsp"), imm(8)); /* 16-byte alignment */
   392→
   393→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_VM), reg("rdi"));
   394→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_CLOSURE), reg("rsi"));
   395→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_SLOTS), reg("rdx"));
   396→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   397→    /*
... (truncated)

result
518→
   519→/* ===== Inline Fast-Path Generators ===== */
   520→
   521→typedef enum { ARITH_ADD, ARITH_SUB, ARITH_MUL } arith_op;
   522→
   523→static void emit_arith_compute(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *src, arith_op op) {
   524→    switch (op) {
   525→    case ARITH_ADD: cj_add(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src)); break;
   526→    case ARITH_SUB: cj_sub(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src)); break;
   527→    case ARITH_MUL:
   528→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   529→        cj_imul(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src));
   530→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   531→        cj_mul(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src));
   532→#endif
   533→        break;
   534→    }
   535→}
   536→
   537→/* Emit inline integer fast path for ADD/SUB/MUL.
   538→   Expects cache->depth >= 2 and cache already flushed to depth 2.
   539→   After this, cache->depth = 1, result in cache->regs[0]. */
   540→static void emit_binop_int_fast(cj_ctx *ctx, reg_cache_t *cache,
   541→                                 arith_op op, void *slow_fn) {
   542→    const char *a_reg = cache->regs[0];
   543→    const char *b_reg = cache->regs[1];
   544→
   545→    cj_label slow = cj_create_label(ctx);
   546→    cj_label done = cj_create_label(ctx);
   547→
   548→    /* Type-check both operands (non-destructive: only REG_TMP1/TMP2 clobbered) */
   549→    emit_int_type_check(ctx, a_reg, slow);
   550→    emit_int_type_check(ctx, b_reg, slow);
   551→
   552→    /* Fast path: extract payloads, compute, mask, retag */
   553→    emit_mask48(ctx, a_reg);
   554→    emit_mask48(ctx, b_reg);
   555→    emit_arith_compute(ctx, a_reg, b_reg, op);
   556→    emit_mask48(ctx, a_reg);
   557→    emit_retag_int(ctx, a_reg);
   558→
   559→    EMIT_JMP(ctx, done);
   560→
   561→    cj_mark_label(ctx, slow);
   562→    /* a and b are unchanged (type checks non-destructive). */
   563→    emit_sync_stack_top(ctx);
   564→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG2), reg(b_reg));
   565→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG1), reg(a_reg));
   566→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG0), reg(REG_VM));
   567→    emit_call_abs(ctx, slow_fn);
   568→    emit_reload_stack_top(ctx);
   569→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(cache->regs[0]), reg(REG_RET));
   570→
   571→    cj_mark_label(ctx, done);
   572→    cache->depth = 1;
   573→}
   574→
   575→/* Emit inline integer fast path for comparison ops (LT/LE/GT/GE).
   576→   Uses signed comparison of shifted payloads.
   577→   After this, cache->depth = 1, result in cache->regs[0]. */
   578→static void emit_cmpop_int_fast(cj_ctx *ctx, reg_cache_t *cache,
   579→                                 cmp_kind kind, void *slow_fn) {
   580→    const char *a_reg = cache->regs[0];
   581→    const char *b_reg = cache->regs[1];
   582→
   583→    cj_label slow = cj_create_label(ctx);
   584→    cj_label done = cj_create_label(ctx);
   585→
   586→    emit_int_type_check(ctx, a_reg, slow);
   587→    emit_int_type_check(ctx, b_reg, slow);
   588→
   589→    /* Shift left by 16 to align sign bit at bit 63
... (truncated)

718→
   719→    int i = 0;
   720→    while (i < len) {
   721→        uint8_t op = chunk->code[i];
   722→        switch (op) {
   723→        case OP_JUMP:
   724→        case OP_JUMP_IF_FALSE: {
   725→            uint8_t lo = chunk->code[i + 1];
   726→            uint8_t hi = chunk->code[i + 2];
   727→            uint16_t offset = (uint16_t)(lo | (hi << 8));
   728→            int target = i + 3 + offset;
   729→            if (target >= 0 && target < len)
   730→                map->is_target[target] = true;
   731→            i += 3;
   732→            break;
   733→        }
   734→        case OP_LOOP: {
   735→            uint8_t lo = chunk->code[i + 1];
   736→            uint8_t hi = chunk->code[i + 2];
   737→            uint16_t offset = (uint16_t)(lo | (hi << 8));
   738→            int target = i + 3 - offset;
   739→            if (target >= 0 && target < len)
   740→                map->is_target[target] = true;
   741→            i += 3;
   742→            break;
   743→        }
   744→        case OP_CLOSURE: {
   745→            uint8_t fn_idx = chunk->code[i + 1];
   746→            lisa_obj_function *cfn = AS_FUNCTION(chunk->constants.values[fn_idx]);
   747→            i += 2 + cfn->upvalue_count * 2;
   748→            break;
   749→        }
   750→        case OP_CONSTANT: case OP_GET_LOCAL: case OP_SET_LOCAL:
   751→        case OP_GET_UPVALUE: case OP_SET_UPVALUE:
   752→        case OP_GET_GLOBAL: case OP_DEF_GLOBAL:
   753→        case OP_CALL: case OP_TAIL_CALL:
   754→        case OP_LIST: case OP_PRINTLN:
   755→            i += 2;
   756→            break;
   757→        default:
   758→            i += 1;
   759→            break;
   760→        }
   761→    }
   762→
   763→    for (i = 0; i < len; i++) {
   764→        if (map->is_target[i])
   765→            map->labels[i] = cj_create_label(ctx);
   766→    }
   767→}
   768→
   769→static void free_label_map(label_map *map) {
   770→    free(map->labels);
   771→    free(map->is_target);
   772→}
   773→
   774→/* ===== Main JIT Compilation ===== */
   775→
   776→bool lisa_jit_compile(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_function *fn) {
   777→    (void)vm;
   778→
   779→    if (fn->jit_code) return true;
   780→
   781→    cj_ctx *ctx = create_cj_ctx();
   782→    if (!ctx) return false;
   783→
   784→    lisa_chunk *chunk = &fn->chunk;
   785→
   786→    label_map map;
   787→    scan_branch_targets(chunk, &map, ctx);
   788→
   789→    cj_label entry_label = cj_create_label(ctx);
   790→
   791→    cj_mark_label(ctx, entry_label);
   792→    emit_prologue(ctx);
   793→
   794→    /* body_label: target for self-tail-call loop (after prologue) */
   795→    cj_label body_label = cj_create_label(ctx);
   796→    cj_mark_label(ctx, body_label);
   797→
   798→    reg_cache_t cache;
   799→    cache_init(&cache);
   800→
   801→    int i = 0;
   802→    while (i < chunk->count) {
   803→        /* At branch targets, ensure cache is empty */
   804→        if (map.is_t

918→            i += 2;

   920→        }
   921→
   922→        /* --- Arithmetic with inline int fast paths --- */
   923→
   924→        case OP_ADD:
   925→            emit_binop(ctx, &cache, ARITH_ADD, (void *)lisa_jit_add);
   926→            i += 1;
   927→            break;
   928→
   929→        case OP_SUB:
   930→            emit_binop(ctx, &cache, ARITH_SUB, (void *)lisa_jit_sub);
   931→            i += 1;
   932→            break;
   933→
   934→        case OP_MUL:
   935→            emit_binop(ctx, &cache, ARITH_MUL, (void *)lisa_jit_mul);
   936→            i += 1;
   937→            break;
   938→
   939→        case OP_DIV: {
   940→            /* Always use helper (produces doubles / edge cases) */
   941→            cache_flush(ctx, &cache);
   942→            emit_pop(ctx, REG_TMP3);
   943→            emit_pop(ctx, REG_TMP2);
   944→            emit_sync_stack_top(ctx);
   945→            cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG2), reg(REG_TMP3));
   946→            cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG1), reg(REG_TMP2));
   947→            cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG0), reg(REG_VM));
   948→            emit_call_abs(ctx, (void *)lisa_jit_div);
   949→            emit_reload_stack_top(ctx);
   950→            cache_push(ctx, &cache, REG_RET);
   951→            i += 1;
   952→            break;
   953→        }
   954→
   955→        case OP_MOD: {
   956→            cache_flush(ctx, &cache);
   957→            emit_pop(ctx, REG_TMP3);
   958→            emit_pop(ctx, REG_TMP2);
   959→            emit_sync_stack_top(ctx);
   960→            cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG2), reg(REG_TMP3));
   961→            cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG1), reg(REG_TMP2));
   962→            cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG0), reg(REG_VM));
   963→            emit_call_abs(ctx, (void *)lisa_jit_mod);
   964→            emit_reload_stack_top(ctx);
   965→            cache_push(ctx, &cache, REG_RET);
   966→            i += 1;
   967→            break;
   968→        }
   969→
   970→        case OP_NEGATE: {
   971→            if (cache.depth >= 1) {

   984→                cj_neg(ctx, reg(a_reg), reg(a_reg));
   985→#endif
   986→                emit_mask48(ctx, a_reg);
   987→                emit_retag_int(ctx, a_reg);
   988→
   989→                EMIT_JMP(ctx, done);
   990→
   991→                cj_mark_label(ctx, slow);
   992→                emit_sync_stack_top(ctx);

  1121→
  1122→        /* --- Function ops --- */
  1123→
  1124→        case OP_CLOSURE: {
  1125→            uint8_t fn_idx = chunk->code[i + 1];
  1126→            lisa_obj_function *closure_fn = AS_FUNCTION(chunk->constants.values[fn_idx]);
  1127→            int uv_count = closure_fn->upvalue_count;
  1128→            uint8_t *uv_ip = &chunk->code[i + 2];
  1129→
  1130→            cache_flush(ctx, &cache);
  1131→            emit_sync_stack_top(ctx);
  1132→            cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG0), reg(REG_VM));
  1133→            cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_ARG1), reg(REG_CLOSURE));
  1134→            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_ARG2, (uint64_t)(uintptr_t)closure_fn);
  1135→            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_ARG3, (uint64_t)(uintptr_t)uv_ip);
  1136→            emit_call_abs(ctx, (void *)lisa_jit_make_closure);
  1137→            emit_reload_stack_top(ctx);
  1138→            cache_push(ctx, &cache, REG_RET);
  1139→
  1140→            i += 2 + uv_count * 2;
  1141→            break;
  1142→        }
  1143→
  1144→        case OP_CALL: {
  1145→            int argc = chunk->code[i + 1];
  1146→            cache_flush(ctx, &cache);
  1147→            emit_call_vm_int(ctx, (void *)lisa_jit_call_helper, argc);
  1148→            /* Result already pushed to memory stack by helper */
  1149→            i += 2;
  1150→            break;
  1151→        }
  1152→
  1153→        case OP_TAIL_CALL: {
  1154→            int argc = chunk->code[i + 1];
  1155→            cache_flush(ctx, &cache);
  1156→            emit_sync_stack_top(ctx);
  1157→
  1158→            /* Runtime self-call check: compare callee with current closure.
  1159→               Callee on stack is NaN-boxed (QNAN|TAG_OBJ|ptr), but REG_CLOSURE
  1160→               is a raw pointer. NaN-box REG_CLOSURE into TMP2 for comparison. */
  1161→            cj_label not_self = cj_create_label(ctx);
  1162→            int32_t callee_off = (int32_t)(-8 * (argc + 1));
  1163→            emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_STKTOP, callee_off);
  1164→            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP2, QNAN | TAG_OBJ);
  1165→            emit_or(ctx, REG_TMP2, REG_CLOSURE);
  1166→            cj_cmp(ctx, reg(REG_TMP1), reg(REG_TMP2));
  1167→            EMIT_JNE(ctx, not_self);
  1168→
  1169→            /* Self-call: move args to slots, reset stack, jump to body */
  1170→            for (int a = 0; a < argc; a++) {
  1171→                int32_t src_off = (int32_t)(-8 * (argc - a));
  1172→                emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_STKTOP, src_off);
  1173→                emit_store64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_SLOTS, (int32_t)((1 + a) * 8));
  1174→            }
  1175→            cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_STKTOP), reg(REG_SLOTS));
  1176→            cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_STKTOP), imm((uint64_t)(argc + 1) * 8));
  1177→            emit_sync_stack_top(ctx);
  1178→            EMIT_JMP(ctx, body_label);
  1
... (truncated)

TARGET=libcj.so
BUILDDIR=bin/
PREFIX=/usr/local/lib/
SOURCES=$(wildcard src/*.c src/arch/*.c src/arch/*/*.c)
override CFLAGS+=-std=c11 -O2 -Wno-gnu

Read — cj/Makefile
{
  "file_path": "cj/Makefile"
}
result
1→TARGET=libcj.so
     2→BUILDDIR=bin/
     3→PREFIX=/usr/local/lib/
     4→SOURCES=$(wildcard src/*.c src/arch/*.c src/arch/*/*.c)
     5→override CFLAGS+=-std=c11 -O2 -Wno-gnu
     6→LDFLAGS+=-shared
     7→DEVFLAGS=-Werror -Wall -g -fPIC -DNDEBUG -Wfloat-equal -Wundef -Wwrite-strings -Wuninitialized -pedantic -O0
     8→
     9→.PHONY: all codegen dev example demo clean install uninstall
    10→
    11→all:
    12→	mkdir -p $(BUILDDIR)
    13→	$(CC) $(SOURCES) -o $(BUILDDIR)$(TARGET) $(CFLAGS) $(LDFLAGS)
    14→
    15→codegen: codegen_x86 codegen_arm64
    16→
    17→codegen_x86:
    18→	@echo "Generating x86-64 backend..."
    19→	node codegen/x86_encoder.js
    20→
    21→codegen_arm64:
    22→	@echo "Generating ARM64 backend..."
    23→	node codegen/arm64_encoder.js
    24→
    25→dev:
    26→	mkdir -p $(BUILDDIR)
    27→	$(CC) $(SOURCES) -o $(BUILDDIR)$(TARGET) $(CFLAGS) $(DEVFLAGS) $(LDFLAGS)
    28→
    29→install: all
    30→	install $(BUILDDIR)$(TARGET) $(PREFIX)$(TARGET)
    31→
    32→uninstall:
    33→	rm -rf $(PREFIX)$(TARGET)
    34→
    35→clean:
    36→	rm -rf $(BUILDDIR)
    37→

{
  "command": "tail -20 cj/src/op.h",

result
#define CJ_REG(NAME)                                                                               \
  static const cj_operand cj_##NAME = {                                                            \
      .type = CJ_REGISTER,                                                                         \
      .mask = NULL,                                                                                \
      .zero_mask = 0,                                                                              \
      .sae = 0,                                                                                    \
      .rounding = CJ_ROUND_DEFAULT,                                                                \
      .reg = #NAME,                                                                                \
      .shift = {.kind = CJ_SHIFT_KIND_NONE, .amount = 0, .has_amount = 0},                         \
      .extend = {.kind = CJ_EXTEND_KIND_NONE, .amount = 0, .has_amount = 0},                       \
  }

#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
#include "arch/x86_64/backend.h"
#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
#include "arch/arm64/backend.h"
#else
#error "Unsupported architecture"
#endif
⚙ Read

The backend is included at the bottom of op.h. So builder.h → op.h → backend.h. That means arm64_parse_reg and arm64_is_64bit are available in builder.h.

Now I have everything I need. Let me implement the builder functions. I'll add them at the end of builder.h, before the closing line.

  "file_path": "cj/src/builder.h",
  "offset": 620,
  "limit": 15
}

   621→  }
   622→

   624→    cj_operand lr = cj_make_register("x30");
   625→    cj_operand slot = cj_make_memory("sp", NULL, 1, (int32_t)lr_offset);
   626→    cj_str(ctx, lr, slot);
   627→  }
   628→#else
   629→  (void)save_lr;
   630→#endif

⚙ Edit

Edit — cj/src/builder.h
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "cj/src/builder.h",
  "old_string": "  if (save_lr) {\n    cj_operand lr = cj_make_register(\"x30\");\n    cj_operand slot = cj_make_memory(\"sp\", NULL, 1, (int32_t)lr_offset);\n    cj_str(ctx, lr, slot);\n  }\n#else\n  (void)save_lr;\n#endif\n}",
  "new_string": "  if (save_lr) {\n    cj_operand lr = cj_make_register(\"x30\");\n    cj_operand slot = cj_make_memory(\"sp\", NULL, 1, (int32_t)lr_offset);\n    cj_str(ctx, lr, slot);\n  }\n#else\n  (void)save_lr;\n#endif\n}\n\n/* ===== Cross-Platform Bitwise and Arithmetic Operations ===== */\n\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n\nstatic inline const char *builder_x86_reg8(const char *name) {\n  if (!name) return \"al\";\n  if (strcmp(name, \"rax\") == 0 || strcmp(name, \"eax\") == 0) return \"al\";\n  if (strcmp(name, \"rbx\") == 0 || strcmp(name, \"ebx\") == 0) return \"bl\";\n  if (strcmp(name, \"rcx\") == 0 || strcmp(name, \"ecx\") == 0) return \"cl\";\n  if (strcmp(name, \"rdx\") == 0 || strcmp(name, \"edx\") == 0) return \"dl\";\n  if (strcmp(name, \"rsi\") == 0 || strcmp(name, \"esi\") == 0) return \"sil\";\n  if (strcmp(name, \"rdi\") == 0 || strcmp(name, \"edi\") == 0) return \"dil\";\n  if (strcmp(name, \"r8\") == 0 || strcmp(name, \"r8d\") == 0) return \"r8b\";\n  if (strcmp(name, \"r9\") == 0 || strcmp(name, \"r9d\") == 0) return \"r9b\";\n  if (strcmp(name, \"r10\") == 0 || strcmp(name, \"r10d\") == 0) return \"r10b\";\n  if (strcmp(name, \"r11\") == 0 || strcmp(name, \"r11d\") == 0) return \"r11b\";\n  if (strcmp(name, \"r12\") == 0 || strcmp(name, \"r12d\") == 0) return \"r12b\";\n  if (strcmp(name, \"r13\") == 0 || strcmp(name, \"r13d\") == 0) return \"r13b\";\n  if (strcmp(name, \"r14\") == 0 || strcmp(name, \"r14d\") == 0) return \"r14b\";\n  if (strcmp(name, \"r15\") == 0 || strcmp(name, \"r15d\") == 0) return \"r15b\";\n  return \"al\";\n}\n\nstatic inline void builder_x86_setcc(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_operand r8, cj_condition cond) {\n  switch (cond) {\n

⚙ Bash ×2

Those are all pre-existing backend warnings, not from my code. The existing tests must suppress them. Let me compile without -Werror first to verify no new errors, then use the same pragmas the JIT uses.

⚙ Grep

Existing tests pass. Now let me also check that cj_builder_cset works correctly — the x86 implementation needs care with flag ordering. Let me re-read what I wrote.

Grep — cj_builder_cset
{
  "pattern": "cj_builder_cset",
  "path": "cj/src/builder.h",
  "output_mode": "content",
  "-n": true,
  "-A": 12
}
result
78:static inline void cj_builder_cset(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_condition cond);
79-static inline void cj_builder_or(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_operand src);
80-static inline void cj_builder_neg(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_operand dst);
81-static inline void cj_builder_mul(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_operand src);
82-static inline cj_operand cj_builder_zero_operand(void);
83-static inline void cj_builder_clear(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_operand dst);
84-static inline cj_operand cj_builder_scratch_reg(unsigned index);
85-static inline unsigned cj_builder_scratch_capacity(void);
86-static inline void cj_builder_scratch_init(cj_builder_scratch *scratch);
87-static inline cj_operand cj_builder_scratch_acquire(cj_builder_scratch *scratch);
88-static inline void cj_builder_scratch_release(cj_builder_scratch *scratch);
89-static inline unsigned cj_builder_arg_int_capacity(void);
90-static inline void cj_builder_call_label(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_label target);
--
769:static inline void cj_builder_cset(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_condition cond) {
770-  if (!ctx) return;
771-#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
772-  /* Zero the full register first, then SETcc into 8-bit sub-register.
773-     XOR zeroes flags but SETcc reads flags from the prior CMP, and
774-     XOR sets ZF=1,SF=0,OF=0,CF=0 — so we must do SETcc BEFORE XOR.
775-     Instead: use MOV reg, 0 which doesn't affect flags. */
776-  cj_mov(ctx, dst, cj_make_constant(0));
777-  const char *r8name = builder_x86_reg8(dst.reg);
778-  cj_operand r8 = cj_make_register(r8name);
779-  builder_x86_setcc(ctx, r8, cond);
780-#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
781-  /* CSET Xd, cond  →  CSINC Xd, XZR, XZR, invert(cond) */
⚙ Edit, Bash

Actually, the x86 cset implementation can be simpler and more correct. Let me fix the ordering — do SETcc first (reads flags), then MOVZX (doesn't affect flags, zero-extends).

Edit — cj/src/builder.h
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "cj/src/builder.h",
  "old_string": "static inline void cj_builder_cset(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_condition cond) {\n  if (!ctx) return;\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n  /* Zero the full register first, then SETcc into 8-bit sub-register.\n     XOR zeroes flags but SETcc reads flags from the prior CMP, and\n     XOR sets ZF=1,SF=0,OF=0,CF=0 — so we must do SETcc BEFORE XOR.\n     Instead: use MOV reg, 0 which doesn't affect flags. */\n  cj_mov(ctx, dst, cj_make_constant(0));\n  const char *r8name = builder_x86_reg8(dst.reg);\n  cj_operand r8 = cj_make_register(r8name);\n  builder_x86_setcc(ctx, r8, cond);",
  "new_string": "static inline void cj_builder_cset(cj_ctx *ctx, cj_operand dst, cj_condition cond) {\n  if (!ctx) return;\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n  /* SETcc into 8-bit sub-register (reads flags), then MOVZX to zero-extend.\n     MOVZX doesn't affect flags, so this is safe after any CMP. */\n  const char *r8name = builder_x86_reg8(dst.reg);\n  cj_operand r8 = cj_make_register(r8name);\n  builder_x86_setcc(ctx, r8, cond);\n  cj_movzx(ctx, dst, r8);"
}
result
The file cj/src/builder.h has been updated successfully.

  "description": "Compile and run builder tests"
}

⚙ Edit

Now let me integrate the builder functions into lisa/jit.c. I need to:

1. Add #include "builder.h" to jit.c

2. Replace emit_lsr_imm with cj_builder_shr

3. Replace emit_lsl_imm with cj_builder_shl

4. Replace emit_mask48 with cj_builder_shl + cj_builder_shr

5. Replace emit_sign_extend48 with cj_builder_shl + cj_builder_sar

6. Replace emit_or with cj_builder_or

7. Replace the ARM64 CSET section + emit_bool_from_flags with cj_builder_cset

8. Replace the MUL #ifdef in emit_arith_compute with cj_builder_mul

9. Replace the NEG #ifdef in OP_NEGATE with cj_builder_neg

Let me start with the includes and work through systematically.

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "#include \"ctx.h\"\n#include \"op.h\"\n#include \"register.h\"\n#pragma GCC diagnostic pop",
  "new_string": "#include \"ctx.h\"\n#include \"op.h\"\n#include \"register.h\"\n#include \"builder.h\"\n#pragma GCC diagnostic pop"
}
result
The file lisa/jit.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Now replace emit_lsr_imm — it takes (ctx, dst, src, shift) where dst and src are strings. The new builder takes (ctx, dst_operand, shift) and modifies in place. So if dst != src, we still need a mov first. Let me replace the function body:

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "/* Logical shift right: dst = src >> shift (zero-extend) */\nstatic void emit_lsr_imm(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *src, int shift) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    if (strcmp(dst, src) != 0)\n        cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src));\n    cj_shr(ctx, reg(dst), imm((uint64_t)shift));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    /* UBFM Xd, Xn, #shift, #63 */\n    int rd = arm64_parse_reg(dst);\n    int rn = arm64_parse_reg(src);\n    uint32_t instr = 0xD340FC00\n        | ((uint32_t)(shift & 0x3f) << 16)\n        | ((uint32_t)(rn & 0x1f) << 5)\n        | (uint32_t)(rd & 0x1f);\n    cj_add_u32(ctx, instr);\n#endif\n}",
  "new_string": "/* Logical shift right: dst = src >> shift (zero-extend) */\nstatic void emit_lsr_imm(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *src, int shift) {\n    if (strcmp(dst, src) != 0)\n        cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src));\n    cj_builder_shr(ctx, reg(dst), shift);\n}"
}
result
The file lisa/jit.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

The LSP diagnostics are just from include path differences — the actual build system handles this. Let me continue replacing emit_lsl_imm:

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "/* Logical shift left: dst = src << shift */\nstatic void emit_lsl_imm(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *src, int shift) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    if (strcmp(dst, src) != 0)\n        cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src));\n    cj_shl(ctx, reg(dst), imm((uint64_t)shift));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    /* UBFM Xd, Xn, #(-shift mod 64), #(63-shift) */\n    int rd = arm64_parse_reg(dst);\n    int rn = arm64_parse_reg(src);\n    int immr = (-shift) & 63;\n    int imms = 63 - shift;\n    uint32_t instr = 0xD3400000\n        | ((uint32_t)(immr & 0x3f) << 16)\n        | ((uint32_t)(imms & 0x3f) << 10)\n        | ((uint32_t)(rn & 0x1f) << 5)\n        | (uint32_t)(rd & 0x1f);\n    cj_add_u32(ctx, instr);\n#endif\n}",
  "new_string": "/* Logical shift left: dst = src << shift */\nstatic void emit_lsl_imm(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *src, int shift) {\n    if (strcmp(dst, src) != 0)\n        cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src));\n    cj_builder_shl(ctx, reg(dst), shift);\n}"
}
result
The file lisa/jit.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Now replace emit_mask48:

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "/* Clear top 16 bits: r &= 0x0000FFFFFFFFFFFF (unsigned 48-bit payload) */\nstatic void emit_mask48(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *r) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    cj_shl(ctx, reg(r), imm(16));\n    cj_shr(ctx, reg(r), imm(16));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    /* UBFM Xd, Xd, #0, #47 = extract bits 47:0, zero-extend */\n    int rd = arm64_parse_reg(r);\n    uint32_t instr = 0xD340BC00\n        | ((uint32_t)(rd & 0x1f) << 5)\n        | (uint32_t)(rd & 0x1f);\n    cj_add_u32(ctx, instr);\n#endif\n}",
  "new_string": "/* Clear top 16 bits: r &= 0x0000FFFFFFFFFFFF (unsigned 48-bit payload) */\nstatic void emit_mask48(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *r) {\n    cj_builder_shl(ctx, reg(r), 16);\n    cj_builder_shr(ctx, reg(r), 16);\n}"
}
result
The file lisa/jit.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Replace emit_sign_extend48:

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "/* Sign-extend from bit 47: r = sign_extend_48(r) */\nstatic void emit_sign_extend48(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *r) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    cj_shl(ctx, reg(r), imm(16));\n    cj_sar(ctx, reg(r), imm(16));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    /* SBFM Xd, Xd, #0, #47 */\n    int rd = arm64_parse_reg(r);\n    uint32_t instr = 0x9340BC00\n        | ((uint32_t)(rd & 0x1f) << 5)\n        | (uint32_t)(rd & 0x1f);\n    cj_add_u32(ctx, instr);\n#endif\n}",
  "new_string": "/* Sign-extend from bit 47: r = sign_extend_48(r) */\nstatic void emit_sign_extend48(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *r) {\n    cj_builder_shl(ctx, reg(r), 16);\n    cj_builder_sar(ctx, reg(r), 16);\n}"
}
result
The file lisa/jit.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Replace emit_or:

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "/* OR dst |= src */\nstatic void emit_or(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *src) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    cj_or(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src));\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    cj_orr(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src));\n#endif\n}",
  "new_string": "/* OR dst |= src */\nstatic void emit_or(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *src) {\n    cj_builder_or(ctx, reg(dst), reg(src));\n}"
}

⚙ Edit

Now replace the ARM64 CSET helper and emit_bool_from_flags. The ARM64-specific CSET section (defines + function) can be removed entirely, and emit_bool_from_flags can be rewritten to use cj_builder_cset + cj_builder_or:

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "/* ===== ARM64 CSET Helper ===== */\n\n#if defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n/* CSINC Xd, XZR, XZR, invert_cond → Xd = (cond) ? 1 : 0 */\n#define ARM64_COND_EQ 0x0\n#define ARM64_COND_NE 0x1\n#define ARM64_COND_LO 0x3\n#define ARM64_COND_GE 0xA\n#define ARM64_COND_LT 0xB\n#define ARM64_COND_GT 0xC\n#define ARM64_COND_LE 0xD\n\nstatic void emit_cset(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, int invert_cond) {\n    int rd = arm64_parse_reg(dst);\n    /* CSINC Xd, XZR, XZR, cond: 1 00 11010100 11111 cond 01 11111 Rd */\n    uint32_t instr = 0x9A9F07E0\n        | ((uint32_t)(invert_cond & 0xf) << 12)\n        | (uint32_t)(rd & 0x1f);\n    cj_add_u32(ctx, instr);\n}\n#endif",
  "new_string": "/* ARM64 CSET defines removed — now using cj_builder_cset from builder.h */"
}
result
The file lisa/jit.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Now replace emit_bool_from_flags. I need to map cmp_kind to cj_condition:

Edit — lisa/jit.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "old_string": "static void emit_bool_from_flags(cj_ctx *ctx, cmp_kind kind) {\n#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n    /* MOV doesn't affect flags, so we can load LISA_FALSE first */\n    emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP1, LISA_FALSE);\n    switch (kind) {\n    case CMP_LT: cj_setl(ctx, reg(\"al\")); break;\n    case CMP_LE: cj_setle(ctx, reg(\"al\")); break;\n    case CMP_GT: cj_setg(ctx, reg(\"al\")); break;\n    case CMP_GE: cj_setge(ctx, reg(\"al\")); break;\n    case CMP_EQ: cj_setz(ctx, reg(\"al\")); break;\n    case CMP_NE: cj_setnz(ctx, reg(\"al\")); break;\n    }\n    /* rax = LISA_FALSE | 0/1 = LISA_FALSE or LISA_TRUE */\n#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n    /* CSET first (reads flags), then load constant (doesn't affect flags) */\n    int invert;\n    switch (kind) {\n    case CMP_LT: invert = ARM64_COND_GE; break;\n    case CMP_LE: invert = ARM64_COND_GT; break;\n    case CMP_GT: invert = ARM64_COND_LE; break;\n    case CMP_GE: invert = ARM64_COND_LT; break;\n    case CMP_EQ: invert = ARM64_COND_NE; break;\n    case CMP_NE: invert = ARM64_COND_EQ; break;\n    }\n    emit_cset(ctx, REG_TMP1, invert); /* TMP1 = 0 or 1 */\n    emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP2, LISA_FALSE);\n    emit_or(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_TMP2); /* TMP1 = LISA_FALSE | 0/1 */\n#endif\n}",
  "new_string": "static void emit_bool_from_flags(cj_ctx *ctx, cmp_kind kind) {\n    /* Map cmp_kind to cj_condition */\n    cj_condition cond;\n    switch (kind) {\n    case CMP_LT: cond = CJ_COND_L;  break;\n    case CMP_LE: cond = CJ_COND_LE; break;\n    case CMP_GT: cond = CJ_COND_G;  break;\n    case CMP_GE: cond = CJ_COND_GE; break;\n    case CMP_EQ: cond = CJ_COND_Z;  break;\n    case CMP_NE: cond = CJ_COND_NZ; break;\n    }\n    /* CSET: TMP1 = 0 or 1 from flags (reads flags, then MOVZX/CSINC) */\n    cj_builder_cset(ctx, reg(REG_TMP1), cond);\n    /* OR with LISA_FALSE to produce LISA_FALSE or LISA_TRUE */\n    emit_load_imm64
... (truncated)

⚙ Read

Let me check which remaining #ifdef blocks are still in jit.c. Lines 27-96 are the register definitions and EMIT_J* macros — those stay (they define platform-specific register names). Let me check what's on lines 115, 135, 162, 187 (those are the low-level emit helpers), and 317+, 357+, 382+, 397+.

Read — lisa/jit.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "offset": 110,
  "limit": 100
}
result
110→}
   111→
   112→/* ===== Low-level Emit Helpers ===== */
   113→
   114→static void emit_load_imm64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, uint64_t value) {
   115→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   116→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), imm(value));
   117→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   118→    cj_operand d = reg(dst);
   119→    if (value == 0) {
   120→        cj_mov(ctx, d, reg("xzr"));
   121→        return;
   122→    }
   123→    uint16_t chunk0 = (uint16_t)(value & 0xFFFF);
   124→    cj_movz(ctx, d, imm(chunk0));
   125→    for (int shift = 16; shift < 64; shift += 16) {
   126→        uint16_t part = (uint16_t)((value >> shift) & 0xFFFF);
   127→        if (!part) continue;
   128→        uint64_t encoded = (uint64_t)part | ((uint64_t)(shift / 16) << 16);
   129→        cj_movk(ctx, d, imm(encoded));
   130→    }
   131→#endif
   132→}
   133→
   134→static void emit_load64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *dst, const char *base, int32_t disp) {
   135→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   136→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));
   137→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   138→    /* ARM64 LDR unsigned offset max = 4095*8 = 32760. */
   139→    if (disp >= 0 && disp <= 32760 && (disp % 8) == 0) {
   140→        cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(base, disp));
   141→    } else if (disp < 0 && (-disp) <= 4095) {
   142→        /* Small negative offset: SUB then LDR */
   143→        cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), reg(base));
   144→        cj_sub(ctx, reg(dst), imm((uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp)));
   145→        cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(dst, 0));
   146→    } else {
   147→        /* Large offset: load into dst, add base, load */
   148→        if (disp >= 0) {
   149→            emit_load_imm64(ctx, dst, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp);
   150→            cj_add(ctx, reg(dst), reg(base));
   151→        } else {
   152→            cj_mov(ctx, reg(dst), reg(base));
   153→            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP4, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp));
   154→            cj_sub(ctx, reg(dst), reg(REG_TMP4));
   155→        }
   156→        cj_ldr(ctx, reg(dst), mem(dst, 0));
   157→    }
   158→#endif
   159→}
   160→
   161→static void emit_store64(cj_ctx *ctx, const char *src, const char *base, int32_t disp) {
   162→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   163→    cj_mov(ctx, mem(base, disp), reg(src));
   164→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   165→    if (disp >= 0 && disp <= 32760 && (disp % 8) == 0) {
   166→        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(base, disp));
   167→    } else if (disp < 0 && (-disp) <= 4095) {
   168→        cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));
   169→        cj_sub(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), imm((uint64_t)(uint32_t)(-disp)));
   170→        cj_str(ctx, reg(src), mem(REG_TMP4, 0));
   171→    } else {
   172→        if (disp >= 0) {
   173→            emit_load_imm64(ctx, REG_TMP4, (uint64_t)(uint32_t)disp);
   174→            cj_add(ctx, reg(REG_TMP4), reg(base));
   175→        } else {
   176→  
... (truncated)
⚙ Read

Let me check the remaining #ifdef blocks on the later lines.

Read — lisa/jit.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/jit.c",
  "offset": 310,
  "limit": 110
}
result
310→    emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_STKTOP, 0);
   311→    return REG_TMP1;
   312→}
   313→
   314→/* ===== Prologue / Epilogue ===== */
   315→
   316→static void emit_prologue(cj_ctx *ctx) {
   317→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   318→    cj_push(ctx, reg("rbp"));
   319→    cj_mov(ctx, reg("rbp"), reg("rsp"));
   320→    cj_push(ctx, reg("rbx"));
   321→    cj_push(ctx, reg("r12"));
   322→    cj_push(ctx, reg("r13"));
   323→    cj_push(ctx, reg("r14"));
   324→    cj_push(ctx, reg("r15"));
   325→    cj_sub(ctx, reg("rsp"), imm(8)); /* 16-byte alignment */
   326→
   327→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_VM), reg("rdi"));
   328→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_CLOSURE), reg("rsi"));
   329→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_SLOTS), reg("rdx"));
   330→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   331→    /* cj_stp ignores pre-indexed mode, so manually adjust SP */
   332→    cj_sub(ctx, reg("sp"), imm(80));
   333→    cj_stp(ctx, reg("x29"), reg("x30"), mem("sp", 0));
   334→    /* cj_mov(x29, sp) generates ORR x29,XZR,XZR=0 (backend bug:
   335→       reg 31 is XZR in ORR, not SP). Use raw ADD x29, sp, #0. */
   336→    cj_add_u32(ctx, 0x910003FD); /* ADD x29, sp, #0 */
   337→    cj_stp(ctx, reg("x19"), reg("x20"), mem("sp", 16));
   338→    cj_stp(ctx, reg("x21"), reg("x22"), mem("sp", 32));
   339→    cj_str(ctx, reg("x23"), mem("sp", 48));
   340→
   341→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_VM), reg("x0"));
   342→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_CLOSURE), reg("x1"));
   343→    cj_mov(ctx, reg(REG_SLOTS), reg("x2"));
   344→#endif
   345→    emit_reload_stack_top(ctx);
   346→
   347→    /* Load constants pointer: closure->function->chunk.constants.values */
   348→    emit_load64(ctx, REG_TMP1, REG_CLOSURE,
   349→                (int32_t)offsetof(lisa_obj_closure, function));
   350→    emit_load64(ctx, REG_CONSTS, REG_TMP1,
   351→                (int32_t)(offsetof(lisa_obj_function, chunk)
   352→                        + offsetof(lisa_chunk, constants)
   353→                        + offsetof(lisa_value_array, values)));
   354→}
   355→
   356→static void emit_epilogue(cj_ctx *ctx) {
   357→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
   358→    cj_add(ctx, reg("rsp"), imm(8));
   359→    cj_pop(ctx, reg("r15"));
   360→    cj_pop(ctx, reg("r14"));
   361→    cj_pop(ctx, reg("r13"));
   362→    cj_pop(ctx, reg("r12"));
   363→    cj_pop(ctx, reg("rbx"));
   364→    cj_pop(ctx, reg("rbp"));
   365→    cj_ret(ctx);
   366→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
   367→    cj_ldp(ctx, reg("x19"), reg("x20"), mem("sp", 16));
   368→    cj_ldp(ctx, reg("x21"), reg("x22"), mem("sp", 32));
   369→    cj_ldr(ctx, reg("x23"), mem("sp", 48));
   370→    cj_ldp(ctx, reg("x29"), reg("x30"), mem("sp", 0));
   371→    cj_add(ctx, reg("sp"), imm(80));
   372→    cj_ret(ctx);
   373→#endif
   374→}
   375→
   376→/* ===== Inline Type-Check Helpers ===== */
   377→
   378→/* Check if val_reg is an integer. Jumps to fail_label if not.
   379→   Clobbers REG_TMP1 (and R
... (truncated)

claude

Everything is done. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

src/builder.h — 7 new cross-platform builder functions:

- cj_builder_shl(ctx, dst, shift) — shift left by immediate

- cj_builder_shr(ctx, dst, shift) — logical shift right by immediate

- cj_builder_sar(ctx, dst, shift) — arithmetic shift right by immediate

- cj_builder_cset(ctx, dst, cond) — conditional set (0/1 from flags)

- cj_builder_or(ctx, dst, src) — bitwise OR

- cj_builder_neg(ctx, dst) — negate in place

- cj_builder_mul(ctx, dst, src) — multiply

Plus internal helpers: builder_x86_reg8, builder_x86_setcc (x86), builder_arm64_cond_code (ARM64).

On x86, these delegate to working backend functions. On ARM64, the shift and cset functions use raw cj_add_u32() encoding to work around backend bugs.

lisa/jit.c — replaced 8 platform-specific #ifdef blocks with builder calls:

- emit_lsr_imm → cj_builder_shr

- emit_lsl_imm → cj_builder_shl

- emit_mask48 → cj_builder_shl + cj_builder_shr

- emit_sign_extend48 → cj_builder_shl + cj_builder_sar

- emit_or → cj_builder_or

- emit_bool_from_flags → cj_builder_cset + cj_builder_or

- emit_arith_compute MUL → cj_builder_mul

- OP_NEGATE → cj_builder_neg

- Removed the entire ARM64 CSET helper section (defines + function)

tests/test_harness_builder.c — 7 new tests covering each builder function.

All lisa tests pass, benchmark maintained (~2x JIT speedup).

human

are those cj builder functions correctly implemented in the builder or should they ideally be in the backend? or is this just instruction difference between amd/arm?

claude

It's a mix of both. Let me break it down:

Genuinely belong in the builder (different instructions/signatures between platforms):

- cj_builder_or — cj_or vs cj_orr (different name)

- cj_builder_neg — cj_neg(dst) vs cj_neg(dst, src) (different arity)

- cj_builder_mul — cj_imul vs cj_mul (different name)

- cj_builder_cset — x86 needs setcc+movzx (multi-instruction pattern), ARM64 is a single CSINC. Even with a fixed backend, this would still need a builder abstraction.

Working around backend bugs (should ideally be backend fixes):

- cj_builder_shl — ARM64 cj_lsl has no immediate path at all

- cj_builder_shr — ARM64 cj_lsr immediate path never reads src.constant

- cj_builder_sar — ARM64 cj_asr same bug as cj_lsr

The three shift functions are doing raw cj_add_u32() encoding on ARM64 to work around the generated backend. If the ARM64 codegen (codegen/arm64_encoder.js) were fixed to properly encode shift-by-immediate, these builder functions could simplify to just cj_shl/cj_shr/cj_sar on both platforms — same as how cj_builder_or just delegates to cj_or/cj_orr. At that point the shift builders would only exist to paper over the naming difference (shl/shr/sar vs lsl/lsr/asr), which is the same kind of thing as or/orr.

So: the or/neg/mul/cset abstractions are the right level for a builder. The shifts are temporary workarounds that should eventually become thin wrappers once the backend codegen is fixed.

human

can you look at the arm code generator and see what’s wrong about?

⚙ Task
Task
{
  "description": "Explore ARM64 codegen bugs",
  "subagent_type": "Explore",
  "prompt": "I need to understand why the ARM64 backend code generator produces broken shift-by-immediate instructions. The codegen script is at `codegen/arm64_encoder.js` and it generates `src/arch/arm64/backend.h`.\n\nThe known bugs are:\n1. `cj_lsr(ctx, dst, src)` with `src.type == CJ_CONSTANT`: the generated code never reads `src.constant` to encode the shift amount into the `immr` field of the UBFM instruction\n2. `cj_asr(ctx, dst, src)` with `src.type == CJ_CONSTANT`: same bug — shift amount never encoded into SBFM\n3. `cj_lsl(ctx, dst, src)`: no immediate path generated at all (only register-register LSLV)\n\nPlease thoroughly explore:\n\n1. Read the full `codegen/arm64_encoder.js` file to understand how it generates instruction encoders\n2. Find where LSR/ASR/LSL instructions are defined in the input data (`codegen/arm64_instructions_full.jsonl` or similar)\n3. Understand the template/pattern the codegen uses to emit the CJ_CONSTANT case for instructions\n4. Identify exactly what's wrong — is it a bug in the template logic? Missing instruction definitions? Wrong field mappings?\n5. Compare with a working instruction that correctly handles immediates (e.g., ADD with immediate works correctly — how does its CJ_CONSTANT case differ?)\n\nShow the relevant code sections with line numbers so I can understand the root cause."
}
result
Excellent! Now I have everything I need. Let me look at the actual generated code one more time to see what's actually being emitted for the immediate case:

Now I have all the information needed to provide a comprehensive analysis. Let me create the final report.

## Comprehensive Analysis of ARM64 Shift-by-Immediate Bugs

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, I've identified the root cause of all three bugs. Here's the detailed technical analysis:

### Problem Summary

The ARM64 backend code generator produces broken shift-by-immediate instructions for `cj_lsr()`, `cj_asr()`, and `cj_lsl()` when the shift amount is a constant. The generated code does not encode the shift amount into the appropriate instruction field.

---

### Bug 1 & 2: LSR and ASR with Immediate - Missing `immr` Field Encoding

**Generated Code Location**: `cj/src/arch/arm64/backend.h`

**For `cj_asr()` with `CJ_CONSTANT` (lines 879-893)**:
```c
879→  if (dst.type == CJ_REGISTER && src.type == CJ_CONSTANT) {
880→    int rd = arm64_parse_reg(dst.reg);
881→    if (rd < 0) return;
882→    int rn = rd;
883→
884→    uint32_t instr = 0x13007C00;  // SBFM instruction base
885→    int sf = arm64_is_64bit(dst.reg) ? 1 : 0;
886→    instr |= (sf << 31);
887→    instr &= ~((1u << 5) - 1u);
888→    instr |= (rd & ((1u << 5) - 1u));
889→    instr &= ~(((1u << 5) - 1u) << 5);
890→    instr |= ((rn & ((1u << 5) - 1u)) << 5);
891→    cj_add_u32(ctx, instr);
892→    return;
893→  }
```

**For `cj_lsr()` with `CJ_CONSTANT` (lines 10640-10654)**:
```c
10640→  if (dst.type == CJ_REGISTER && src.type == CJ_CONSTANT) {
10641→    int rd = arm64_parse_reg(dst.reg);
10642→    if (rd < 0) return;
10643→    int rn = rd;
10644→
10645→    uint32_t instr = 0x53007C00;  // UBFM instruction base
10646→    int sf = arm64_is_64bit(dst.reg) ? 1 : 0;
10647→    instr |= (sf << 31);
10648→    instr &= ~((1u << 5) - 1u);
10649→    instr |= (rd & ((1u << 5) - 1u));
10650→    instr &= ~(((1u << 5) - 1u) << 5);
10651→    instr |= ((rn & ((1u << 5) - 1u)) << 5);
10652→    cj_add_u32(ctx, instr);
10653→    return;
10654→  }
```

**The Problem**: Both blocks set `Rd` and `Rn` fields (bits [4:0] and [9:5]), but **neither encodes the shift amount into the `immr` field (bits [21:16])**. This is the critical missing code.

**Instruction Encoding Reference**:
- **LSR_UBFM_32M_bitfield** encoding: `0x53007c00`
  - Fields: `sf[31]`, `opc[30:29]='10'`, `N[22]`, **`immr[21:16]`**, `imms[15:10]='x11111'`, `Rn[9:5]`, `Rd[4:0]`
  - The `immr` field should contain the **shift amount**
  
- **ASR_SBFM_32M_bitfield** encoding: `0x13007c00`
  - Fields: `sf[31]`, `opc[30:29]='00'`, `N[22]`, **`immr[21:16]`**, `imms[15:10]='x11111'`, `Rn[9:5]`, `Rd[4:0]`
  - Same issue: `immr` field is never populated with shift amount

---

### Bug 3: LSL with Immediate - No Immediate Path Generated at All

**Generated Code Location**: `cj/src/arch/arm64/backend.h` (lines 10554-10573)

```c
10554→static inline void cj_lsl(cj_ctx* ctx, cj_oper
... (truncated)
claude

The root cause is in codegen/arm64_encoder.js at line 1825. The encoder has a list of recognized immediate field names:

const immVarNames = ['imm12', 'imm9', 'imm6', 'imm5', 'imm4', 'imm3', 'imm'];

But the shift instructions (LSR/ASR/LSL, which are aliases for UBFM/SBFM) use a field called "shift" that maps to the immr bitfield at bits [21:16]. Since "shift" isn't in that list, the encoder:

- ASR/LSR: Generates a CJ_CONSTANT case but only encodes Rd and Rn — the immr field (the actual shift amount from src.constant) is never written

- LSL: Doesn't generate a CJ_CONSTANT case at all, so only register-register (LSLV) works

For comparison, cj_add works correctly because ADD uses an sh field, which the encoder explicitly handles via a separate shField check at line 1881. The shift instructions use a different field naming convention that the encoder doesn't recognize.

The fix would be in the encoder to recognize "shift" (and likely "immr"/"imms") as immediate operand names, then generate code that reads src.constant and encodes it into the correct bitfield position. Want me to fix the encoder?

human

yes.

⚙ Read ×2
Read — cj/codegen/arm64_encoder.js
{
  "file_path": "cj/codegen/arm64_encoder.js",
  "limit": 100
}
result
1→/**
     2→ * WATCH OUT: I LET AN LLM GENERATE THESE COMMENTS. TAKE WITH A GRAIN OF SALT.
     3→ * ARM64 Instruction Encoder Generator
     4→ *
     5→ * This code generator creates C functions for encoding ARM64 instructions.
     6→ * It reads instruction definitions from arm64_instructions_full.jsonl and
     7→ * generates efficient encoder functions in src/arch/arm64/backend.h
     8→ *
     9→ * Key features:
    10→ * - Supports 2,516+ ARM64 A64 instructions (99% coverage)
    11→ * - Handles multiple instruction variants (32/64-bit, FP/integer, etc.)
    12→ * - Generates type-safe operand checking
    13→ * - Optimizes for common addressing modes
    14→ *
    15→ * Architecture:
    16→ * 1. Load and deduplicate instruction definitions
    17→ * 2. Filter to supported instructions (A64, valid C identifiers)
    18→ * 3. Group by mnemonic and sort by priority
    19→ * 4. Generate C encoder functions with proper register field mapping
    20→ */
    21→
    22→const fs = require('fs');
    23→
    24→// Read ARM64 instruction data with operands
    25→const lines = fs.readFileSync('./codegen/arm64_instructions_full.jsonl', 'utf8').split('\n').filter(l => l.trim());
    26→const allInstructions = lines.map(line => JSON.parse(line));
    27→
    28→// Deduplicate by ID (some entries appear multiple times)
    29→const seenIds = new Set();
    30→const instructions = allInstructions.filter(inst => {
    31→  if (seenIds.has(inst.id)) return false;
    32→  seenIds.add(inst.id);
    33→  return true;
    34→});
    35→
    36→console.error(`Loaded ${instructions.length} ARM64 instruction encodings (${allInstructions.length - instructions.length} duplicates removed)`);
    37→
    38→// Exclude complex instruction sets that need special handling
    39→// Accept everything else - we have complete encodings!
    40→const EXCLUDED_PATTERNS = [
    41→  // SIMD/NEON - complex operations that need special handling
    42→  '.*_asimddiff.*',     // SIMD long/narrow operations with invalid mnemonics (ADDHN{2}, etc)
    43→
    44→  // ALL INSTRUCTION SETS NOW SUPPORTED:
    45→  // - SIMD/NEON: All operations except asimddiff
    46→  // - Cryptography: AES, SHA, etc.
    47→  // - System instructions: MSR, MRS, SYS, SYSL
    48→  // - Pointer authentication
    49→  // - SVE (Scalable Vector Extension): Z and P registers - NOW SUPPORTED!
    50→];
    51→
    52→function matchesPattern(id, patterns) {
    53→  if (!id) return false;
    54→  for (const pat of patterns) {
    55→    const regex = new RegExp('^' + pat + '$');
    56→    if (regex.test(id)) return true;
    57→  }
    58→  return false;
    59→}
    60→

    65→  if (mnemonic.endsWith('{2}')) {

result
257→function extractFieldName(operand) {
   258→  const hover = operand.hover || '';
   259→  const match = hover.match(/field\s+"([^"]+)"/);
   260→  return match ? match[1] : null;
   261→}
   262→
   263→/**
   264→ * Find a variable field by name in an instruction
   265→ * @param {Object} inst - Instruction object
   266→ * @param {string} name - Variable name (e.g., 'Rd', 'Rn', 'Rm')
   267→ * @returns {Object|null} Variable object or null if not found
   268→ */
   269→function findVariable(inst, name) {
   270→  if (!inst || !inst.variables) return null;
   271→  return inst.variables.find(v => v.name === name) || null;
   272→}
   273→
   274→/**
   275→ * Generate a bit mask expression for a given width
   276→ * @param {number} width - Bit width
   277→ * @returns {string} C expression for the bit mask
   278→ */
   279→function bitMask(width) {
   280→  if (width >= 32) return '0xFFFFFFFFu';
   281→  return `((1u << ${width}) - 1u)`;
   282→}
   283→
   284→/**
   285→ * Generate code to encode a register into an instruction field
   286→ * @param {string} regVar - Register variable name (e.g., 'rd', 'rn')
   287→ * @param {number} bitPos - Bit position in instruction (0, 5, 16, etc.)
   288→ * @param {string} comment - Optional comment to add
   289→ * @returns {string} C code to encode the register field
   290→ */
   291→function generateRegFieldEncoding(regVar, bitPos) {
   292→  if (bitPos === 0) {
   293→    return `    instr &= ~0x1f;\n    instr |= (${regVar} & 0x1f);${commentStr}\n`;
   294→  }
   295→  return `    instr &= ~(0x1f << ${bitPos});\n    instr |= ((${regVar} & 0x1f) << ${bitPos});${commentStr}\n`;
   296→}
   297→
   298→/**
   299→ * Encode a field using its metadata (lo, hi, width)
   300→ * This is the metadata-driven version that doesn't hardcode bit positions
   301→ *
   302→ * @param {Object} field - Field metadata with {name, lo, hi, width}
   303→ * @param {string} cVarName - C variable name to encode
   304→ * @returns {string} C code to encode the field
   305→ */
   306→function encodeFieldFromMetadata(field, cVarName) {

  1591→  // Add SIMD prolog if both scalar and SIMD variants exist
  1592→  if (simdPrologCode) {
  1593→    output += simdPrologCode;
  1594→  }
  1595→
  1596→  const variantsBySignature = {};
  1597→
  1598→  for (const inst of variants) {
  1599→    const operands = inst.operands || [];
  1600→    if (operands.length < 2) continue;
  1601→
  1602→    const mappedOps = operands.map(op => mapOperandType(op));
  1603→    const fields = operands.map(op => extractFieldName(op));
  1604→
  1605→    const filteredOps = [];
  1606→    const filteredFields = [];
  1607→    for (let i = 0; i < mappedOps.length; i++) {
  1608→      const opType = mappedOps[i];
  1609→      const fieldName = fields[i];
  1610→      if (fieldName === 'sh' || fieldName === 'shift' || fieldName === 'imm6') {
  1611→        continue;  // Optional shift amount handled implicitly
  1612→      }
  1613→      if (opType.type === 'arrangement' || opType.type === 'vec_extra') {
  1614→        continue;
  1615→      }
  1616→      filteredOps.push(opType);
  1617→      filteredFields.push(fields[i]);
  1618→    }
  1619→
  1620→    if (filteredOps.length < 2) continue;
  1621→
  1622→    const tupleCount = operands.reduce((max, operand) => {
  1623→      const link = (operand.link || '').toLowerCase();
  1624→      if (!link.startsWith('vt')) return max;
  1625→      const suffix = link.slice(2);
  1626→      const idx = suffix ? parseInt(suffix, 10) : 1;
  1627→      if (!Number.isFinite(idx)) return max;
  1628→      return Math.max(max, idx);
  1629→    }, 0);

  1654→      }

  1659→      console.error(mnemonic + ' skipping'

result
164→function mapOperandType(operand) {
   165→  const linkRaw = operand.link || '';
   166→  const link = linkRaw.toLowerCase();
   167→  const hover = operand.hover || '';
   168→  const hoverLower = hover.toLowerCase();
   169→
   170→  if (link.match(/^[xw](d|n|m|t)/)) {
   171→    const is64bit = link[0] === 'x';
   172→    return { type: 'reg', size: is64bit ? 64 : 32 };
   173→  } else if (link.match(/^[hsd](d|n|m)/)) {
   174→    const size = link[0] === 'h' ? 16 : (link[0] === 's' ? 32 : 64);
   175→    return { type: 'fpreg', size: size };
   176→  } else if (link === 'vt') {
   177→    return { type: 'vec' };
   178→  } else if (link === 't' || link === 'size' || link.startsWith('size:')) {
   179→    return { type: 'arrangement' };
   180→  } else if (/^vt\d+$/.test(link)) {
   181→    return { type: 'vec_extra' };
   182→  } else if (link.startsWith('shift')) {
   183→    return { type: 'imm' };
   184→  } else if (hoverLower.includes('general-purpose')) {
   185→    const is64bit = hoverLower.includes('64-bit') || link.startsWith('x');
   186→    return { type: 'reg', size: is64bit ? 64 : 32 };
   187→  } else if (link === 'imm' || link === 'amount' || link === 'shift' || link === 'extend' || link.startsWith('pimm') || link.startsWith('simm') || link.startsWith('imm')) {
   188→    const match = hover.match(/\[(\d+)-(\d+)\]/);
   189→    if (match) {
   190→      return { type: 'imm', min: parseInt(match[1]), max: parseInt(match[2]) };
   191→    }
   192→    return { type: 'imm' };
   193→  }
   194→  return { type: 'unknown' };
   195→}
   196→
   197→// ============================================================================
   198→// Helper Functions for Code Generation
   199→// ============================================================================
   200→
   201→/**
   202→ * Detect instruction pattern from field structure (replaces mnemonic matching)
   203→ */
   204→function detectInstructionPattern(variants, mnemonic) {
   205→  if (!variants || variants.length === 0) return null;
   206→  const inst = variants[0];
   207→  const fields = inst.variables || [];
   208→  const operands = inst.operands || [];
   209→  const opCount = operands.length;
   210→
   211→  const hasRt = fields.some(f => f.name === 'Rt');
   212→  const hasRt2 = fields.some(f => f.name === 'Rt2');
   213→  const hasRs = fields.some(f => f.name === 'Rs');
   214→  const hasRn = fields.some(f => f.name === 'Rn');
   215→  const hasA = fields.some(f => f.name === 'A');
   216→  const hasR = fields.some(f => f.name === 'R');
   217→  const hasO = fields.some(f => f.name === 'o0');
   218→
   219→  // Load/Store Pair: 3-4 operands + Rt + Rt2
   220→  // Return with load flag based on mnemonic
   221→  if (opCount >= 3 && hasRt && hasRt2 && hasRn && !hasRs) {
   222→    return mnemonic.startsWith('ld') ? 'pair_load' : 'pair_store';
   223→  }
   224→
   225→  // Exclusive store pair: 4 operands + Rs + Rt + Rt2
   226→  if (opCount === 4 && hasRs 
... (truncated)
