result
1→# Add Cross-Platform Builder Functions to `src/builder.h`
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→The JIT rewrite (`lisa/jit.c`) is complete and working (~2x speedup), but uses extensive `#ifdef __x86_64__ / __aarch64__` blocks because the cj builder layer lacks several primitives that the JIT needs. Meanwhile, several ARM64 backend functions have encoding bugs (shift-by-immediate never encodes the shift amount, CSET is empty, etc.). The backend is auto-generated from codegen scripts, so fixing those is a separate task.
     6→
     7→This plan adds **7 new cross-platform builder functions** to `src/builder.h`. On x86-64, they delegate to working backend functions. On ARM64, they use raw `cj_add_u32()` encoding to work around backend bugs.
     8→
     9→## Files Modified
    10→
    11→- **`src/builder.h`** — add 7 new functions + internal helpers
    12→- **`tests/test_harness_builder.c`** — add tests for each new function
    13→
    14→## New Builder Functions
    15→
    16→### 1. `cj_builder_shl(ctx, dst, shift_amount)`
    17→Shift left by immediate. `dst` is modified in place.
    18→
    19→- **x86**: `cj_shl(ctx, dst, cj_make_constant(shift))` — backend works correctly
    20→- **ARM64**: raw-encode `UBFM Xd, Xn, #(64-N), #(63-N)` via `cj_add_u32()` (backend has no immediate path for LSL)
    21→
    22→ARM64 encoding detail:
    23→```
    24→LSL Xd, Xn, #N  →  UBFM Xd, Xn, #(-N MOD 64), #(63-N)
    25→int immr = (64 - shift) & 63;
    26→int imms = 63 - shift;
    27→uint32_t instr = 0xD3400000 | (rd & 0x1f) | ((rn & 0x1f) << 5)
    28→               | ((imms & 0x3f) << 10) | ((immr & 0x3f) << 16);
    29→// For 32-bit (w-regs): base=0x53000000, size=32 instead of 64
    30→```
    31→
    32→### 2. `cj_builder_shr(ctx, dst, shift_amount)`
    33→Logical shift right by immediate. `dst` modified in place.
    34→
    35→- **x86**: `cj_shr(ctx, dst, cj_make_constant(shift))` — backend works
    36→- **ARM64**: raw-encode `UBFM Xd, Xn, #N, #63` via `cj_add_u32()` (backend bug: never reads `src.constant`)
    37→
    38→ARM64 encoding:
    39→```
    40→LSR Xd, Xn, #N  →  UBFM Xd, Xn, #N, #63
    41→uint32_t instr = 0xD340FC00 | (rd & 0x1f) | ((rn & 0x1f) << 5)
    42→               | ((shift & 0x3f) << 16);
    43→// 0xD340FC00 = sf=1, N=1, immr=0, imms=63 baked in
    44→// For 32-bit: base=0x53007C00, imms=31
    45→```
    46→
    47→### 3. `cj_builder_sar(ctx, dst, shift_amount)`
    48→Arithmetic shift right by immediate. `dst` modified in place.
    49→
    50→- **x86**: `cj_sar(ctx, dst, cj_make_constant(shift))` — backend works
    51→- **ARM64**: raw-encode `SBFM Xd, Xn, #N, #63` via `cj_add_u32()` (backend bug: same as LSR)
    52→
    53→ARM64 encoding:
    54→```
    55→ASR Xd, Xn, #N  →  SBFM Xd, Xn, #N, #63
    56→uint32_t instr = 0x9340FC00 | (rd & 0x1f) | ((rn & 0x1f) << 5)
    57→               | ((shift & 0x3f) << 16);
    58→// For 32-bit: base=0x13007C00
    59→```
    60→
    61→### 4. `cj_builder_cset(ctx, dst, c
... (truncated)
Read — lisa/vm.h
{
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.h"
}
result
1→#ifndef LISA_VM_H
     2→#define LISA_VM_H
     3→
     4→#include "value.h"
     5→#include "object.h"
     6→#include "chunk.h"
     7→
     8→#define STACK_MAX 4096
     9→#define FRAMES_MAX 256
    10→
    11→typedef struct {
    12→    lisa_obj_closure *closure;
    13→    uint8_t *ip;
    14→    lisa_value *slots; /* pointer into vm stack */
    15→} lisa_call_frame;
    16→
    17→typedef struct {
    18→    lisa_call_frame frames[FRAMES_MAX];
    19→    int frame_count;
    20→
    21→    lisa_value stack[STACK_MAX];
    22→    lisa_value *stack_top;
    23→
    24→    /* Global variables: hash table of string -> value */
    25→    lisa_obj_string **global_names;
    26→    lisa_value *global_values;
    27→    int global_count;
    28→    int global_capacity;
    29→
    30→    lisa_obj_upvalue *open_upvalues;
    31→
    32→    bool jit_enabled;
    33→
    34→    lisa_gc gc;
    35→} lisa_vm;
    36→
    37→typedef enum {
    38→    INTERPRET_OK,
    39→    INTERPRET_COMPILE_ERROR,
    40→    INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR,
    41→} lisa_interpret_result;
    42→
    43→void lisa_vm_init(lisa_vm *vm);
    44→void lisa_vm_free(lisa_vm *vm);
    45→lisa_interpret_result lisa_interpret(lisa_vm *vm, const char *source);
    46→
    47→/* Run the bytecode interpreter starting from the current top frame.
    48→   Stops when frame_count drops to base_frame. */
    49→lisa_interpret_result lisa_run(lisa_vm *vm, int base_frame);
    50→
    51→/* JIT trampoline sentinel: top 16 bits = 0xDEAD (invalid as any lisa_value tag),
    52→   low 8 bits = argc for the pending tail call. */
    53→#define LISA_TAIL_PENDING_BASE ((uint64_t)0xDEAD000000000000)
    54→#define LISA_TAIL_PENDING(argc) (LISA_TAIL_PENDING_BASE | (uint64_t)(argc))
    55→#define IS_TAIL_PENDING(v) (((v) >> 48) == 0xDEAD)
    56→#define TAIL_PENDING_ARGC(v) ((int)((v) & 0xFF))
    57→

     8→
     9→typedef enum {
    10→    OBJ_STRING,
    11→    OBJ_FUNCTION,
    12→    OBJ_CLOSURE,
    13→    OBJ_UPVALUE,
    14→    OBJ_LIST,
    15→    OBJ_NATIVE,
    16→} lisa_obj_type;
    17→
    18→struct lisa_obj {
    19→    lisa_obj_type type;
    20→    bool is_marked;
    21→    struct lisa_obj *next;
    22→};
    23→
    24→typedef struct {
    25→    lisa_obj obj;
    26→    int length;
    27→    uint32_t hash;
    28→    char chars[];   /* flexible array */
    29→} lisa_obj_string;
    30→
    31→typedef struct {
    32→    lisa_obj obj;
    33→    int arity;
    34→    int upvalue_count;
    35→    lisa_chunk chunk;
    36→    lisa_obj_string *name;
    37→    void *jit_code;    /* JIT-compiled native code, or NULL */
    38→    void *jit_ctx;     /* cj_ctx* for cleanup, or NULL */
    39→} lisa_obj_function;
    40→
    41→typedef struct lisa_obj_upvalue {
    42→    lisa_obj obj;
    43→    lisa_value *location;
    44→    lisa_value closed;
    45→    struct lisa_obj_upvalue *next;
    46→} lisa_obj_upvalue;
    47→
    48→typedef struct {
    49→    lisa_obj obj;
    50→    lisa_obj_function *function;

    54→

    66→    const char *name;
    67→    int arity; /* -1 for variadic */

    82→#define AS_CLOSURE(value)  ((lisa_obj_closure*)AS_OBJ(value))
    83→#define AS_UPVALUE(value)  ((lisa_obj_upvalue*)AS_OBJ(value))
    84→#define AS_NATIVE(value)   ((lisa_obj_native*)AS_OBJ(value))
    85→#define AS_LIST(value)     ((lisa_obj_list*)AS_OBJ(value))

    10→
    11→typedef lisa_value (*lisa_jit_fn)(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_closure *closure,
    12→                                  lisa_value *slots);
    13→
    14→static lisa_value jit_trampoline(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value result);
    15→
    16→/* --- Stack operations --- */
    17→
    18→static void push(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value value) {
    19→    *vm->stack_top = value;
    20→    vm->stack_top++;
    21→}
    22→
    23→static lisa_value pop(lisa_vm *vm) {
    24→    vm->stack_top--;
    25→    return *vm->stack_top;
    26→}
    27→
    28→static lisa_value peek(lisa_vm *vm, int distance) {
    29→    return vm->stack_top[-1 - distance];
    30→}
    31→
    32→static void reset_stack(lisa_vm *vm) {
    33→    vm->stack_top = vm->stack;
    34→    vm->frame_count = 0;
    35→    vm->open_upvalues = NULL;
    36→}
    37→
    38→static void runtime_error(lisa_vm *vm, const char *format, ...) {
    39→    va_list args;
    40→    va_start(args, format);
    41→    vfprintf(stderr, format, args);
    42→    va_end(args);
    43→    fputs("\n", stderr);
    44→
    45→    for (int i = vm->frame_count - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
    46→        lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[i];
    47→        lisa_obj_function *fn = frame->closure->function;
    48→        size_t offset = (size_t)(frame->ip - fn->chunk.code - 1);
    49→        int line = fn->chunk.lines[offset];
    50→        fprintf(stderr, "[line %d] in ", line);
    51→        if (fn->name == NULL) {
    52→            fprintf(stderr, "script\n");
    53→        } else {
    54→            fprintf(stderr, "%s()\n", fn->name->chars);
    55→        }
    56→    }
    57→
    58→    reset_stack(vm);
    59→}
    60→
    61→/* --- Globals hash table --- */
    62→
    63→static void globals_grow(lisa_vm *vm) {
    64→    int new_cap = vm->global_capacity < 8 ? 8 : vm->global_capacity * 2;
    65→    lisa_obj_string **new_names = calloc((size_t)new_cap, sizeof(lisa_obj_string*));
    66→    lisa_value *new_values = calloc((size_t)new_cap, sizeof(lisa_value));
    67→
    68→    for (int i = 0; i < vm->global_capacity; i++) {
    69→        if (vm->global_names[i] == NULL) continue;
    70→        uint32_t idx = vm->global_names[i]->hash % (uint32_t)new_cap;
    71→        while (new_names[idx] != NULL) {
    72→            idx = (idx + 1) % (uint32_t)new_cap;
    73→        }
    74→        new_names[idx] = vm->global_names[i];
    75→        new_values[idx] = vm->global_values[i];
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    free(vm->global_names);
    79→    free(vm->global_values);
    80→    vm->global_names = new_names;
    81→    vm->global_values = new_values;
    82→    vm->global_capacity = new_cap;
    83→}
    84→
    85→static int globals_find(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_string

result
105→    vm->global_values[idx] = value;

   107→
   108→/* --- Native functions --- */
   109→
   110→static void define_native(lisa_vm *vm, const char *name, lisa_native_fn fn, int arity) {
   111→    lisa_obj_string *name_str = lisa_copy_string(&vm->gc, name, (int)strlen(name));
   112→    push(vm, LISA_OBJ(name_str)); /* GC protect */
   113→    lisa_obj_native *native = lisa_new_native(&vm->gc, fn, name, arity);
   114→    push(vm, LISA_OBJ(native)); /* GC protect */
   115→    globals_set(vm, name_str, LISA_OBJ(native));
   116→    pop(vm);
   117→    pop(vm);
   118→}
   119→
   120→/* Built-in native functions for when operators are used as values */
   121→static lisa_value native_add(int argc, lisa_value *args) {
   122→    (void)argc;
   123→    if (IS_INT(args[0]) && IS_INT(args[1])) return LISA_INT(AS_INT(args[0]) + AS_INT(args[1]));
   124→    return lisa_double(lisa_as_number(args[0]) + lisa_as_number(args[1]));
   125→}
   126→
   127→static lisa_value native_sub(int argc, lisa_value *args) {
   128→    (void)argc;
   129→    if (argc == 1) {
   130→        if (IS_INT(args[0])) return LISA_INT(-AS_INT(args[0]));
   131→        return lisa_double(-AS_DOUBLE(args[0]));
   132→    }
   133→    if (IS_INT(args[0]) && IS_INT(args[1])) return LISA_INT(AS_INT(args[0]) - AS_INT(args[1]));
   134→    return lisa_double(lisa_as_number(args[0]) - lisa_as_number(args[1]));
   135→}
   136→
   137→static lisa_value native_mul(int argc, lisa_value *args) {
   138→    (void)argc;
   139→    if (IS_INT(args[0]) && IS_INT(args[1])) return LISA_INT(AS_INT(args[0]) * AS_INT(args[1]));
   140→    return lisa_double(lisa_as_number(args[0]) * lisa_as_number(args[1]));
   141→}
   142→
   143→static lisa_value native_div(int argc, lisa_value *args) {
   144→    (void)argc;
   145→    return lisa_double(lisa_as_number(args[0]) / lisa_as_number(args[1]));
   146→}
   147→
   148→/* --- Upvalue management --- */
   149→
   150→static lisa_obj_upvalue *capture_upvalue(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value *local) {
   151→    lisa_obj_upvalue *prev = NULL;
   152→    lisa_obj_upvalue *upvalue = vm->open_upvalues;
   153→
   154→    while (upvalue != NULL && upvalue->location > local) {

result
650→
   651→#undef READ_BYTE
   652→#undef READ_SHORT
   653→#undef READ_CONSTANT
   654→#undef BINARY_OP
   655→}
   656→
   657→/* --- Public API --- */
   658→
   659→void lisa_vm_init(lisa_vm *vm) {
   660→    reset_stack(vm);
   661→    lisa_gc_init(&vm->gc);
   662→    vm->global_names = NULL;
   663→    vm->global_values = NULL;
   664→    vm->global_count = 0;
   665→    vm->global_capacity = 0;
   666→    vm->jit_enabled = true;
   667→
   668→    /* Register native functions */
   669→    define_native(vm, "+", native_add, 2);
   670→    define_native(vm, "-", native_sub, -1);
   671→    define_native(vm, "*", native_mul, 2);
   672→    define_native(vm, "/", native_div, 2);
   673→}
   674→
   675→void lisa_vm_free(lisa_vm *vm) {
   676→    free(vm->global_names);
   677→    free(vm->global_values);
   678→    lisa_gc_free(&vm->gc);
   679→    vm->global_names = NULL;
   680→    vm->global_values = NULL;
   681→    vm->global_count = 0;
   682→    vm->global_capacity = 0;
   683→}
   684→
   685→lisa_interpret_result lisa_interpret(lisa_vm *vm, const char *source) {
   686→    lisa_parser parser;
   687→    lisa_parser_init(&parser, source);
   688→
   689→    int count;
   690→    lisa_ast **exprs = lisa_parse(&parser, &count);
   691→
   692→    if (parser.had_error) {
   693→        lisa_parse_free(exprs, count);
   694→        return INTERPRET_COMPILE_ERROR;
   695→    }
   696→
   697→    lisa_obj_function *fn = lisa_compile(&vm->gc, exprs, count);
   698→    lisa_parse_free(exprs, count);
   699→
   700→    if (fn == NULL) return INTERPRET_COMPILE_ERROR;
   701→
   702→    /* Wrap in closure and push as frame 0 */
   703→    push(vm, LISA_OBJ(fn));
   704→    lisa_obj_closure *closure = lisa_new_closure(&vm->gc, fn);
   705→    pop(vm);
   706→    push(vm, LISA_OBJ(closure));
   707→    call_closure(vm, closure, 0);
   708→
   709→    return lisa_run(vm, 0);
   710→}
   711→
   712→/* --- JIT helper functions --- */
   713→
   714→static void sync_gc_roots(lisa_vm *vm) {
   715→    vm->gc.stack = vm->stack;
   716→    vm->gc.stack_count = (int)(vm->stack_top - vm->stack);
   717→    vm->gc.open_upvalues = vm->open_upvalues;
   718→}
   719→
   720→/* Handle pending JIT tail calls iteratively (trampoline).
   721→   Called when a JIT function returns LISA_TAIL_PENDING(argc). */
   722→static lisa_value jit_trampoline(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value result) {
   723→    while (IS_TAIL_PENDING(result)) {
   724→        int argc = TAIL_PENDING_ARGC(result);
   725→        lisa_value callee = vm->stack_top[-1 - argc];
   726→
   727→        if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {
   728→            call_value(vm, callee, argc);
   729→            return vm->stack_top[-1];
   730→        }
   731→
   732→        if (!IS_OBJ(callee) || OBJ_TYPE(callee) != OBJ_CLOSURE) {
   733→            runtime_error(vm, "Can only call functions and closures.");
   734→            return LISA_NIL;
   735→        }
   736→
   737→        lisa_o

⚙ Read ×2

Now let me check the native function call dispatch and the GC marking to understand how fibers will interact.

Read — lisa/vm.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "offset": 165,
  "limit": 80
}
result
165→
   166→    if (prev == NULL) {
   167→        vm->open_upvalues = created;
   168→    } else {
   169→        prev->next = created;
   170→    }
   171→
   172→    return created;
   173→}
   174→
   175→static void close_upvalues(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value *last) {
   176→    while (vm->open_upvalues != NULL && vm->open_upvalues->location >= last) {
   177→        lisa_obj_upvalue *upvalue = vm->open_upvalues;
   178→        upvalue->closed = *upvalue->location;
   179→        upvalue->location = &upvalue->closed;
   180→        vm->open_upvalues = upvalue->next;
   181→    }
   182→}
   183→
   184→/* --- Call --- */
   185→
   186→static bool call_closure(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_closure *closure, int argc) {
   187→    if (argc != closure->function->arity) {
   188→        runtime_error(vm, "Expected %d arguments but got %d.",
   189→                      closure->function->arity, argc);
   190→        return false;
   191→    }
   192→    if (vm->frame_count >= FRAMES_MAX) {
   193→        runtime_error(vm, "Stack overflow.");
   194→        return false;
   195→    }
   196→    /* JIT compile on first call (skip top-level script) */
   197→    if (vm->jit_enabled && !closure->function->jit_code &&
   198→        vm->frame_count > 0) {
   199→        lisa_jit_compile(vm, closure->function);
   200→    }
   201→    lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count++];
   202→    frame->closure = closure;
   203→    frame->ip = closure->function->chunk.code;
   204→    frame->slots = vm->stack_top - argc - 1;
   205→    return true;
   206→}
   207→
   208→static bool call_value(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value callee, int argc) {
   209→    if (IS_OBJ(callee)) {
   210→        switch (OBJ_TYPE(callee)) {
   211→        case OBJ_CLOSURE:
   212→            return call_closure(vm, AS_CLOSURE(callee), argc);
   213→        case OBJ_NATIVE: {
   214→            lisa_obj_native *native = AS_NATIVE(callee);
   215→            if (native->arity != -1 && native->arity != argc) {
   216→                runtime_error(vm, "Expected %d arguments but got %d.", native->arity, argc);
   217→                return false;
   218→            }
   219→            lisa_value result = native->function(argc, vm->stack_top - argc);
   220→            vm->stack_top -= argc + 1;
   221→            push(vm, result);
   222→            return true;
   223→        }
   224→        default:
   225→            break;
   226→        }
   227→    }
   228→    runtime_error(vm, "Can only call functions and closures.");
   229→    return false;
   230→}
   231→
   232→/* --- String concatenation --- */
   233→
   234→static void concatenate(lisa_vm *vm) {
   235→    lisa_obj_string *b = AS_STRING(peek(vm, 0));
   236→    lisa_obj_string *a = AS_STRING(peek(vm, 1));
   237→
   238→    int length = a->length + b->length;
   239→    char *chars = malloc((size_t)length + 1);
   240→    memcpy(chars, a->chars, (size_t)a->length);
   241→    memcpy(chars + a->length, b->chars, (size_t)b->
... (truncated)
Read — lisa/object.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/object.c",
  "offset": 240,

result
240→
   241→static void mark_object(lisa_obj *obj) {
   242→    if (obj == NULL || obj->is_marked) return;
   243→    obj->is_marked = true;
   244→
   245→    switch (obj->type) {
   246→    case OBJ_STRING:
   247→        break;
   248→    case OBJ_UPVALUE:
   249→        mark_value(((lisa_obj_upvalue*)obj)->closed);
   250→        break;
   251→    case OBJ_FUNCTION: {
   252→        lisa_obj_function *fn = (lisa_obj_function*)obj;
   253→        if (fn->name) mark_object((lisa_obj*)fn->name);
   254→        for (int i = 0; i < fn->chunk.constants.count; i++) {
   255→            mark_value(fn->chunk.constants.values[i]);
   256→        }
   257→        break;
   258→    }
   259→    case OBJ_CLOSURE: {
   260→        lisa_obj_closure *closure = (lisa_obj_closure*)obj;
   261→        mark_object((lisa_obj*)closure->function);
   262→        for (int i = 0; i < closure->upvalue_count; i++) {
   263→            if (closure->upvalues[i]) {
   264→                mark_object((lisa_obj*)closure->upvalues[i]);
   265→            }
   266→        }
   267→        break;
   268→    }
   269→    case OBJ_LIST: {
   270→        lisa_obj_list *list = (lisa_obj_list*)obj;
   271→        mark_value(list->car);
   272→        mark_value(list->cdr);
   273→        break;
   274→    }
   275→    case OBJ_NATIVE:
   276→        break;
   277→    }
   278→}
   279→
   280→static void mark_roots(lisa_gc *gc) {
   281→    /* Mark stack values */
   282→    for (int i = 0; i < gc->stack_count; i++) {
   283→        mark_value(gc->stack[i]);
   284→    }
   285→    /* Mark open upvalues */
   286→    lisa_obj_upvalue *uv = gc->open_upvalues;
   287→    while (uv != NULL) {
   288→        mark_object((lisa_obj*)uv);
   289→        uv = uv->next;
   290→    }
   291→}
   292→
   293→static void free_object(lisa_gc *gc, lisa_obj *obj) {
   294→    switch (obj->type) {
   295→    case OBJ_STRING: {
   296→        lisa_obj_string *str = (lisa_obj_string*)obj;
   297→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_string) + (size_t)str->length + 1;
   298→        string_table_remove(gc, str);
   299→        free(obj);
   300→        break;
   301→    }
   302→    case OBJ_FUNCTION: {
   303→        lisa_obj_function *fn = (lisa_obj_function*)obj;
   304→        lisa_jit_free(fn);
   305→        lisa_chunk_free(&fn->chunk);
   306→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_function);
   307→        free(obj);
   308→        break;
   309→    }
   310→    case OBJ_CLOSURE: {
   311→        lisa_obj_closure *closure = (lisa_obj_closure*)obj;
   312→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_upvalue*) * (size_t)closure->upvalue_count;
   313→        free(closure->upvalues);
   314→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_closure);
   315→        free(obj);
   316→        break;
   317→    }
   318→    case OBJ_UPVALUE:
   319→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_upvalue);
   320→        free(obj);
   321→        break;
   322→    case OBJ_LIST:
   323→

Let me check the value representation and the OP_CALL dispatch path.

Read — lisa/value.h
{
  "file_path": "lisa/value.h"
}
result
1→#ifndef LISA_VALUE_H
     2→#define LISA_VALUE_H
     3→
     4→#include <stdbool.h>
     5→#include <stdint.h>
     6→#include <stdio.h>
     7→
     8→/*
     9→ * NaN-boxed value representation.
    10→ *
    11→ * IEEE 754 double: if bits [62:52] are all 1 and bit 51 is 1, it's a quiet NaN.
    12→ * We use the remaining payload bits for non-double values.
    13→ *
    14→ * Layout:
    15→ *   Double: any bit pattern that is NOT a quiet NaN with our tag prefix
    16→ *   Tagged: [sign=1][exp=0x7FF][quiet=1][tag 50:48][payload 47:0]
    17→ *
    18→ * Tags (bits 50:48):
    19→ *   000 = nil
    20→ *   001 = bool (payload bit 0)
    21→ *   010 = int  (48-bit sign-extended integer)
    22→ *   011 = object pointer (48-bit)
    23→ */
    24→
    25→typedef uint64_t lisa_value;
    26→
    27→/* The quiet NaN mask: sign(1) + exponent(0x7FF) + quiet(1) = bits 63,62:52,51 */
    28→#define QNAN    ((uint64_t)0x7FFC000000000000)
    29→#define SIGN_BIT ((uint64_t)0x8000000000000000)
    30→
    31→/* Tag values shifted into bits 50:48 */
    32→#define TAG_NIL    ((uint64_t)0x0000000000000000)  /* 000 */
    33→#define TAG_BOOL   ((uint64_t)0x0001000000000000)  /* 001 */
    34→#define TAG_INT    ((uint64_t)0x0002000000000000)  /* 010 */
    35→#define TAG_OBJ    ((uint64_t)0x0003000000000000)  /* 011 */
    36→
    37→#define TAG_MASK   ((uint64_t)0x0003000000000000)
    38→#define PAYLOAD_MASK ((uint64_t)0x0000FFFFFFFFFFFF) /* 48 bits */
    39→
    40→/* Construct values */
    41→#define LISA_NIL        (QNAN | TAG_NIL)
    42→#define LISA_TRUE       (QNAN | TAG_BOOL | 1)
    43→#define LISA_FALSE      (QNAN | TAG_BOOL | 0)
    44→#define LISA_BOOL(b)    ((b) ? LISA_TRUE : LISA_FALSE)
    45→#define LISA_INT(i)     (QNAN | TAG_INT | ((uint64_t)(i) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
    46→#define LISA_OBJ(ptr)   (QNAN | TAG_OBJ | ((uint64_t)(uintptr_t)(ptr) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
    47→
    48→static inline lisa_value lisa_double(double d) {
    49→    union { double d; uint64_t u; } conv;
    50→    conv.d = d;
    51→    return conv.u;
    52→}
    53→
    54→/* Type checks */
    55→#define IS_NIL(v)    ((v) == LISA_NIL)
    56→#define IS_BOOL(v)   (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_BOOL))
    57→#define IS_INT(v)    (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_INT))
    58→#define IS_OBJ(v)    (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_OBJ))
    59→#define IS_DOUBLE(v) (((v) & QNAN) != QNAN)
    60→
    61→/* Extract values */
    62→#define AS_BOOL(v)   ((v) & 1)
    63→
    64→static inline int64_t AS_INT(lisa_value v) {
    65→    /* Sign-extend the 48-bit payload */
    66→    uint64_t raw = v & PAYLOAD_MASK;
    67→    if (raw & ((uint64_t)1 << 47)) {
    68→        raw |= (uint64_t)0xFFFF000000000000;
    69→    }
    70→    return (int64_t)raw;
    71→}
    72→
    73→static inline double AS_DOUBLE(lisa_value v) {
    74→    union { uint64_t u; double d; } conv;
    75→    conv.u = v;
    76→    return conv.d;
    77→}
    78→
    79→#define AS_
... (truncated)

480→                /* JIT function returned; pop its frame */
   481→                close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);
   482→                vm->frame_count--;
   483→                vm->stack_top = frame->slots;
   484→                push(vm, result);
   485→                frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
   486→            }
   487→            break;
   488→        }
   489→
   490→        case OP_TAIL_CALL: {
   491→            int argc = READ_BYTE();
   492→            lisa_value callee = peek(vm, argc);
   493→
   494→            /* Native functions: no frame to reuse, fall through to normal call */
   495→            if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {
   496→                if (!call_value(vm, callee, argc)) {
   497→                    return INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR;
   498→                }
   499→                frame = &vm->frames[vm->frame_count - 1];
   500→                break;
   501→            }
   502→
   503→            if (!IS_OBJ(callee) || OBJ_TYPE(callee) != OBJ_CLOSURE) {
   504→                runtime_error(vm, "Can only call functions and closures.");
   505→                return INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR;
   506→            }
   507→
   508→            lisa_obj_closure *closure = AS_CLOSURE(callee);
   509→            if (argc != closure->function->arity) {
   510→                runtime_error(vm, "Expected %d arguments but got %d.",
   511→                              closure->function->arity, argc);
   512→                return INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR;
   513→            }
   514→
   515→            /* Close upvalues for the current frame */
   516→            close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);
   517→
   518→            /* Slide callee + args down over the current frame */
   519→            lisa_value *src = vm->stack_top - argc - 1;
   520→            memmove(frame->slots, src, (size_t)(argc + 1) * sizeof(lisa_value));
   521→            vm->stack_top = frame->slots + argc + 1;
   522→
   523→            /* Reuse the current frame */
   524→            frame->closure = closure;
   525→            frame->ip = closure->function->chunk.code;
   526→            /* JIT dispatch for tail calls */
   527→            if (closure->function->jit_code) {
   528→                lisa_jit_fn jit_fn = (lisa_jit_fn)closure->function->jit_code;
   529→                lisa_value result = jit_fn(vm, frame->closure, frame->slots);
   530→                if (IS_TAIL_PENDING(result))
   531→                    result = jit_trampoline(vm, result);
   532→                close_upvalues(vm, frame->slots);
   533→                vm->frame_count--;
   534→                if (vm->frame_count == base_frame) {
   535→                    if (base_frame == 0) pop(vm);
   536→                    else {
   537→                        vm->stack_top = frame->slots;
   538→                        push(vm, result);
   539→                    }
   540→                    return INTERPRET_OK;
   541→                }

  "file_path": "<claude-internal>",
  "content": "# Implement Fibers and Channels for Lisa\n\n## Context\n\nLisa is a Lisp-like language with a bytecode VM and JIT compiler (using cj). The user wants green threads / fiber support with Go-style channel syntax (`chan`, `spawn`, `send`, `recv`). We chose asymmetric coroutines as the underlying primitive, with full JIT support. The key insight is: each fiber gets its own C stack (mmap'd), so JIT-compiled code runs unmodified — yield happens inside C helper functions, never mid-JIT-code.\n\n## Design Overview\n\n- **Fiber**: owns a lisa VM stack + call frames + a C stack for JIT code. Cooperative scheduling.\n- **Channel**: unbuffered, synchronizing. `send` blocks until a receiver is ready; `recv` blocks until a sender is ready.\n- **Scheduler**: simple run queue (circular buffer). `yield`/`send`/`recv` return to the scheduler, which picks the next runnable fiber.\n- **No compiler changes**: `chan`, `spawn`, `send`, `recv` are native functions resolved via `OP_GET_GLOBAL` + `OP_CALL`.\n\n## Files Modified\n\n1. **`lisa/fiber.h`** (new) — fiber and channel structs, scheduler API\n2. **`lisa/fiber.c`** (new) — implementation: context switch, scheduler, channel ops\n3. **`lisa/object.h`** — add `OBJ_FIBER`, `OBJ_CHANNEL` types\n4. **`lisa/object.c`** — allocation, GC marking, freeing for fiber/channel objects\n5. **`lisa/vm.h`** — add `lisa_fiber *current_fiber` + scheduler state to `lisa_vm`; change stack/frames to pointers\n6. **`lisa/vm.c`** — register native functions, swap fiber context on yield, update GC sync\n7. **`lisa/value.h`** — (no changes — fibers/channels are OBJ-tagged values like closures)\n\n## Step 1: Fiber Object (`lisa/fiber.h`, `lisa/object.h`)\n\n```c\n// In object.h — add to lisa_obj_type enum:\nOBJ_FIBER,\nOBJ_CHANNEL,\n\n// In fiber.h:\ntypedef enum {\n    FIBER_READY,      // in run queue, not yet started or resumed\n    FIBER_RUNNING,    // currently executing\n    FIBER_SUSPENDED,  // yielded

session 6. fibers and channels

human

Implement the following plan:

Implement Fibers and Channels for Lisa

Context

Lisa is a Lisp-like language with a bytecode VM and JIT compiler (using cj). The user wants green threads / fiber support with Go-style channel syntax (chan, spawn, send, recv). We chose asymmetric coroutines as the underlying primitive, with full JIT support. The key insight is: each fiber gets its own C stack (mmap'd), so JIT-compiled code runs unmodified — yield happens inside C helper functions, never mid-JIT-code.

Design Overview

- Fiber: owns a lisa VM stack + call frames + a C stack for JIT code. Cooperative scheduling.

- Channel: unbuffered, synchronizing. send blocks until a receiver is ready; recv blocks until a sender is ready.

- Scheduler: simple run queue (circular buffer). yield/send/recv return to the scheduler, which picks the next runnable fiber.

- No compiler changes: chan, spawn, send, recv are native functions resolved via OP_GET_GLOBAL + OP_CALL.

Files Modified

1. lisa/fiber.h (new) — fiber and channel structs, scheduler API

2. lisa/fiber.c (new) — implementation: context switch, scheduler, channel ops

3. lisa/object.h — add OBJ_FIBER, OBJ_CHANNEL types

4. lisa/object.c — allocation, GC marking, freeing for fiber/channel objects

5. lisa/vm.h — add lisa_fiber *current_fiber + scheduler state to lisa_vm; change stack/frames to pointers

6. lisa/vm.c — register native functions, swap fiber context on yield, update GC sync

7. lisa/value.h — (no changes — fibers/channels are OBJ-tagged values like closures)

Step 1: Fiber Object (lisa/fiber.h, lisa/object.h)

// In object.h — add to lisa_obj_type enum:
OBJ_FIBER,
OBJ_CHANNEL,

// In fiber.h:
typedef enum {
    FIBER_READY,      // in run queue, not yet started or resumed
    FIBER_RUNNING,    // currently executing
    FIBER_SUSPENDED,  // yielded or blocked on channel
    FIBER_DONE,       // function returned
} lisa_fiber_state;

typedef struct lisa_fiber {
    lisa_obj obj;
    lisa_fiber_state state;

    // Lisa VM state (owned by this fiber)
    lisa_value *stack;          // heap-allocated, STACK_MAX
    lisa_value *stack_top;
    lisa_call_frame *frames;   // heap-allocated, FRAMES_MAX
    int frame_count;
    lisa_obj_upvalue *open_upvalues;

    // C stack for JIT (mmap'd with guard page)
    void *c_stack;             // base of mmap region
    size_t c_stack_size;       // total mmap size including guard
    void *c_sp;                // saved C stack pointer (for context switch)

    // Coroutine state
    lisa_value result;         // value passed into/out of yield
    lisa_obj_closure *entry;   // closure to call when first started
    int argc;                  // argument count for entry
} lisa_fiber;

Step 2: Channel Object (lisa/fiber.h, lisa/object.h)

typedef struct lisa_channel {
    lisa_obj obj;
    lisa_value value;          // buffered value (for handoff)
    lisa_fiber *sender;        // fiber blocked on send, or NULL
    lisa_fiber *receiver;      // fiber blocked on recv, or NULL
    bool closed;
} lisa_channel;

Unbuffered semantics:

- send(ch, val): if a receiver is waiting, hand off directly and wake it. Otherwise, block sender.

- recv(ch): if a sender is waiting, take value and wake it. Otherwise, block receiver.

Step 3: Scheduler (lisa/fiber.h)

// In lisa_vm (vm.h):
typedef struct {
    lisa_fiber **queue;      // circular buffer
    int head, tail, capacity;
} lisa_scheduler;

// Add to lisa_vm:
lisa_fiber *current_fiber;
lisa_scheduler scheduler;

Scheduler operations:

- sched_enqueue(vm, fiber) — add fiber to run queue tail

- sched_dequeue(vm) → fiber — remove from head (returns NULL if empty)

- sched_run(vm) — main loop: dequeue next fiber, context-switch to it, repeat until all done

Step 4: VM Stack/Frame Changes (lisa/vm.h, lisa/vm.c)

Currently lisa_vm has:

lisa_call_frame frames[FRAMES_MAX];  // fixed array
lisa_value stack[STACK_MAX];         // fixed array

Change to pointers that point into the current fiber's arrays:

lisa_call_frame *frames;   // points to current_fiber->frames
int frame_count;           // keep (mirrors current_fiber->frame_count)
lisa_value *stack;         // points to current_fiber->stack
lisa_value *stack_top;     // keep (mirrors current_fiber->stack_top)

On lisa_vm_init, create a "main fiber" that owns the original stack/frames. On fiber switch, save VM state into outgoing fiber, load from incoming fiber.

The save/restore is just pointer swaps (O(1)):

static void fiber_save(lisa_vm *vm) {
    lisa_fiber *f = vm->current_fiber;
    f->stack_top = vm->stack_top;
    f->frame_count = vm->frame_count;
    f->open_upvalues = vm->open_upvalues;
}

static void fiber_restore(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_fiber *f) {
    vm->current_fiber = f;
    vm->stack = f->stack;
    vm->stack_top = f->stack_top;
    vm->frames = f->frames;
    vm->frame_count = f->frame_count;
    vm->open_upvalues = f->open_upvalues;
}

Step 5: Context Switch (Assembly)

Each fiber has its own C stack (for JIT-compiled code and C helper calls). Context switching saves/restores callee-saved registers + stack pointer.

ARM64 context switch
// fiber_switch(void **save_sp, void *restore_sp)
// Saves callee-saved regs (x19-x28, x29/fp, x30/lr) + sp onto old stack,
// stores sp into *save_sp, loads new sp from restore_sp, restores regs, returns.
x86-64 context switch
// fiber_switch(void **save_sp, void *restore_sp)
// Saves callee-saved regs (rbx, rbp, r12-r15) + return address onto old stack,
// stores rsp into *save_sp, loads new rsp from restore_sp, restores regs, returns.

Both are ~15 instructions of inline assembly in fiber.c behind #ifdef.

C Stack Allocation
#define FIBER_C_STACK_SIZE (64 * 1024)  // 64 KB per fiber
// mmap with MAP_ANON|MAP_PRIVATE, guard page at bottom via mprotect(PROT_NONE)

When a fiber starts for the first time, we set up its C stack so that fiber_switch "returns" into a trampoline function that calls the fiber's entry closure.

Step 6: Native Functions (lisa/vm.c)

(chan) → creates a new channel
static lisa_value native_chan(int argc, lisa_value *args);
// Returns: LISA_OBJ(new channel)
(spawn fn arg1 arg2 ...) → creates a fiber, enqueues it
static lisa_value native_spawn(int argc, lisa_value *args);
// args[0] = closure, args[1..] = arguments
// Creates fiber with entry=closure, copies args onto fiber's stack
// Enqueues fiber, returns LISA_OBJ(fiber)
(send ch val) → send value on channel
static lisa_value native_send(int argc, lisa_value *args);
// If receiver waiting: hand off, wake receiver, return nil
// Else: block sender, yield to scheduler
(recv ch) → receive value from channel
static lisa_value native_recv(int argc, lisa_value *args);
// If sender waiting: take value, wake sender, return value
// Else: block receiver, yield to scheduler
(yield) / (yield val) → cooperative yield
static lisa_value native_yield(int argc, lisa_value *args);
// Save current fiber, enqueue it, switch to scheduler

Native function signature change: Currently lisa_native_fn is lisa_value (*)(int argc, lisa_value *args) — it doesn't have access to lisa_vm. The channel/spawn natives need the VM pointer. Two options:

Option A: Change lisa_native_fn to lisa_value (*)(lisa_vm *vm, int argc, lisa_value *args). This touches all existing natives (4 functions: +, -, *, /) and the call sites in vm.c and jit.c.

Option B: Add a separate lisa_native_vm_fn type and OBJ_NATIVE_VM object type. More surgical but adds a parallel path.

Recommended: Option A. Only 4 existing natives + 2 call sites. The JIT's lisa_jit_call_helper already has the VM pointer and passes args from the stack, so the change is mechanical.

Step 7: GC Integration (lisa/object.c)

Marking
case OBJ_FIBER: {
    lisa_fiber *fiber = (lisa_fiber*)obj;
    // Mark fiber's stack values
    for (lisa_value *slot = fiber->stack; slot < fiber->stack_top; slot++)
        mark_value(*slot);
    // Mark fiber's open upvalues
    for (lisa_obj_upvalue *uv = fiber->open_upvalues; uv; uv = uv->next)
        mark_object((lisa_obj*)uv);
    // Mark frame closures
    for (int i = 0; i < fiber->frame_count; i++)
        mark_object((lisa_obj*)fiber->frames[i].closure);
    // Mark entry closure
    if (fiber->entry) mark_object((lisa_obj*)fiber->entry);
    mark_value(fiber->result);
    break;
}
case OBJ_CHANNEL: {
    lisa_channel *ch = (lisa_channel*)obj;
    mark_value(ch->value);
    if (ch->sender) mark_object((lisa_obj*)ch->sender);
    if (ch->receiver) mark_object((lisa_obj*)ch->receiver);
    break;
}
Freeing
case OBJ_FIBER: {
    lisa_fiber *fiber = (lisa_fiber*)obj;
    free(fiber->stack);
    free(fiber->frames);
    if (fiber->c_stack) munmap(fiber->c_stack, fiber->c_stack_size);
    free(obj);
    break;
}
case OBJ_CHANNEL:
    free(obj);
    break;
Root marking update

In sync_gc_roots / mark_roots, iterate ALL fibers (current + run queue + blocked on channels), not just the current stack. The simplest approach: keep a linked list of all live fibers in the VM and walk it during GC.

Step 8: JIT Compatibility

No JIT changes needed. Here's why:

- Each fiber has its own C stack. JIT-compiled code runs on the C stack.

- send/recv/yield are native functions. JIT calls them via lisa_jit_call_helper → call_value → native->function(...).

- Inside the native, we do fiber_switch which saves/restores the C stack pointer. The JIT's call frame is preserved on the C stack.

- When the fiber is resumed later, fiber_switch restores the C stack, and the native function returns normally. The JIT code continues from where it left off.

The only requirement: lisa_jit_call_helper must call sync_gc_roots before invoking natives that might yield (already done — sync_gc_roots is called before all helper dispatches).

Implementation Order

1. Add OBJ_FIBER, OBJ_CHANNEL to object.h enum + type checks/cast macros

2. Create lisa/fiber.h with structs

3. Change VM stack/frames from arrays to pointers; create main fiber in lisa_vm_init

4. Verify all existing tests still pass (refactor only, no new behavior)

5. Change lisa_native_fn signature to include lisa_vm *vm; update existing natives + call sites

6. Verify tests pass again

7. Implement fiber allocation, C stack setup, context switch assembly in fiber.c

8. Implement scheduler (enqueue/dequeue/run loop)

9. Implement native functions: spawn, yield, chan, send, recv

10. Add GC marking/freeing for fiber/channel

11. Test with examples

Verification

1. make dev compiles on both x86-64 and ARM64

2. All existing tests pass (bin/test_builder, bin/test_arm64/bin/test_x86)

3. Lisa basics still work:

```

bin/lisa -e '(def fib (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) n (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2)))))) (println (fib 30))'

```

4. Simple fiber test:

```

bin/lisa -e '(def ch (chan)) (spawn (fn [] (send ch 42))) (println (recv ch))'

;; should print 42

```

5. Producer-consumer:

```

bin/lisa -e '

(def ch (chan))

(spawn (fn []

(def loop (fn [i]

(if (<= i 5) (do (send ch i) (loop (+ i 1))))))

(loop 1)))

(def collect (fn [n acc]

(if (<= n 0) acc

(collect (- n 1) (+ acc (recv ch))))))

(println (collect 5 0))

'

;; should print 15

```

6. JIT + fibers:

```

bin/lisa -e '

(def ch (chan))

(def fib (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) n (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2))))))

(spawn (fn [] (send ch (fib 20))))

(println (recv ch))

'

;; should print 6765, with fib JIT-compiled inside the spawned fiber

```

If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: <claude-internal>

    12→    OBJ_CLOSURE,
    13→    OBJ_UPVALUE,
    14→    OBJ_LIST,
    15→    OBJ_NATIVE,
    16→} lisa_obj_type;
    17→
    18→struct lisa_obj {
    19→    lisa_obj_type type;
    20→    bool is_marked;
    21→    struct lisa_obj *next;
    22→};
    23→
    24→typedef struct {
    25→    lisa_obj obj;
    26→    int length;
    27→    uint32_t hash;
    28→    char chars[];   /* flexible array */
    29→} lisa_obj_string;
    30→
    31→typedef struct {
    32→    lisa_obj obj;
    33→    int arity;
    34→    int upvalue_count;
    35→    lisa_chunk chunk;
    36→    lisa_obj_string *name;
    37→    void *jit_code;    /* JIT-compiled native code, or NULL */
    38→    void *jit_ctx;     /* cj_ctx* for cleanup, or NULL */
    39→} lisa_obj_function;
    40→
    41→typedef struct lisa_obj_upvalue {
    42→    lisa_obj obj;
    43→    lisa_value *location;
    44→    lisa_value closed;
    45→    struct lisa_obj_upvalue *next;
    46→} lisa_obj_upvalue;
    47→
    48→typedef struct {
    49→    lisa_obj obj;
    50→    lisa_obj_function *function;

    54→

    60→

    62→
    63→typedef struct {
    64→    lisa_obj obj;
    65→    lisa_native_fn function;
    66→    const char *name;

result
1→#include "object.h"
     2→#include "jit.h"
     3→#include <stdio.h>
     4→#include <stdlib.h>
     5→#include <string.h>
     6→
     7→/* --- Hashing --- */
     8→
     9→static uint32_t hash_string(const char *key, int length) {
    10→    uint32_t hash = 2166136261u;
    11→    for (int i = 0; i < length; i++) {
    12→        hash ^= (uint8_t)key[i];
    13→        hash *= 16777619u;
    14→    }
    15→    return hash;
    16→}
    17→
    18→/* --- Allocation helpers --- */
    19→
    20→static lisa_obj *allocate_object(lisa_gc *gc, size_t size, lisa_obj_type type) {
    21→    lisa_obj *obj = malloc(size);
    22→    obj->type = type;
    23→    obj->is_marked = false;
    24→    obj->next = gc->objects;
    25→    gc->objects = obj;
    26→    gc->bytes_allocated += size;
    27→    return obj;
    28→}
    29→
    30→#define ALLOCATE_OBJ(gc, type, obj_type) \
    31→    ((type*)allocate_object(gc, sizeof(type), obj_type))
    32→
    33→/* --- String interning --- */
    34→
    35→static void string_table_grow(lisa_gc *gc) {
    36→    int new_cap = gc->string_capacity < 8 ? 8 : gc->string_capacity * 2;
    37→    lisa_obj_string **new_table = calloc((size_t)new_cap, sizeof(lisa_obj_string*));
    38→
    39→    /* Rehash */
    40→    for (int i = 0; i < gc->string_capacity; i++) {
    41→        lisa_obj_string *s = gc->strings[i];
    42→        if (s == NULL) continue;
    43→        int idx = (int)(s->hash % (uint32_t)new_cap);
    44→        while (new_table[idx] != NULL) {
    45→            idx = (idx + 1) % new_cap;
    46→        }
    47→        new_table[idx] = s;
    48→    }
    49→
    50→    free(gc->strings);
    51→    gc->strings = new_table;
    52→    gc->string_capacity = new_cap;
    53→}
    54→
    55→static lisa_obj_string *string_table_find(lisa_gc *gc, const char *chars, int length, uint32_t hash) {
    56→    if (gc->string_count == 0) return NULL;
    57→
    58→    int idx = (int)(hash % (uint32_t)gc->string_capacity);
    59→    for (;;) {
    60→        lisa_obj_string *s = gc->strings[idx];
    61→        if (s == NULL) return NULL;
    62→        if (s->length == length && s->hash == hash &&
    63→            memcmp(s->chars, chars, (size_t)length) == 0) {
    64→            return s;
    65→        }
    66→        idx = (idx + 1) % gc->string_capacity;
    67→    }
    68→}
    69→
    70→static void string_table_set(lisa_gc *gc, lisa_obj_string *str) {
    71→    if (gc->string_count + 1 > gc->string_capacity * 3 / 4) {
    72→        string_table_grow(gc);
    73→    }
    74→    int idx = (int)(str->hash % (uint32_t)gc->string_capacity);
    75→    while (gc->strings[idx] != NULL) {
    76→        idx = (idx + 1) % gc->string_capacity;
    77→    }
    78→    gc->strings[idx] = str;
    79→    gc->string_count++;
    80→}
    81→
    82→static void string_table_remove(lisa_gc *gc, lisa_obj_string *str) {
    83→    if (gc->string_count == 0) return;
    84→    int idx = (int)(str->hash % (
... (truncated)

1→#ifndef LISA_VM_H
     2→#define LISA_VM_H
     3→
     4→#include "value.h"
     5→#include "object.h"
     6→#include "chunk.h"
     7→
     8→#define STACK_MAX 4096
     9→#define FRAMES_MAX 256
    10→
    11→typedef struct {
    12→    lisa_obj_closure *closure;
    13→    uint8_t *ip;
    14→    lisa_value *slots; /* pointer into vm stack */
    15→} lisa_call_frame;
    16→
    17→typedef struct {
    18→    lisa_call_frame frames[FRAMES_MAX];
    19→    int frame_count;
    20→
    21→    lisa_value stack[STACK_MAX];
    22→    lisa_value *stack_top;
    23→
    24→    /* Global variables: hash table of string -> value */
    25→    lisa_obj_string **global_names;
    26→    lisa_value *global_values;
    27→    int global_count;
    28→    int global_capacity;
    29→
    30→    lisa_obj_upvalue *open_upvalues;
    31→
    32→    bool jit_enabled;
    33→
    34→    lisa_gc gc;
    35→} lisa_vm;
    36→
    37→typedef enum {
    38→    INTERPRET_OK,
    39→    INTERPRET_COMPILE_ERROR,
    40→    INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR,
    41→} lisa_interpret_result;
    42→
    43→void lisa_vm_init(lisa_vm *vm);
    44→void lisa_vm_free(lisa_vm *vm);
    45→lisa_interpret_result lisa_interpret(lisa_vm *vm, const char *source);
    46→
    47→/* Run the bytecode interpreter starting from the current top frame.
    48→   Stops when frame_count drops to base_frame. */
    49→lisa_interpret_result lisa_run(lisa_vm *vm, int base_frame);
    50→
    51→/* JIT trampoline sentinel: top 16 bits = 0xDEAD (invalid as any lisa_value tag),
    52→   low 8 bits = argc for the pending tail call. */
    53→#define LISA_TAIL_PENDING_BASE ((uint64_t)0xDEAD000000000000)
    54→#define LISA_TAIL_PENDING(argc) (LISA_TAIL_PENDING_BASE | (uint64_t)(argc))
    55→#define IS_TAIL_PENDING(v) (((v) >> 48) == 0xDEAD)
    56→#define TAIL_PENDING_ARGC(v) ((int)((v) & 0xFF))
    57→
    58→/* Helpers called by JIT-compiled code */
    59→lisa_value lisa_jit_call_helper(lisa_vm *vm, int argc);
    60→lisa_value lisa_jit_get_global(lisa_vm *vm, int name_idx);
    61→void lisa_jit_def_global(lisa_vm *vm, int name_idx, lisa_value value);
    62→lisa_value lisa_jit_get_upvalue(lisa_obj_closure *closure, int idx);

    65→lisa_value lisa_jit_make_closure(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_closure *enclosing,

    67→void lisa_jit_runtime_error(lisa_vm *vm, const char *msg);

    69→lisa_value lisa_jit_sub(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value a, lisa_value b);

    72→lisa_value lisa_jit_mod(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value a, lisa_value b);

1→#include "vm.h"
     2→#include "jit.h"
     3→#include "compiler.h"
     4→#include "parser.h"
     5→#include <stdarg.h>
     6→#include <stdio.h>
     7→#include <stdlib.h>
     8→#include <string.h>
     9→#include <math.h>
    10→
    11→typedef lisa_value (*lisa_jit_fn)(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_closure *closure,
    12→                                  lisa_value *slots);
    13→
    14→static lisa_value jit_trampoline(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value result);
    15→
    16→/* --- Stack operations --- */
    17→
    18→static void push(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value value) {
    19→    *vm->stack_top = value;
    20→    vm->stack_top++;
    21→}
    22→
    23→static lisa_value pop(lisa_vm *vm) {
    24→    vm->stack_top--;
    25→    return *vm->stack_top;
    26→}
    27→
    28→static lisa_value peek(lisa_vm *vm, int distance) {
    29→    return vm->stack_top[-1 - distance];
    30→}
    31→
    32→static void reset_stack(lisa_vm *vm) {
    33→    vm->stack_top = vm->stack;
    34→    vm->frame_count = 0;
    35→    vm->open_upvalues = NULL;
    36→}
    37→
    38→static void runtime_error(lisa_vm *vm, const char *format, ...) {
    39→    va_list args;
    40→    va_start(args, format);
    41→    vfprintf(stderr, format, args);
    42→    va_end(args);
    43→    fputs("\n", stderr);
    44→
    45→    for (int i = vm->frame_count - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
    46→        lisa_call_frame *frame = &vm->frames[i];
    47→        lisa_obj_function *fn = frame->closure->function;
    48→        size_t offset = (size_t)(frame->ip - fn->chunk.code - 1);
    49→        int line = fn->chunk.lines[offset];
    50→        fprintf(stderr, "[line %d] in ", line);
    51→        if (fn->name == NULL) {
    52→            fprintf(stderr, "script\n");
    53→        } else {
    54→            fprintf(stderr, "%s()\n", fn->name->chars);
    55→        }
    56→    }
    57→
    58→    reset_stack(vm);
    59→}
    60→
    61→/* --- Globals hash table --- */
    62→
    63→static void globals_grow(lisa_vm *vm) {
    64→    int new_cap = vm->global_capacity < 8 ? 8 : vm->global_capacity * 2;
    65→    lisa_obj_string **new_names = calloc((size_t)new_cap, sizeof(lisa_obj_string*));
    66→    lisa_value *new_values = calloc((size_t)new_cap, sizeof(lisa_value));
    67→
    68→    for (int i = 0; i < vm->global_capacity; i++) {
    69→        if (vm->global_names[i] == NULL) continue;
    70→        uint32_t idx = vm->global_names[i]->hash % (uint32_t)new_cap;
    71→        while (new_names[idx] != NULL) {
    72→            idx = (idx + 1) % (uint32_t)new_cap;
    73→        }
    74→        new_names[idx] = vm->global_names[i];
    75→        new_values[idx] = vm->global_values[i];
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    free(vm->global_names);
    79→    free(vm->global_values);
    80→    vm->global_names = new_names;
    81→    vm->global_values = new_values;
    82→    vm->global_capacity = new_cap;
    83→}
    84→
    85→static int globals_find(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_string
... (truncated)

result
1→#ifndef LISA_VALUE_H
     2→#define LISA_VALUE_H
     3→
     4→#include <stdbool.h>
     5→#include <stdint.h>
     6→#include <stdio.h>
     7→
     8→/*
     9→ * NaN-boxed value representation.
    10→ *
    11→ * IEEE 754 double: if bits [62:52] are all 1 and bit 51 is 1, it's a quiet NaN.
    12→ * We use the remaining payload bits for non-double values.
    13→ *
    14→ * Layout:
    15→ *   Double: any bit pattern that is NOT a quiet NaN with our tag prefix
    16→ *   Tagged: [sign=1][exp=0x7FF][quiet=1][tag 50:48][payload 47:0]
    17→ *
    18→ * Tags (bits 50:48):
    19→ *   000 = nil
    20→ *   001 = bool (payload bit 0)
    21→ *   010 = int  (48-bit sign-extended integer)
    22→ *   011 = object pointer (48-bit)
    23→ */
    24→
    25→typedef uint64_t lisa_value;
    26→
    27→/* The quiet NaN mask: sign(1) + exponent(0x7FF) + quiet(1) = bits 63,62:52,51 */
    28→#define QNAN    ((uint64_t)0x7FFC000000000000)
    29→#define SIGN_BIT ((uint64_t)0x8000000000000000)
    30→
    31→/* Tag values shifted into bits 50:48 */
    32→#define TAG_NIL    ((uint64_t)0x0000000000000000)  /* 000 */
    33→#define TAG_BOOL   ((uint64_t)0x0001000000000000)  /* 001 */
    34→#define TAG_INT    ((uint64_t)0x0002000000000000)  /* 010 */
    35→#define TAG_OBJ    ((uint64_t)0x0003000000000000)  /* 011 */
    36→
    37→#define TAG_MASK   ((uint64_t)0x0003000000000000)
    38→#define PAYLOAD_MASK ((uint64_t)0x0000FFFFFFFFFFFF) /* 48 bits */
    39→
    40→/* Construct values */
    41→#define LISA_NIL        (QNAN | TAG_NIL)
    42→#define LISA_TRUE       (QNAN | TAG_BOOL | 1)
    43→#define LISA_FALSE      (QNAN | TAG_BOOL | 0)
    44→#define LISA_BOOL(b)    ((b) ? LISA_TRUE : LISA_FALSE)
    45→#define LISA_INT(i)     (QNAN | TAG_INT | ((uint64_t)(i) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
    46→#define LISA_OBJ(ptr)   (QNAN | TAG_OBJ | ((uint64_t)(uintptr_t)(ptr) & PAYLOAD_MASK))
    47→
    48→static inline lisa_value lisa_double(double d) {
    49→    union { double d; uint64_t u; } conv;
    50→    conv.d = d;
    51→    return conv.u;
    52→}
    53→
    54→/* Type checks */
    55→#define IS_NIL(v)    ((v) == LISA_NIL)
    56→#define IS_BOOL(v)   (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_BOOL))
    57→#define IS_INT(v)    (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_INT))
    58→#define IS_OBJ(v)    (((v) & (QNAN | TAG_MASK)) == (QNAN | TAG_OBJ))
    59→#define IS_DOUBLE(v) (((v) & QNAN) != QNAN)
    60→
    61→/* Extract values */
    62→#define AS_BOOL(v)   ((v) & 1)
    63→
    64→static inline int64_t AS_INT(lisa_value v) {
    65→    /* Sign-extend the 48-bit payload */
    66→    uint64_t raw = v & PAYLOAD_MASK;
    67→    if (raw & ((uint64_t)1 << 47)) {
    68→        raw |= (uint64_t)0xFFFF000000000000;
    69→    }
    70→    return (int64_t)raw;
    71→}
    72→
    73→static inline double AS_DOUBLE(lisa_value v) {
    74→    union { uint64_t u; double d; } conv;
    75→    conv.u = v;
    76→    return conv.d;
    77→}
    78→
    79→#define AS_
... (truncated)

1→#ifndef LISA_CHUNK_H
     2→#define LISA_CHUNK_H
     3→
     4→#include "value.h"
     5→#include <stdint.h>
     6→
     7→typedef enum {
     8→    OP_CONSTANT,      /* [idx]          push constants[idx] */
     9→    OP_NIL,           /*                push nil */
    10→    OP_TRUE,          /*                push true */
    11→    OP_FALSE,         /*                push false */
    12→    OP_POP,           /*                pop top */
    13→
    14→    OP_GET_LOCAL,     /* [slot]         push stack[base+slot] */
    15→    OP_SET_LOCAL,     /* [slot]         stack[base+slot] = peek */
    16→    OP_GET_UPVALUE,   /* [idx]          push *upvalues[idx]->location */
    17→    OP_SET_UPVALUE,   /* [idx]          *upvalues[idx]->location = peek */
    18→    OP_GET_GLOBAL,    /* [idx]          push globals[constants[idx]] */
    19→    OP_DEF_GLOBAL,    /* [idx]          globals[constants[idx]] = pop */
    20→
    21→    OP_ADD,
    22→    OP_SUB,
    23→    OP_MUL,
    24→    OP_DIV,
    25→    OP_MOD,
    26→    OP_NEGATE,
    27→
    28→    OP_EQUAL,
    29→    OP_NOT_EQUAL,
    30→    OP_LESS,
    31→    OP_LESS_EQUAL,
    32→    OP_GREATER,
    33→    OP_GREATER_EQUAL,
    34→
    35→    OP_NOT,
    36→
    37→    OP_JUMP,          /* [lo][hi]       ip += offset */
    38→    OP_JUMP_IF_FALSE, /* [lo][hi]       if falsey(pop) ip += offset */
    39→    OP_LOOP,          /* [lo][hi]       ip -= offset */
    40→
    41→    OP_CLOSURE,       /* [idx] then pairs of [is_local, index] */
    42→    OP_CALL,          /* [argc]         call top function with argc args */
    43→    OP_TAIL_CALL,     /* [argc]         tail call: reuse current frame */
    44→    OP_RETURN,        /*                return top of stack */
    45→
    46→    OP_CLOSE_UPVALUE, /*                close upvalue at stack top */
    47→
    48→    OP_CONS,          /*                push cons(pop2, pop1) */
    49→    OP_CAR,           /*                push car(pop) */
    50→    OP_CDR,           /*                push cdr(pop) */
    51→    OP_LIST,          /* [n]            pop n items, build list */
    52→
    53→    OP_PRINTLN,       /* [argc]         print argc values with spaces, newline */
    54→} lisa_op;
    55→
    56→/* Dynamic array of constants */
    57→typedef struct {
    58→    int count;
    59→    int capacity;
    60→    lisa_value *values;
    61→} lisa_value_array;
    62→
    63→void lisa_value_array_init(lisa_value_array *arr);
    64→void lisa_value_array_write(lisa_value_array *arr, lisa_value value);
    65→void lisa_value_array_free(lisa_value_array *arr);
    66→
    67→/* Bytecode chunk */
    68→typedef struct {
    69→    int count;
    70→    int capacity;
    71→    uint8_t *code;
    72→    int *lines;          /* source line per bytecode byte */
    73→    lisa_value_array constants;
    74→} lisa_chunk;
    75→
    76→void lisa_chunk_init(lisa_chunk *chunk);
    77→void lisa_chunk_write(lisa_chunk *chunk, uint8_t byte, int l

1→#include "jit.h"
     2→#include "chunk.h"
     3→#include "vm.h"
     4→
     5→#pragma GCC diagnostic push
     6→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wunused-parameter"
     7→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wunused-but-set-variable"
     8→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wunused-variable"
     9→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wc23-extensions"
    10→#include "ctx.h"
    11→#include "op.h"
    12→#include "register.h"
    13→#include "builder.h"
    14→#pragma GCC diagnostic pop
    15→
    16→#include <assert.h>
    17→#include <stddef.h>
    18→#include <stdio.h>
    19→#include <stdlib.h>
    20→#include <string.h>
    21→
    22→typedef lisa_value (*lisa_jit_fn)(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_closure *closure,
    23→                                  lisa_value *slots);
    24→
    25→/* ===== Platform Register Definitions ===== */
    26→
    27→#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
    28→
    29→#define REG_VM       "rbx"
    30→#define REG_SLOTS    "r12"
    31→#define REG_CLOSURE  "r13"
    32→#define REG_STKTOP   "r14"
    33→#define REG_CONSTS   "r15"
    34→
    35→#define REG_CACHE0   "r8"
    36→#define REG_CACHE1   "r9"
    37→#define REG_CACHE2   "r10"
    38→#define REG_CACHE3   "r11"
    39→
    40→#define REG_TMP1     "rax"
    41→#define REG_TMP2     "rcx"
    42→#define REG_TMP3     "rdx"
    43→#define REG_TMP4     "rsi"
    44→#define REG_TMP5     "rdi"
    45→#define REG_CALLADDR "r10"
    46→
    47→#define REG_ARG0     "rdi"
    48→#define REG_ARG1     "rsi"
    49→#define REG_ARG2     "rdx"
    50→#define REG_ARG3     "rcx"
    51→#define REG_RET      "rax"
    52→
    53→#define EMIT_JEQ(ctx, label) cj_jz(ctx, label)
    54→#define EMIT_JNE(ctx, label) cj_jnz(ctx, label)
    55→#define EMIT_JLT(ctx, label) cj_jl(ctx, label)
    56→#define EMIT_JLE(ctx, label) cj_jle(ctx, label)
    57→#define EMIT_JGT(ctx, label) cj_jg(ctx, label)
    58→#define EMIT_JGE(ctx, label) cj_jge(ctx, label)
    59→#define EMIT_JMP(ctx, label) cj_jmp(ctx, label)
    60→#define EMIT_JB(ctx, label)  cj_jb(ctx, label)
    61→
    62→#elif defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
    63→
    64→#define REG_VM       "x19"
    65→#define REG_SLOTS    "x20"
    66→#define REG_CLOSURE  "x21"
    67→#define REG_STKTOP   "x22"
    68→#define REG_CONSTS   "x23"
    69→
    70→#define REG_CACHE0   "x10"
    71→#define REG_CACHE1   "x11"
    72→#define REG_CACHE2   "x12"
    73→#define REG_CACHE3   "x13"
    74→
    75→#define REG_TMP1     "x0"
    76→#define REG_TMP2     "x1"
    77→#define REG_TMP3     "x2"
    78→#define REG_TMP4     "x3"
    79→#define REG_TMP5     "x4"
    80→#define REG_CALLADDR "x9"
    81→
    82→#define REG_ARG0     "x0"
    83→#define REG_ARG1     "x1"
    84→#define REG_ARG2     "x2"
    85→#define REG_ARG3     "x3"
    86→#define REG_RET      "x0"
    87→
    88→#define EMIT_JEQ(ctx, label) cj_beq(ctx, label)
    89→#define EMIT_JNE(ctx, label) cj_bne(ctx, label)
    90→#define EMIT_JLT(ctx, label) cj_blt(ctx, label)
    91→

Bash — Read Makefile
{
  "command": "cat lisa/Makefile 2>/dev/null || cat cj/Makefile",
  "description": "Read Makefile"
}
result
CC ?= cc
CJ_DIR = ../src
CFLAGS = -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wno-gnu -O2 -I$(CJ_DIR)
DEVFLAGS = -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wno-gnu -Werror -g -O0 -I$(CJ_DIR)
SOURCES = main.c value.c object.c lexer.c parser.c ast.c chunk.c compiler.c vm.c debug.c jit.c $(CJ_DIR)/ctx.c
TARGET = bin/lisa

.PHONY: all dev clean

all:
	mkdir -p bin
	$(CC) $(SOURCES) -o $(TARGET) $(CFLAGS) -lm

dev:
	mkdir -p bin
	$(CC) $(SOURCES) -o $(TARGET) $(DEVFLAGS) -lm

clean:
	rm -rf bin
⚙ Read

Let me also check the main.c and understand how things are currently structured.

Read — lisa/main.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/main.c"
}
result
1→#include "vm.h"
     2→#include <stdio.h>
     3→#include <stdlib.h>
     4→#include <string.h>
     5→
     6→static char *read_file(const char *path) {
     7→    FILE *file = fopen(path, "rb");
     8→    if (file == NULL) {
     9→        fprintf(stderr, "Could not open file '%s'.\n", path);
    10→        return NULL;
    11→    }
    12→
    13→    fseek(file, 0, SEEK_END);
    14→    long size = ftell(file);
    15→    rewind(file);
    16→
    17→    char *buffer = malloc((size_t)size + 1);
    18→    size_t bytes_read = fread(buffer, 1, (size_t)size, file);
    19→    buffer[bytes_read] = '\0';
    20→
    21→    fclose(file);
    22→    return buffer;
    23→}
    24→
    25→static int run_string(const char *source, bool jit) {
    26→    lisa_vm vm;
    27→    lisa_vm_init(&vm);
    28→    vm.jit_enabled = jit;
    29→    lisa_interpret_result result = lisa_interpret(&vm, source);
    30→    lisa_vm_free(&vm);
    31→
    32→    if (result == INTERPRET_COMPILE_ERROR) return 65;
    33→    if (result == INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR) return 70;
    34→    return 0;
    35→}
    36→
    37→static int run_file(const char *path, bool jit) {
    38→    char *source = read_file(path);
    39→    if (source == NULL) return 74;
    40→    int result = run_string(source, jit);
    41→    free(source);
    42→    return result;
    43→}
    44→
    45→int main(int argc, char *argv[]) {
    46→    bool jit = true;
    47→    int argi = 1;
    48→
    49→    if (argi < argc && strcmp(argv[argi], "--no-jit") == 0) {
    50→        jit = false;
    51→        argi++;
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    if (argi < argc && argi == argc - 1 && strcmp(argv[argi], "-e") != 0) {
    55→        return run_file(argv[argi], jit);
    56→    }
    57→
    58→    if (argi + 1 < argc && strcmp(argv[argi], "-e") == 0) {
    59→        return run_string(argv[argi + 1], jit);
    60→    }
    61→
    62→    fprintf(stderr, "Usage: lisa [--no-jit] <file.lisa>\n");
    63→    fprintf(stderr, "       lisa [--no-jit] -e \"<expression>\"\n");
    64→    return 64;
    65→}
    66→

⚙ Edit

Now I need to add forward declarations for lisa_fiber and lisa_channel in object.h since they're used in the cast macros. Actually, the structs will be defined in fiber.h — but object.h needs the forward declarations. Let me add them.

Edit — lisa/object.h
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/object.h",
  "old_string": "/* GC state */",
  "new_string": "/* Forward declarations for fiber types (defined in fiber.h) */\ntypedef struct lisa_fiber lisa_fiber;\ntypedef struct lisa_channel lisa_channel;\n\n/* GC state */"
}
result
The file lisa/object.h has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Now I also need to add a lisa_fiber linked list pointer to lisa_gc for root marking — we'll need to iterate all fibers during GC. Let me add that.

Edit — lisa/object.h
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/object.h",
  "old_string": "    /* GC marking state — set externally by the VM */\n    lisa_value *stack;\n    int stack_count;\n    lisa_obj_upvalue *open_upvalues;\n} lisa_gc;",
  "new_string": "    /* GC marking state — set externally by the VM */\n    lisa_value *stack;\n    int stack_count;\n    lisa_obj_upvalue *open_upvalues;\n    lisa_fiber *all_fibers;  /* linked list of all live fibers for GC */\n} lisa_gc;"
}
result
The file lisa/object.h has been updated successfully.
⚙ Write

Now create fiber.h:

Write — lisa/fiber.h
{
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.h",
  "content": "#ifndef LISA_FIBER_H\n#define LISA_FIBER_H\n\n#include \"object.h\"\n\ntypedef enum {\n    FIBER_READY,\n    FIBER_RUNNING,\n    FIBER_SUSPENDED,\n    FIBER_DONE,\n} lisa_fiber_state;\n\nstruct lisa_fiber {\n    lisa_obj obj;\n    lisa_fiber_state state;\n\n    /* Lisa VM state (owned by this fiber) */\n    lisa_value *stack;\n    lisa_value *stack_top;\n    lisa_call_frame *frames;\n    int frame_count;\n    lisa_obj_upvalue *open_upvalues;\n\n    /* C stack for JIT (mmap'd with guard page) */\n    void *c_stack;        /* base of mmap region */\n    size_t c_stack_size;  /* total mmap size including guard */\n    void *c_sp;           /* saved C stack pointer (for context switch) */\n\n    /* Coroutine state */\n    lisa_value result;    /* value passed into/out of yield */\n    lisa_obj_closure *entry;  /* closure to call when first started */\n    int argc;\n\n    /* Linked list for GC traversal */\n    lisa_fiber *next_fiber;\n};\n\nstruct lisa_channel {\n    lisa_obj obj;\n    lisa_value value;       /* buffered value (for handoff) */\n    lisa_fiber *sender;     /* fiber blocked on send, or NULL */\n    lisa_fiber *receiver;   /* fiber blocked on recv, or NULL */\n    bool closed;\n};\n\n/* Scheduler */\ntypedef struct {\n    lisa_fiber **queue;\n    int head, tail, capacity;\n} lisa_scheduler;\n\n/* Forward declaration — full definition in vm.h */\ntypedef struct lisa_vm lisa_vm;\n\n/* Fiber lifecycle */\nlisa_fiber *lisa_new_fiber(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_closure *entry, int argc,\n                           lisa_value *args);\nlisa_fiber *lisa_new_main_fiber(lisa_vm *vm);\nvoid lisa_fiber_free_stacks(lisa_fiber *fiber);\n\n/* Channel */\nlisa_channel *lisa_new_channel(lisa_vm *vm);\n\n/* Scheduler */\nvoid lisa_sched_init(lisa_scheduler *sched);\nvoid lisa_sched_free(lisa_scheduler *sched);\nvoid lisa_sched_enqueue(lisa_scheduler *sched, lisa_fiber *fiber);\nlisa_fiber *lisa_sched_dequeue(lisa_scheduler *sched);\
... (truncated)
result
File created successfully at: lisa/fiber.h
⚙ Edit

The lisa_call_frame is defined in vm.h which includes object.h. There's a circular dependency issue. I need to forward-declare lisa_call_frame or restructure. Let me forward-declare it in fiber.h.

Edit — lisa/fiber.h
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.h",
  "old_string": "#include \"object.h\"\n\ntypedef enum {",
  "new_string": "#include \"object.h\"\n\n/* Forward declaration (defined in vm.h) */\ntypedef struct lisa_call_frame lisa_call_frame;\n\ntypedef enum {"
}

⚙ Edit

Now Step 2: Change VM stack/frames from fixed arrays to pointers and create a main fiber.

Edit — lisa/vm.h

  "file_path": "lisa/vm.h",

result
The file lisa/vm.h has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Now change the lisa_vm struct to use pointers and add fiber/scheduler fields. The struct also needs to be named for the forward declaration in fiber.h.

Edit — lisa/vm.h
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.h",
  "old_string": "typedef struct {\n    lisa_call_frame frames[FRAMES_MAX];\n    int frame_count;\n\n    lisa_value stack[STACK_MAX];\n    lisa_value *stack_top;\n\n    /* Global variables: hash table of string -> value */\n    lisa_obj_string **global_names;\n    lisa_value *global_values;\n    int global_count;\n    int global_capacity;\n\n    lisa_obj_upvalue *open_upvalues;\n\n    bool jit_enabled;\n\n    lisa_gc gc;\n} lisa_vm;",
  "new_string": "struct lisa_vm {\n    lisa_call_frame *frames;  /* points to current_fiber->frames */\n    int frame_count;\n\n    lisa_value *stack;        /* points to current_fiber->stack */\n    lisa_value *stack_top;\n\n    /* Global variables: hash table of string -> value */\n    lisa_obj_string **global_names;\n    lisa_value *global_values;\n    int global_count;\n    int global_capacity;\n\n    lisa_obj_upvalue *open_upvalues;\n\n    bool jit_enabled;\n\n    /* Fiber support */\n    lisa_fiber *current_fiber;\n    lisa_fiber *main_fiber;\n    lisa_scheduler scheduler;\n\n    lisa_gc gc;\n};\ntypedef struct lisa_vm lisa_vm;"
}
result
The file lisa/vm.h has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

The issue is a duplicate typedef. Since fiber.h already forward-declares typedef struct lisa_vm lisa_vm;, and vm.h includes fiber.h, I just need the struct definition in vm.h without a second typedef.

Edit — lisa/vm.h
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.h",
  "old_string": "    lisa_gc gc;\n};\ntypedef struct lisa_vm lisa_vm;",
  "new_string": "    lisa_gc gc;\n};"
}
result
The file lisa/vm.h has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit ×2

Now I need to update vm.c — the reset_stack function references vm->stack which was an array but now is a pointer. Also lisa_vm_init needs to allocate the main fiber and set up the pointers.

Edit — lisa/vm.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "old_string": "void lisa_vm_init(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    reset_stack(vm);\n    lisa_gc_init(&vm->gc);\n    vm->global_names = NULL;\n    vm->global_values = NULL;\n    vm->global_count = 0;\n    vm->global_capacity = 0;\n    vm->jit_enabled = true;\n\n    /* Register native functions */\n    define_native(vm, \"+\", native_add, 2);\n    define_native(vm, \"-\", native_sub, -1);\n    define_native(vm, \"*\", native_mul, 2);\n    define_native(vm, \"/\", native_div, 2);\n}",
  "new_string": "void lisa_vm_init(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    lisa_gc_init(&vm->gc);\n    vm->global_names = NULL;\n    vm->global_values = NULL;\n    vm->global_count = 0;\n    vm->global_capacity = 0;\n    vm->jit_enabled = true;\n\n    /* Create main fiber */\n    vm->main_fiber = lisa_new_main_fiber(vm);\n    vm->current_fiber = vm->main_fiber;\n    vm->stack = vm->main_fiber->stack;\n    vm->stack_top = vm->main_fiber->stack;\n    vm->frames = vm->main_fiber->frames;\n    vm->frame_count = 0;\n    vm->open_upvalues = NULL;\n\n    lisa_sched_init(&vm->scheduler);\n\n    /* Register native functions */\n    define_native(vm, \"+\", native_add, 2);\n    define_native(vm, \"-\", native_sub, -1);\n    define_native(vm, \"*\", native_mul, 2);\n    define_native(vm, \"/\", native_div, 2);\n}"
}
result
The file lisa/vm.c has been updated successfully.
Edit — lisa/vm.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "old_string": "void lisa_vm_free(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    free(vm->global_names);\n    free(vm->global_values);\n    lisa_gc_free(&vm->gc);\n    vm->global_names = NULL;\n    vm->global_values = NULL;\n    vm->global_count = 0;\n    vm->global_capacity = 0;\n}",
  "new_string": "void lisa_vm_free(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    lisa_sched_free(&vm->scheduler);\n    free(vm->global_names);\n    free(vm->global_values);\n    lisa_gc_free(&vm->gc);\n    vm->global_names = NULL;\n    vm->global_values = NULL;\n    vm->global_count = 0;\n    vm->global_capacity = 0;\n}"
}
result
The file lisa/vm.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Now update reset_stack — it still references vm->stack which is now a pointer:

Edit — lisa/vm.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "old_string": "static void reset_stack(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    vm->stack_top = vm->stack;\n    vm->frame_count = 0;\n    vm->open_upvalues = NULL;\n}",
  "new_string": "static void reset_stack(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    if (vm->stack) vm->stack_top = vm->stack;\n    vm->frame_count = 0;\n    vm->open_upvalues = NULL;\n}"
}
result
The file lisa/vm.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit, TaskUpdate ×2

Now I also need to update the offsetof references in jit.c. The stack_top field offset in lisa_vm has changed because the struct layout changed. Let me check what the JIT accesses. The JIT uses offsetof(lisa_vm, stack_top) — that's still valid since stack_top is still a member. The key thing is that the offsets are computed at compile time, so they'll auto-adjust. Good.

Now let me also add the #include "fiber.h" to vm.c:

Edit — lisa/vm.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "old_string": "#include \"vm.h\"\n#include \"jit.h\"\n#include \"compiler.h\"\n#include \"parser.h\"",
  "new_string": "#include \"vm.h\"\n#include \"fiber.h\"\n#include \"jit.h\"\n#include \"compiler.h\"\n#include \"parser.h\""
}
result
The file lisa/vm.c has been updated successfully.

Step 3: Change lisa_native_fn signature to include lisa_vm *vm.

Edit — lisa/object.h
{
  "replace_all": false,

The file lisa/object.h has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

But wait — lisa_vm is not forward-declared at this point in object.h. The lisa_vm forward declaration is in fiber.h, but object.h doesn't include fiber.h. I need to add a forward declaration in object.h itself. Let me check the include order.

object.h includes value.h and chunk.h. fiber.h includes object.h. vm.h includes object.h and fiber.h. So object.h doesn't know about lisa_vm. I need to add a forward declaration.

Edit — lisa/object.h
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/object.h",
  "old_string": "/* Forward declarations for fiber types (defined in fiber.h) */\ntypedef struct lisa_fiber lisa_fiber;\ntypedef struct lisa_channel lisa_channel;",
  "new_string": "/* Forward declarations (defined in fiber.h / vm.h) */\ntypedef struct lisa_vm lisa_vm;\ntypedef struct lisa_fiber lisa_fiber;\ntypedef struct lisa_channel lisa_channel;"
}
result
The file lisa/object.h has been updated successfully.

{

}
result
1→#ifndef LISA_OBJECT_H
     2→#define LISA_OBJECT_H
     3→
     4→#include "value.h"
     5→#include "chunk.h"
     6→#include <stdint.h>
     7→#include <stdbool.h>
     8→
     9→typedef enum {
    10→    OBJ_STRING,
    11→    OBJ_FUNCTION,
    12→    OBJ_CLOSURE,
    13→    OBJ_UPVALUE,
    14→    OBJ_LIST,
    15→    OBJ_NATIVE,
    16→    OBJ_FIBER,
    17→    OBJ_CHANNEL,
    18→} lisa_obj_type;
    19→
    20→struct lisa_obj {
    21→    lisa_obj_type type;
    22→    bool is_marked;
    23→    struct lisa_obj *next;
    24→};
    25→
    26→typedef struct {
    27→    lisa_obj obj;
    28→    int length;
    29→    uint32_t hash;
    30→    char chars[];   /* flexible array */
    31→} lisa_obj_string;
    32→
    33→typedef struct {
    34→    lisa_obj obj;
    35→    int arity;
    36→    int upvalue_count;
    37→    lisa_chunk chunk;
    38→    lisa_obj_string *name;
    39→    void *jit_code;    /* JIT-compiled native code, or NULL */
    40→    void *jit_ctx;     /* cj_ctx* for cleanup, or NULL */
    41→} lisa_obj_function;
    42→
    43→typedef struct lisa_obj_upvalue {
    44→    lisa_obj obj;
    45→    lisa_value *location;
    46→    lisa_value closed;
    47→    struct lisa_obj_upvalue *next;
    48→} lisa_obj_upvalue;
    49→
    50→typedef struct {
    51→    lisa_obj obj;
    52→    lisa_obj_function *function;
    53→    lisa_obj_upvalue **upvalues;
    54→    int upvalue_count;
    55→} lisa_obj_closure;
    56→
    57→typedef struct {
    58→    lisa_obj obj;
    59→    lisa_value car;
    60→    lisa_value cdr;
    61→} lisa_obj_list;
    62→
    63→typedef lisa_value (*lisa_native_fn)(lisa_vm *vm, int argc, lisa_value *args);
    64→
    65→typedef struct {
    66→    lisa_obj obj;
    67→    lisa_native_fn function;
    68→    const char *name;
    69→    int arity; /* -1 for variadic */
    70→} lisa_obj_native;
    71→
    72→/* Type checks */

    76→#define IS_CLOSURE(value)  (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_CLOSURE)

    80→#define IS_CHANNEL(value)  (IS_OBJ(value) && OBJ_TYPE(value) == OBJ_CHANNEL)
    81→
    82→/* Cast macros */
    83→#define AS_STRING(value)   ((lisa_obj_string*)AS_OBJ(value))
    84→#define AS_CSTRING(value)  (((lisa_obj_string*)AS_OBJ(value))->chars)
    85→#define AS_FUNCTION(value) ((lisa_obj_function*)AS_OBJ(value))
    86→#define AS_CLOSURE(value)  ((lisa_obj_closure*)AS_OBJ(value))
    87→#define AS_UPVALUE(value)  ((lisa_obj_upvalue*)AS_OBJ(value))
    88→#define AS_NATIVE(value)   ((lisa_obj_native*)AS_OBJ(valu

result
1→#ifndef LISA_FIBER_H
     2→#define LISA_FIBER_H
     3→
     4→#include "object.h"
     5→
     6→/* Forward declaration (defined in vm.h) */
     7→typedef struct lisa_call_frame lisa_call_frame;
     8→
     9→typedef enum {
    10→    FIBER_READY,
    11→    FIBER_RUNNING,
    12→    FIBER_SUSPENDED,
    13→    FIBER_DONE,
    14→} lisa_fiber_state;
    15→
    16→struct lisa_fiber {
    17→    lisa_obj obj;
    18→    lisa_fiber_state state;
    19→
    20→    /* Lisa VM state (owned by this fiber) */
    21→    lisa_value *stack;
    22→    lisa_value *stack_top;
    23→    lisa_call_frame *frames;
    24→    int frame_count;
    25→    lisa_obj_upvalue *open_upvalues;
    26→
    27→    /* C stack for JIT (mmap'd with guard page) */
    28→    void *c_stack;        /* base of mmap region */
    29→    size_t c_stack_size;  /* total mmap size including guard */
    30→    void *c_sp;           /* saved C stack pointer (for context switch) */
    31→
    32→    /* Coroutine state */
    33→    lisa_value result;    /* value passed into/out of yield */
    34→    lisa_obj_closure *entry;  /* closure to call when first started */
    35→    int argc;
    36→
    37→    /* Linked list for GC traversal */
    38→    lisa_fiber *next_fiber;
    39→};
    40→
    41→struct lisa_channel {
    42→    lisa_obj obj;
    43→    lisa_value value;       /* buffered value (for handoff) */
    44→    lisa_fiber *sender;     /* fiber blocked on send, or NULL */
    45→    lisa_fiber *receiver;   /* fiber blocked on recv, or NULL */
    46→    bool closed;
    47→};
    48→
    49→/* Scheduler */
    50→typedef struct {
    51→    lisa_fiber **queue;
    52→    int head, tail, capacity;
    53→} lisa_scheduler;
    54→
    55→/* Forward declaration — full definition in vm.h */
    56→typedef struct lisa_vm lisa_vm;

    60→                           lisa_value *args);

    63→
    64→/* Channel */

    72→bool lisa_sched_empty(lisa_scheduler *sched);
    73→
    74→/* Context switch (saves callee-saved regs + SP) */

    76→
    77→/* Fiber save/restore VM state */

Now update the existing native functions in vm.c to accept the new signature:

Edit — lisa/vm.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "old_string": "/* Built-in native functions for when operators are used as values */\nstatic lisa_value native_add(int argc, lisa_value *args) {\n    (void)argc;\n    if (IS_INT(args[0]) && IS_INT(args[1])) return LISA_INT(AS_INT(args[0]) + AS_INT(args[1]));\n    return lisa_double(lisa_as_number(args[0]) + lisa_as_number(args[1]));\n}\n\nstatic lisa_value native_sub(int argc, lisa_value *args) {\n    (void)argc;\n    if (argc == 1) {\n        if (IS_INT(args[0])) return LISA_INT(-AS_INT(args[0]));\n        return lisa_double(-AS_DOUBLE(args[0]));\n    }\n    if (IS_INT(args[0]) && IS_INT(args[1])) return LISA_INT(AS_INT(args[0]) - AS_INT(args[1]));\n    return lisa_double(lisa_as_number(args[0]) - lisa_as_number(args[1]));\n}\n\nstatic lisa_value native_mul(int argc, lisa_value *args) {\n    (void)argc;\n    if (IS_INT(args[0]) && IS_INT(args[1])) return LISA_INT(AS_INT(args[0]) * AS_INT(args[1]));\n    return lisa_double(lisa_as_number(args[0]) * lisa_as_number(args[1]));\n}\n\nstatic lisa_value native_div(int argc, lisa_value *args) {\n    (void)argc;\n    return lisa_double(lisa_as_number(args[0]) / lisa_as_number(args[1]));\n}",
  "new_string": "/* Built-in native functions for when operators are used as values */\nstatic lisa_value native_add(lisa_vm *vm_, int argc, lisa_value *args) {\n    (void)vm_; (void)argc;\n    if (IS_INT(args[0]) && IS_INT(args[1])) return LISA_INT(AS_INT(args[0]) + AS_INT(args[1]));\n    return lisa_double(lisa_as_number(args[0]) + lisa_as_number(args[1]));\n}\n\nstatic lisa_value native_sub(lisa_vm *vm_, int argc, lisa_value *args) {\n    (void)vm_;\n    if (argc == 1) {\n        if (IS_INT(args[0])) return LISA_INT(-AS_INT(args[0]));\n        return lisa_double(-AS_DOUBLE(args[0]));\n    }\n    if (IS_INT(args[0]) && IS_INT(args[1])) return LISA_INT(AS_INT(args[0]) - AS_INT(args[1]));\n    return lisa_double(lisa_as_number(args[0]) - lisa_as_number(args[1]));\n}\n\nstatic lisa_
