{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "old_string": "        case OBJ_NATIVE: {\n            lisa_obj_native *native = AS_NATIVE(callee);\n            if (native->arity != -1 && native->arity != argc) {\n                runtime_error(vm, \"Expected %d arguments but got %d.\", native->arity, argc);\n                return false;\n            }\n            lisa_value result = native->function(argc, vm->stack_top - argc);\n            vm->stack_top -= argc + 1;\n            push(vm, result);\n            return true;\n        }",
  "new_string": "        case OBJ_NATIVE: {\n            lisa_obj_native *native = AS_NATIVE(callee);\n            if (native->arity != -1 && native->arity != argc) {\n                runtime_error(vm, \"Expected %d arguments but got %d.\", native->arity, argc);\n                return false;\n            }\n            lisa_value result = native->function(vm, argc, vm->stack_top - argc);\n            vm->stack_top -= argc + 1;\n            push(vm, result);\n            return true;\n        }"
}

⚙ Write

Now Step 4: Implement fiber.c — the big one. This includes fiber allocation, C stack setup, context switch assembly, scheduler, and native functions.

Write — lisa/fiber.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.c",
  "content": "#include \"fiber.h\"\n#include \"vm.h\"\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n\n#if defined(__unix__) || defined(__APPLE__)\n#include <sys/mman.h>\n#include <unistd.h>\n#endif\n\n#define FIBER_C_STACK_SIZE (64 * 1024) /* 64 KB per fiber */\n\n/* ===== Context Switch (inline assembly) ===== */\n\n#if defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n\n/*\n * lisa_fiber_switch(void **save_sp, void *restore_sp)\n *\n * Saves callee-saved registers (x19-x28, x29/fp, x30/lr) onto the current\n * stack, stores SP into *save_sp, loads SP from restore_sp, restores regs,\n * and returns.\n */\n__attribute__((naked))\nvoid lisa_fiber_switch(void **save_sp, void *restore_sp) {\n    __asm__ volatile(\n        /* Save callee-saved regs + lr onto current stack */\n        \"stp x29, x30, [sp, #-16]!\\n\"\n        \"stp x27, x28, [sp, #-16]!\\n\"\n        \"stp x25, x26, [sp, #-16]!\\n\"\n        \"stp x23, x24, [sp, #-16]!\\n\"\n        \"stp x21, x22, [sp, #-16]!\\n\"\n        \"stp x19, x20, [sp, #-16]!\\n\"\n        /* Save SP into *save_sp (x0) */\n        \"mov x2, sp\\n\"\n        \"str x2, [x0]\\n\"\n        /* Load SP from restore_sp (x1) */\n        \"mov sp, x1\\n\"\n        /* Restore callee-saved regs */\n        \"ldp x19, x20, [sp], #16\\n\"\n        \"ldp x21, x22, [sp], #16\\n\"\n        \"ldp x23, x24, [sp], #16\\n\"\n        \"ldp x25, x26, [sp], #16\\n\"\n        \"ldp x27, x28, [sp], #16\\n\"\n        \"ldp x29, x30, [sp], #16\\n\"\n        \"ret\\n\"\n    );\n}\n\n#elif defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n\n__attribute__((naked))\nvoid lisa_fiber_switch(void **save_sp, void *restore_sp) {\n    __asm__ volatile(\n        /* Save callee-saved regs */\n        \"pushq %%rbp\\n\"\n        \"pushq %%rbx\\n\"\n        \"pushq %%r12\\n\"\n        \"pushq %%r13\\n\"\n        \"pushq %%r14\\n\"\n        \"pushq %%r15\\n\"\n        /* Save RSP into *save_sp (rdi) */\n        \"movq %%rsp, (%%rdi)\\n\"
... (truncated)
result
File created successfully at: lisa/fiber.c
⚙ Edit

I need to rethink the fiber.c implementation. There are several issues:

1. fiber_trampoline_entry as a naked function with symbol references is fragile

2. call_value is static in vm.c

Let me take a simpler approach with a global variable for the VM pointer and expose call_value.

First, expose call_value in vm.h:

Edit — lisa/vm.h
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.h",
  "old_string": "/* Run the bytecode interpreter starting from the current top frame.\n   Stops when frame_count drops to base_frame. */\nlisa_interpret_result lisa_run(lisa_vm *vm, int base_frame);",
  "new_string": "/* Run the bytecode interpreter starting from the current top frame.\n   Stops when frame_count drops to base_frame. */\nlisa_interpret_result lisa_run(lisa_vm *vm, int base_frame);\n\n/* Call a value (closure or native) with argc arguments on the stack.\n   Returns true on success; for closures, sets up the call frame (use lisa_run after). */\nbool lisa_call_value(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value callee, int argc);"
}
result

⚙ Write

Now rewrite fiber.c properly with the global variable approach for the trampoline and correct stack layout:

Write — lisa/fiber.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.c",
  "content": "#include \"fiber.h\"\n#include \"vm.h\"\n#include \"jit.h\"\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n\n#if defined(__unix__) || defined(__APPLE__)\n#include <sys/mman.h>\n#include <unistd.h>\n#endif\n\n#define FIBER_C_STACK_SIZE (64 * 1024) /* 64 KB per fiber */\n\n/* ===== Context Switch (inline assembly) ===== */\n\n#if defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)\n\n/*\n * lisa_fiber_switch(void **save_sp, void *restore_sp)\n *\n * Saves callee-saved registers (x19-x28, x29/fp, x30/lr) onto the current\n * stack, stores SP into *save_sp, loads SP from restore_sp, restores regs,\n * and returns (via restored x30).\n */\n__attribute__((naked))\nvoid lisa_fiber_switch(void **save_sp, void *restore_sp) {\n    (void)save_sp; (void)restore_sp;\n    __asm__ volatile(\n        \"stp x19, x20, [sp, #-16]!\\n\"\n        \"stp x21, x22, [sp, #-16]!\\n\"\n        \"stp x23, x24, [sp, #-16]!\\n\"\n        \"stp x25, x26, [sp, #-16]!\\n\"\n        \"stp x27, x28, [sp, #-16]!\\n\"\n        \"stp x29, x30, [sp, #-16]!\\n\"\n        /* Save SP into *save_sp (x0) */\n        \"mov x2, sp\\n\"\n        \"str x2, [x0]\\n\"\n        /* Load SP from restore_sp (x1) */\n        \"mov sp, x1\\n\"\n        /* Restore callee-saved regs */\n        \"ldp x29, x30, [sp], #16\\n\"\n        \"ldp x27, x28, [sp], #16\\n\"\n        \"ldp x25, x26, [sp], #16\\n\"\n        \"ldp x23, x24, [sp], #16\\n\"\n        \"ldp x21, x22, [sp], #16\\n\"\n        \"ldp x19, x20, [sp], #16\\n\"\n        \"ret\\n\"\n    );\n}\n\n#elif defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)\n\n__attribute__((naked))\nvoid lisa_fiber_switch(void **save_sp, void *restore_sp) {\n    (void)save_sp; (void)restore_sp;\n    __asm__ volatile(\n        /* Save callee-saved regs */\n        \"pushq %%rbp\\n\"\n        \"pushq %%rbx\\n\"\n        \"pushq %%r12\\n\"\n        \"pushq %%r13\\n\"\n        \"pushq %%r14\\n\"\n        \"pushq %%r15\\n\"\n        /* Save RSP into

⚙ Read

Step 5: Add GC marking/freeing for fiber/channel objects in object.c.

Read — lisa/object.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/object.c",
  "offset": 233,
  "limit": 100
}
result
233→/* --- GC --- */
   234→
   235→static void mark_object(lisa_obj *obj);
   236→
   237→static void mark_value(lisa_value value) {
   238→    if (IS_OBJ(value)) mark_object(AS_OBJ(value));
   239→}
   240→
   241→static void mark_object(lisa_obj *obj) {
   242→    if (obj == NULL || obj->is_marked) return;
   243→    obj->is_marked = true;
   244→
   245→    switch (obj->type) {
   246→    case OBJ_STRING:
   247→        break;
   248→    case OBJ_UPVALUE:
   249→        mark_value(((lisa_obj_upvalue*)obj)->closed);
   250→        break;
   251→    case OBJ_FUNCTION: {
   252→        lisa_obj_function *fn = (lisa_obj_function*)obj;
   253→        if (fn->name) mark_object((lisa_obj*)fn->name);
   254→        for (int i = 0; i < fn->chunk.constants.count; i++) {
   255→            mark_value(fn->chunk.constants.values[i]);
   256→        }
   257→        break;
   258→    }
   259→    case OBJ_CLOSURE: {
   260→        lisa_obj_closure *closure = (lisa_obj_closure*)obj;
   261→        mark_object((lisa_obj*)closure->function);
   262→        for (int i = 0; i < closure->upvalue_count; i++) {
   263→            if (closure->upvalues[i]) {
   264→                mark_object((lisa_obj*)closure->upvalues[i]);
   265→            }
   266→        }
   267→        break;
   268→    }
   269→    case OBJ_LIST: {
   270→        lisa_obj_list *list = (lisa_obj_list*)obj;
   271→        mark_value(list->car);
   272→        mark_value(list->cdr);
   273→        break;
   274→    }
   275→    case OBJ_NATIVE:
   276→        break;
   277→    }
   278→}
   279→
   280→static void mark_roots(lisa_gc *gc) {
   281→    /* Mark stack values */
   282→    for (int i = 0; i < gc->stack_count; i++) {
   283→        mark_value(gc->stack[i]);
   284→    }
   285→    /* Mark open upvalues */
   286→    lisa_obj_upvalue *uv = gc->open_upvalues;
   287→    while (uv != NULL) {
   288→        mark_object((lisa_obj*)uv);
   289→        uv = uv->next;
   290→    }
   291→}
   292→
   293→static void free_object(lisa_gc *gc, lisa_obj *obj) {
   294→    switch (obj->type) {
   295→    case OBJ_STRING: {
   296→        lisa_obj_string *str = (lisa_obj_string*)obj;
   297→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_string) + (size_t)str->length + 1;
   298→        string_table_remove(gc, str);
   299→        free(obj);
   300→        break;
   301→    }
   302→    case OBJ_FUNCTION: {
   303→        lisa_obj_function *fn = (lisa_obj_function*)obj;
   304→        lisa_jit_free(fn);
   305→        lisa_chunk_free(&fn->chunk);
   306→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_function);
   307→        free(obj);
   308→        break;
   309→    }
   310→    case OBJ_CLOSURE: {
   311→        lisa_obj_closure *closure = (lisa_obj_closure*)obj;
   312→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_upvalue*) * (size_t)closure->upvalue_count;
   313→        free(closure->upvalues);
   314→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_closure);
   315→        free(obj);
   31
... (truncated)

⚙ Edit

Now add marking cases for OBJ_FIBER and OBJ_CHANNEL:

Edit — lisa/object.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/object.c",
  "old_string": "    case OBJ_NATIVE:\n        break;\n    }\n}\n\nstatic void mark_roots(lisa_gc *gc) {",
  "new_string": "    case OBJ_NATIVE:\n        break;\n    case OBJ_FIBER: {\n        lisa_fiber *fiber = (lisa_fiber*)obj;\n        /* Mark fiber's stack values */\n        if (fiber->stack) {\n            for (lisa_value *slot = fiber->stack; slot < fiber->stack_top; slot++)\n                mark_value(*slot);\n        }\n        /* Mark fiber's open upvalues */\n        for (lisa_obj_upvalue *uv2 = fiber->open_upvalues; uv2; uv2 = uv2->next)\n            mark_object((lisa_obj*)uv2);\n        /* Mark frame closures */\n        for (int i = 0; i < fiber->frame_count; i++)\n            mark_object((lisa_obj*)fiber->frames[i].closure);\n        /* Mark entry closure */\n        if (fiber->entry) mark_object((lisa_obj*)fiber->entry);\n        mark_value(fiber->result);\n        break;\n    }\n    case OBJ_CHANNEL: {\n        lisa_channel *ch = (lisa_channel*)obj;\n        mark_value(ch->value);\n        if (ch->sender) mark_object((lisa_obj*)ch->sender);\n        if (ch->receiver) mark_object((lisa_obj*)ch->receiver);\n        break;\n    }\n    }\n}\n\nstatic void mark_roots(lisa_gc *gc) {"
}
result
The file lisa/object.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Now update mark_roots to mark all fibers (not just the current stack):

Edit — lisa/object.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/object.c",
  "old_string": "static void mark_roots(lisa_gc *gc) {\n    /* Mark stack values */\n    for (int i = 0; i < gc->stack_count; i++) {\n        mark_value(gc->stack[i]);\n    }\n    /* Mark open upvalues */\n    lisa_obj_upvalue *uv = gc->open_upvalues;\n    while (uv != NULL) {\n        mark_object((lisa_obj*)uv);\n        uv = uv->next;\n    }\n}",
  "new_string": "static void mark_roots(lisa_gc *gc) {\n    /* Mark current stack values */\n    for (int i = 0; i < gc->stack_count; i++) {\n        mark_value(gc->stack[i]);\n    }\n    /* Mark open upvalues */\n    lisa_obj_upvalue *uv = gc->open_upvalues;\n    while (uv != NULL) {\n        mark_object((lisa_obj*)uv);\n        uv = uv->next;\n    }\n    /* Mark all live fibers (traverses each fiber's stack/frames/upvalues) */\n    for (lisa_fiber *f = gc->all_fibers; f != NULL; f = f->next_fiber) {\n        mark_object((lisa_obj*)f);\n    }\n}"
}
result
The file lisa/object.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Now add free cases for OBJ_FIBER and OBJ_CHANNEL:

Edit — lisa/object.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/object.c",
  "old_string": "    case OBJ_NATIVE:\n        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_native);\n        free(obj);\n        break;\n    }\n}",
  "new_string": "    case OBJ_NATIVE:\n        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_native);\n        free(obj);\n        break;\n    case OBJ_FIBER: {\n        lisa_fiber *fiber = (lisa_fiber*)obj;\n        lisa_fiber_free_stacks(fiber);\n        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_fiber);\n        free(obj);\n        break;\n    }\n    case OBJ_CHANNEL:\n        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_channel);\n        free(obj);\n        break;\n    }\n}"
}

There's one more switch that needs updating — the lisa_print_object function:

The file lisa/object.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Read

Now I need to also handle the all_fibers linked list cleanup in lisa_gc_free — when the GC frees all objects, we need to clear the fibers list. Also need to remove freed fibers from the all_fibers list during sweep. Actually, since free_object already frees fibers when they're unreachable, and all_fibers just tracks them, I need to also update the linked list. But this gets tricky during sweep — the all_fibers list would have dangling pointers.

Let me handle this differently: rebuild the all_fibers list after sweep.

Read — lisa/object.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/object.c",
  "offset": 350,
  "limit": 60
}
result
350→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_closure);
   351→        free(obj);
   352→        break;
   353→    }
   354→    case OBJ_UPVALUE:
   355→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_upvalue);
   356→        free(obj);
   357→        break;
   358→    case OBJ_LIST:
   359→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_list);
   360→        free(obj);
   361→        break;
   362→    case OBJ_NATIVE:
   363→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_obj_native);
   364→        free(obj);
   365→        break;
   366→    case OBJ_FIBER: {
   367→        lisa_fiber *fiber = (lisa_fiber*)obj;
   368→        lisa_fiber_free_stacks(fiber);
   369→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_fiber);
   370→        free(obj);
   371→        break;
   372→    }
   373→    case OBJ_CHANNEL:
   374→        gc->bytes_allocated -= sizeof(lisa_channel);
   375→        free(obj);
   376→        break;
   377→    }
   378→}
   379→
   380→static void sweep(lisa_gc *gc) {
   381→    lisa_obj *prev = NULL;
   382→    lisa_obj *obj = gc->objects;
   383→    while (obj != NULL) {
   384→        if (obj->is_marked) {
   385→            obj->is_marked = false;
   386→            prev = obj;
   387→            obj = obj->next;
   388→        } else {
   389→            lisa_obj *unreached = obj;
   390→            obj = obj->next;
   391→            if (prev != NULL) {
   392→                prev->next = obj;
   393→            } else {
   394→                gc->objects = obj;
   395→            }
   396→            free_object(gc, unreached);
   397→        }
   398→    }
   399→}
   400→
   401→void lisa_gc_collect(lisa_gc *gc) {
   402→    mark_roots(gc);
   403→    sweep(gc);
   404→    gc->next_gc = gc->bytes_allocated * 2;
   405→}
   406→
   407→void lisa_gc_init(lisa_gc *gc) {
   408→    gc->objects = NULL;
   409→    gc->strings = NULL;
⚙ Edit

I need to rebuild the all_fibers list after sweep to remove freed fibers:

Edit — lisa/object.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/object.c",
  "old_string": "void lisa_gc_collect(lisa_gc *gc) {\n    mark_roots(gc);\n    sweep(gc);\n    gc->next_gc = gc->bytes_allocated * 2;\n}",
  "new_string": "static void rebuild_fiber_list(lisa_gc *gc) {\n    /* Rebuild the all_fibers linked list from surviving objects */\n    gc->all_fibers = NULL;\n    for (lisa_obj *obj = gc->objects; obj != NULL; obj = obj->next) {\n        if (obj->type == OBJ_FIBER) {\n            lisa_fiber *f = (lisa_fiber *)obj;\n            f->next_fiber = gc->all_fibers;\n            gc->all_fibers = f;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nvoid lisa_gc_collect(lisa_gc *gc) {\n    mark_roots(gc);\n    sweep(gc);\n    rebuild_fiber_list(gc);\n    gc->next_gc = gc->bytes_allocated * 2;\n}"
}
result
The file lisa/object.c has been updated successfully.

  "file_path": "lisa/object.c",
  "old_string": "    gc->stack = NULL;\n    gc->stack_count = 0;\n    gc->open_upvalues = NULL;\n}",
  "new_string": "    gc->stack = NULL;\n    gc->stack_count = 0;\n    gc->open_upvalues = NULL;\n    gc->all_fibers = NULL;\n}"
}

⚙ Edit

Now register the fiber native functions in lisa_vm_init in vm.c:

Edit — lisa/vm.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "old_string": "    /* Register native functions */\n    define_native(vm, \"+\", native_add, 2);\n    define_native(vm, \"-\", native_sub, -1);\n    define_native(vm, \"*\", native_mul, 2);\n    define_native(vm, \"/\", native_div, 2);\n}",
  "new_string": "    /* Register native functions */\n    define_native(vm, \"+\", native_add, 2);\n    define_native(vm, \"-\", native_sub, -1);\n    define_native(vm, \"*\", native_mul, 2);\n    define_native(vm, \"/\", native_div, 2);\n\n    /* Fiber/channel native functions */\n    define_native(vm, \"chan\", native_chan, 0);\n    define_native(vm, \"spawn\", native_spawn, -1);\n    define_native(vm, \"send\", native_send, 2);\n    define_native(vm, \"recv\", native_recv, 1);\n    define_native(vm, \"yield\", native_yield, -1);\n}"
}
result
The file lisa/vm.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Now I need to run the scheduler after lisa_interpret completes (or before the final result). The scheduler needs to run if any fibers were spawned. Let me update lisa_interpret to run the scheduler:

Edit — lisa/vm.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/vm.c",
  "old_string": "    call_closure(vm, closure, 0);\n\n    return lisa_run(vm, 0);\n}",
  "new_string": "    call_closure(vm, closure, 0);\n\n    lisa_interpret_result result = lisa_run(vm, 0);\n\n    /* Run any spawned fibers */\n    if (!lisa_sched_empty(&vm->scheduler)) {\n        lisa_run_scheduler(vm);\n    }\n\n    return result;\n}"
}
result

⚙ Bash

Existing functionality works. Now test fibers:

result
Exit code 139
⚙ Bash ×4

Segfault. Let me debug. The issue is likely in the context switch or trampoline setup. Let me test with --no-jit first:

Bash — Test fibers with no JIT
{
  "command": "bin/lisa --no-jit -e '(def ch (chan)) (spawn (fn [] (send ch 42))) (println (recv ch))' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test fibers with no JIT"
}
result
Exit code 139
Bash — Debug segfault with lldb

  "description": "Debug segfault with lldb"
}
result

Current executable set to 'lisa/bin/lisa' (arm64).
(lldb) run --no-jit /tmp/test_fiber.lisa
Process 64488 launched: 'lisa/bin/lisa' (arm64)
Process 64488 stopped
* thread #1, queue = 'com.apple.main-thread', stop reason = EXC_BAD_ACCESS (code=1, address=0x0)
    frame #0: 0x00000001000160c0 lisa`lisa_fiber_switch at fiber.c:29:5
   26  	__attribute__((naked))
   27  	void lisa_fiber_switch(void **save_sp __attribute__((unused)),
   28  	                       void *restore_sp __attribute__((unused))) {
-> 29  	    __asm__ volatile(
    	    ^
   30  	        "stp x19, x20, [sp, #-16]!\n"
   31  	        "stp x21, x22, [sp, #-16]!\n"
   32  	        "stp x23, x24, [sp, #-16]!\n"
Target 0: (lisa) stopped.
⚙ Read ×2

The crash is because the main fiber tries to context-switch but has no separate C stack (c_sp is NULL). When recv is called from the main fiber, it tries to yield to a scheduler that isn't running yet.

The fix: when the main fiber blocks, it runs the scheduler loop inline on the OS stack instead of context-switching.

Read — lisa/fiber.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.c",
  "offset": 290,
  "limit": 80
}
result
290→#endif
   291→}
   292→
   293→/* ===== Fiber Creation ===== */
   294→
   295→lisa_fiber *lisa_new_main_fiber(lisa_vm *vm) {
   296→    lisa_fiber *fiber = malloc(sizeof(lisa_fiber));
   297→    fiber->obj.type = OBJ_FIBER;
   298→    fiber->obj.is_marked = false;
   299→    fiber->obj.next = vm->gc.objects;
   300→    vm->gc.objects = (lisa_obj *)fiber;
   301→    vm->gc.bytes_allocated += sizeof(lisa_fiber);
   302→
   303→    fiber->state = FIBER_RUNNING;
   304→    fiber->stack = malloc(sizeof(lisa_value) * STACK_MAX);
   305→    fiber->stack_top = fiber->stack;
   306→    fiber->frames = malloc(sizeof(lisa_call_frame) * FRAMES_MAX);
   307→    fiber->frame_count = 0;
   308→    fiber->open_upvalues = NULL;
   309→
   310→    fiber->c_stack = NULL; /* main fiber uses the OS stack */
   311→    fiber->c_stack_size = 0;
   312→    fiber->c_sp = NULL;
   313→
   314→    fiber->result = LISA_NIL;
   315→    fiber->entry = NULL;
   316→    fiber->argc = 0;
   317→
   318→    fiber->next_fiber = vm->gc.all_fibers;
   319→    vm->gc.all_fibers = fiber;
   320→
   321→    return fiber;
   322→}
   323→
   324→lisa_fiber *lisa_new_fiber(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_obj_closure *entry, int argc,
   325→                           lisa_value *args) {
   326→    lisa_fiber *fiber = malloc(sizeof(lisa_fiber));
   327→    fiber->obj.type = OBJ_FIBER;
   328→    fiber->obj.is_marked = false;
   329→    fiber->obj.next = vm->gc.objects;
   330→    vm->gc.objects = (lisa_obj *)fiber;
   331→    vm->gc.bytes_allocated += sizeof(lisa_fiber);
   332→
   333→    fiber->state = FIBER_READY;
   334→    fiber->stack = malloc(sizeof(lisa_value) * STACK_MAX);
   335→    fiber->stack_top = fiber->stack;
   336→    fiber->frames = malloc(sizeof(lisa_call_frame) * FRAMES_MAX);
   337→    fiber->frame_count = 0;
   338→    fiber->open_upvalues = NULL;
   339→
   340→    fiber->result = LISA_NIL;
   341→    fiber->entry = entry;
   342→    fiber->argc = argc;
   343→
   344→    /* Push the closure onto the fiber's stack (slot 0), then the args */
   345→    *fiber->stack_top++ = LISA_OBJ(entry);
   346→    for (int i = 0; i < argc; i++) {
   347→        *fiber->stack_top++ = args[i];
   348→    }
   349→
   350→    /* Set up C stack for JIT / context switch */
   351→    setup_c_stack(fiber);
   352→
   353→    fiber->next_fiber = vm->gc.all_fibers;
   354→    vm->gc.all_fibers = fiber;
   355→
   356→    return fiber;
   357→}
   358→
   359→void lisa_fiber_free_stacks(lisa_fiber *fiber) {
   360→    free(fiber->stack);
   361→    free(fiber->frames);
   362→    fiber->stack = NULL;
   363→    fiber->frames = NULL;
   364→    free_c_stack(fiber->c_stack, fiber->c_stack_size);
   365→    fiber->c_stack = NULL;
   366→}
   367→
   368→/* ===== Channel ===== */
   369→
Read — lisa/fiber.c
{
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.c",
  "offset": 395,
  "limit": 120
}
result
395→void lisa_sched_free(lisa_scheduler *sched) {
   396→    free(sched->queue);
   397→    sched->queue = NULL;
   398→    sched->capacity = 0;
   399→    sched->head = sched->tail = 0;
   400→}
   401→
   402→static int sched_count(lisa_scheduler *sched) {
   403→    return (sched->tail - sched->head + sched->capacity) % sched->capacity;
   404→}
   405→
   406→void lisa_sched_enqueue(lisa_scheduler *sched, lisa_fiber *fiber) {
   407→    if (sched_count(sched) >= sched->capacity - 1) {
   408→        int old_cap = sched->capacity;
   409→        int new_cap = old_cap * 2;
   410→        lisa_fiber **new_q = malloc(sizeof(lisa_fiber *) * (size_t)new_cap);
   411→        int n = sched_count(sched);
   412→        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
   413→            new_q[i] = sched->queue[(sched->head + i) % old_cap];
   414→        }
   415→        free(sched->queue);
   416→        sched->queue = new_q;
   417→        sched->head = 0;
   418→        sched->tail = n;
   419→        sched->capacity = new_cap;
   420→    }
   421→    sched->queue[sched->tail] = fiber;
   422→    sched->tail = (sched->tail + 1) % sched->capacity;
   423→}
   424→
   425→lisa_fiber *lisa_sched_dequeue(lisa_scheduler *sched) {
   426→    if (sched->head == sched->tail) return NULL;
   427→    lisa_fiber *f = sched->queue[sched->head];
   428→    sched->head = (sched->head + 1) % sched->capacity;
   429→    return f;
   430→}
   431→
   432→bool lisa_sched_empty(lisa_scheduler *sched) {
   433→    return sched->head == sched->tail;
   434→}
   435→
   436→/* ===== Fiber Save / Restore ===== */
   437→
   438→void lisa_fiber_save(lisa_vm *vm) {
   439→    lisa_fiber *f = vm->current_fiber;
   440→    f->stack_top = vm->stack_top;
   441→    f->frame_count = vm->frame_count;
   442→    f->open_upvalues = vm->open_upvalues;
   443→}
   444→
   445→void lisa_fiber_restore(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_fiber *f) {
   446→    vm->current_fiber = f;
   447→    vm->stack = f->stack;
   448→    vm->stack_top = f->stack_top;
   449→    vm->frames = f->frames;
   450→    vm->frame_count = f->frame_count;
   451→    vm->open_upvalues = f->open_upvalues;
   452→}
   453→
   454→/* ===== Yield to Scheduler ===== */
   455→
   456→static void yield_to_scheduler(lisa_vm *vm) {
   457→    lisa_fiber *current = vm->current_fiber;
   458→    lisa_fiber_save(vm);
   459→
   460→    /* Switch C stacks: save our SP, restore main fiber's SP */
   461→    lisa_fiber_switch(&current->c_sp, vm->main_fiber->c_sp);
   462→
   463→    /* When we resume, the scheduler has restored us as current_fiber */
   464→}
   465→
   466→/* ===== Scheduler Run Loop ===== */
   467→
   468→static void scheduler_run(lisa_vm *vm) {
   469→    while (!lisa_sched_empty(&vm->scheduler)) {
   470→        lisa_fiber *next = lisa_sched_dequeue(&vm->scheduler);
   471→        if (next->state == FIBER_DONE) continue;
   472→
   473→        next->state = FIBER_RUNNING;
   474→        lisa_fiber_restore(vm, next);
   475→
   476→      
... (truncated)
⚙ Edit, Bash ×2

Now I'll replace yield_to_scheduler and scheduler_run with the fixed version that handles the main fiber properly:

Edit — lisa/fiber.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.c",
  "old_string": "/* ===== Yield to Scheduler ===== */\n\nstatic void yield_to_scheduler(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    lisa_fiber *current = vm->current_fiber;\n    lisa_fiber_save(vm);\n\n    /* Switch C stacks: save our SP, restore main fiber's SP */\n    lisa_fiber_switch(&current->c_sp, vm->main_fiber->c_sp);\n\n    /* When we resume, the scheduler has restored us as current_fiber */\n}\n\n/* ===== Scheduler Run Loop ===== */\n\nstatic void scheduler_run(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    while (!lisa_sched_empty(&vm->scheduler)) {\n        lisa_fiber *next = lisa_sched_dequeue(&vm->scheduler);\n        if (next->state == FIBER_DONE) continue;\n\n        next->state = FIBER_RUNNING;\n        lisa_fiber_restore(vm, next);\n\n        /* Set global vm pointer for trampoline entry (new fibers) */\n        g_trampoline_vm = vm;\n\n        /* Switch C stacks: save main fiber's SP, jump to next fiber */\n        lisa_fiber_switch(&vm->main_fiber->c_sp, next->c_sp);\n\n        /* Back on main fiber's C stack. Restore main fiber as current. */\n        lisa_fiber_restore(vm, vm->main_fiber);\n    }\n}",
  "new_string": "/* ===== Scheduler Core ===== */\n\n/*\n * Run one iteration of the scheduler: dequeue a fiber, switch to it,\n * return when it yields or completes. Runs on the main fiber's (OS) C stack.\n * Returns false if the queue was empty.\n */\nstatic bool scheduler_step(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    lisa_fiber *next = lisa_sched_dequeue(&vm->scheduler);\n    if (!next) return false;\n    if (next->state == FIBER_DONE) return true; /* skip, try next */\n\n    /* If the dequeued fiber is the main fiber, just return —\n       the main fiber resumes in-place (no context switch needed). */\n    if (next == vm->main_fiber) {\n        next->state = FIBER_RUNNING;\n        lisa_fiber_restore(vm, next);\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    next->state = FIBER_RUNNING;\n    lisa_fiber_restore(vm, next);\n\n    /* Set global vm pointer for trampoline en
... (truncated)

Bash — Test producer-consumer (with JIT)

⚙ Bash

Now test yield:

Bash — Test yield (no JIT)
{
  "command": "bin/lisa --no-jit -e '\n  (def ch (chan))\n  (spawn (fn [] (yield) (send ch 99)))\n  (spawn (fn [] (send ch 1)))\n  (println (recv ch))\n  (println (recv ch))\n' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test yield (no JIT)"
}
result
1
99
⚙ Bash

The yield causes the first fiber to run after the second, so 1 is received first, then 99. Let me also test multiple spawns with JIT:

Bash — Test multiple fibers
{
  "command": "bin/lisa -e '\n  (def ch (chan))\n  (spawn (fn [] (send ch 10)))\n  (spawn (fn [] (send ch 20)))\n  (spawn (fn [] (send ch 30)))\n  (println (+ (recv ch) (+ (recv ch) (recv ch))))\n' 2>&1",
  "description": "Test multiple fibers"
}
result
60

TaskUpdate
{
  "taskId": "6",
  "status": "completed"
}
result
Updated task #6 status
claude

All done. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

Implementation Summary

New files

- lisa/fiber.h — Fiber, channel, scheduler structs and API

- lisa/fiber.c — Full implementation: context switch, scheduler, channel ops, native functions

Modified files

- lisa/object.h — Added OBJ_FIBER, OBJ_CHANNEL to enum; forward declarations for lisa_vm, lisa_fiber, lisa_channel; changed lisa_native_fn signature to (lisa_vm*, int, lisa_value*); added all_fibers list to lisa_gc; type check/cast macros for fiber/channel

- lisa/object.c — GC mark/free/print cases for fiber/channel; root marking walks all fibers; rebuild_fiber_list after sweep

- lisa/vm.h — lisa_vm struct changed from fixed arrays to pointers for stack/frames; added current_fiber, main_fiber, scheduler; exposed lisa_call_value; lisa_call_frame made a named struct

- lisa/vm.c — lisa_vm_init creates main fiber; native function signatures updated; call_value made public as lisa_call_value; scheduler runs after lisa_interpret; fiber natives registered (chan, spawn, send, recv, yield)

- lisa/Makefile — Added fiber.c to sources

Key design decisions

- Main fiber uses OS stack — no mmap'd C stack needed; when main fiber blocks, it runs the scheduler loop inline

- Spawned fibers get 64KB mmap'd C stacks with guard pages

- Context switch is ~15 instructions of inline assembly (ARM64/x86-64), saving/restoring callee-saved registers

- Global g_trampoline_vm passes VM pointer to new fiber entry (safe because fibers are cooperative)

- Unbuffered channels — send blocks until receiver ready, recv blocks until sender ready

- No compiler/JIT changes needed — fiber primitives are native functions resolved via OP_GET_GLOBAL + OP_CALL

human

let’s generate some example files in an examples directory.

1→# lisa
     2→
     3→wherein we vibe-code a jitted lisp usng [cj](https://github.com/hellerve-pl-experiments/cj).
     4→
     5→## usage
     6→
     7→code looks like clojure, just more basic.
     8→
     9→```
    10→bin/lisa -e '(def fib (fn [n] (if (<= n 1) n (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2)))))) (println (fib 25))'
    11→```
    12→
    13→features bytecode vm with whole function jit and tco.
    14→
    15→<hr/>
    16→
    17→have fun!

Bash — Create examples directory
{
  "command": "mkdir -p lisa/examples",
  "description": "Create examples directory"
}
Write — lisa/examples/ping-pong.lisa
{
  "file_path": "lisa/examples/ping-pong.lisa",
  "content": "; Two fibers play ping-pong over a pair of channels.\n\n(def ping-ch (chan))\n(def pong-ch (chan))\n\n(spawn (fn []\n  (def loop (fn [n]\n    (if (> n 0)\n      (do\n        (send ping-ch \"ping\")\n        (recv pong-ch)\n        (loop (- n 1))))))\n  (loop 5)\n  (send ping-ch \"done\")))\n\n(def loop (fn []\n  (def msg (recv ping-ch))\n  (if (= msg \"done\")\n    (println \"finished after 5 rounds\")\n    (do\n      (println msg)\n      (send pong-ch \"pong\")\n      (loop)))))\n(loop)\n"
}
result
File created successfully at: lisa/examples/ping-pong.lisa
Write — lisa/examples/producer-consumer.lisa
{
  "file_path": "lisa/examples/producer-consumer.lisa",
  "content": "; A producer sends values 1..10 on a channel.\n; A consumer receives them and sums them up.\n\n(def ch (chan))\n\n(spawn (fn []\n  (def produce (fn [i]\n    (if (<= i 10)\n      (do\n        (send ch i)\n        (produce (+ i 1))))))\n  (produce 1)))\n\n(def consume (fn [n acc]\n  (if (<= n 0) acc\n    (consume (- n 1) (+ acc (recv ch))))))\n\n(println (consume 10 0))\n; => 55\n"
}
result
File created successfully at: lisa/examples/producer-consumer.lisa
Write — lisa/examples/fan-out.lisa
{
  "file_path": "lisa/examples/fan-out.lisa",
  "content": "; Fan-out: spawn N workers that each compute fib(n),\n; collect all results through a shared channel.\n\n(def results (chan))\n\n(def fib (fn [n]\n  (if (<= n 1) n\n    (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2))))))\n\n; Spawn 5 workers computing fib(15) through fib(19)\n(def spawn-workers (fn [i]\n  (if (<= i 19)\n    (do\n      (def n i)\n      (spawn (fn [] (send results (fib n))))\n      (spawn-workers (+ i 1))))))\n(spawn-workers 15)\n\n; Collect 5 results\n(def collect (fn [remaining acc]\n  (if (<= remaining 0) acc\n    (collect (- remaining 1) (+ acc (recv results))))))\n\n(def total (collect 5 0))\n(println total)\n; fib(15)+fib(16)+fib(17)+fib(18)+fib(19) = 610+987+1597+2584+4181 = 9959\n"
}
result
File created successfully at: lisa/examples/fan-out.lisa
Write — lisa/examples/pipeline.lisa
{
  "file_path": "lisa/examples/pipeline.lisa",
  "content": "; Pipeline: chain three stages with channels.\n;   stage 1: generate numbers 1..5\n;   stage 2: square each number\n;   stage 3: print each result\n\n(def ch1 (chan))\n(def ch2 (chan))\n\n; Stage 1: producer\n(spawn (fn []\n  (def loop (fn [i]\n    (if (<= i 5)\n      (do (send ch1 i) (loop (+ i 1))))))\n  (loop 1)\n  (send ch1 0))) ; sentinel\n\n; Stage 2: squarer\n(spawn (fn []\n  (def loop (fn []\n    (def x (recv ch1))\n    (if (= x 0)\n      (send ch2 0)       ; forward sentinel\n      (do\n        (send ch2 (* x x))\n        (loop)))))\n  (loop)))\n\n; Stage 3: printer (runs on main fiber)\n(def loop (fn []\n  (def x (recv ch2))\n  (if (= x 0)\n    (println \"done\")\n    (do (println x) (loop)))))\n(loop)\n; => 1 4 9 16 25 done\n"
}
result
File created successfully at: lisa/examples/pipeline.lisa
Write — lisa/examples/sieve.lisa
{
  "file_path": "lisa/examples/sieve.lisa",
  "content": "; Concurrent prime sieve (CSP-style, after Hoare / Go).\n;\n; Each prime gets its own fiber that filters multiples\n; from its input channel and forwards non-multiples\n; to the next stage.\n\n(def generate (fn [ch limit]\n  (def loop (fn [i]\n    (if (<= i limit)\n      (do (send ch i) (loop (+ i 1))))))\n  (loop 2)\n  (send ch 0))) ; sentinel\n\n(def filter-stage (fn [in out p]\n  (def loop (fn []\n    (def x (recv in))\n    (if (= x 0)\n      (send out 0) ; forward sentinel\n      (do\n        (if (= (% x p) 0)\n          nil           ; divisible — drop\n          (send out x)) ; not divisible — forward\n        (loop)))))\n  (loop)))\n\n(def sieve (fn [ch]\n  (def p (recv ch))\n  (if (= p 0)\n    nil\n    (do\n      (println p)\n      (def next-ch (chan))\n      (spawn (fn [] (filter-stage ch next-ch p)))\n      (sieve next-ch)))))\n\n(def source (chan))\n(spawn (fn [] (generate source 30)))\n(sieve source)\n"
}
result
File created successfully at: lisa/examples/sieve.lisa

⚙ Write ×4

The issue is def creates globals in Lisa, so loop is being overwritten. Let me fix the examples to use unique names:

Write — lisa/examples/ping-pong.lisa
{
  "file_path": "lisa/examples/ping-pong.lisa",
  "content": "; Two fibers play ping-pong over a pair of channels.\n\n(def ping-ch (chan))\n(def pong-ch (chan))\n\n(spawn (fn []\n  (def ping-loop (fn [n]\n    (if (> n 0)\n      (do\n        (send ping-ch \"ping\")\n        (recv pong-ch)\n        (ping-loop (- n 1))))))\n  (ping-loop 5)\n  (send ping-ch \"done\")))\n\n(def pong-loop (fn []\n  (def msg (recv ping-ch))\n  (if (= msg \"done\")\n    (println \"finished after 5 rounds\")\n    (do\n      (println msg)\n      (send pong-ch \"pong\")\n      (pong-loop)))))\n(pong-loop)\n"
}

Write — lisa/examples/pipeline.lisa
{
  "file_path": "lisa/examples/pipeline.lisa",
  "content": "; Pipeline: chain three stages with channels.\n;   stage 1: generate numbers 1..5\n;   stage 2: square each number\n;   stage 3: print each result\n\n(def ch1 (chan))\n(def ch2 (chan))\n\n; Stage 1: producer\n(spawn (fn []\n  (def produce (fn [i]\n    (if (<= i 5)\n      (do (send ch1 i) (produce (+ i 1))))))\n  (produce 1)\n  (send ch1 0))) ; sentinel\n\n; Stage 2: squarer\n(spawn (fn []\n  (def square-loop (fn []\n    (def x (recv ch1))\n    (if (= x 0)\n      (send ch2 0)       ; forward sentinel\n      (do\n        (send ch2 (* x x))\n        (square-loop)))))\n  (square-loop)))\n\n; Stage 3: printer (runs on main fiber)\n(def print-loop (fn []\n  (def x (recv ch2))\n  (if (= x 0)\n    (println \"done\")\n    (do (println x) (print-loop)))))\n(print-loop)\n; => 1 4 9 16 25 done\n"
}
result
The file lisa/examples/pipeline.lisa has been updated successfully.
Write — lisa/examples/sieve.lisa
{
  "file_path": "lisa/examples/sieve.lisa",
  "content": "; Concurrent prime sieve (CSP-style, after Hoare / Go).\n;\n; Each prime gets its own fiber that filters multiples\n; from its input channel and forwards non-multiples\n; to the next stage.\n\n(def generate (fn [ch limit]\n  (def gen-loop (fn [i]\n    (if (<= i limit)\n      (do (send ch i) (gen-loop (+ i 1))))))\n  (gen-loop 2)\n  (send ch 0))) ; sentinel\n\n(def filter-stage (fn [in out p]\n  (def filt-loop (fn []\n    (def x (recv in))\n    (if (= x 0)\n      (send out 0) ; forward sentinel\n      (do\n        (if (= (% x p) 0)\n          nil           ; divisible — drop\n          (send out x)) ; not divisible — forward\n        (filt-loop)))))\n  (filt-loop)))\n\n(def sieve (fn [ch]\n  (def p (recv ch))\n  (if (= p 0)\n    nil\n    (do\n      (println p)\n      (def next-ch (chan))\n      (spawn (fn [] (filter-stage ch next-ch p)))\n      (sieve next-ch)))))\n\n(def source (chan))\n(spawn (fn [] (generate source 30)))\n(sieve source)\n"
}

⚙ Edit

Three issues to fix:

1. fan-out: def n i creates a global, so all fibers see the same n

2. sieve: the scheduler_step changes main fiber state from READY to RUNNING, which makes the yield loop never exit

3. sieve deadlock message loops: should return once, not loop

Let me fix the scheduler bug first:

Edit — lisa/fiber.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.c",
  "old_string": "    /* If the dequeued fiber is the main fiber, just return —\n       the main fiber resumes in-place (no context switch needed). */\n    if (next == vm->main_fiber) {\n        next->state = FIBER_RUNNING;\n        lisa_fiber_restore(vm, next);\n        return true;\n    }",
  "new_string": "    /* If the dequeued fiber is the main fiber, don't touch its state —\n       yield_to_scheduler will see state == FIBER_READY and exit its loop. */\n    if (next == vm->main_fiber) {\n        return true;\n    }"
}

Now fix the fan-out example to properly capture the value in a closure:

Write — lisa/examples/fan-out.lisa
{
  "file_path": "lisa/examples/fan-out.lisa",
  "content": "; Fan-out: spawn N workers that each compute fib(n),\n; collect all results through a shared channel.\n\n(def results (chan))\n\n(def fib (fn [n]\n  (if (<= n 1) n\n    (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2))))))\n\n; Helper that captures n in a closure\n(def spawn-one (fn [n]\n  (spawn (fn [] (send results (fib n))))))\n\n; Spawn 5 workers computing fib(15) through fib(19)\n(def spawn-workers (fn [i]\n  (if (<= i 19)\n    (do\n      (spawn-one i)\n      (spawn-workers (+ i 1))))))\n(spawn-workers 15)\n\n; Collect 5 results\n(def collect (fn [remaining acc]\n  (if (<= remaining 0) acc\n    (collect (- remaining 1) (+ acc (recv results))))))\n\n(def total (collect 5 0))\n(println total)\n; fib(15)+fib(16)+fib(17)+fib(18)+fib(19) = 610+987+1597+2584+4181 = 9959\n"
}

⚙ Write, Bash

Fan-out is fixed (9959). The sieve issue is that def creates globals, so p and next-ch get overwritten on each recursive sieve call, corrupting the spawned filter closures. Let me fix the sieve and also make the deadlock message not loop:

Write — lisa/examples/sieve.lisa
{
  "file_path": "lisa/examples/sieve.lisa",
  "content": "; Concurrent prime sieve (CSP-style, after Hoare / Go).\n;\n; Each prime gets its own fiber that filters multiples\n; from its input channel and forwards non-multiples\n; to the next stage.\n\n(def generate (fn [ch limit]\n  (def gen-loop (fn [i]\n    (if (<= i limit)\n      (do (send ch i) (gen-loop (+ i 1))))))\n  (gen-loop 2)\n  (send ch 0))) ; sentinel\n\n(def filter-stage (fn [in out prime]\n  (def filt-loop (fn []\n    (def x (recv in))\n    (if (= x 0)\n      (send out 0)\n      (do\n        (if (= (% x prime) 0)\n          nil\n          (send out x))\n        (filt-loop)))))\n  (filt-loop)))\n\n; Capture in/out/prime via function args so the spawn closure\n; holds upvalues (not globals that get overwritten).\n(def spawn-filter (fn [in out prime]\n  (spawn (fn [] (filter-stage in out prime)))))\n\n(def sieve (fn [ch]\n  (def p (recv ch))\n  (if (= p 0)\n    nil\n    (do\n      (println p)\n      (def nch (chan))\n      (spawn-filter ch nch p)\n      (sieve nch)))))\n\n(def source (chan))\n(spawn (fn [] (generate source 30)))\n(sieve source)\n"
}

There's still a deeper scheduling issue. Let me add some debug tracing to understand where it deadlocks:

Bash — Test sieve with small limit
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/sieve_small.lisa << 'EOF'\n(def generate (fn [ch limit]\n  (def gen-loop (fn [i]\n    (if (<= i limit)\n      (do (send ch i) (gen-loop (+ i 1))))))\n  (gen-loop 2)\n  (send ch 0)))\n\n(def filter-stage (fn [in out prime]\n  (def filt-loop (fn []\n    (def x (recv in))\n    (if (= x 0)\n      (send out 0)\n      (do\n        (if (= (% x prime) 0)\n          nil\n          (send out x))\n        (filt-loop)))))\n  (filt-loop)))\n\n(def spawn-filter (fn [in out prime]\n  (spawn (fn [] (filter-stage in out prime)))))\n\n(def sieve (fn [ch]\n  (def p (recv ch))\n  (if (= p 0)\n    nil\n    (do\n      (println p)\n      (def nch (chan))\n      (spawn-filter ch nch p)\n      (sieve nch)))))\n\n(def source (chan))\n(spawn (fn [] (generate source 3)))\n(sieve source)\nEOF\ntimeout 3 bin/lisa --no-jit /tmp/sieve_small.lisa 2>&1",

human

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:

Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. The user asked to implement a detailed plan for fibers and channels in Lisa (a Lisp-like language with bytecode VM and JIT compiler using cj). The plan was very detailed and included:

- Fiber objects with own stacks

- Channel objects (unbuffered)

- Scheduler (circular buffer run queue)

- Context switch assembly

- Native functions (chan, spawn, send, recv, yield)

- GC integration

- No JIT changes needed

2. I read the key files: object.h, object.c, vm.h, vm.c, value.h, jit.h, jit.c, chunk.h, main.c, Makefile

3. I created task list with 6 tasks and started implementing:

Task 1: Added OBJ_FIBER, OBJ_CHANNEL to object.h enum, added forward declarations, type check/cast macros, created fiber.h with structs.

- Had to fix circular dependency issues with forward declarations

- Made lisa_call_frame a named struct for forward declaration compatibility

Task 2: Changed VM stack/frames from fixed arrays to pointers, added fiber/scheduler fields to lisa_vm struct.

- Changed struct lisa_vm to be a named struct

- Removed duplicate typedef

- Updated vm.c init/free to create main fiber and scheduler

Task 3: Changed lisa_native_fn signature from (int argc, lisa_value *args) to (lisa_vm *vm, int argc, lisa_value *args)

- Updated all 4 existing natives (add, sub, mul, div)

- Updated call site in call_value

- Fixed forward declaration ordering issues

Task 4: Implemented fiber.c with context switch, scheduler, natives

- First version had issues: used naked assembly trampoline with symbol references (fragile)

- call_value was static in vm.c

- Rewrote to use global variable approach for VM pointer passing

- Exposed call_value as lisa_call_value

Task 5: Added GC marking/freeing for fiber/channel in object.c

- Added mark cases for OBJ_FIBER (stack, upvalues, frames, entry, result)

- Added mark cases for OBJ_CHANNEL (value, sender, receiver)

- Added free cases

- Updated mark_roots to walk all_fibers linked list

- Added rebuild_fiber_list after sweep

- Added print cases

Task 6: Updated Makefile, registered natives, tested

- Added fiber.c to sources

- Registered chan, spawn, send, recv, yield natives

- Added scheduler run after lisa_interpret

First test revealed a segfault: main fiber tried to context-switch but had no separate C stack (c_sp was NULL). When recv was called from the main fiber, it tried to yield to a scheduler that wasn't running.

Fix: Redesigned yield_to_scheduler to handle main fiber specially - runs scheduler loop inline on OS stack instead of context switching. Added scheduler_step function. When main fiber blocks, it runs scheduler_step in a loop until it's woken up.

After fix, all basic tests passed:

- Simple channel send/recv

- Producer-consumer (sum 1..5 = 15)

- JIT + fibers (fib(20) in spawned fiber = 6765)

- Yield ordering

- Multiple fibers

- Both debug and release builds

Then user asked: "let's generate some example files in an examples directory."

I created 5 example files:

1. ping-pong.lisa - Two fibers playing ping-pong

2. producer-consumer.lisa - Producer sends 1..10, consumer sums

3. fan-out.lisa - N workers computing fib, results collected

4. pipeline.lisa - Three-stage pipeline

5. sieve.lisa - Concurrent prime sieve (CSP-style)

Testing revealed issues:

1. ping-pong: name collision with loop (def creates globals) - fixed by using unique names

2. fan-out: wrong answer (20905 vs 9959) because def n i creates a global shared by all fibers - fixed by using spawn-one helper function that captures n as closure parameter

3. sieve: deadlock - scheduler_step was changing main fiber state from READY to RUNNING, causing yield_to_scheduler's while loop to never exit. Fixed by not changing state in scheduler_step for main fiber. But sieve STILL deadlocked.

Further sieve debugging: the global variables p and nch created by def inside sieve were being overwritten on each recursive call, corrupting spawned filter closures. Fixed by using spawn-filter helper function that captures values via function parameters (upvalues).

After fix, sieve still deadlocked. Tested with smaller limit (3) and it worked. Was about to test with progressively larger limits to find the failure point when the summary was requested.

The user sent "continue." after I made call_value changes, which was to continue implementing.

Summary:

1. Primary Request and Intent:

The user asked to implement a detailed plan for fibers (green threads) and channels with Go-style syntax (chan, spawn, send, recv) in Lisa, a Lisp-like language with a bytecode VM and JIT compiler (using the cj JIT framework). The plan specified asymmetric coroutines with each fiber owning its own C stack (mmap'd), cooperative scheduling, unbuffered channels, and no compiler/JIT changes needed (fiber primitives are native functions). After implementation, the user asked to "generate some example files in an examples directory."

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- Cooperative green threads (fibers) with per-fiber VM stacks and C stacks

- Unbuffered synchronizing channels (send blocks until receiver ready, recv blocks until sender ready)

- Cooperative scheduler with circular buffer run queue

- Context switch via inline assembly (ARM64: save/restore x19-x28, x29, x30; x86-64: save/restore rbx, rbp, r12-r15)

- Main fiber uses OS stack (no mmap'd C stack), runs scheduler loop inline when blocking

- NaN-boxed value representation (uint64_t)

- JIT compatibility: each fiber's C stack preserves JIT call frames across yield

- GC integration: linked list of all fibers for root marking, rebuild after sweep

- Lisa's def creates globals (not locals), causing closure capture issues in examples

- Global variable g_trampoline_vm to pass VM pointer to new fiber entry (safe because cooperative)

3. Files and Code Sections:

- lisa/object.h — Core object type definitions. Added OBJ_FIBER, OBJ_CHANNEL to enum, forward declarations for lisa_vm/lisa_fiber/lisa_channel, changed lisa_native_fn to (lisa_vm *vm, int argc, lisa_value *args), added all_fibers to lisa_gc, added IS_FIBER/IS_CHANNEL/AS_FIBER/AS_CHANNEL macros.

```c

typedef lisa_value (*lisa_native_fn)(lisa_vm *vm, int argc, lisa_value *args);

```

```c

lisa_fiber *all_fibers; /* linked list of all live fibers for GC */

```

- lisa/fiber.h (new) — Fiber/channel/scheduler structs and API declarations.

```c

typedef struct lisa_call_frame lisa_call_frame; // forward decl

typedef enum { FIBER_READY, FIBER_RUNNING, FIBER_SUSPENDED, FIBER_DONE } lisa_fiber_state;

struct lisa_fiber {

lisa_obj obj;

lisa_fiber_state state;

lisa_value *stack;

lisa_value *stack_top;

lisa_call_frame *frames;

int frame_count;

lisa_obj_upvalue *open_upvalues;

void *c_stack; // mmap'd region base

size_t c_stack_size;

void *c_sp; // saved C stack pointer

lisa_value result;

lisa_obj_closure *entry;

int argc;

lisa_fiber *next_fiber; // GC traversal list

};

struct lisa_channel {

lisa_obj obj;

lisa_value value;

lisa_fiber *sender;

lisa_fiber *receiver;

bool closed;

};

typedef struct {

lisa_fiber **queue;

int head, tail, capacity;

} lisa_scheduler;

```

- lisa/fiber.c (new) — Full fiber runtime implementation. Contains:

- lisa_fiber_switch — naked assembly context switch for ARM64 and x86-64

- alloc_c_stack / free_c_stack — mmap with guard page

- fiber_trampoline_entry / fiber_trampoline — entry point for new fibers using global g_trampoline_vm

- setup_c_stack — arranges fake stack frame so fiber_switch "returns" into trampoline

- lisa_new_main_fiber / lisa_new_fiber — fiber allocation

- lisa_new_channel — channel allocation

- Scheduler: lisa_sched_init/free/enqueue/dequeue/empty

- lisa_fiber_save / lisa_fiber_restore — swap VM state between fibers

- scheduler_step — dequeue one fiber, context-switch to it

- yield_to_scheduler — main fiber runs scheduler inline; spawned fibers context-switch

- Native functions: native_chan, native_spawn, native_send, native_recv, native_yield

Key scheduler logic:

```c

static bool scheduler_step(lisa_vm *vm) {

lisa_fiber *next = lisa_sched_dequeue(&vm->scheduler);

if (!next) return false;

if (next->state == FIBER_DONE) return true;

if (next == vm->main_fiber) {

return true; // don't change state; yield loop checks FIBER_READY

}

next->state = FIBER_RUNNING;

lisa_fiber_restore(vm, next);

g_trampoline_vm = vm;

lisa_fiber_switch(&vm->main_fiber->c_sp, next->c_sp);

lisa_fiber_restore(vm, vm->main_fiber);

return true;

}

static void yield_to_scheduler(lisa_vm *vm) {

lisa_fiber *current = vm->current_fiber;

lisa_fiber_save(vm);

if (current == vm->main_fiber) {

while (current->state != FIBER_READY) {

if (!scheduler_step(vm)) {

fprintf(stderr, "deadlock: main fiber blocked with no runnable fibers\n");

return;

}

}

current->state = FIBER_RUNNING;

lisa_fiber_restore(vm, current);

} else {

lisa_fiber_switch(&current->c_sp, vm->main_fiber->c_sp);

}

}

```

- lisa/vm.h — VM struct changed to use pointers instead of fixed arrays:

```c

struct lisa_vm {

lisa_call_frame *frames; // points to current_fiber->frames

int frame_count;

lisa_value *stack; // points to current_fiber->stack

lisa_value *stack_top;

// ... globals ...

lisa_fiber *current_fiber;

lisa_fiber *main_fiber;

lisa_scheduler scheduler;

lisa_gc gc;

};

```

Added bool lisa_call_value(lisa_vm *vm, lisa_value callee, int argc); declaration.

Made lisa_call_frame a named struct: typedef struct lisa_call_frame { ... } lisa_call_frame;

- lisa/vm.c — Major changes:

- lisa_vm_init: creates main fiber, initializes scheduler, registers fiber natives

- call_value renamed to lisa_call_value (public)

- All internal call_value calls updated to lisa_call_value

- Native functions updated to new signature (lisa_vm *vm_, int argc, lisa_value *args)

- lisa_interpret runs scheduler after lisa_run:

```c

lisa_interpret_result result = lisa_run(vm, 0);

if (!lisa_sched_empty(&vm->scheduler)) {

lisa_run_scheduler(vm);

}

return result;

```

- lisa/object.c — GC integration:

- Added #include "fiber.h"

- mark_object: added OBJ_FIBER case (marks stack, upvalues, frames, entry, result) and OBJ_CHANNEL case (marks value, sender, receiver)

- mark_roots: added loop over gc->all_fibers to mark all fibers

- free_object: added OBJ_FIBER case (calls lisa_fiber_free_stacks) and OBJ_CHANNEL case

- lisa_print_object: added <fiber> and <channel> print cases

- Added rebuild_fiber_list after sweep

- lisa_gc_init: initializes all_fibers = NULL

- lisa/Makefile — Added fiber.c to SOURCES

- lisa/examples/ping-pong.lisa — Two fibers playing ping-pong over channels (5 rounds)

- lisa/examples/producer-consumer.lisa — Producer sends 1..10, consumer sums to 55

- lisa/examples/fan-out.lisa — 5 workers compute fib(15..19), results collected = 9959

- lisa/examples/pipeline.lisa — Three-stage pipeline: generate → square → print

- lisa/examples/sieve.lisa — Concurrent prime sieve (CSP-style, after Hoare/Go)

4. Errors and Fixes:

- Circular dependency / typedef redefinition: fiber.h and vm.h both tried to typedef lisa_vm. Fixed by putting forward declarations in object.h (earliest in include chain) and removing duplicates from fiber.h.

- lisa_call_frame unknown type in fiber.h: Forward declared as typedef struct lisa_call_frame lisa_call_frame; in fiber.h, and made the definition in vm.h use typedef struct lisa_call_frame { ... } lisa_call_frame;.

- Forward declaration ordering: lisa_native_fn used lisa_vm* before it was declared. Fixed by moving forward declarations to top of object.h.

- Naked function with non-asm statements: (void)save_sp; (void)restore_sp; in naked functions caused clang error. Fixed by using __attribute__((unused)) on parameters instead.

- Segfault on first fiber test: Main fiber's c_sp was NULL when yield_to_scheduler tried to context-switch. Root cause: main fiber uses OS stack, can't context-switch away. Fixed by redesigning yield_to_scheduler to run scheduler loop inline for the main fiber.

- scheduler_step changing main fiber state: When scheduler_step dequeued the main fiber, it set state to FIBER_RUNNING, causing the yield_to_scheduler while loop (while state != FIBER_READY) to never exit. Fixed by not changing state in scheduler_step when the dequeued fiber is the main fiber.

- fan-out wrong answer (20905 vs 9959): def n i creates a global, so all spawned fibers shared the last value (19). Fixed by using spawn-one helper function that captures n via function parameter (upvalue).

- Example name collisions: def loop used in multiple places created global collisions. Fixed by using unique names like ping-loop, pong-loop, etc.

- Sieve deadlock: Two issues: (1) scheduler_step state bug (fixed above), (2) def p and def nch in recursive sieve created globals overwritten on each call, corrupting filter closures. Fixed by using spawn-filter helper that captures values as function parameters. Still deadlocks for larger limits (works for limit=3).

5. Problem Solving:

- All basic fiber/channel operations work correctly (simple send/recv, producer-consumer, fan-out, pipeline, ping-pong)

- JIT + fibers works (fib computed inside spawned fiber)

- Both debug (-O0 -g -Werror) and release (-O2) builds compile cleanly and pass tests

- The sieve example works for small limits (e.g., 3 → prints 2, 3) but deadlocks for larger limits (e.g., 30). This is an ongoing issue likely related to complex multi-fiber scheduling with many channels.

- The deadlock message loops infinitely because yield_to_scheduler prints and returns (native_recv returns LISA_NIL, interpreter continues, hits recv again). Need to either abort or propagate error.

6. All User Messages:

- "Implement the following plan: [detailed fiber/channel implementation plan with 8 steps, verification criteria, and link to planning transcript]"

- "continue." (after assistant paused mid-implementation when making call_value changes)

- "let's generate some example files in an examples directory."

7. Pending Tasks:

- The sieve example deadlocks for limits > 3. It works for limit=3 (prints "2 3") but fails for limit=30. The small test was just run and succeeded. Need to test with progressively larger limits to find where it breaks.

- The deadlock detection needs improvement — currently prints repeatedly and doesn't halt execution.

8. Current Work:

I was testing example files in lisa/examples/. The sieve example (examples/sieve.lisa) was deadlocking with generate source 30. After fixing the closure capture issue (using spawn-filter helper), I tested with a smaller limit of 3 and it succeeded:

```

$ timeout 3 bin/lisa --no-jit /tmp/sieve_small.lisa

2

3

```

I was about to test with progressively larger limits to find the threshold where the sieve breaks, and then debug the scheduling issue.

9. Optional Next Step:

Continue debugging the sieve example by testing with progressively larger limits (5, 7, 10, etc.) to find where the deadlock occurs, then trace the scheduling to identify the root cause. The user's request was "let's generate some example files in an examples directory" — the ping-pong, producer-consumer, fan-out, and pipeline examples all work. The sieve is the remaining example that needs fixing for larger inputs.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: <claude-internal>

Please continue the conversation from where we left off without asking the user any further questions. Continue with the last task that you were asked to work on.

Bash — Check current small sieve test file
{
  "command": "cd ~/Documents/Code/Github/lang/cj && cat /tmp/sieve_small.lisa",
  "description": "Check current small sieve test file"
}
result
(def generate (fn [ch limit]
  (def gen-loop (fn [i]
    (if (<= i limit)
      (do (send ch i) (gen-loop (+ i 1))))))
  (gen-loop 2)
  (send ch 0)))

(def filter-stage (fn [in out prime]
  (def filt-loop (fn []
    (def x (recv in))
    (if (= x 0)
      (send out 0)
      (do
        (if (= (% x prime) 0)
          nil
          (send out x))
        (filt-loop)))))
  (filt-loop)))

(def spawn-filter (fn [in out prime]
  (spawn (fn [] (filter-stage in out prime)))))

(def sieve (fn [ch]
  (def p (recv ch))
  (if (= p 0)
    nil
    (do
      (println p)
      (def nch (chan))
      (spawn-filter ch nch p)
      (sieve nch)))))

(def source (chan))
(spawn (fn [] (generate source 3)))
(sieve source)

1→#include "fiber.h"
     2→#include "vm.h"
     3→#include "jit.h"
     4→#include <stdlib.h>
     5→#include <string.h>
     6→#include <stdio.h>
     7→
     8→#if defined(__unix__) || defined(__APPLE__)
     9→#include <sys/mman.h>
    10→#include <unistd.h>
    11→#endif
    12→
    13→#define FIBER_C_STACK_SIZE (64 * 1024) /* 64 KB per fiber */
    14→
    15→/* ===== Context Switch (inline assembly) ===== */
    16→
    17→#if defined(__aarch64__) || defined(_M_ARM64)
    18→
    19→/*
    20→ * lisa_fiber_switch(void **save_sp, void *restore_sp)
    21→ *
    22→ * Saves callee-saved registers (x19-x28, x29/fp, x30/lr) onto the current
    23→ * stack, stores SP into *save_sp, loads SP from restore_sp, restores regs,
    24→ * and returns (via restored x30).
    25→ */
    26→__attribute__((naked))
    27→void lisa_fiber_switch(void **save_sp __attribute__((unused)),
    28→                       void *restore_sp __attribute__((unused))) {
    29→    __asm__ volatile(
    30→        "stp x19, x20, [sp, #-16]!\n"
    31→        "stp x21, x22, [sp, #-16]!\n"
    32→        "stp x23, x24, [sp, #-16]!\n"
    33→        "stp x25, x26, [sp, #-16]!\n"
    34→        "stp x27, x28, [sp, #-16]!\n"
    35→        "stp x29, x30, [sp, #-16]!\n"
    36→        /* Save SP into *save_sp (x0) */
    37→        "mov x2, sp\n"
    38→        "str x2, [x0]\n"
    39→        /* Load SP from restore_sp (x1) */
    40→        "mov sp, x1\n"
    41→        /* Restore callee-saved regs */
    42→        "ldp x29, x30, [sp], #16\n"
    43→        "ldp x27, x28, [sp], #16\n"
    44→        "ldp x25, x26, [sp], #16\n"
    45→        "ldp x23, x24, [sp], #16\n"
    46→        "ldp x21, x22, [sp], #16\n"
    47→        "ldp x19, x20, [sp], #16\n"
    48→        "ret\n"
    49→    );
    50→}
    51→
    52→#elif defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_X64)
    53→
    54→__attribute__((naked))
    55→void lisa_fiber_switch(void **save_sp __attribute__((unused)),
    56→                       void *restore_sp __attribute__((unused))) {
    57→    __asm__ volatile(
    58→        /* Save callee-saved regs */
    59→        "pushq %%rbp\n"
    60→        "pushq %%rbx\n"
    61→        "pushq %%r12\n"
    62→        "pushq %%r13\n"
    63→        "pushq %%r14\n"
    64→        "pushq %%r15\n"
    65→        /* Save RSP into *save_sp (rdi) */
    66→        "movq %%rsp, (%%rdi)\n"
    67→        /* Load RSP from restore_sp (rsi) */
    68→        "movq %%rsi, %%rsp\n"
    69→        /* Restore callee-saved regs */
    70→        "popq %%r15\n"
    71→        "popq %%r14\n"
    72→        "popq %%r13\n"
    73→        "popq %%r12\n"
    74→        "popq %%rbx\n"
    75→        "popq %%rbp\n"
    76→        "retq\n"
    77→    );
    78→}
    79→
    80→#endif
    81→
    82→/* ===== C Stack Allocation ===== */
    83→
    84→static void *alloc_c_stack(size_t *out_size) {
    85→#if defined(__unix__) || defined(__APPLE__)
    86→    long page_size = sysconf(_SC_PAGESIZE);
    87→

result
460→ */
   461→static bool scheduler_step(lisa_vm *vm) {
   462→    lisa_fiber *next = lisa_sched_dequeue(&vm->scheduler);
   463→    if (!next) return false;
   464→    if (next->state == FIBER_DONE) return true; /* skip, try next */
   465→
   466→    /* If the dequeued fiber is the main fiber, don't touch its state —
   467→       yield_to_scheduler will see state == FIBER_READY and exit its loop. */
   468→    if (next == vm->main_fiber) {
   469→        return true;
   470→    }
   471→
   472→    next->state = FIBER_RUNNING;
   473→    lisa_fiber_restore(vm, next);
   474→
   475→    /* Set global vm pointer for trampoline entry (new fibers) */
   476→    g_trampoline_vm = vm;
   477→
   478→    /* Switch C stacks: save main fiber's SP, jump to next fiber */
   479→    lisa_fiber_switch(&vm->main_fiber->c_sp, next->c_sp);
   480→
   481→    /* Back on main fiber's C stack. Restore main fiber as current. */
   482→    lisa_fiber_restore(vm, vm->main_fiber);
   483→    return true;
   484→}
   485→
   486→/* ===== Yield to Scheduler ===== */
   487→
   488→static void yield_to_scheduler(lisa_vm *vm) {
   489→    lisa_fiber *current = vm->current_fiber;
   490→    lisa_fiber_save(vm);
   491→
   492→    if (current == vm->main_fiber) {
   493→        /*
   494→         * Main fiber: can't context-switch away (uses OS stack).
   495→         * Instead, run the scheduler loop inline until we're woken up.
   496→         * Another fiber will set our state to FIBER_READY and enqueue us.
   497→         */
   498→        while (current->state != FIBER_READY) {
   499→            if (!scheduler_step(vm)) {
   500→                /* Queue empty but we're still suspended — deadlock */
   501→                fprintf(stderr, "deadlock: main fiber blocked with no runnable fibers\n");
   502→                return;
   503→            }
   504→        }
   505→        /* Woken up. Restore our state. */
   506→        current->state = FIBER_RUNNING;
   507→        lisa_fiber_restore(vm, current);
   508→    } else {
   509→        /* Spawned fiber: switch C stacks to return to the scheduler
   510→           (which is running on the main fiber's OS stack). */
   511→        lisa_fiber_switch(&current->c_sp, vm->main_fiber->c_sp);
   512→        /* Resumed by the scheduler */
   513→    }
   514→}
   515→
   516→/* ===== Scheduler Run Loop ===== */
   517→
   518→static void scheduler_run(lisa_vm *vm) {
   519→    while (!lisa_sched_empty(&vm->scheduler)) {

Restore main fiber as current. */\n    lisa_fiber_restore(vm, vm->main_fiber);\n    return true;\n}",
  "new_string": "static int g_debug_sched = 0;\n\nstatic bool scheduler_step(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    lisa_fiber *next = lisa_sched_dequeue(&vm->scheduler);\n    if (!next) return false;\n    if (g_debug_sched) fprintf(stderr, \"[sched] dequeued fiber %p state=%d (main=%p)\\n\",\n        (void*)next, next->state, (void*)vm->main_fiber);\n    if (next->state == FIBER_DONE) return true; /* skip, try next */\n\n    /* If the dequeued fiber is the main fiber, don't touch its state —\n       yield_to_scheduler will see state == FIBER_READY and exit its loop. */\n    if (next == vm->main_fiber) {\n        if (g_debug_sched) fprintf(stderr, \"[sched] skipping main fiber (state=%d)\\n\", next->state);\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    next->state = FIBER_RUNNING;\n    lisa_fiber_restore(vm, next);\n\n    /* Set global vm pointer for trampoline entry (new fibers) */\n    g_trampoline_vm = vm;\n\n    /* Switch C stacks: save main fiber's SP, jump to next fiber */\n    if (g_debug_sched) fprintf(stderr, \"[sched] switching to fiber %p\\n\", (void*)next);\n    lisa_

  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.c",
  "old_string": "lisa_value native_send(lisa_vm *vm, int argc, lisa_value *args) {\n    (void)argc;\n    if (!IS_CHANNEL(args[0])) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"send: first argument must be a channel\\n\");\n        return LISA_NIL;\n    }\n    lisa_channel *ch = AS_CHANNEL(args[0]);\n    lisa_value val = args[1];\n\n    if (ch->receiver) {\n        /* A receiver is waiting — hand off directly */\n        lisa_fiber *recv_fiber = ch->receiver;\n        ch->receiver = NULL;\n        recv_fiber->result = val;\n        recv_fiber->state = FIBER_READY;\n        lisa_sched_enqueue(&vm->scheduler, recv_fiber);\n        return LISA_NIL;\n    }\n\n    /* No receiver — block the sender */\n    lisa_fiber *current = vm->current_fiber;\n    ch->sender = current;\n    ch->value = val;\n    current->state = FIBER_SUSPENDED;\n\n    yield_to_scheduler(vm);\n\n    return LISA_NIL;\n}",
  "new_string": "lisa_value native_send(lisa_vm *vm, int argc, lisa_value *args) {\n    (void)argc;\n    if (!IS_CHANNEL(args[0])) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"send: first argument must be a channel\\n\");\n        return LISA_NIL;\n    }\n    lisa_channel *ch = AS_CHANNEL(args[0]);\n    lisa_value val = args[1];\n\n    if (ch->receiver) {\n        /* A receiver is waiting — hand off directly */\n        lisa_fiber *recv_fiber = ch->receiver;\n        ch->receiver = NULL;\n        recv_fiber->result = val;\n        recv_fiber->state = FIBER_READY;\n        lisa_sched_enqueue(&vm->scheduler, recv_fiber);\n        if (g_debug_sched) fprintf(stderr, \"[send] ch=%p handed off to fiber %p, sender fiber=%p\\n\",\n            (void*)ch, (void*)recv_fiber, (void*)vm->current_fiber);\n        return LISA_NIL;\n    }\n\n    /* No receiver — block the sender */\n    lisa_fiber *current = vm->current_fiber;\n    ch->sender = current;\n    ch->value = val;\n    current->state = FIBER_SUSPENDED;\n\n    if (g_debug_sched) fprintf(stderr, \"[send] ch=%p blockin

Edit — lisa/fiber.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.c",
  "old_string": "lisa_value native_recv(lisa_vm *vm, int argc, lisa_value *args) {\n    (void)argc;\n    if (!IS_CHANNEL(args[0])) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"recv: first argument must be a channel\\n\");\n        return LISA_NIL;\n    }\n    lisa_channel *ch = AS_CHANNEL(args[0]);\n\n    if (ch->sender) {\n        /* A sender is waiting — take the value and wake it */\n        lisa_fiber *send_fiber = ch->sender;\n        lisa_value val = ch->value;\n        ch->sender = NULL;\n        ch->value = LISA_NIL;\n        send_fiber->state = FIBER_READY;\n        lisa_sched_enqueue(&vm->scheduler, send_fiber);\n        return val;\n    }\n\n    /* No sender — block the receiver */\n    lisa_fiber *current = vm->current_fiber;\n    ch->receiver = current;\n    current->state = FIBER_SUSPENDED;\n\n    yield_to_scheduler(vm);\n\n    /* Resumed — result was placed in fiber->result by the sender */\n    return vm->current_fiber->result;\n}",
  "new_string": "lisa_value native_recv(lisa_vm *vm, int argc, lisa_value *args) {\n    (void)argc;\n    if (!IS_CHANNEL(args[0])) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"recv: first argument must be a channel\\n\");\n        return LISA_NIL;\n    }\n    lisa_channel *ch = AS_CHANNEL(args[0]);\n\n    if (ch->sender) {\n        /* A sender is waiting — take the value and wake it */\n        lisa_fiber *send_fiber = ch->sender;\n        lisa_value val = ch->value;\n        ch->sender = NULL;\n        ch->value = LISA_NIL;\n        send_fiber->state = FIBER_READY;\n        lisa_sched_enqueue(&vm->scheduler, send_fiber);\n        if (g_debug_sched) fprintf(stderr, \"[recv] ch=%p took value from fiber %p, recv fiber=%p\\n\",\n            (void*)ch, (void*)send_fiber, (void*)vm->current_fiber);\n        return val;\n    }\n\n    /* No sender — block the receiver */\n    lisa_fiber *current = vm->current_fiber;\n    ch->receiver = current;\n    current->state = FIBER_SUSPENDED;\n\n    if (g_debug_sc
... (truncated)

The file lisa/fiber.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Now let me also add tracing to the spawn native so we can track fiber IDs, and enable debug mode via an env var.

Edit — lisa/fiber.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.c",
  "old_string": "lisa_value native_spawn(lisa_vm *vm, int argc, lisa_value *args) {\n    if (argc < 1 || !IS_CLOSURE(args[0])) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"spawn: first argument must be a function\\n\");\n        return LISA_NIL;\n    }\n    lisa_obj_closure *closure = AS_CLOSURE(args[0]);\n    int fn_argc = argc - 1;\n    lisa_value *fn_args = args + 1;\n\n    lisa_fiber *fiber = lisa_new_fiber(vm, closure, fn_argc, fn_args);\n    lisa_sched_enqueue(&vm->scheduler, fiber);\n\n    return LISA_OBJ(fiber);\n}",
  "new_string": "lisa_value native_spawn(lisa_vm *vm, int argc, lisa_value *args) {\n    if (argc < 1 || !IS_CLOSURE(args[0])) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"spawn: first argument must be a function\\n\");\n        return LISA_NIL;\n    }\n    lisa_obj_closure *closure = AS_CLOSURE(args[0]);\n    int fn_argc = argc - 1;\n    lisa_value *fn_args = args + 1;\n\n    lisa_fiber *fiber = lisa_new_fiber(vm, closure, fn_argc, fn_args);\n    lisa_sched_enqueue(&vm->scheduler, fiber);\n\n    if (g_debug_sched) fprintf(stderr, \"[spawn] fiber %p (closure %p)\\n\",\n        (void*)fiber, (void*)closure);\n    return LISA_OBJ(fiber);\n}"
}
result
The file lisa/fiber.c has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit

Now enable via env var and add tracing to yield_to_scheduler deadlock path:

Edit — lisa/fiber.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.c",
  "old_string": "static int g_debug_sched = 0;",
  "new_string": "static int g_debug_sched = -1; /* -1 = uninitialized */\n\nstatic void init_debug_sched(void) {\n    if (g_debug_sched < 0) {\n        const char *env = getenv(\"LISA_DEBUG_SCHED\");\n        g_debug_sched = (env && env[0] == '1') ? 1 : 0;\n    }\n}"
}
result

}

⚙ Read

Now I can see the bug! Let me trace through the key part of the output:

After main prints 2 and spawns filter-2, filter-2 first correctly does recv on source channel (0x600003bc5950). But after being woken up with value 4, filter-2's next recv goes to channel_1 (0x600003bc5860) instead of source!

The root cause: (def filt-loop ...) inside filter-stage creates a global variable. When filter-stage is called the second time (for filter-3), it overwrites the global filt-loop with a new closure that has different upvalues (in = channel_1 instead of source). When filter-2 recurses via (filt-loop), it does a global lookup and gets the wrong closure!

This is the same class of bug as the previous p/nch global issue. The fix: restructure filter-stage to be directly recursive, avoiding inner def closures that create globals.

Let me also fix the deadlock handler — currently it just prints and returns, causing an infinite spawn loop. Let me read the current sieve example and fix both issues.

Read — lisa/examples/sieve.lisa
{
  "file_path": "lisa/examples/sieve.lisa"
}
result
1→; Concurrent prime sieve (CSP-style, after Hoare / Go).
     2→;
     3→; Each prime gets its own fiber that filters multiples
     4→; from its input channel and forwards non-multiples
     5→; to the next stage.
     6→
     7→(def generate (fn [ch limit]
     8→  (def gen-loop (fn [i]
     9→    (if (<= i limit)
    10→      (do (send ch i) (gen-loop (+ i 1))))))
    11→  (gen-loop 2)
    12→  (send ch 0))) ; sentinel
    13→
    14→(def filter-stage (fn [in out prime]
    15→  (def filt-loop (fn []
    16→    (def x (recv in))
    17→    (if (= x 0)
    18→      (send out 0)
    19→      (do
    20→        (if (= (% x prime) 0)
    21→          nil
    22→          (send out x))
    23→        (filt-loop)))))
    24→  (filt-loop)))
    25→
    26→; Capture in/out/prime via function args so the spawn closure
    27→; holds upvalues (not globals that get overwritten).
    28→(def spawn-filter (fn [in out prime]
    29→  (spawn (fn [] (filter-stage in out prime)))))
    30→
    31→(def sieve (fn [ch]
    32→  (def p (recv ch))
    33→  (if (= p 0)
    34→    nil
    35→    (do
    36→      (println p)
    37→      (def nch (chan))
    38→      (spawn-filter ch nch p)
    39→      (sieve nch)))))
    40→
    41→(def source (chan))
    42→(spawn (fn [] (generate source 30)))
    43→(sieve source)
    44→
⚙ Write

Now let me fix the sieve — make filter-step and gen-step directly recursive (no inner closures that become conflicting globals):

Write — lisa/examples/sieve.lisa
{
  "file_path": "lisa/examples/sieve.lisa",
  "content": "; Concurrent prime sieve (CSP-style, after Hoare / Go).\n;\n; Each prime gets its own fiber that filters multiples\n; from its input channel and forwards non-multiples\n; to the next stage.\n;\n; Important: inner recursive closures via `def` create globals\n; that get overwritten when multiple fibers run concurrently.\n; We avoid this by making helper functions directly recursive\n; with all state passed as parameters.\n\n(def gen-step (fn [ch limit i]\n  (if (<= i limit)\n    (do (send ch i) (gen-step ch limit (+ i 1)))\n    (send ch 0))))\n\n(def filter-step (fn [in out prime]\n  (def x (recv in))\n  (if (= x 0)\n    (send out 0)\n    (do\n      (if (= (% x prime) 0)\n        nil\n        (send out x))\n      (filter-step in out prime)))))\n\n(def spawn-filter (fn [in out prime]\n  (spawn (fn [] (filter-step in out prime)))))\n\n(def sieve (fn [ch]\n  (def p (recv ch))\n  (if (= p 0)\n    nil\n    (do\n      (println p)\n      (def nch (chan))\n      (spawn-filter ch nch p)\n      (sieve nch)))))\n\n(def source (chan))\n(spawn (fn [] (gen-step source 30 2)))\n(sieve source)\n"
}
result
The file lisa/examples/sieve.lisa has been updated successfully.
⚙ Edit, Bash ×3

Now let me also fix the deadlock handler to abort instead of returning (which causes the infinite loop):

Edit — lisa/fiber.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.c",
  "old_string": "        while (current->state != FIBER_READY) {\n            if (!scheduler_step(vm)) {\n                /* Queue empty but we're still suspended — deadlock */\n                fprintf(stderr, \"deadlock: main fiber blocked with no runnable fibers\\n\");\n                return;\n            }\n        }",
  "new_string": "        while (current->state != FIBER_READY) {\n            if (!scheduler_step(vm)) {\n                /* Queue empty but we're still suspended — deadlock */\n                fprintf(stderr, \"deadlock: main fiber blocked with no runnable fibers\\n\");\n                exit(70);\n            }\n        }"
}

⚙ Bash

The sieve works! All primes up to 30 are printed correctly. The debug trace shows it's working. Let me also test with a larger limit and with JIT:

  "description": "Test sieve with limit=100"
}

⚙ Edit ×2

Works with JIT too. Now let me remove the debug tracing and clean up:

Edit — lisa/fiber.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.c",
  "old_string": "static int g_debug_sched = -1; /* -1 = uninitialized */\n\nstatic void init_debug_sched(void) {\n    if (g_debug_sched < 0) {\n        const char *env = getenv(\"LISA_DEBUG_SCHED\");\n        g_debug_sched = (env && env[0] == '1') ? 1 : 0;\n    }\n}\n\nstatic bool scheduler_step(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    init_debug_sched();\n    lisa_fiber *next = lisa_sched_dequeue(&vm->scheduler);\n    if (!next) return false;\n    if (g_debug_sched) fprintf(stderr, \"[sched] dequeued fiber %p state=%d (main=%p)\\n\",\n        (void*)next, next->state, (void*)vm->main_fiber);\n    if (next->state == FIBER_DONE) return true; /* skip, try next */\n\n    /* If the dequeued fiber is the main fiber, don't touch its state —\n       yield_to_scheduler will see state == FIBER_READY and exit its loop. */\n    if (next == vm->main_fiber) {\n        if (g_debug_sched) fprintf(stderr, \"[sched] skipping main fiber (state=%d)\\n\", next->state);\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    next->state = FIBER_RUNNING;\n    lisa_fiber_restore(vm, next);\n\n    /* Set global vm pointer for trampoline entry (new fibers) */\n    g_trampoline_vm = vm;\n\n    /* Switch C stacks: save main fiber's SP, jump to next fiber */\n    if (g_debug_sched) fprintf(stderr, \"[sched] switching to fiber %p\\n\", (void*)next);\n    lisa_fiber_switch(&vm->main_fiber->c_sp, next->c_sp);\n\n    /* Back on main fiber's C stack. Restore main fiber as current. */\n    if (g_debug_sched) fprintf(stderr, \"[sched] returned from fiber %p (state=%d)\\n\",\n        (void*)next, next->state);\n    lisa_fiber_restore(vm, vm->main_fiber);\n    return true;\n}",
  "new_string": "static bool scheduler_step(lisa_vm *vm) {\n    lisa_fiber *next = lisa_sched_dequeue(&vm->scheduler);\n    if (!next) return false;\n    if (next->state == FIBER_DONE) return true; /* skip, try next */\n\n    /* If the dequeued fiber is the main fiber, don't touch its state —\n       yield_to_sch
... (truncated)

Edit — lisa/fiber.c
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/fiber.c",
  "old_string": "        if (g_debug_sched) fprintf(stderr, \"[send] ch=%p handed off to fiber %p, sender fiber=%p\\n\",\n            (void*)ch, (void*)recv_fiber, (void*)vm->current_fiber);\n        return LISA_NIL;",
  "new_string": "        return LISA_NIL;"
}
