human

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:

Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. Context from previous session: The conversation is a continuation of a previous session. The summary at the start tells us that:

- def inside functions was changed to create local variables instead of globals

- Several bugs were found and fixed: JIT register cache vs GET_LOCAL, self-referencing closures, uninitialized memory reads, upvalue clobber in end_scope_with_result, JIT SET_LOCAL stale cache

- A new opcode OP_CLOSE_UPVALUES_AT was added

- All files in lisa/ are untracked by git

2. First message in this session: I provided a summary of completed work. The user confirmed "good" and asked to create docs.

3. Docs creation: User asked for "a docs dir with comprehensive, but terse, all lowercase docs." I explored the codebase thoroughly using an Agent, read compiler.c, vm.c, value.h, object.h, ast.h, examples, etc. Created four doc files:

- lisa/docs/language.md - syntax, special forms, operators, built-ins, values, tail calls

- lisa/docs/concurrency.md - fibers, channels, CSP patterns

- lisa/docs/cli.md - usage, flags, exit codes

- lisa/docs/internals.md - architecture, file map, nan-boxing, opcodes, gc, jit, scoping

4. User asked "did you notice any issues?": I found stale comment in sieve.lisa (lines 7-10 about def creating globals being a workaround) and fixed it.

5. User pushed harder: "so the whole lisa codebase is good? no issues?": I did a thorough code review and found two real bugs:

- GC doesn't mark globals: mark_roots in object.c doesn't walk global_names/global_values. The loop in vm.c:779-785 was supposed to do this but has an empty body.

- lisa_take_string memory leak: When creating a new interned string, allocate_string copies chars into flexible array but never frees the original buffer passed from lisa_take_string.

6. User said "yes" to fix:

- Fixed lisa_take_string by adding free(chars) after allocate_string returns

- Fixed GC globals by adding global_names, global_values, global_capacity fields to lisa_gc struct, marking them in mark_roots, setting them in VM's GC trigger and sync_gc_roots

7. User asked for JIT/no-JIT benchmark comparison: Built release binary, benchmarked all examples + compute-heavy benchmarks (fib(35), tail-loop(5M), string-build(10K)). Initial results showed string benchmark using 53MB with JIT vs 2.6MB without.

8. User asked "any simple fix for this?": Diagnosed that the JIT trampoline never triggers GC. First attempted fix: GC check at top of trampoline loop - didn't help because old frame slots still on stack at that point. Moved GC check after memmove - still didn't help because allocation happens INSIDE the JIT function call (via native str), not between trampoline iterations. Added debug printf to confirm GC only fires once at 50MB. Final fix: added GC check in lisa_jit_call_helper after native function calls return. This brought JIT memory from 53MB down to 2.7MB (matching VM's 2.6MB).

9. User: "alright, is lisa done then?": I gave an honest assessment - solid feature set, suggested possible extensions but said none needed for a successful demonstration.

10. User asked for conversation log formatting for blog post: Asked to format the complete log from claude logs in an explorable way without revealing filesystem info. I:

- Found 8 session JSONL files

- Wrote lisa/docs/format_log.py to convert JSONL to HTML

- Generated lisa/docs/conversation.html (2.6MB, 8 sessions)

- Sanitized paths, stripped thinking blocks, stripped system reminders

11. User: "can we collapse all the tool and work output by default": Rewrote the format_log.py to group consecutive tool_use/tool_result blocks into collapsible <details> work sections. Reduced visible conversation from 665 messages to 129.

12. User: "also collapse the intermediate thinking steps": Added post-processing that folds assistant narration text into adjacent work groups when the text immediately precedes a work group. The narration becomes the work group's description, visible only when expanded.

13. User: "did you ensure there's no sensitive information?": Audited thoroughly. Found leaks:

- Username <user> in ls output and .claude paths

- Session UUIDs in tool results

- `<task-output> paths

Added more sanitization patterns and SCRUB_WORDS = ['<user>']. Got to 0 real leaks (remaining hits were self-referential audit commands).

14. User: "can we have the session navigation fixed on the left side": Started modifying the CSS/HTML layout to use a fixed sidebar. I edited the CSS in generate_html to add .layout, .sidebar, .main classes with fixed positioning, and responsive design (hide sidebar on narrow screens, show inline nav). I was in the middle of this edit when the summary was requested.

Current state of the format_log.py edit: I changed the CSS section to add sidebar layout styles but haven't yet updated the HTML template to use the new .layout, .sidebar, and .main structure. The CSS is updated but the HTML body still uses the old <nav class="session-nav"> inline navigation.

Files modified in this session:

- lisa/docs/language.md (created)

- lisa/docs/concurrency.md (created)

- lisa/docs/cli.md (created)

- lisa/docs/internals.md (created)

- lisa/examples/sieve.lisa (removed stale comment)

- lisa/object.c (take_string leak fix, GC globals marking, GC debug print added/removed)

- lisa/object.h (added global fields to lisa_gc)

- lisa/vm.c (GC globals sync, GC in trampoline, GC in jit_call_helper)

- lisa/docs/format_log.py (created, then rewritten multiple times)

- lisa/docs/conversation.html (generated multiple times)

Summary:

1. Primary Request and Intent:

The user's requests across this session were:

- Create comprehensive but terse, all-lowercase documentation in a docs/ directory

- Identify any issues in the Lisa codebase (asked twice with increasing scrutiny)

- Fix the identified bugs (GC globals marking, lisa_take_string memory leak)

- Create a JIT vs no-JIT runtime/memory benchmark comparison of all examples

- Fix the 53MB memory blowup in JIT mode for string-heavy workloads

- Assess whether Lisa is "done"

- Format the complete multi-session conversation log into an explorable HTML document for a blog post, without leaking filesystem/personal information

- Collapse all tool/work output by default so only the conversation is visible

- Also collapse intermediate assistant narration/thinking steps by default

- Ensure no sensitive information in the generated HTML

- Make the session navigation fixed on the left side when scrolling (current/in-progress task)

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- Lisa: a Lisp with bytecode compiler, stack-based VM, and optional JIT (x86-64/ARM64)

- NaN-boxed 64-bit value representation (lisa_value = uint64_t)

- Mark-and-sweep GC with roots: stack, open upvalues, all fibers, globals

- String interning with hash table

- JIT trampoline for tail calls (jit_trampoline in vm.c)

- JIT call helper (lisa_jit_call_helper) for calling functions from JIT code

- sync_gc_roots() to synchronize GC root pointers before collection

- Cooperative concurrency via fibers and channels (CSP-style)

- Claude Code JSONL conversation log format (types: user, assistant, system, progress, file-history-snapshot, queue-operation)

- HTML <details> elements for collapsible content (collapsed by default without open attribute)

3. Files and Code Sections:

- lisa/docs/language.md (created)

- Comprehensive language reference: syntax, special forms (def/fn/let/if/do), operators, built-in functions, value types, tail calls

- lisa/docs/concurrency.md (created)

- Fibers, channels, send/recv/spawn/yield API, CSP patterns (producer-consumer, pipeline, fan-out)

- lisa/docs/cli.md (created)

- CLI usage, flags (--no-jit), exit codes (0/64/65/70/74)

- lisa/docs/internals.md (created)

- Architecture diagram, file map, NaN-boxing layout, heap objects, all opcodes, GC, JIT, scoping mechanics

- lisa/examples/sieve.lisa (edited)

- Removed stale comment (lines 7-10) about def creating globals being a workaround — no longer true after the def-as-local change

- lisa/object.h (edited)

- Added global variable table fields to lisa_gc struct for GC marking:

```c

/* Global variable table — set by the VM before collection */

lisa_obj_string **global_names;

lisa_value *global_values;

int global_capacity;

```

- lisa/object.c (edited — 3 changes)

- Fixed lisa_take_string memory leak by freeing the original buffer:

```c

lisa_obj_string *lisa_take_string(lisa_gc *gc, char *chars, int length) {

uint32_t h = hash_string(chars, length);

lisa_obj_string *interned = string_table_find(gc, chars, length, h);

if (interned != NULL) {

free(chars);

return interned;

}

lisa_obj_string *str = allocate_string(gc, chars, length, h);

free(chars); // <-- NEW: free the original buffer after copying into flexible array

return str;

}

```

- Added global marking to mark_roots:

```c

/* Mark global variable names and values */

for (int i = 0; i < gc->global_capacity; i++) {

if (gc->global_names[i] != NULL) {

mark_object((lisa_obj*)gc->global_names[i]);

mark_value(gc->global_values[i]);

}

}

```

- Initialized new GC fields in lisa_gc_init:

```c

gc->global_names = NULL;

gc->global_values = NULL;

gc->global_capacity = 0;

```

- lisa/vm.c (edited — 4 changes)

- Replaced empty globals loop in GC trigger (vm.c ~778) with proper sync:

```c

vm->gc.global_names = vm->global_names;

vm->gc.global_values = vm->global_values;

vm->gc.global_capacity = vm->global_capacity;

```

- Updated sync_gc_roots to include globals:

```c

static void sync_gc_roots(lisa_vm *vm) {

vm->gc.stack = vm->stack;

vm->gc.stack_count = (int)(vm->stack_top - vm->stack);

vm->gc.open_upvalues = vm->open_upvalues;

vm->gc.global_names = vm->global_names;

vm->gc.global_values = vm->global_values;

vm->gc.global_capacity = vm->global_capacity;

}

```

- Added GC check in jit_trampoline after memmove (for tail-call-heavy code):

```c

frame->closure = closure;

frame->ip = closure->function->chunk.code;

if (vm->gc.bytes_allocated > vm->gc.next_gc) {

sync_gc_roots(vm);

lisa_gc_collect(&vm->gc);

}

```

- Added GC check in lisa_jit_call_helper after native function calls (the critical fix for string memory):

```c

if (IS_OBJ(callee) && OBJ_TYPE(callee) == OBJ_NATIVE) {

if (vm->gc.bytes_allocated > vm->gc.next_gc) {

sync_gc_roots(vm);

lisa_gc_collect(&vm->gc);

}

return vm->stack_top[-1];

}

```

- lisa/docs/format_log.py (created, then rewritten multiple times)

- Converts Claude Code JSONL conversation logs to HTML

- Key features: path sanitization, username scrubbing, UUID removal, thinking block hiding, system-reminder stripping

- Groups consecutive tool_use/tool_result blocks into collapsible <details class="work-group"> sections

- Folds assistant narration text into adjacent work groups (post-processing pass)

- Currently being modified to add fixed sidebar navigation

- The CSS has been updated with .layout, .sidebar, .main classes but the HTML template has NOT yet been updated to use them

- lisa/docs/conversation.html (generated)

- 8 sessions, 131 visible conversation messages, 567 collapsed work groups, ~2.5MB

4. Errors and Fixes:

- GC not marking globals: mark_roots in object.c didn't walk global variable table. The VM had an empty loop (vm.c:779-785) that was clearly meant to do this but the body was never written. Fixed by adding global fields to lisa_gc struct and marking them in mark_roots.

- lisa_take_string memory leak: allocate_string copies chars into a flexible array member but never frees the original buffer. When called from lisa_take_string (which is supposed to take ownership), the original malloc'd buffer leaked. Fixed by adding free(chars) after allocate_string returns.

- 53MB JIT memory blowup on string benchmark: Three attempts to fix:

1. GC check at top of trampoline loop — failed because old frame slots (including old acc string) were still on the stack below stack_top, keeping them marked as roots

2. GC check after memmove in trampoline — failed because the string allocation happens INSIDE the JIT function call (via lisa_jit_call_helper → native_str), not between trampoline iterations. Added debug printf to lisa_gc_collect confirming GC only fired once at 50MB.

3. GC check in lisa_jit_call_helper after native calls return — success. This is where str and other allocating natives return to. GC now fires ~2858 times for 10K iterations, collecting almost everything each time. Memory dropped from 53MB to 2.7MB.

- Sensitive info in HTML: Found username <user> in ls outputs and .claude/projects/ paths, session UUIDs in JSONL filenames, messageId UUIDs. Fixed by adding more PATH_REPLACEMENTS patterns and SCRUB_WORDS = ['<user>'] to the sanitizer.

5. Problem Solving:

- All bugs identified and fixed: GC globals, take_string leak, JIT GC triggering

- Stale comment in sieve.lisa removed

- Conversation log formatter created with privacy sanitization, collapsible work groups, and narration folding

- Benchmarking confirmed JIT provides 1.9-5.3x speedup on compute-bound code, comparable memory after fixes

- Final benchmark results after all fixes:

```

fib(35): 1070ms vm, 550ms jit (1.9x speedup), same memory

tail-loop(5M): 160ms vm, 30ms jit (5.3x speedup), same memory

string-build(10K): 60ms vm, 60ms jit (1.0x), same memory (~2.7MB)

```

6. All User Messages:

- "good. let's add a docs dir with comprehensive, but terse, all lowercase docs."

- "did you notice any issues while working on this task?"

- "so the whole lisa codebase is good? no issues?"

- "yes." (to fix the two bugs)

- "looks like a successful experiment in creating the language, right? make a jit/no-jit runtime/mem comparison of the examples."

- "any simple fix for this?" (about the 53MB JIT memory)

- "continue." (after assistant was debugging the GC trigger location)

- "alright, is lisa done then? seems like a job well done!"

- "very good. proud moment: you wrote all of that code. i would like to write a blog post about our exploration together. can we format this conversations complete log from the claude logs in a way that is both explorable and informative for explorers and doesn't give too much information about my filesystem etc.?"

- "much better. also collapse the intermediate thinking steps by default please."

- "did you ensure there's no sensitive information in there?"

- "can we have the session navigation fixed on the lefgt side when scrolling for quicker navigation?"

2. Move nav links into the sidebar

4. Optionally add JS to highlight the active session in the sidebar while scrolling

{
  "file_path": "lisa/docs/format_log.py"
}
result
1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→Convert Claude Code JSONL conversation logs into a readable HTML document.
     4→
     5→Usage: python3 format_log.py [-o output.html] logfile1.jsonl [logfile2.jsonl ...]
     6→
     7→Strips filesystem paths, hides thinking blocks. Tool calls and results are
     8→grouped into collapsible "work" sections so the reader sees only the
     9→conversation by default and can expand to see what Claude did.
    10→"""
    11→
    12→import json
    13→import sys
    14→import os
    15→import re
    16→import html
    17→import argparse
    18→
    19→# paths and identifiers to strip from output
    20→PATH_REPLACEMENTS = [
    21→    (re.compile(r'~/]+/Documents/Code/Github/lang/cj/lisa/'), 'lisa/'),
    22→    (re.compile(r'~/]+/Documents/Code/Github/lang/cj/'), 'cj/'),
    23→    (re.compile(r'~/]+/\.claude/[^\s"\']+'), '<claude-internal>'),
    24→    (re.compile(r'~/]+/'), '~/'),
    25→    (re.compile(r'~/]+/'), '~/'),
    26→    (re.compile(r'/tmp/lisa_bench_\w+\.lisa'), '<benchmark>'),
    27→    # temp task output paths
    28→    (re.compile(r'/private/tmp/claude-\d+/[^\s]+'), '<task-output>'),
    29→    # claude project slugs containing username-derived paths
    30→    (re.compile(r'-Users-[A-Za-z0-9_]+-Documents-[^\s"\'*/]+'), '<project>'),
    31→    # session UUIDs (8-4-4-4-12 hex)
    32→    (re.compile(r'[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}'), '<uuid>'),
    33→    # username from ls -la output (e.g. "<user>  staff")
    34→    (re.compile(r'(?<=\d )\S+(?=\s+staff\s)'), 'user'),
    35→]
    36→
    37→# additional words to scrub (usernames, hostnames, etc.)
    38→SCRUB_WORDS = ['<user>']
    39→
    40→# session titles (order matches chronological sort)
    41→SESSION_TITLES = [
    42→    "exploring cj",
    43→    "building lisa: bytecode vm",
    44→    "tail call optimization",
    45→    "whole-function jit",
    46→    "jit rewrite: register cache + inline fast paths",
    47→    "fibers and channels",
    48→    "string primitives + json parser",
    49→    "def as local + bug fixes + docs + gc fixes",
    50→]
    51→
    52→
    53→def sanitize(text):
    54→    """Strip filesystem paths and sensitive words."""
    55→    for pattern, replacement in PATH_REPLACEMENTS:
    56→        text = pattern.sub(replacement, text)
    57→    for word in SCRUB_WORDS:
    58→        text = text.replace(word, '<user>')
    59→    return text
    60→
    61→
    62→def escape(text):
    63→    """HTML-escape and sanitize."""
    64→    return sanitize(html.escape(text))
    65→
    66→
    67→def format_code_block(text, lang=''):
    68→    """Wrap text in a <pre><code> block."""
    69→    return f'<pre><code class="{lang}">{escape(text)}</code></pre>'
    70→
    71→
    72→def render_markdown(text):
    73→    """Render simple markdown to HTML."""
    74→    text = sanitize(text)
    75→    lines = text.split('\n')
    76→    out = []
    77→    in_code = False
    7
... (truncated)

Edit — lisa/docs/format_log.py
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "lisa/docs/format_log.py",
  "old_string": "    parts.append('</body></html>')",
  "new_string": "    # add JS to highlight active session in sidebar while scrolling\n    parts.append(\"\"\"\n<script>\n(function() {\n    var links = document.querySelectorAll('.sidebar a[data-session]');\n    var headers = document.querySelectorAll('.session-header');\n    if (!links.length || !headers.length) return;\n    var ticking = false;\n    window.addEventListener('scroll', function() {\n        if (!ticking) {\n            ticking = true;\n            requestAnimationFrame(function() {\n                var scrollY = window.scrollY + 80;\n                var active = 0;\n                for (var i = 0; i < headers.length; i++) {\n                    if (headers[i].offsetTop <= scrollY) active = i;\n                }\n                for (var j = 0; j < links.length; j++) {\n                    links[j].classList.toggle('active', j === active);\n                }\n                ticking = false;\n            });\n        }\n    });\n    // initial highlight\n    if (links[0]) links[0].classList.add('active');\n})();\n</script>\n\"\"\")\n    parts.append('</div></div></body></html>')"
}
